- **java — visitor-interface Javadoc emits raw `</ul>` / `</ol>` from backticked HTML element references, breaking checkstyle**: the visitor-bridge codegen routed Rust callback docstrings through `sanitize_rust_idioms` (which strips backticks) but skipped the `escape_javadoc_line` pass that wraps backticked code spans in `{@code ...}` and HTML-escapes bare `<`, `>`, `&`. Generated `HtmlVisitor.java` produced `/** Called after processing a list \`</ul>\` or \`</ol>\`. */` (with the backticks discarded by minijinja escaping), which checkstyle's Javadoc parser rejected with `no viable alternative at input '</'` — the bare `</ul>` looked like an unbalanced HTML element. Fixed by applying `escape_javadoc_line` per line in `sanitize_callback_doc`, mirroring the FFI-class doc emission path. Backticked spans now render as `{@code &lt;/ul&gt;}` and bare angle brackets are HTML-escaped, restoring checkstyle's parser. (`src/backends/java/gen_visitor/helpers.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

<!-- wasm-r3 -->
- **e2e/typescript,wasm — mock-server pattern tests time out due to missing SUT_URL env var**: when running e2e tests against a mock-server (the "mock-server pattern"), the globalSetup.ts set only `MOCK_SERVER_URL` to the server's base URL. However, HTTP test code generated from TypeScript fixtures uses `process.env.SUT_URL` as the default server address (not `MOCK_SERVER_URL`). For WASM and other backends that fall back to the mock-server pattern when the App class is excluded from bindings, all tests would timeout trying to connect to the default port (8001) instead of the dynamically-allocated mock-server port, causing 100% test failure. Fixed by setting both `MOCK_SERVER_URL` and `SUT_URL` to the same value in globalSetup.ts, ensuring tests connect to the correct server regardless of which env var they check. Wasm e2e tests are now restored to 556/556 pass (was 0/556 after the round-2 fixes). (`src/e2e/templates/typescript/globalSetup.ts.jinja`, `src/e2e/fixture.rs`)

<!-- elixir-alias-r3 -->
- **e2e/elixir — harness app_class reference is unqualified (App instead of Spikard.App)**: the Elixir e2e server-pattern harness template correctly applied a module prefix to `RouteBuilder`, `Method`, and `ServerConfig` classes (e.g., `Spikard.RouteBuilder`), but the `app_class` alias and all method calls like `App.new()` and `App.config(...)` remained unqualified. When a binding exports its App class under a package namespace (e.g., `Spikard.App`), the harness would emit `alias App` and fail with `(UndefinedFunctionError) function App.new/0 is undefined`. Additionally, the Jinja2 template used `{%-` whitespace stripping that incorrectly combined the method-enum alias with the conditional server-config alias onto one line. Fixed by: (1) computing `app_class_name` by prepending `module_prefix` to the unqualified app class (mirroring how other classes are prefixed), (2) using `{% if ... -%}...{% endif %}` syntax with leading/trailing strippers on the conditional lines to preserve proper line breaks, ensuring each alias statement gets its own line. Harnesses now correctly emit `alias Spikard.App` and call `Spikard.App.new()` / `Spikard.App.config(...)`. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/elixir/app_harness.exs.jinja`)

## [0.23.8] - 2026-06-05

### Fixed

<!-- java-visit-result-identifiers -->
- **java — generated `VisitResult` sealed interface emits invalid identifiers and reserved keywords**: tuple-variant enum payloads (e.g. `Custom(String)`) were rendered as `record Custom(String 0)` because the Rust IR represents unnamed tuple fields with positional names (`0`, `_0`, `1`, ...) and the Java visitor codegen routed those names through `to_java_name` unchanged. Likewise the static factory for the `Continue` variant emitted `static VisitResult continue()`, which is a reserved Java keyword. Generated files failed `javac` with `not a statement` and `<identifier> expected` errors. Fixed by routing tuple-field names through a new `java_payload_field_name` helper that substitutes the synthetic `value` identifier when the field name is a positional index (matching the established Go backend pattern from v0.22.28), and by escaping factory method names that collide with `JAVA_KEYWORDS` with a trailing underscore (`continue_`). (`src/backends/java/gen_visitor/files.rs`)

<!-- go-non-opaque-no-serde-methods -->
- **go — emitted method calls to non-existent `_to_json` / `_from_json` FFI helpers for non-opaque types without serde derives**: the Go binding's method wrapper always marshals a non-opaque receiver through `C.{prefix}_{type}_from_json(...)` and converts Named return values via `C.{prefix}_{type}_to_json(...)`. However, the FFI backend only emits those helpers when the underlying Rust type carries `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]` — types with manual `serde::Serialize` / `serde::Deserialize` impls (e.g. `html_to_markdown::NodeContext<'a>`, which hand-rolls serde to handle the borrowed `Cow<'_, str>` fields and lifetime-erased `Deserialize for 'static`) are classified as `has_serde = false`. The Go binding emitted calls like `C.htm_node_context_from_json(...)` and `C.htm_node_context_to_json(...)` that no FFI symbol provides, breaking `cgo` linking. Fixed by skipping method emission for non-opaque types whose `has_serde` flag is false; such types are still reachable via the visitor flow, which uses a separate JSON serialization path generated in `visitor.go`. (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

<!-- rustler-visit-result-atoms -->
- **rustler — generated Elixir `apply_visitor_callback` case-clause emits invalid atom syntax for capitalized variant names**: the visitor-helper template emitted unquoted atoms `:Continue`, `:Skip`, `:PreserveHtml` for `VisitResult` unit variants whose `wire_name` retained the Rust PascalCase form. In Elixir, atoms beginning with an uppercase letter must be quoted (`:"Continue"`) — the unquoted form is a compile error (`syntax error before: 'Continue'`). Generated `html_to_markdown.ex` failed to compile, blocking the Elixir binding. Fixed by emitting the quoted atom form `:"<wire_name>"` in the case-clause template, which is universally safe across snake_case and PascalCase wire names. (`src/backends/rustler/template_env.rs`)

<!-- pyo3-overload-templates -->
- **pyo3 — runtime panic on template lookup for converters/overload_*.jinja**: templates for `@overload` decorators on `_to_rust_*_config` helpers were defined in the template directory but not embedded in the alef binary via `include_str!()` or registered in the `TEMPLATES` array in `template_env.rs`. At runtime, the pyo3 backend's code generation would panic with "template converters/overload_none.jinja not found". Fixed by adding both templates to the TEMPLATES array with their respective `include_str!()` embeds. (`src/backends/pyo3/template_env.rs`)

<!-- swift-harness-serve -->
- **e2e/swift — harness probe and SUT_URL hardcoded to port 8009 instead of configured harness.port**: the Swift test setup class spawned the Harness subprocess and probed `http://127.0.0.1:8009/` to wait for readiness, while simultaneously setting the `SUT_URL` environment variable to the same hardcoded port. However, `alef.toml` configured `[crates.e2e.harness] port = 8000`, so the Harness binary was actually listening on port 8000. Tests would timeout waiting for port 8009 to respond, fail with `XCTUnwrap failed: expected non-nil value of type NSHTTPURLResponse`, and all 558 tests would be marked as failures. Fixed by capturing the `e2e_config.harness.host` and `e2e_config.harness.port` in the `render_test_file` function and interpolating them into the probe URL and SUT_URL environment string, so the test setup correctly targets the configured harness port. The generated tests now use `http://127.0.0.1:8000` (the actual configured port) instead of the hardcoded `8009`. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

<!-- pyo3-mypy-overloads -->
- **pyo3 — mypy cannot narrow `_to_rust_*_config` return types in conditional expressions**: the generated `_to_rust_process_config(value)` converter helpers had return type `ProcessConfig | None`, but mypy could not narrow this to `ProcessConfig` when the caller checked `if value is not None` and passed the result directly. Code like `_to_rust_process_config(config) if config is not None else ProcessConfig()` still reported a mypy error: `expected ProcessConfig, got ProcessConfig | None`. This broke downstream `prek run --all-files mypy` in tslp after alef 0.23.7. Fixed by emitting `@overload` decorators on the `_to_rust_*_config` helpers so mypy can properly narrow return types: an overload for `def _to_rust_{name}(value: None) -> None: ...` and one for `def _to_rust_{name}(value: Config | dict | str) -> _rust.Config: ...`, with the main implementation following both. Mypy now understands that non-None inputs produce non-None outputs, eliminating false positives in conditional narrowing. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

<!-- node-compression-wire -->
- **e2e/node — compression fixture assertions fail with `Z_DATA_ERROR: incorrect header check`**: the Node.js e2e harness attempted to validate per-route compression config but the framework's public `RouteBuilder` API exposed no `compression()` method. Compression was only configurable at the server level via `ServerConfig`, not per-route. Fixture JSON specified per-route `middleware.compression` config, and the test harness expected to wire it via `builder.compression(...)`, but calling a non-existent method would fail or be ignored. The root cause: `RouteBuilder` in the consumer framework crate (the public facade) lacked compression support, even though `CompressionConfig` existed and was configurable at the server level. Fixed by: (1) adding `compression: Option<CompressionConfig>` field to `RouteBuilder` struct in the consumer framework crate, (2) adding `pub fn compression(...)` method to `RouteBuilder` impl block, (3) including compression in the `into_metadata` method to propagate it to `RouteMetadata`, (4) adding `compression: Option<CompressionConfig>` field to `RouteMetadata` struct in the consumer crate (via serde skip-if-none), (5) updating the TypeScript e2e harness template to import and wire per-route compression middleware via `builder.compression(new CompressionConfig(...))`, matching the established pattern for CORS middleware. (`src/e2e/templates/typescript/app_harness.mjs.jinja`, consumer binding regeneration required)

<!-- node-cors-register -->
- **e2e/node — CORS preflight handler registration fails with 405 METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED**: the Node.js e2e harness template used `app.route(optionsBuilder, corsHandlerFn)` for CORS OPTIONS handlers. However, the NAPI-RS `App.route()` method is a single-argument decorator (`route(builder): (fn) => ...`) that returns a function, not a two-argument direct registration method. The two-argument direct form `app.register_route(builder, handler)` exists for explicit handler registration. Generated harnesses were silently ignoring the handler argument and never registering the OPTIONS handler, causing Axum to return 405 METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED for preflight requests instead of the expected 204 with CORS headers. Fixed by hardcoding `registerRoute` (the camelCased form of `register_route`) in the TypeScript e2e harness codegen, ensuring both normal route handlers and CORS preflight handlers use the correct two-argument explicit registration method. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/config.rs`)

<!-- ruby-cors-middleware -->
- **e2e/ruby — CORS fixture blob omits middleware field, returning 405 instead of 403/204**: the Ruby fixture JSON serializer in `render_app_harness` did not include the `middleware` field from the handler config. The app_harness.rb template consumes `handler_config[:middleware]` and threads CORS config through to the RouteBuilder, but received an empty dict when middleware was missing. Without CORS config wired to the route, Spikard core's automatic OPTIONS handler auto-generation never fired, and Axum returned 405 METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED for OPTIONS requests instead of 403 (invalid CORS) or 204 (valid preflight). Python's harness correctly included middleware via a `build_middleware_value` helper. Fixed by: (1) adding `build_middleware_value` helper to map the fixture's `HttpMiddleware` struct (with field name remapping: `allow_*` → `allowed_*` to match Magnus-bound `CorsConfig`), (2) including the middleware value in the handler object when serializing the fixture JSON. (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`)

<!-- dart-frb-handler-r2 -->
- **dart FRB — handler parameter executor rewrite passes Task to callback instead of invoking executor**: Round 1 (v0.23.7) changed `handler.executeSync(SyncTask(...))` to `handler(SyncTask(...))` to distinguish handler parameters from class field access. However, this is still incorrect: the handler callback parameter takes a String (the request JSON), not a `SyncTask` object. FRB's behavior is to serialize the callback and pass it to Rust, where Rust invokes it. The Dart wrapper should execute the task and return the result, not invoke the handler directly. Fixed by rewriting `handler.executeSync(Task(...))` → `Task(...).executeSync()` and `handler.executeNormal(Task(...))` → `await Task(...).executeNormal()` — moving the executor method call from the callback parameter to the task itself. This allows the task executor to manage the FFI call and handler serialization. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

<!-- python-cors-handler -->
- **e2e/python — CORS preflight handler always returns 403 due to reading kwargs instead of request dict**: the generated Python e2e harness `make_cors_preflight_handler` called `get_header_value(kwargs, "origin")` to extract CORS headers, but `kwargs` is always empty—the request data (including headers) is passed as a single positional argument `args[0]`. As a result, `origin` was always `""`, `is_origin_allowed` always returned `False`, and the handler always returned 403. Tests expecting 403 on invalid origins accidentally passed; tests expecting 204 on valid preflight requests failed. Fixed by extracting headers from the request dict at `request_data = args[0] or {}; headers_dict = request_data.get("headers", {})` and passing `headers_dict` to `get_header_value` instead of `kwargs`. (`src/e2e/templates/python/app_harness.py.jinja`)

<!-- pyo3-data-enum-kwargs -->
- **pyo3 data enums — generated constructor rejected idiomatic Python kwargs**: tagged-enum wrappers emitted from `pyo3_data_enum.jinja` (e.g. `PageAction`) had a single positional `value` parameter, so `PageAction(selector="#open", type="click")` raised `TypeError: __new__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'selector'`. Callers had to construct a dict and pass it positionally, which is not how PyO3 classes built from tagged enums are typically used in test fixtures and downstream code. Fixed by widening the `#[new]` signature to `#[pyo3(signature = (value=None, **kwargs))]`: when only kwargs are present, the constructor assembles them into a Python dict via `json.dumps` and feeds that to serde_json, identical to the dict-positional path. The existing string-positional shape (unit variants) and dict-positional shape continue to work. (`src/codegen/templates/generators/enums/pyo3_data_enum.jinja`)

<!-- go-e2e-mock-server-kill -->
- **e2e/go — `Test I/O incomplete` / `WaitDelay expired before I/O complete` on test exit**: the generated `main_test.go` `TestMain` ended with `os.Exit(m.Run())`, which skips deferred functions including `defer cmd.Process.Kill()` for the mock-server child. The child kept running with its inherited stderr fd alive, so the Go test runner reported the test binary as having incomplete I/O for 60 seconds and exited the job with status 201 even though every test had passed. Fixed by capturing the exit code first, explicitly killing and waiting on the mock-server process, then calling `os.Exit(code)` — mirroring the harness-spawn path that already did this correctly. The bootstrap `defer` is kept as panic-safety. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

<!-- elixir-app-alias -->
- **e2e/elixir — service module not found at runtime**: the Rustler service module was emitted at the top-level namespace (`defmodule App do`) instead of inheriting the package namespace like sibling modules (`<Prefix>.RouteBuilder`, `<Prefix>.Method`, etc.). When the e2e harness was spawned as a subprocess with `-pa` code paths, the unqualified module could not be resolved despite being present in the compiled ebin directories. Fixed by: (1) namespacing the service module under the package prefix when a module_prefix is present (e.g., `defmodule <Prefix>.App do` instead of `defmodule App do`), matching the structure of all other generated Elixir modules, (2) the e2e harness template now generates the correctly-qualified alias, which resolves successfully. This ensures the service module naming is consistent and resolvable in all loading contexts. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

<!-- java-native-bundle -->
- **java e2e — native library not bundled in JAR, `UnsatisfiedLinkError` at runtime**: the generated e2e `pom.xml` for Java was missing a build step to copy the native library (e.g. `lib{ffi_lib_name}.dylib`) from the Rust `target/release/` directory into the JAR resources at `natives/{os-arch}/lib{ffi_lib_name}.{dylib,so,dll}`. At runtime, `NativeLib.loadNativeLibrary()` failed with `Failed to load ... native library. Expected resource: /natives/{rid}/lib{ffi_lib_name}.dylib ... Ensure the library is bundled in the JAR` when the library was not found. Fixed by adding a `maven-antrun-plugin` execution in the `process-resources` phase that detects the OS and architecture, constructs the target RID (`macos-arm64`, `linux-x64`, etc.), and copies the native library from the local Rust build output into `src/main/resources/natives/{rid}/`. The copy step is guarded by `include_native_lib_path` so it only runs in local e2e test mode (registry-mode consumers pull pre-bundled JARs from Maven Central). Uses a parameterized template variable `{{ ffi_lib_name }}` (e.g. `{ffi_lib_name}`) instead of hardcoded library names. (`src/e2e/templates/java/pom.xml.jinja`, `src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **php — `Required parameter $X follows optional $Y` PHP 8.1 deprecation in facade wrappers**: when the static facade reordered nothing (Rust IR param order preserved) and an IR-required param followed an IR-optional `no_arg_constructor_types` param (e.g. a `CrawlEngineHandle` that has a no-arg `new()`), the wrapper emitted `?CrawlEngineHandle $engine = null, string $url` — flagged by phpdoc-lint and phpstan as PHP 8.1 deprecated. The downstream `?? new T()` coercion was a useless null-coalesce (`nullCoalesce.variable`). Fixed by reverse-walking the param list to compute a tail-optional flag: a param keeps its `= null` default only when every later param is also optional. The matching call-site coercion (`$p ?? new T()`) is now guarded by the same flag so a required wrapper param is passed as `$p`. (`src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

## [0.23.7] - 2026-06-05

### Fixed

- **java trait bridges — checkstyle LineLength and MethodLength violations in emitted Bridge.java files**: the Panama FFM trait bridge codegen emitted all vtable field layout declarations on a single line (e.g., 374 chars for OcrBackendBridge with 14 slots), exceeding the project checkstyle 200-char `LineLength` limit across all 6 trait bridges. Additionally, constructor bodies (inline upcall stub allocation) reached 162 lines in OcrBackendBridge, exceeding the 150-line `MethodLength` limit. Fixed by: (1) wrapping `MemoryLayout.structLayout(...)` field declarations across multiple lines in the trait_bridge.jinja template (changing from flat `{{ vtable_layout_fields }}` string to a looped `vtable_layout_fields_list` with 4-space indentation), (2) extracting the upcall stub initialization into a private `initializeStubs()` helper method that the constructor calls, reducing the constructor to 11 lines and keeping the helper at ~200 lines (within limits). Verified: `mvn checkstyle:check` now passes zero violations on all 6 trait bridge files (RendererBridge, ValidatorBridge, PostProcessorBridge, DocumentExtractorBridge, EmbeddingBackendBridge, OcrBackendBridge). (`src/backends/java/templates/trait_bridge.jinja`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs:626`)

- **e2e/go and e2e/dart — `MOCK_SERVERS=` sentinel line ignored, multi-page crawl fixtures 404 on discovered links**: the mock-server emits two startup sentinel lines on stdout — `MOCK_SERVER_URL=<shared-listener>` always, and `MOCK_SERVERS={"<fixture_id>":"<per-fixture-url>",...}` when any fixture has origin-root routes (e.g. inline `<a href="/page1">` anchors, or `/robots*` / `/sitemap*` discovery paths) that need a dedicated per-fixture listener. The Go harness (`main_test.go`) and Dart per-test setup only parsed the first line and broke out of the scanner loop, so fixtures whose seed page contains inline host-absolute anchors crawled the SUT against the shared listener — where root-relative `/page1` resolved to `MOCK_SERVER_URL/page1` (404) instead of the per-fixture listener that actually serves `/page1`. Symptom: every multi-page crawl test in Go and Dart returned `pages.len() == 1` (seed only). Fixed by: (Go) extending `main_test.go` to also parse `MOCK_SERVERS=`, set `MOCK_SERVERS` env, and export `MOCK_SERVER_<UPPER_FIXTURE_ID>` for each entry (matching the existing Python conftest and Ruby spec_helper pattern); (Dart) adding a top-level `Map<String, String> _fixtureUrls` populated by the spawn `setUpAll` body, a `_fixtureUrl(fixtureId)` helper that prefers the per-fixture URL (from `_fixtureUrls` or `Platform.environment['MOCK_SERVERS']`) and falls back to `${_sutUrl()}/fixtures/<id>`, and applying that helper to `mock_url` and `mock_url_list` arg emission. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **swift — optional parameters in free function forwarders lack `= nil` defaults**: Swift free function forwarders for public functions (e.g., `createClient(apiKey:, baseUrl:, timeoutSecs:, maxRetries:, modelHint:)`) had optional parameters declared without default values, requiring callers to pass explicit `nil` instead of omitting them. This caused e2e test compilation to fail with `error: missing arguments for parameters 'timeoutSecs', 'maxRetries', 'modelHint'` when the codegen emitted calls like `try createClient(apiKey: "test-key", baseUrl: url)`. Fixed by adding `= nil` default values to all optional parameters in both sync (`emit_single_free_function_forwarder`) and async (`emit_async_free_function_forwarder`) free function forwarders, matching Swift convention and allowing callers to omit optional parameters. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs` lines 3730–3733 and 3864–3866)

- **e2e/csharp — streaming aggregator vars declared but never updated**: after the 0.23.4 streaming codegen split, `render_streaming_test_method` declared `streamContent`/`chunks`/`streamComplete` in the chat-stream foreach body but emitted assertions referencing `lastFinishReason`, `toolCallsJson`, `toolCalls0FunctionName`, and `totalTokens` without ever declaring or assigning them — producing `CS0103: The name '…' does not exist in the current context` on every streaming fixture that asserts on finish reason or tool calls. Pre-scan the fixture's assertions for those four virtual fields and, when present, declare matching nullable locals and accumulate them inside the existing `if (choice != null)` block. `FinishReason` is the JSON-converter-driven enum, so the snake_case API value is recovered via `System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(...).Trim('"')`; `Usage.TotalTokens` is widened from `ulong` to `long?`. (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`)

<!-- kotlin-android-jvmtoolchain -->
- **kotlin_android — gradle test fails with javaLauncher property error when JDK 17 not installed**: the `build.gradle.kts` and e2e `build.gradle.kts` pinned `jvmToolchain(17)` to ensure compatibility with JDK 25+ host environments. However, when JDK 17 is not available locally and `org.gradle.java.installations.auto-detect` is not configured, Gradle attempts to download JDK 17 from a toolchain repository, which is not configured by default, causing: `ToolchainProvisioningException: Cannot find a Java installation on your machine ... matching: {languageVersion=17, ...}`. Additionally, the Android library's `build.gradle.kts` was hardcoded to `jvmToolchain(21)` instead of matching the e2e target (17). Fixed by: (1) aligning library `jvmToolchain(21)` → `jvmToolchain({android_jvm_target})` to use the same target as e2e (17), (2) adding `gradle.properties` to both library and e2e projects with `org.gradle.java.installations.auto-detect=true`, enabling Gradle to fall back to the host JDK when the requested version is not found. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_build_gradle.rs` line 141, `src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_gradle_properties.rs` new file, `src/backends/kotlin_android/mod.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/kotlin_android.rs`)

<!-- elixir-e2e-harness -->
- **e2e/elixir — harness has syntax error and calls undefined config method**: the Elixir server-pattern e2e harness template contained a Haskell-style comment (`{-# ... #}`) instead of an Elixir comment (`# ...`), causing a `TokenMissingError: missing terminator: }` at line 78 (end of file) because the parser expected the `{` to be closed. Additionally, the harness unconditionally called `App.config(app, config)` to bind the server to a configured port. When the consumer's `alef.toml` excludes `App.config` via `[crates.exclude].methods = ["App.config", ...]`, the method is not exposed in the Elixir binding, causing a runtime `(UndefinedFunctionError)` when the harness tries to call it. Fixed by: (1) correcting the comment syntax from Haskell to Elixir style, (2) threading `config: &ResolvedCrateConfig` through the harness renderer, (3) checking whether `App.config` is in the excluded methods list, and (4) emitting the config struct construction and config method call only when the method is available. Also added a missing `alias` for the app class. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/elixir/app_harness.exs.jinja`)

<!-- dart-frb-handler -->
- **dart FRB — handler executor call rewrite breaks class field access**: FRB 2.x generates `handler.executeSync()` and `handler.executeNormal()` calls for service methods, intending these for callback function parameters. The prior fix (v0.23.6) blindly rewrote all instances to direct invocations (`handler()`), but this broke methods that access the inherited class field `super.handler` (a `BaseHandler` object that is not directly callable). Methods without a `handler` parameter now preserve the original `handler.executeSync/executeNormal` method calls. Fixed by detecting whether `handler` appears in the function parameter list before applying the rewrite: only parameters are rewritten, class fields are preserved. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

<!-- go-e2e-harness -->
- **e2e/go — harness crashes on builder.Free() due to Rust FFI ownership transfer**: the Go e2e harness called `builder.Free()` after registering a route builder with the app. However, Rust FFI functions that use `Box::from_raw(builder)` take ownership of the builder and free it automatically. Calling builder.Free() afterward causes a double-free crash with `SIGABRT: abort`. Fixed by removing the builder.Free() call in the e2e harness template and adding a comment explaining that the builder ownership is transferred to the Rust FFI layer during registration. (`src/e2e/templates/go/harness_main.go.jinja`)

<!-- pyo3-e2e-harness -->
- **e2e/python — app harness calls undefined app.config when App.config is excluded**: the PyO3 Python server-pattern e2e harness generated code that unconditionally called `app.config(_config)` to bind the server to a configured port. When the consumer's `alef.toml` excludes `App.config` via `[crates.exclude].methods = ["App.config", ...]`, the method is not exposed in the Python binding, causing a runtime `AttributeError: 'App' object has no attribute 'config'`. Fixed by threading `config: &ResolvedCrateConfig` through the harness renderer, checking whether `App.config` is in the excluded methods list, and emitting the config call only when the method is available. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/config.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/python/app_harness.py.jinja`)

- **e2e/python — batch test fixtures pass dict literals instead of typed BatchFileItem/BatchBytesItem instances**: when fixture JSON contains arrays with `element_type` set (e.g., `element_type = "BatchFileItem"` for batch extraction calls), the Python e2e codegen was emitting raw dict literals like `[{"path": "...", "mime_type": "..."}, ...]` instead of constructor calls like `[BatchFileItem(path="...", mime_type="..."), ...]`. This caused `TypeError: argument 'items': 'dict' object is not an instance of 'BatchFileItem'` at test runtime because PyO3 bindings expect typed pyclass instances, not dicts. Fixed by adding `emit_python_typed_instance` helper to construct typed instances and updating `emit_json_object_arg` to use it when `element_type` is set. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/test_function.rs`)

- **pyo3 — excluded functions still emitted in api.py, **init**.py, and .pyi stubs**: functions listed in `[crates.python].exclude_functions` were correctly omitted from the native Rust module (`lib.rs`), but the public Python package generators (`api.py`, `__init__.py`, `_module.pyi`) iterated `api.functions` without applying the same filter. This caused `AttributeError: module '<consumer_pkg>._<module>' has no attribute '<name>'` at runtime because `api.py` generated a wrapper that delegated to the absent native function, and `__init__.py` re-exported it in `__all__`. Fixed by threading an `exclude_functions: &AHashSet<String>` parameter into `gen_api_py`, `gen_init_py`, and `gen_stubs`, and skipping excluded functions in all three generation loops. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/errors.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_stubs.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

<!-- napi-e2e-harness -->
- **e2e/napi — harness calls undefined app_run when App.run is excluded**: the NAPI service-API codegen generated a TypeScript `app_harness` that imported `app_run` from the native binding and a service wrapper (`service.ts`) that called `app_run(this._registrations)` for the `App.run` entrypoint. When the consumer's `alef.toml` excludes `App.run` via `[crates.exclude].methods = ["App.run", ...]`, the native function is never exported (because it's behind an excluded method), but the codegen still tried to import and call it, causing a runtime `TypeError: app_run is not a function`. Fixed by checking whether each entrypoint method is in the excluded methods list; if excluded, skip generating the import, the native function, and the TypeScript method wrapper entirely. The napi service API generator now takes `config: &ResolvedCrateConfig` and checks `config.exclude.methods` for each entrypoint. (`src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

<!-- wasm-e2e-harness -->
- **e2e/wasm — server-pattern harness fails when App class is excluded**: when wasm binding excludes the `App` type (because the service API is not exposed for wasm), the e2e code generator attempted to emit a server-pattern app harness that imported and used the missing `App` class, causing a `SyntaxError: The requested module does not provide an export named 'App'` at test setup time. The harness also imported from the wrong package (the wasm-binding package name instead of the actual wasm package name). Fixed by detecting when `App` is in the wasm backend's `exclude_types` and disabling the server-pattern harness, falling back to the mock-server pattern which does not require the app class. This allows wasm HTTP fixtures to run against a standalone mock-server binary instead of the application's app class. (`src/e2e/codegen/wasm.rs`)

<!-- swift-e2e-harness -->
- **swift e2e — harness binary path hardcoded to release build**: the generated XCTest setUp code hardcoded `.build/release/Harness`, but `swift test` performs a debug build by default, resulting in a file-not-found fatal error (`Error Domain=NSCocoaErrorDomain Code=4`). Changed the generated path to `.build/debug/Harness` to match the harness binary produced by the default debug build. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs:626`)

<!-- magnus-e2e-harness -->
- **e2e/ruby — app harness config method not exposed in Magnus wrapper**: the Ruby server-pattern e2e harness tried to call `APP.config(::ServerConfig.new(...))` to bind the server to a dynamic port within a retry loop. However, the Magnus binding for Ruby does not expose a `config` method on the App class; the Rust core provides it, but the Ruby wrapper only exposes route registration and run. Changed the harness to report the fixed port via HARNESS_PORT and skip the config call, relying on the consumer app's ServerConfig default (which may be environment-configured by the test orchestrator). This mirrors the Python harness pattern where port binding happens outside the harness subprocess. (`src/e2e/templates/ruby/app_harness.rb.jinja`)

- **napi plugin trait-bridge — accept both camelCase and snake_case method names**: the NAPI trait bridge validates that a JS object passed to `registerDocumentExtractor` or `registerRenderer` has all required methods, checking for the camelCase version (e.g., `extractBytes`). To support both JavaScript convention (camelCase) and Rust convention (snake_case) property names, the constructor now accepts either form. Method lookup (`get_named_property`) also tries both forms, falling back to snake_case if camelCase is not found. This allows plugin implementations to use either naming convention without validation failure. (`src/backends/napi/trait_bridge_constructor.jinja`, `src/backends/napi/templates/sync_method_*.jinja`, `src/backends/napi/templates/async_method_*.jinja`, `src/backends/napi/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **extendr — async function generation omits critical body parts**: `gen_extendr_json_bridged_function` used `format!()` macros with named placeholders (`{body_preamble}`, `{named_let_bindings}`, `{rt_new}`, `{core_call}`, `{convert}`) but only passed `err_map` and `result_convert` as named arguments. Missing parameters caused the formatter to panic or render empty strings, resulting in R async functions missing the `let result =` binding and final conversion logic. This produced syntax errors like "mismatched closing delimiter" in generated `lib.rs`. Fixed by adding all required parameters to the `format!()` calls: `body_preamble`, `named_let_bindings`, `rt_new`, `core_call`, and `convert`. (`src/backends/extendr/gen_bindings/mod.rs:2236–2265`)

- **swift backend — Vec<String> with &[&str] signature in free functions**: when a core function parameter is `&[&str]` and the Swift binding receives `Vec<String>` (deserialized via swift-bridge), `swift_call_arg` (used for free-function shim conversion) fell through to emit `&{name}` directly, producing a type mismatch (`Vec<String>` does not deref to `[&str]`). The method-wrapper path in `wrappers.rs` (lines 979–987) already had the correct conversion. Now `swift_call_arg` checks for `TypeRef::Vec(String)` with `p.is_ref && p.vec_inner_is_ref` and emits `&{name}.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect::<Vec<_>>()`, matching the method-wrapper pattern exactly. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/shims.rs:292–297`)

- **swift binding — Codable value struct incorrectly assigned isOwned**: when a free function returns `Vec<T>` where `T` is a Codable value struct (e.g., `PatternMatch`, a first-class DTO), the return-conversion suffix attempted `item.isOwned = false` on a type that has no `isOwned` field. Codable value structs (in `known_dto_names`) are immutable and do not have ownership semantics; only opaque classes (swift-bridge typealias'd types not in `known_dto_names`) have `isOwned`. Fixed the suffix emission in `forwarder_return_conversion_suffix_inner` and the loop-based conversion path in `emit_single_free_function_forwarder` to branch: Codable value types use simple conversion without `isOwned`, opaque classes assign `isOwned = false`. Also gated the dead variable with `#[allow(dead_code)]`. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs:3957–3966, 4319–4327`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/test_function.rs:998`)

- **swift backend — serde enum by-value parameters incorrectly rejected**: the `is_bridgeable_fn` filter in swift-generated Rust shims rejected functions with serde enums (unit enums) as parameters when either `p.is_ref` OR the enum lacked serde. This logic was too strict: by-value serde enums can be serialized across the FFI boundary, so they should only be rejected when the enum is passed by ref (cannot serialize refs) OR has no serde (cannot serialize). Fixed the condition to separate these cases: accept by-value serde enums, reject only refs and no-serde variants. This allows functions with enum parameters like `extractRegionWithVlm(region_kind: RegionKind)` to be bridged. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/shims.rs:38–45`)

- **dart FRB — Vec<String> with &[&str] signature in free functions**: when a core function parameter is `&[&str]` and the Dart-FRB binding receives `Vec<String>` (deserialized by FRB), `dart_call_arg` (used for free-function call site expressions) fell through to emit `&{name}` directly, producing a type mismatch (`Vec<String>` does not deref to `[&str]`). The opaque-method paths in `mod.rs` (lines 2359, 2545) already had the correct conversion. Now `dart_call_arg` checks for `TypeRef::Vec(String)` with `p.is_ref && p.vec_inner_is_ref` and emits a pre-call temporary: `&{name}.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect::<Vec<_>>()`. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/conversions.rs:252–254`)

- **sync-versions — zig hash version-component not refreshed when `version` field absent**: `sync_registry_package_versions` skipped any package entry that lacked an explicit `version` field, so `[crates.e2e.registry.packages.zig]` entries that carry only `hash = "<pkg>-<version>-<base64>"` never had their embedded version updated on workspace bumps. Result: every workspace bump after the first one left the zig hash advertising the previous RC, and `alef all` (and any `e2e/codegen/zig` consumer) then failed with `zig registry package hash is stale`. Added `extract_zig_hash_version` to derive the embedded version straight from the hash string for zig entries, so the version-substitution path runs even without an explicit `version` field. (`src/cli/pipeline/version.rs`)

- **jni backend — Vec<String> with &[&str] signature**: when a core function parameter is `&[&str]` and the JNI binding receives `Vec<String>` (deserialized from JSON), `emit_function_shim` in the free-function branch (`_ =>` fallback at line ~800) and multi-param branch (lines ~1050) incorrectly emitted `&{name}` directly, producing a type mismatch (`Vec<String>` does not deref to `[&str]`). The instance-method path (lines 999–1009) already had the correct conversion. Now both paths emit a pre-call binding `let {name}_refs: Vec<&str> = {name}.iter().map(String::as_str).collect();` and pass `&{name}_refs` as the call argument, matching the instance-method pattern exactly. (`src/backends/jni/gen_shims.rs`)

## [0.23.6] - 2026-06-05

### Fixed

- **e2e/typescript — embed_texts async override not applied**: the TypeScript e2e codegen read `call_config.r#async` (the base-level bool) when computing `call_is_async`, ignoring any per-language override's `async` field. A call with `async = false` at the base level and `async = true` in the `[overrides.node]` section produced a sync test body that called `await`-required async functions without `await`. Fixed by reading `call_config.overrides.get(lang).and_then(|o| o.r#async).unwrap_or(call_config.r#async)`. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`)

- **e2e/csharp — enum types wrongly excluded from trait stubs**: `collect_hidden_named_types` added any `TypeRef::Named` name to the excluded set when the name was not found in `type_by_name` (which only contains `TypeDef` entries, not `EnumDef` entries). As a result, enum-typed method fields like `OcrBackendType` and `ProcessingStage` were substituted with `string` in C# plugin-API stubs, causing CS0738 interface mismatch errors. Added `trait_bridge_excluded_type_names_with_enums` which receives a `known_enum_names` set and skips excluding names that correspond to known enums. Updated the C# e2e codegen to pass enum names through. (`src/e2e/codegen/recipe.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`)

- **kotlin-android — InternalDocument leaks into trait interface**: `emit_trait_methods` called `kotlin_type_str_pub` for method parameter and return types without substituting excluded/internal types. `InternalDocument`, excluded via `excluded_type_paths`, appeared verbatim in `IDocumentExtractor.kt`, causing a Kotlin compile error. Added `kotlin_type_str_visible` helper that substitutes any `Named` type not visible in the binding with `String`, matching the Java backend's `java_type_visible` pattern. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **dart FRB — await in non-async sync accessors**: `fix_handler_executor_calls` replaced `handler.executeSync(` with `await handler(`, but `executeSync` methods are synchronous and cannot use `await`. Changed the replacement to `handler(` (no `await`). Also tightened `ensure_handler_closures_are_async` to skip class/mixin declarations and to not add `async` to function signatures that already contain it. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

## [0.23.5] - 2026-06-05

### Fixed

- **r scaffold — DCF blank line between BugReports and License**: the `repository_lines` string for R DESCRIPTION files had a trailing `\n` after the `BugReports:` line, which inserted an empty line before `License:` in the DCF file. CRAN and `R CMD check` reject DESCRIPTION files with empty lines between fields. Removed the trailing newline. (`src/scaffold/languages/r.rs`)

## [0.23.4] - 2026-06-05

### Fixed

- **e2e/rust — keywords assertion field name**: use `extracted_keywords` instead of `result_keywords` in `render_keywords_assertion` and `render_keywords_count_assertion`. The generated Rust e2e test previously referred to a non-existent field `result_keywords`, causing a compile error in every fixture that asserts on keyword results. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/assertion_synthetic.rs`)

- **zig backend — vtable passed by pointer instead of by value**: the `register_fn_body.jinja` template emitted `&vtable` when calling the C FFI registration function, but the C ABI takes the vtable struct by value. Changed to `vtable` (no address-of). (`src/backends/zig/templates/register_fn_body.jinja`)

- **java backend — Javadoc @param names in snake_case**: `render_javadoc_sections` emitted Rust snake_case argument names (e.g., `mime_type`) directly as `@param` tags. Java checkstyle requires camelCase parameter names matching the Java binding (e.g., `mimeType`). Now converts each name with `heck::ToLowerCamelCase` before emitting. (`src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`)

- **csharp backend — CA2264 on enum parameters**: the null-check guard (`ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull`) was emitted for ALL required `TypeRef::Named` parameters, including enums. C# enums are value types and ThrowIfNull on them is a no-op that triggers CA2264. Now skips the null guard for any named type that appears in `enum_names`. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **pyo3 backend — SyntaxError for has-default params before required params**: `classify_pages` and `translate_result` have a required-by-IR `ExtractionResult` parameter that is given `| None = None` in the Python wrapper (because its type is in `default_types`). When that parameter appeared before a truly-required parameter like `config`, Python raised `SyntaxError: non-default argument follows default argument`. Fixed by pre-scanning params to add `is_has_default_param` items to `promoted_params` before partitioning into required/optional groups. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **rustler backend — &mut ExtractionResult param**: when a `default_types` param is both `is_ref` and `is_mut`, the codegen incorrectly emitted `{name}_core.as_ref().unwrap_or(&Default::default())` (immutable borrow) for a function that expects `&mut T`. Now emits a `let mut {name}_mut = {name}_core.unwrap_or_default(); &mut {name}_mut` binding. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **rustler backend — PathBuf::from(Option<String>) type error**: when a `TypeRef::Path` param is optional, the codegen emitted `PathBuf::from(param)` where `param: Option<String>`, but `PathBuf` does not implement `From<Option<String>>`. Now emits `param.map(std::path::PathBuf::from)` for optional-path params and `param.as_deref().map(std::path::Path::new)` when both optional and by-ref. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **rustler backend — non-exhaustive From<core::FormatMetadata> match**: when a flat data enum has excluded variants (cfg-gated with `binding_excluded`), the generated `From<core::FormatMetadata>` match is non-exhaustive at compile time when those features are enabled. Now emits a `#[allow(unreachable_patterns)] _ => Self::default()` wildcard arm when `enum_def.excluded_variants` is non-empty. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **e2e/csharp — streaming test codegen generic-stream leakage**: detect chat-completion-shaped streams (with assertions on `stream_content`, `finish_reason`, `tool_calls`, or `usage`) separately from non-chat streams (CrawlEvent, etc. with custom `result_fields`). When a streaming fixture has no chat-completion assertions, skip the hardcoded `chunk.Choices[0].Delta.Content` check and the `streamContent` StringBuilder; instead emit only `chunks.Add(chunk)` in the loop. Also wrap `mock_url_list` arguments in the request type (e.g., `new BatchCrawlStreamRequest { Urls = urls }`) before passing to the streaming method, fixing CS1503 type-mismatch errors. Eliminates generic-codegen shape bleed where every streaming type was treated as chat-completion regardless of actual item type. (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`)

- **csharp backend — opaque streaming static wrapper doc prefix**: iterate `method.doc.lines()` and prefix each with `///` instead of inlining the entire multi-line doc after a single `///`. The previous emission embedded raw `\n` characters in the format string, leaving continuation paragraphs of multi-line rustdoc without their `///` prefix and breaking C# parsing on every opaque streaming wrapper (e.g. `KreuzcrawlLib.CrawlStreamAsync`). (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **csharp backend — multi-paragraph doc comments with intra-doc links**: preserve blank lines and backticks in rustdoc to prevent `/// ` prefix loss on continuation paragraphs. The `sanitize_doc_for_csharp()` helper was over-aggressively stripping ALL backticks and filtering blank lines, causing C# XML doc parser errors (CS1002, CS1519, CS1056) on multi-paragraph docstrings with intra-doc links like `` [`CrawlEvent`] ``. Now delegates proper sanitization to `emit_csharp_doc()` which handles Rust-idiom stripping and XML escaping correctly. Added test verifying multi-paragraph docs with intra-doc links round-trip correctly. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`, `src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`)

## [0.23.3] - 2026-06-04

### Fixed

- **ffi/napi backends — Cow<str> param passing**: emit `{name}.into()` (owned) or `{name}.map(std::borrow::Cow::Owned)` (optional) when the IR records `core_wrapper = CoreWrapper::Cow` for a `String`-typed parameter. Previously both backends passed the raw `String` value directly, causing E0308 type mismatch when the Rust core function expects `Cow<'_, str>` or `Option<Cow<'_, str>>`. The extractor now sets `ParamDef.core_wrapper = CoreWrapper::Cow` when the original type is `Cow<'_, str>` (detected by inspecting the syn AST for the `Cow<'_, str>` path before type erasure). (`src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/functions.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **ffi/napi backends — BTreeMap param passing**: emit `.into_iter().collect::<std::collections::BTreeMap<_, _>>()` when the IR records `map_is_btree = true` for a `Map`-typed parameter. Previously both backends passed `HashMap` values directly to functions expecting `BTreeMap`, causing E0308 type mismatch. The extractor now sets `ParamDef.map_is_btree = true` when the original type is `BTreeMap` (detected by inspecting the container ident before type erasure). For `is_ref = true` params the collection is wrapped in `&` to produce `&BTreeMap<_, _>`, extending the temporary to the enclosing call statement. (`src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/functions.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **php backend — `attributes()` return type BTreeMap→HashMap**: add explicit `TypeRef::Map` arm to `php_wrap_return` so methods returning `&BTreeMap` get `.iter().map(|(k, v)| (k.clone(), v.clone())).collect::<HashMap<_, _>>()` and methods returning owned `BTreeMap` get `.into_iter().collect::<HashMap<_, _>>()`. Previously `Map` fell through to the catch-all arm, emitting an empty conversion suffix and producing `&BTreeMap<String, String>` where the PHP binding declared `HashMap<String, String>`, causing E0308 type mismatch for any method that returns a map (e.g. `attributes()` on `NodeContext`). (`src/backends/php/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **napi/php backends — usize/u64 cast in `From` impl**: preserve `as {core_type}` cast for `TypeRef::Primitive` fields that require widening/narrowing when `cast_large_ints_to_i64 = true`. Previously the `TypeRef::Primitive(_) | TypeRef::String | TypeRef::Unit` match arm was shared, so splitting off `TypeRef::String` / `TypeRef::Unit` broke the i64→usize cast path. Now `TypeRef::Primitive(p)` is its own arm that checks `needs_i64_cast(p)` and emits `val.{field} as {core_type}` when casting is needed. Fixes `From<JsNodeContext>` passing `val.depth: i64` where `usize` was expected. (`src/codegen/conversions/binding_to_core.rs`, `src/codegen/conversions/mod.rs`)

- **dart backend**: emit `build.rs` with rustfmt-canonical let-chain layout so CI's `cargo fmt --check` passes without manual fixup. When a file write error occurs in `fix_handler_executor_calls()` / `patch_published_loader()`, convert nested `if let Err(...) { ... }` into a let-chain with the opening brace on its own line (`if X && let Err(...)\n{`), matching rustfmt 1.9.0+ output for Rust 2024 edition. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs:540-548`)

- **e2e/swift**: replace the read-only `ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment["SUT_URL"] = "…"` assignment in the generated harness with a nested `withCString { … setenv(key, url, 1) }` block. The swift compiler rejects subscript-assignment on `ProcessInfo`'s read-only dictionary; the C `setenv` actually mutates the process environment so subsequent `getenv("SUT_URL")` / `ProcessInfo` lookups see the URL. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs:677-682`)

- **extract/functions**: skip private functions at the extraction boundary. Previously `extract_function()` only checked visibility at the call site, so private helpers could slip into the IR in edge cases where the call-site filter didn't fire — and once in the IR, any backend (FFI, Go, Python, …) would emit wrappers / C-header declarations for symbols that don't exist in the consumer's compiled crate, producing `undefined symbol` link errors. Now `extract_function` defensively returns `None` whenever `!is_pub(item.vis)`, ensuring private items can never be associated with any type or emitted by any backend. (`src/extract/extractor/functions.rs:50`)

- **e2e/elixir**: register routes at `/fixtures/{fixture_id}` only, dropping the spurious `route` field concatenation. The previous template emitted `"/fixtures/#{fixture_id}#{route}"`, producing paths like `/fixtures/abc/api/data` while every test request just hits `/fixtures/abc` — all routes returned 404. The handler's `route` field describes the intended target route the fixture simulates (for assertion/reference), not the path to register on the SUT. (`src/e2e/templates/elixir/app_harness.exs.jinja:55`)

- **e2e/java**: discover an available port dynamically via a `ServerSocket` probe in the generated `HarnessMain` when `SUT_URL` is not preset, then emit `SUT_URL=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` on stdout immediately before blocking on `app.run()`. Previously, sequential test fixtures spawned the harness on a fixed port and the second spawn failed with `Address already in use`, never emitting `SUT_URL` and tripping the 15s timeout. Mirrors the ruby fix from `58741afc8`. (`src/e2e/templates/java/harness_main.jinja:47-51`)

- **dart backend (FRB)**: automatically strip the invalid `async` keyword from FRB-generated class declarations in the alef-emitted `build.rs` post-processor (`fix_handler_executor_calls`), eliminating the need for consumers to apply their own band-aid fix. FRB 2.x emits `class RustLibApiImpl implements RustLibApi async {` whenever the base class has async methods, but `async` is only valid on function/method declarations in dart, so the bridge fails to compile. Same rewrite was already present in `frb_rewrite.rs:522`; this fix adds it to the build.rs path so consumers no longer have to maintain a parallel patch. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs:538`)

- **e2e/python — CORS OPTIONS method casing**: use `getattr(method_enum_class, "OPTIONS", None)` (uppercase, HTTP convention) instead of `"Options"` (PascalCase). The pyo3 enum exposes the variant as `Method.OPTIONS`, so the previous lookup returned `None` and the preflight handler never registered, falling through to a 405. (`src/e2e/templates/python/app_harness.py.jinja:173`)

- **e2e/python — CORS OPTIONS**: port the typescript harness's case-insensitive header lookup helper to the python `app_harness.py.jinja`, and serialize `handler.parameters` into the per-fixture JSON so the generated OPTIONS handler can rebuild the same `params_schema_json` (and therefore extract the right headers) as the underlying handler. Partial fix — 4 CORS preflight fixtures still return 405 instead of 403/204 because `getattr(Method, "Options", None)` returns `None` when the pyo3 enum exposes the variant as `Method.OPTIONS` (uppercase); follow-up will reconcile the Method-enum casing convention on the python side. (`src/e2e/templates/python/app_harness.py.jinja`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/config.rs`)

- **magnus backend (ruby)**: two interrelated scoping fixes so consumer code can reference `<Module>::App` after the native extension loads. (1) Always register opaque types in the magnus module-init even when they have no instance methods — previously the `class_used` predicate excluded opaque types with all methods filtered out, leaving the class unregistered and unreachable as a Ruby constant. (2) Wrap the generated `service.rb` class definition in a `module <Module>` block instead of defining it at top level, so consumers can reach the service via `<Module>::App`. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/functions.rs:1019`, `src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **e2e validation (global `result_fields` fallback)**: `field_is_explicit_result_mapping` now consults the effective `result_fields` via `E2eConfig::effective_result_fields(call_config)` instead of the per-call `call_config.result_fields` set directly. Previously, fixtures asserting on common response fields (`usage.total_tokens`, `finish_reason`, `tool_calls`) failed the recipe validator with `requires assertion recipe 'streaming'` even when the consumer's `alef.toml` declared those names in the global `[crates.e2e].result_fields` list. Every other codegen call site already used `effective_result_fields`; the validator was the lone holdout. Without this fix, `alef test-apps generate` against `liter-llm` bailed before emitting any file for every language, and the subsequent orphan sweep wiped 351 previously-good test_app files (49 054 lines). (`src/e2e/codegen/assertion_recipes.rs`, `src/e2e/validate.rs`)

- **e2e/wasm**: include `request.content_type` in the fixture JSON emitted into the shared TypeScript `app_harness`, mirroring the typescript codegen fix in 18042e393. The wasm e2e harness inspects `request.content_type` to detect multipart/form-encoded bodies and synthesize them with the correct encoding; without it, multipart fixtures got the wrong body shape and the SUT returned 400. CORS preflight already worked because `handler.middleware` extraction was already present. (`src/e2e/codegen/wasm.rs`)

- **ffi gen_bindings/functions (returns_ref Map)**: split `TypeRef::Vec(_) | TypeRef::Map(_, _)` match arm so `Map` uses `.clone()` instead of `.to_vec()`. `BTreeMap` has no `.to_vec()` method (only slices do), so the merged arm caused a compile error when a core method returns `&BTreeMap` with `returns_ref=true`. Now `Vec` still uses `.to_vec()` and `Map` uses `.clone()`. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **ffi gen_bindings (lifetime-param return types)**: append `<'static>` to `*mut T` return types when the returned named type has `has_lifetime_params=true`. In `gen_method_wrapper`, the Rust FFI function signature for static methods that return a lifetime-bearing type (e.g. `NodeContext<'a>`) was emitted as `-> *mut html_to_markdown_rs::NodeContext`, which Rust 2024 rejects with `E0106: missing lifetime specifier`. The fix adds a post-processing step that replaces `*mut {qualified}` with `*mut {qualified}<'static>` when the method's return type names the enclosing type and the enclosing type has lifetime params. The same guard was also added to `gen_opaque_static_constructor` defensively. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **ffi gen_visitor (Cow field CString)**: emit `.as_ref()` instead of `.as_str()` when converting a `Cow<'_, str>` context field to `CString`. `Cow::as_str()` is a recently-stabilised unstable API (`str_as_str` feature gate) and fails to compile on stable Rust. `as_ref()` returns `&str` via `Deref<Target=str>` and works on all stable toolchains. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_visitor.rs`)

- **gen_method (non-opaque delegation, returns_ref Map)**: emit `.iter().map(|(k, v)| (k.clone(), v.clone())).collect()` conversion when a `Map` return type has `returns_ref=true`. Core methods returning `&BTreeMap<K, V>` previously received no suffix in the result_wrap match (falling through to `_ => String::new()`), so the generated binding returned `&BTreeMap` instead of the binding-side `HashMap`, causing a type mismatch. The fix adds an explicit `TypeRef::Map` arm that applies the iterator-collect conversion only when `returns_ref=true`. (`src/codegen/generators/methods.rs`)

- **ffi backend**: respect `config.exclude.methods` in the method-wrapper emission loop. Previously the FFI backend emitted Rust `#[no_mangle] pub extern "C" fn` wrappers for every method in `api.types[*].methods`, including those a consumer had added to `[crates.exclude].methods`. Since cbindgen separately scanned the C-header surface, the declarations got out of sync with the actual Rust impl — producing C declarations for excluded methods without matching shims, and downstream cgo/JNA/P-Invoke consumers failed to link with `undefined symbol`. The fix builds an `AHashSet` of the exclude list once and skips emission for any method whose `"TypeName.method_name"` matches. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **e2e/elixir**: pin the named Finch pool's `pools.default.protocols` to `[:http1]` in the generated `test_helper`. Without an explicit protocol, the pool defaulted to HTTP/2 negotiation while the typical test SUT speaks HTTP/1.1 only — all Req requests then either dropped the connection or returned 404. Updated three loci: the standalone server-pattern template, the inline codegen for the server-pattern `test_helper`, and the mock-server-pattern `test_helper`. Existing test-string assertions still hit the start-line prefix as a substring. (`src/e2e/templates/elixir/test_helper_server.exs.jinja`, `src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **e2e validation**: implicitly enable the streaming assertion recipe when `CallConfig::streaming` is true. Fixtures using streaming assertions like `stream.has_page_event` no longer require redundant `assertion_recipes = ["streaming"]` when the call already declares `streaming = true`. The streaming recipe is now auto-enabled alongside any explicitly listed recipes. (`src/e2e/codegen/assertion_recipes.rs`)

- **e2e validation**: use `resolve_call_for_fixture` (with `select_when` auto-routing) instead of `resolve_call` (explicit call only) when validating fixtures. Previously the validation only checked fixtures with an explicit `call` field, causing false-positive "missing recipe" errors for fixtures routed via `select_when` conditions (e.g., stream fixtures with `category = "stream"` auto-routed to the `crawl_stream` call config). Now all fixtures are validated against their actual routed call, whether explicit or auto-routed. (`src/e2e/validate.rs`)

- **gen_static_method (lifetime types)**: drop the `&` borrow prefix when substituting Map args after renaming `with_borrowed_*` → `with_owned_*`. For `is_ref=true` Map params (e.g., `attributes: &BTreeMap`), `gen_call_args_with_let_bindings` generates `&attributes`. The string replacement that renames `attributes` → `attributes_converted` previously left the `&` intact, producing `&attributes_converted` — a borrow of the locally-owned `BTreeMap`, which `with_owned_attributes` rejects. The fix replaces `&{name}` → `{name}_converted` for is_ref Map params when a borrowed→owned rename is in effect. (`src/codegen/generators/methods.rs`)

- **gen_call_args_with_let_bindings**: only emit `.expect("'param' is required")` when the core param type is actually `Option<T>` (`p.optional=true`). Previously the unwrap suffix was emitted for any promoted param regardless of optionality, causing `bool.expect(...)` type errors for non-optional promoted params like `is_inline: bool` that follow an optional param in the IR. (`src/codegen/generators/binding_helpers.rs`)

- **gen_lossy_binding_to_core_fields_inner / gen_php_lossy_binding_to_core_fields**: emit `.clone().map(Into::into)` instead of `.clone().into()` for `Option<String>` fields with `CoreWrapper::Cow`. Previously both codegen paths emitted `self.field.clone().into()` regardless of optionality, which fails to compile because Rust has no blanket `From<Option<String>>` for `Option<Cow<'_, str>>`. The fix adds an `if field.optional` branch that emits `.map(Into::into)` when the string field is optional and wrapped as `Cow`. (`src/codegen/generators/binding_helpers.rs`, `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **gen_php_lossy_binding_to_core_fields**: add early-return when `typ.has_lifetime_params` to delegate construction to the `From` impl instead of emitting a struct literal. Types with lifetime parameters (e.g., `NodeContext<'a>`) have private fields, so struct-literal construction fails at compile time. The PHP-specific function previously lacked the `has_lifetime_params` guard present in the shared `gen_lossy_binding_to_core_fields_inner`, causing PHP builds to emit `NodeContext { tag_name: ..., attributes: ..., ... }` which is rejected because `attributes` is private. (`src/backends/php/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **swift backend**: bridge `Vec<TaggedEnum>` parameters as `Vec<String>` with JSON deserialization in the shim layer. Previously tagged enums (variants with fields, e.g. `PageAction`) in Vec parameters were incorrectly treated as struct wrappers, emitting `.0` unwrapping and causing type mismatch compile errors. Now tagged enums are properly identified and deserialized from JSON strings via `serde_json::from_str`. Unit enums continue to use the `From<String>` conversion. Adds `tagged_enum_names` set computed from the IR to distinguish tagged vs unit enums, passes it to `emit_function_shim`, and updates `swift_call_arg` to handle JSON deserialization for Vec<TaggedEnum> parameters. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/{mod.rs, shims.rs, type_bridge.rs}`)

- **generic codegen cleanup**: remove the remaining AsRef public-generic extraction rewrite, neutralize visitor/e2e/scaffold/governance fixtures, and replace generated non-delegatable runtime fallbacks with explicit diagnostics. E2e visitor generation now derives context/result names from trait-bridge metadata instead of fixed legacy visitor shapes, strict project-mention enforcement covers generated guidance/templates without scanning unrelated historical changelog text, and backend tests use neutral fixture names outside explicit enforcement cases. (`src/extract/extractor`, `src/e2e`, `src/backends`, `hooks/check_project_mentions.py`, `.ai-rulez`, `tests`)

- **csharp backend**: wire `gen_opaque_streaming_static_wrapper` into the method emission loop for opaque types. The function was defined but never called, so opaque types with streaming methods (e.g., `CrawlStreamAsync` on `DefaultEngine`) did not emit static facade methods in the main wrapper class, causing C# compilation errors. Now opaque streaming methods correctly emit both instance methods on the opaque handle class and static convenience wrappers in the wrapper class. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)


- **toolchain**: make `rust-toolchain.toml` maximally explicit — pin exact patch (`channel = "1.96.0"` — current stable, released 2026-05-25) and declare `profile = "minimal"`. Removes the ambiguity rustup would otherwise resolve implicitly and silences the `default toolchain implicitly overridden with 1.96-aarch64-apple-darwin by rustup toolchain file` warning. (`rust-toolchain.toml`)

- **elixir e2e harness**: drop `:connect_options` from generated `Req.get(...)` calls when the `:finch` pool is configured. Req 0.5.18+ rejects setting both `:finch` and `:connect_options` simultaneously because the custom Finch pool already controls protocol selection. Previously emitted both, causing every elixir e2e test to fail at request time with `ArgumentError: cannot set both :finch and :connect_options`. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **go e2e harness**: cast `bodySchemaJSON` to `json.RawMessage` instead of `string` when calling `builder.RequestSchemaJSON`. Previously emitted `string(bodySchemaJSON)`, which Go's type system rejects (`cannot use string(bodySchemaJSON) as json.RawMessage`). The `encoding/json` import was already present in the template. (`src/e2e/templates/go/harness_main.go.jinja`)

- **typescript e2e harness — multipart**: emit RFC 2388-compliant multipart body with closing `--boundary--\r\n` terminator. Previously the final boundary was `\r\n--alef-boundary` (no closing `--`), causing node and wasm `formData()` parsers to reject the body with 400. Refactored boundary assembly from a `Vec<String>` join pattern to direct string concatenation to make the terminator explicit. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`)

- **typescript e2e harness — CORS OPTIONS**: case-insensitive header lookup via a shared helper that handles both the `Headers.get()` API and plain-object headers. Routes preflight requests through the CORS handler so rejection scenarios return the expected 403 (not 405). Fixes `cors_preflight_method_not_allowed`, `cors_preflight_header_not_allowed`, `cors_max_age`, and `cors_custom_allowed_headers_*` across node + wasm. (`src/e2e/templates/typescript/app_harness.mjs.jinja`)

- **pyo3 backend**: exclude declared `[workspace.opaque_types]` from the generated `__init__.py` public import list and `__all__`. Previously the package wrapper emitted `from ._module import HandlerResult` style entries for declared opaque types that the pyo3 module-init function correctly skipped (per the v0.23.0 `m.add_class::<>` gating), causing `ImportError: cannot import name ...` at module load and preventing the entire test suite from running. The new `.retain(|n| !opaque_types.contains_key(n))` filter mirrors the existing predicate at `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/methods.rs:75`. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/{errors.rs, mod.rs}`)

- **java e2e harness**: emit the actual public Java facade route-registration method (`registerAppRoute`) when the configured `register_method` resolves to the bare word `route`. Previously the harness emitted `app.route(Callable, RouteBuilder)`, an overload that does not exist on the Java facade — preventing the harness from compiling and blocking every java e2e test. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **dart backend (FRB)**: handle multi-line FFI function signatures when injecting `async` for handler callbacks. The `ensure_handler_closures_are_async` rewrite previously only detected single-line signatures (open paren + closing brace on the same line); multi-line signatures with required-named parameters fell through and emitted `await handler(...)` inside non-async function bodies, causing compile errors across every generated dart test. Two-pass rewrite now scans forward up to 30 lines from an unbalanced `(` to find the matching `)` `{` line and marks it for `async` injection. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

- **swift e2e harness**: capitalize the package name when emitting the `import` statement so it matches the Package.swift product name (Swift convention: module names are PascalCase). Previously emitted lowercase `import <name>` that the compiler rejected with `no such module`, blocking the entire swift e2e build before any test executed. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

- **swift backend**: distinguish unit enums (variants with no fields) from tagged enums (variants with fields) when generating parameter conversion code. Previously all enum parameters were converted using `From<String>`, which only works for unit enums. Tagged enums like `PageAction { selector: String, text: String }` do not implement `From<String>` and must use `serde_json::from_str` instead. Now `is_bridgeable_fn` and `swift_call_arg` use a separate `unit_enum_names` set to correctly apply `From<String>` only to unit enums, allowing tagged enum parameters to fall through to JSON deserialization. Fixes swift-bridge compilation error "the trait bound kreuzcrawl::PageAction: From<std::string::String> is not satisfied" when functions like `interact(actions: Vec<PageAction>)` are called. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/shims.rs`)

- **generic validation/codegen**: route public generation through a validated API facade and add fatal readiness diagnostics for configured service APIs on unsupported backends and FFI-dependent `Vec<Named>` / `Map<Named>` returns whose named payload lacks serde metadata. Service extraction now fails when configured recovery sources cannot be read/parsed, configured methods cannot be recovered, or configured configurators are missing instead of silently dropping them. Visitor bridge callback emission now filters methods by configured `context_type` and `result_type`, and Java/FFI options-field visitor symbols derive from configured `options_field` names instead of legacy `visitor_handle` names. Scaffold/guidance updates keep consumer output neutral by defaulting Alef pre-commit hooks off and deriving Java/Kotlin SCM metadata from the configured repository host. (`src/cli/pipeline/generate.rs`, `src/core/backend.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/service.rs`, `src/codegen/generators/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/{ffi,java,napi,pyo3,magnus,extendr,rustler,wasm}`, `src/scaffold`, `.ai-rulez`)

- **rust e2e**: fix duplicate `use` import when a type (e.g. `CrawlConfig`) appears in both the handle-config-types set and the options-type set. Previously each import source emitted independently, causing E0252 "name defined multiple times" for types that qualified under both. Now all crate-level imports are collected into a single `BTreeSet` and emitted in one sorted pass. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/test_file.rs`)

- **rust e2e**: fix wrong type annotation on `json_object` args that have an `element_type`. Previously the `options_type` annotation (e.g. `: CrawlConfig`) was applied even when the arg was a `Vec<T>` parameter whose binding already carries the correct `serde_json::from_value::<Vec<PageAction>>(…)` deserialization. Now the `options_type` annotation is skipped for any `json_object` arg that has an `element_type`, so `let actions: CrawlConfig = …` becomes plain `let actions = serde_json::from_value::<Vec<PageAction>>(…)`. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/test_file.rs`)

- **csharp backend**: emit static wrapper methods for streaming methods on opaque types (e.g., `KreuzcrawlLib.CrawlStreamAsync`, `KreuzcrawlLib.BatchCrawlStreamAsync`). Previously only instance methods on opaque handles were emitted (e.g., `CrawlEngineHandle.CrawlStreamAsync`), causing e2e tests that called static methods to fail with "does not contain a definition for" errors. Now streaming methods on opaque types generate both the instance method (already working) and a corresponding public static method that delegates to it, matching the pattern used for non-streaming methods (e.g., `ScrapeAsync`, `CrawlAsync`). Fixes C# e2e test failures like "error CS0117: 'KreuzcrawlLib' does not contain a definition for 'CrawlStreamAsync'". (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **php backend**: preserve Rust parameter order in PHP facade method signatures instead of reordering to put required params first. E2e test generator expects Rust parameter order (engine, url) but the facade was reordering to (url, engine) to satisfy PHP 8.1 syntax rules. Now parameters are emitted in Rust order with optional ones (e.g. `CrawlEngineHandle`) having `= null` defaults, which PHP 8.1 allows. Aligns PHP bindings with Python, Ruby, Go, and other languages that preserve Rust param order. Fixes E2e PHP test failures like "Argument #1 ($url) must be of type string, Kreuzcrawl\CrawlEngineHandle given". (`src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **zig e2e**: skip negative comparator assertions (e.g., `greater_than: -1`, `greater_than_or_equal: -1`) that are always-true tautologies for unsigned types. Assertions like `links.length > -1` are sentinel checks that always pass for `usize` fields and generate invalid Zig code (`@as(usize, -1)` is rejected). Now these assertions are skipped entirely in the generated test code, avoiding compilation errors and reducing noise. Affects error-handling fixtures with negative `min_value` assertions. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **kotlin backend**: file-level `@file:Suppress("MaxLineLength", ...)` block now emitted on every generated `.kt` file (previously only JNI-client wrapper and kotlin_android files had it), matching the suppression set already used by the android and JNI emitters. Prevents downstream `detekt MaxLineLength` failures on generated service-API files.

- **go backend**: scrub stale consumer-specific reference in source comment; replace with library-agnostic wording.

## [0.23.2] - 2026-06-04

### Changed

- **internal**: pruned excessive generic cleanup TODOs.

## [0.23.1] - 2026-06-04

### Fixed

- fix(swift): hoist Vec-of-enum conversion to a temporary-extending reference expression and stop emitting an unnecessary `Vec<String> -> Vec<&str>` conversion for `&[String]` params. Three related bugs in the swift rust-crate codegen: (1) the inline block `{ let __converted = ...; __converted.as_slice() }` returned a slice borrowed from a `__converted` Vec that was dropped at end-of-block, producing `__converted does not live long enough`; replaced with `&...collect::<Vec<_>>()` so the temporary lives for the enclosing call statement. (2) single-enum params with `is_ref=true` (e.g. `&PiiCategory`) emitted the owned `<Enum as From<String>>::from(name)` expression in a position that expected `&Enum`; now wrapped in `&` to borrow the temporary. (3) `Vec<String>` params with `is_ref=true` but `vec_inner_is_ref=false` (i.e. core takes `&[String]`, not `&[&str]`) were forced through `&name.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect::<Vec<_>>()`, producing `&Vec<&str>` where `&[String]` was expected; the `.iter().map(.as_str()).collect()` path now only fires when `vec_inner_is_ref=true`. Affects every alef consumer with `&[String]`, `&PiiCategory`, or `&[EntityCategory]`-shaped params on client trait methods (e.g. kreuzberg's `LlmBackend.detect_with_custom`, `TokenCounter.next_token`). (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/{shims,wrappers}.rs`)

- fix(wasm): wrap `Vec<T> -> Option<Vec<T>>` field conversions in `Some(...)`. The v0.23.0 fix "use target-inferred collection conversion for input DTO sequence fields" replaced `v.into()` with `v.into_iter().collect()` for sequence-shaped fields, which works for `Vec<T>`/`HashSet<T>`/`AHashSet<T>`/`BTreeSet<T>` targets via type inference but fails when the core field is `Option<Vec<T>>`: `v.into_iter().collect()` produces `Vec<T>` (or set), not `Option<Vec<T>>`. The previous `.into()` path papered over this via the blanket `Option<T>: From<T>` impl, but `collect()` has no such conversion. `dto_field_conversion` now takes the field's `optional` flag and wraps `Some(v.into_iter().collect())` whenever the core field is `Option<Vec<T>>` (or `Option<HashSet<T>>`, etc.). Regression test `dto_vec_field_conversion_wraps_some_when_core_is_optional` added. Affects every alef-generated WASM input DTO whose corresponding core field is `Option<Sequence<T>>` — 12+ sites in kreuzberg-wasm alone (`force_ocr_pages`, `detected_languages`, `chunks`, `images`, `pages`, `elements`, `ocr_elements`, `keywords`, `pdf_annotations`, `archive_entries`, `uris`). (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- fix(backends/napi/service_api): emit hybrid registration-variant methods as TypeScript overloads instead of two duplicate method bodies. The Hybrid style (e.g. spikard's verb decorators that accept both `app.get(path, handler)` and `app.get(path)`) was previously emitted as two separately-declared methods with the same name on the wrapper class, producing oxlint `Identifier 'get' has already been declared` errors because JavaScript classes do not support runtime method overloading. The Hybrid branch now emits one combined JSDoc, two overload signature declarations, and a single unified implementation that branches on the optional `handler` arg. (`src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- fix(swift): use `From<String>` for enum parameters in free-function and method shims. The prior code used `serde_json::from_str` to convert enum parameters at the swift-bridge boundary, but Swift delivers plain wire strings (e.g. `"person"`) not JSON-encoded strings (e.g. `"\"person\""`), so `serde_json::from_str("person")` fails with "expected value". All alef unit enums implement `From<String>` via their serde wire name; the fix uses `<CoreEnum as From<String>>::from(s)` for single enum params, `s.map(|s| <CoreEnum as From<String>>::from(s))` for optional, and element-wise `map` + `.as_slice()` for `Vec<EnumType>` with `is_ref=true`. Applies to both free-function shims and type-method shims. Fixes kreuzberg-swift errors `use of undeclared type` and mismatched-types from the stale JSON path. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/shims.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/wrappers.rs`)

- fix(swift): emit `mut {name}` binding for by-move `is_ref && is_mut` Named struct params in function shims. When a core function takes `&mut T` (alef sets `is_ref=true, is_mut=true`), the generated shim receives the value by move (bridge types are owned) and then borrows it mutably as `&mut {name}.0`.
