  out of opaque struct fields). `traits.dart` was still emitting its own copies of these
  types, producing dart compile errors like `'OcrBackendType' is exported from both
  'package:.../kreuzberg_bridge_generated/lib.dart' and 'package:.../traits.dart'`
  whenever both files are re-exported from the package root. Only `SyncExtractor` stays
  in the stub block because its Rust source carries `#[cfg_attr(alef, alef(skip))]` and
  is therefore NOT emitted by the bridge. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/dart_traits.rs`)

- **dart type_map: map `Vec<primitive>` to FRB-aligned typed lists.** Trait abstract
  method signatures and e2e plugin stub fixtures rendered `Vec<f32>`/`Vec<f64>`/integers
  as `List<double>`/`List<int>`, but FRB widens every Rust integer to `i64` and every
  float to `f64` in the FRB-facing mirror crate and then maps those to `Int64List` and
  `Float64List` in Dart. The user-facing factory parameters therefore expected
  `FutureOr<List<Float64List>>` while the abstract method declared
  `Future<List<List<double>>>` — a type-incompatible signature the dart compiler
  rejects (`A value of type 'Future<List<List<double>>>' can't be returned from a
  function with return type 'FutureOr<List<Float64List>>'`). `DartMapper::map_type` and
  `render_type` now mirror alef's FRB-widening: integer primitives → `Int64List`, float
  primitives → `Float64List`, `Vec<u8>` → `Uint8List`. (`src/backends/dart/type_map.rs`,
  `src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/render_type.rs`)

- **dart e2e stub defaults: emit `1` for integer returns and use the real
  `ProcessingStage` variant.** `EmbeddingBackend::dimensions()` registration validation
  rejects backends reporting `dimensions() == 0`, so the e2e plugin stubs failed at
  registration time when integer methods defaulted to `0`. Integer primitives now
  default to `1` (matching the python e2e generator policy). The hardcoded
  `ProcessingStage` default was also stale (`preProcessing` from the old in-traits stub
  enum); the Rust enum is `Early | Middle | Late` so the variant must be `early` to
  match the FRB-generated Dart enum mirror. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **java native_lib: wrap wide vtable `MemoryLayout.structLayout(...)` across multiple
  lines.** Visitor and plugin trait bridges with more than four vtable slots produced a
  single inline `LINKER.downcallHandle(s, FunctionDescriptor.of(..., MemoryLayout.structLayout(VL.ADDRESS, VL.ADDRESS, ...)))`
  expression that exceeded the project checkstyle 200-char `LineLength` limit, failing
  `mvn verify` during `java-verify` pre-commit. The fix splits the layout fields across
  lines when the vtable has five or more slots.
  (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/native_lib.rs`)

- **go: scan emitted body for runtime import detection.** The Go codegen was unconditionally
  adding a `runtime` import whenever ANY function or method had a `TypeRef::Bytes` parameter,
  but `runtime.Pinner` is only actually emitted by the `bytes_to_c_pointer.jinja` template
  during FFI parameter conversion. The fix refactors `gen_go_file` to a 2-pass approach:
  generate the body first, then scan it for `runtime.` usage to conditionally emit the import.
  This fixes "imported and not used" Go compiler errors. (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **java Panama trait bridges: match the C vtable ABI.** Registration downcalls now pass
  the vtable as a by-value struct, callback slots use the FFI-owned method set, and C
  ABI booleans use `i32` layouts with Java-side boolean conversion.
  (`src/backends/java`)

- **elixir rustler trait bridges: register GenServer pids and dispatch ordered callback args.**
  Scaffolded plugin bridges now register the started GenServer pid with the required
  `Plugin.name/0` value instead of the caller pid/module fallback, e2e GenServer stubs
  convert decoded JSON argument maps into signature-ordered argument lists before
  `apply/3`, and unregister/clear public specs now match their `:ok | :error` NIF
  returns. (`src/backends/rustler`, `src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`,
  `src/scaffold/languages/elixir.rs`)

- **napi/typescript e2e: require plugin names and align Node fixture emission.**
  NAPI plugin bridge constructors now reject registered objects that do not expose
  `name`, generated TypeScript trait interfaces require `name()` for Plugin-backed
  bridges and wrap async callback returns in `Promise<...>`, and Node e2e generation
  strips `Js` prefixes from type imports, keeps fixture `setup` metadata out of
  runtime arguments, and emits byte fixture content as `Uint8Array`/`Buffer` values.
  (`src/backends/napi/{trait_bridge.rs,templates/trait_bridge_constructor.jinja,gen_bindings/errors.rs}`,
  `src/e2e/codegen/typescript/{mod.rs,test_file.rs}`)

- **go trait bridges: propagate callback JSON errors and use FFI string free.** Callback
  trampolines now return JSON marshal/unmarshal failures through `outError` instead of
  silently continuing, and clear-registry wrappers free Rust-owned error strings with
  `{prefix}_free_string`. (`src/backends/go/trait_bridge.rs`,
  `src/backends/go/templates/clear_c_call.jinja`)

- **dart trait bridge: emit bare `DartFnFuture` ident in factory `impl Fn` signatures so FRB can synthesise Dart-callable closure params.** `dart_fn_future_params_and_ret` (used by both `dart_fn_future_callback_type` for `Box<dyn Fn>` struct fields and `dart_fn_future_factory_param_type` for the factory's `impl Fn` parameters) emitted the fully-qualified `flutter_rust_bridge::DartFnFuture<…>`. FRB v2's closure-parameter parser inspects the first path segment of the return type; a qualified name makes the first segment resolve to `flutter_rust_bridge` and the parser bails with "DartFn does not support return types except `DartFnFuture<T>` yet", silently dropping the entire `create_{trait}_dart_impl` factory. The fix emits the bare `DartFnFuture<…>` ident — already in scope via the `pub use flutter_rust_bridge::DartFnFuture;` re-export at the top of every alef-generated lib.rs — for both call sites. End-to-end effect: `createDocumentExtractorDartImpl`, `createOcrBackendDartImpl`, `createPostProcessorDartImpl`, `createRendererDartImpl`, `createValidatorDartImpl`, and `createEmbeddingBackendDartImpl` are now real Dart functions on the binding instead of "error during generation" comments in lib.dart. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **extractor: do not emit binding-visible opaque wrappers for `pub(crate)` types whose `impl` blocks declare `pub` methods.** `process_impl_block` synthesises an opaque `TypeDef` when it encounters `impl Foo { pub fn … }` for a `Foo` not registered by the first-pass struct extractor (which only picks up `pub` structs). The synthesised entry was flagged `binding_excluded = false`, so downstream emitters generated `pub struct Foo { pub(crate) inner: this_crate::path::Foo }` wrappers that fail to compile with E0603 ("struct `Foo` is private") because the binding crate cannot name a `pub(crate)` source type. Rust allows `pub` methods on `pub(crate)` structs (their effective visibility is still capped at `pub(crate)`), so this pattern legitimately occurs in source crates — the symptom in kreuzberg was the `Document`/`table_page_numbers` wrapper emitted from `crates/kreuzberg/src/extraction/docx/parser.rs`. Synthesised entries are now flagged `binding_excluded = true` with reason `synthetic-opaque-from-impl-block (source visibility unverified)`; callers that genuinely want such a type surfaced can re-add it via an explicit `alef.toml` config entry. (`src/extract/extractor/functions.rs`)

- **napi visitor bridge: store persistent `ObjectRef` instead of raw `napi_value`
  pointers — fixes silently dropped callbacks.** The previous bridge struct stored
  raw `napi_env`/`napi_value` pointers extracted via `transmute_copy` from the
  `Object<'_>` constructor argument, then reconstructed an `Object` per callback
  via `Object::from_raw`. The `napi_value` is a _local handle_ tied to the
  HandleScope active at the time the bridge was constructed; by the time visitor
  methods fired deep inside the `convert()` call, the scope had moved and
  `get_named_property("visitText")` silently returned `Err`, so every callback
  fell through to `VisitResult::Continue` without dispatching. The fix uses
  napi-rs's `Object::create_ref::<false>()` to obtain a persistent `ObjectRef`
  that holds a strong reference until `unref()` (called from `Drop`). Each
  callback calls `obj_ref.get_value(&env)` to materialize a fresh local handle
  inside its own active scope. The constructor now returns `Result<Self>` since
  `create_ref` can fail; the four call-site templates
  (`bridge_optional_wrap`, `bridge_required_wrap`,
  `options_visitor_extract_optional`, `options_visitor_extract_required`) and
  the inline `visitor_wrap` emitter in `trait_bridge.rs` were updated to
  propagate via `and_then(... .ok().map(...))` or `?` so a failed reference
  creation cleanly skips the visitor wrap. Resolves downstream h2m issue #395.
  (`src/backends/napi/templates/{visitor_bridge,visitor_method,bridge_optional_wrap,bridge_required_wrap,options_visitor_extract_optional,options_visitor_extract_required}.jinja`,
  `src/backends/napi/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **go service_api: fix variant C symbol and missing free-arg emission.** Two bugs in `gen_registration_variant`: (1) the C FFI symbol was built as `{prefix}_{service}_{reg_method}_{variant}` but the ABI exports it as `{prefix}_{service}_{variant}` — removed the `_{reg_method}` segment. (2) When a `wrapper_call` is present, extra C call args must come from `wc.args` (free args only); the old loop over `reg.metadata_params` never matched the wrapper type name against override/sig-param names so produced no args at all, causing "not enough arguments" link errors. (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **kotlin service_api: fix variant template newline and Rust-path leak.** Registration variant methods now emit the doc comment on its own line (not merged with `fun`), always include `handler: (String) -> String` in the variant signature, and translate Rust enum path expressions (`my_crate::Type::Variant`) to Kotlin form (`Type.VARIANT`) in both direct-override and wrapper-constructor modes. (`src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/templates/registration_variant.kt.jinja`)

- **dart trait bridge: emit named-field opaque wrapper + add `serde` derive dep.** Builds on the prior "hide trait-bridge callbacks behind opaque Arc wrapper" change. (1) The opaque wrapper is now `pub struct {Trait}DartImpl { pub field0: Arc<dyn {Trait} + Send + Sync> }` — a NAMED field literally called `field0`, not a tuple struct. FRB v2's auto-opaque codegen accesses the inner value as `api_that_guard.field0` regardless of source shape; a tuple struct's `.0` accessor failed compilation with E0609 ("no field `field0` on type `RwLockReadGuard<…>`"). The register-forwarder template now uses `impl_.field0` to match. (2) The trait ident inside `Arc<dyn …>` is emitted UNQUALIFIED because FRB v2 strips `dyn` and the qualified path when copying the inner type into `frb_generated.rs`. A sibling `pub use {trait_path};` re-export is emitted right before the wrapper so the bare ident resolves via `frb_generated.rs`'s `use crate::*;` preamble. (3) `dart/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs` now adds `serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }` to the binding's Cargo deps when any `[[trait_bridges]]` are configured — the existing `serde_json` dep does not pull in the `serde` derive macros, so without this the `pub struct InternalDocumentBridge` `#[derive(serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]` failed with E0433 ("cannot find module or crate `serde`"). End-to-end effect: `kreuzberg-dart` went from 229 codegen-related compile errors to 5 (unrelated pre-existing alef regressions). (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs`, `src/backends/dart/templates/rust_trait_register_forwarder.jinja`)

- **Magnus service.rs: fix variant-wrapper type extraction.** Variant-wrapper types
  now extract using the local wrapper struct name (not the source-crate-qualified path),
  then access `.inner.as_ref().clone()` to get the owned source type value. This fixes
  E0277 trait-bound errors in `TryConvert` when registering variant-wrapped parameters.
  (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **native/ABI generators: harden byte, string-length, and handle-lifetime paths.**
  Kotlin/JNI byte API pairing is covered by focused generator tests to keep Kotlin
  `ByteArray` declarations matched to Rust `jbyteArray` symbols. Java/Panama now
  binds FFI `_len` companions for C-string returns, decodes through bounded memory
  segments, and checks `_from_json` handle creation before invoking the primary FFI
  function. Zig now checks `_from_json` handles immediately and defers non-null
  handle cleanup across fallible returns. FFI error/clear paths reset
  `LAST_RETURN_LEN`, and Go pins byte-slice params with `runtime.Pinner` before
  passing pointers to C. (`src/backends/{ffi,go,java,zig}`, `tests/backends_{ffi,go,java,zig,kotlin_jni,jni}_*`)

- **swift/dart generators: close Swift unregister and Dart `InternalDocument` trait-bridge regressions.** Swift
  bridge-registration `name:` overloads now delegate explicitly to `RustBridge.unregister*`
  instead of relying on overload resolution that can recurse into the helper itself.
  SwiftPM scaffold docs now describe the actual generated-package workflow: build the
  Rust bridge crate, rerun Alef so it materializes swift-bridge Swift/C outputs, then
  run SwiftPM. Dart trait bridges no longer substitute Rust's internal
  `InternalDocument` contract with the public `ExtractionResult` DTO; they emit an
  explicit `InternalDocumentBridge` JSON carrier and serialize/deserialize at the Rust
  bridge edge. (`src/backends/{swift,dart}`, `src/scaffold/languages/swift.rs`)

- **high-level binding generators: align trait registry declarations and generated docs.** Node `.d.ts`
  trait-bridge interfaces now use the method names and JSON-string return contract the NAPI runtime
  bridge actually calls, and registry functions type their backend parameter as the generated trait
  interface. PHP emits registry methods on the native Api class and extension stubs with the same
  camelCase public names used by the facade. Python `.pyi` and Ruby RBS stubs now include exported
  trait registry functions, Ruby e2e helpers use configured gem/module names, R wrappers synthesize
  default config objects instead of advertising invalid `NULL` defaults for required config params,
  and Java public docs no longer hardcode `SampleCrateRsException`. (`src/backends/{napi,php,pyo3,magnus,extendr,java}`,
  `src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`)

- **dart: hide trait-bridge callback closures behind a single-field opaque wrapper so the FRB factory survives codegen.** The non-`type_alias` plugin/OCR/post-processor/renderer/validator/embedding-backend bridge previously emitted `#[frb(opaque)] pub struct {Trait}DartImpl { Box<dyn Fn(...)> }` and exposed it to FRB directly. FRB v2 walks opaque-struct fields; when it finds `Box<dyn Fn(...)>` callbacks it cannot bridge, it silently drops the surrounding `create_{trait}_dart_impl` factory (`packages/dart/lib/src/kreuzberg_bridge_generated/lib.dart` listed all six `create_*_dart_impl` functions under "error during generation"). Switching the factory params alone to `impl Fn(...) -> DartFnFuture<R> + Send + Sync + 'static` is not enough — FRB still walks the returned opaque struct's fields and bails. The emitter now splits the bridge into two Rust items: (1) a PRIVATE `struct {Trait}DartCallbacks` that holds the `Box<dyn Fn(...)>` fields and the Plugin/Trait impls (invisible to FRB); (2) a public `#[frb(opaque)] pub struct {Trait}DartImpl(pub std::sync::Arc<dyn {Trait} + Send + Sync>)` wrapper with no closure fields, which FRB bridges without trouble. The factory takes closures as `impl Fn(...) -> DartFnFuture<R> + Send + Sync + 'static` (mirroring the existing `type_alias` visitor path), boxes each one into the private callbacks struct, wraps it in `Arc<dyn Trait>`, and returns the wrapper. The register forwarder pulls the inner `Arc` directly via `impl_.0` instead of re-wrapping. This is structurally the same shape the `type_alias` (`VisitorHandle`) path already uses successfully and unblocks the canonical `final impl = await createDocumentExtractorDartImpl(extractBytes: (content, mimeType, config) => …); await KreuzbergBridge.registerDocumentExtractor(impl);` documented public Dart API for every plugin bridge. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/dart/templates/rust_trait_register_forwarder.jinja`)

- **magnus: fix Ruby Rakefile codegen — revert to `Rake::ExtensionTask` with explicit `ext_dir` parameter.**
  The v0.21.0 pattern wrapped `RbSys::ExtensionTask` initialization in `Dir.chdir(EXT_NATIVE_DIR)` and declared
  file tasks for `Cargo.lock` and `Cargo.toml` within that block. However, rake loses the chdir context when
  executing those file tasks, causing `rake compile` to fail with `Don't know how to build task Cargo.lock`
  across all 5 Ruby platforms (macos-x86_64, macos-arm64, linux, linux-aarch64, windows-x64). Reverted to
  the v3.5.5 pattern using `Rake::ExtensionTask` with explicit `ext_dir` parameter; the extconf.rb already
  handles the native directory context. (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **csharp: trait-bridge catch variable unused in options-field binding.** C# trait-bridge
  callbacks declared `catch (Exception ex)` unconditionally for non-primitive returns, but
  only used `ex` when `bind_via != "options_field"`. Projects with `<TreatWarningsAsErrors>`
  failed with CS0168. The emitter now emits `catch (Exception)` without binding the variable
  when the exception won't be used (primitive returns or options-field binding).
- **extendr: export `conversion_options()` helper in R `NAMESPACE`.** The
  `gen_namespace` emitter walked API functions and added `export(<fn>)` for each, but the
  standalone `conversion_options()` helper (generated alongside the `ConversionOptions`
  opaque so R users can construct options with named args) was emitted into `R/options.R`
  without a matching NAMESPACE entry. Calling `conversion_options(heading_style="setext")`
  from a downstream R package then raised `could not find function "conversion_options"`
  at runtime, and any test that built `ConversionOptions` via the helper (h2m's R smoke
  suite, for instance) failed with `options must be a named list` from extendr-api 0.9.0
  because the fallback path tried to construct from an unnamed list. The emitter now
  emits `export(conversion_options)` alongside the other exports whenever a
  `ConversionOptions` type exists. (`src/backends/extendr/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **java (Panama FFM): use `Optional.orElse(null)` instead of `Optional.orElseThrow()`
  for the `LAST_ERROR_CODE` / `LAST_ERROR_CONTEXT` symbol-lookup handles in the generated
  `NativeLib.<clinit>`.** When the loaded native library happens not to export the
  optional error-context symbols (older FFI build, stripped binary, partial backport),
  the static initializer raised `NoSuchElementException: No value present` from
  `Optional.orElseThrow()` mid-clinit, which the JVM then rethrew as
  `NoClassDefFoundError: ... NativeLib` for every subsequent access. The handles are
  consulted at error-reporting time and the rest of the loader already null-checks them
  before use, so falling back to `null` is the safe choice — the library loads, basic
  calls work, and only the extended error-context message degrades gracefully when the
  symbols genuinely aren't present.
  (`src/backends/java/templates/native_lib.jinja`)

- **dart trait bridges: take callback closures as `impl Fn` factory parameters so Dart user code can pass them.** The non-`type_alias` (plugin / OCR / post-processor / renderer / validator / embedding-backend) factory shape emitted callback parameters as `Box<dyn Fn(...) -> DartFnFuture<R> + Send + Sync>`. FRB v2 renders `Box<dyn Fn(...)>` parameters as opaque `BoxFn…` Dart classes that have no public constructor, so Dart code calling e.g. `createDocumentExtractorDartImpl(extractBytes: (content, mimeType, config) => …)` failed strong-mode type checks against the generated `BoxFnVecU8StringExtractionConfigDartFnFutureExtractionResult` parameter. FRB v2 only synthesises a Dart-callable function type for closure PARAMETERS when the Rust signature uses bare `impl Fn(...) -> DartFnFuture<R> + Send + Sync + 'static` (the same shape already used by the `type_alias` visitor pattern). The factory now emits `impl Fn` parameters and boxes each argument as it stores it in the existing `Box<dyn Fn(...)>` struct fields, so the struct shape and forwarder wiring are unchanged. A new `dart_fn_future_factory_param_type` helper produces the `impl Fn` signature; `dart_fn_future_callback_type` (used for struct fields) continues to emit `Box<dyn Fn>`. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **napi: invert `never_skip_cfg_field_names` membership test in struct emission.** The `skip_cfg_bridge_field` predicate was `field.cfg.is_some() && never_skip_cfg_field_names.contains(&field.name)`, which inverted the intent: cfg-gated fields whose names appeared in the never-skip allowlist were the ones getting `#[serde(skip)]` attached. The correct semantics — emit `#[serde(skip)]` for cfg-gated fields _not_ in the allowlist — needs the negation `!never_skip_cfg_field_names.contains(&field.name)`. Allowlisted cfg-gated bridge fields stay serializable; trait-bridge wrapper fields whose wrapper types lack serde impls remain skipped. (`src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **magnus: obtain `Ruby` handle via `Ruby::get()` inside `app_run` rather than a `&Ruby` fn parameter.** `#[magnus::function]` runs on a Ruby thread that already holds the GVL, so passing `ruby: &Ruby` was unnecessary plumbing that complicated the magnus-side function signature contract. `app_run` now declares its parameters as `(registrations, …entrypoint params)` and obtains the Ruby handle inline via `Ruby::get().expect("…")`. This matches the GVL-holding-thread guidance documented in the broader magnus 0.8 modernization fix above and keeps the magnus function-registration glue uniform across services that do and don't need a Ruby handle. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **magnus: fix service.rs codegen for magnus 0.8.2 API (imports, with_gvl removal, RArray accessors, Opaque construction, rb-sys thread integration).** Generated `service.rs` failed to compile against magnus 0.8.2 with 52 errors. Root causes: `Opaque` was not re-exported at top level (now import from `magnus::value::Opaque`); `Ruby::with_gvl` does not exist in 0.8 (now use `Ruby::get()` for GVL-holding threads, or raw `rb_sys::rb_thread_call_with_gvl` for async-spawned workers); `RArray::get` was replaced by `entry::<T>(offset as isize)` in 0.8; `Opaque::new(value)` was replaced by `value.into()` or `Opaque::from(value)`; loop over untyped RArray requires indexed iteration `for i in 0..arr.len() { arr.entry::<Value>(i as isize)? }` instead of `iter()`. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **dart e2e: emit typed closure params and `throw UnimplementedError()` Named-type defaults in trait stubs.** Generated trait-bridge test stubs emitted bare closures `(content, mimeType, config) => …` which Dart strong-mode infers as `Function(dynamic, dynamic, dynamic)` and rejects against the FRB factory's typed `BoxFn…` parameter. They also defaulted Named return types via `T()`, which is invalid for FRB-generated DTOs whose constructors require named params (`ExtractionResult(content: …, mimeType: …, …)`). Closure parameters now carry explicit Dart types derived from the method IR, and Named-type defaults are emitted as `throw UnimplementedError()` — the stubs are registration-only and the methods are never invoked, so a `Never` body is type-safe and project-agnostic. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **magnus: only `.into()` non-String, non-primitive variant-wrapper constructor args.** `magnus_variant_wrapper_constructor` was emitting `<core>::new({param_name})` verbatim, which produced `spikard::RouteBuilder::new(method, path)` where `method` was the magnus binding-side `Method` mirror rather than the core `spikard::Method`. Naively wrapping every argument in `.into()` then produced E0283 ambiguity on `String` against `impl Into<String>` targets that have many impls. The fix wraps only `TypeRef::Named` / `Optional` / `Vec` / `Map` args — the ones that map to binding-side newtypes — and passes `String` / primitive args through unchanged. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **kotlin_android: keep parameters on short generated interface signatures.** The long-signature formatter now preserves parameter lists for signatures below the wrapping threshold instead of accidentally emitting zero-argument methods, while still wrapping long suspend signatures for AGP compatibility. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **magnus: wire generated `service.rs` into the extension's `lib.rs`.** When `api.services` is non-empty the magnus `generate_bindings` pass now emits `pub mod service;` so that the service-API entrypoint functions (decorated with `#[magnus::function]`) are compiled into the extension crate. The `ruby_init` module-initializer now also registers each service entrypoint as a module-level function so Ruby callers can invoke it by name. Previously the generated `service.rs` was orphaned — it existed on disk but was never referenced by `lib.rs`, causing the entrypoints to be silently dropped from the native extension. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/{mod,functions}.rs`)

- **high-level bindings: make generated extraction defaults callable across Node, PHP, and R.** Node `.d.ts` emission no longer creates TypeScript-invalid required parameters after optional slots, while defaultable config parameters are surfaced as optional. PHP native/facade wrappers now accept omitted/null default config objects and materialize Rust defaults before calling core functions. R wrapper functions now default optional/default config and MIME parameters to `NULL`. Elixir Native stubs de-duplicate trait lifecycle NIFs, and the shared generated header no longer uses `sample_crate` provenance in its default issues URL. (`src/backends/{napi,php,extendr,rustler}`, `src/core/hash.rs`)

- **swift/dart trait bridges: align public callback surfaces and Swift compile glue.** Swift JSON-config overloads now remain valid for async functions and always declare `throws` because JSON decoding can fail; generated bridge-registration adapters also emit Plugin lifecycle stubs and keep async trait stubs async. Dart trait bridge `InternalDocument` substitution remains aligned across public traits, FRB callback signatures, and call-site conversions. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/{mod,trait_bridge}.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_*`)

- **native/ABI backends: tighten callback and byte-API contracts.** Kotlin/JNI byte APIs now have a focused pairing test that verifies Kotlin `ByteArray` declarations match Rust `jbyteArray` exported shims. Go and Java trait callback returns no longer JSON-quote raw string payloads before Rust decodes them, and Java excluded named-type JSON callbacks pass raw JSON strings through instead of double-encoding. C# generated P/Invoke declarations and temporary JSON buffers now use explicit UTF-8 marshalling/allocation rather than LPStr/HGlobalAnsi paths. Zig complex trait-vtable return thunks now emit an explicit unsupported `@compileError` instead of silently returning placeholder null/error output. (`src/backends/{csharp,go,java,kotlin,zig}`, `tests/backends_{csharp,go,kotlin}_*`)

- **swift: preserve populated RustBridgeC.h when cargo build output is missing.** When `emit_swift_bridge_files` runs before `cargo build -p {binding_crate}`, it fell through to emitting a minimal placeholder header. Previously, this branch always overwrote any existing on-disk `RustBridgeC.h`, even when a full 3000+ line concatenated header from a prior successful build was present. The regression broke users regenerating bindings between builds — their committed populated header was silently replaced with the minimal placeholder, breaking Swift compilation. Now, before emitting a placeholder, the function checks if an existing header carries the marker string "Concatenates SwiftBridgeCore.h" (present only in populated headers); if it does, the function preserves it unchanged by returning `Ok(None)`. The placeholder is only emitted when no populated header exists on disk. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

### Added

- **rustler: ensure `Native` alias is emitted for variant-wrapper opaque modules.** `gen_elixir_opaque_module` already emits the Elixir `def new(...)` wrapper for variant-wrapper types via the general `typ.methods` loop (the static `new` is placed in `typ.methods` by the IR extractor). However, the `needs_native_alias` guard that controls whether `alias AppModule.Native` is emitted was only `has_default || !methods.is_empty()`. For a variant-wrapper type whose only method is the static `new`, the condition was already true — but this adds `|| is_variant_wrapper` as a defensive explicit guard so the alias is guaranteed regardless of future method-list changes. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **php: emit `#[php(constructor)]` on variant-wrapper opaque types.** When `TypeDef.is_variant_wrapper` is set and no `client_constructors` entry overrides it, the php backend now emits a `#[php_impl] impl TypeName { #[php(constructor)] pub fn new(sig_params) -> Self { Self { inner: Arc::new(CoreType::new(call_args)) } } }` block delegating to the core type. This mirrors the existing `client_constructors` path but is driven from the IR's static `new` method rather than a hand-written body template. Without this, variant bodies emitting `new TypeName(args)` PHP constructor syntax raised a fatal error at runtime. (`src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **napi: emit `#[napi(constructor)]` companion on variant-wrapper opaque types.** When `TypeDef.is_variant_wrapper` is set and no `client_constructors` entry overrides it, the napi backend now emits a separate `#[napi] impl T { #[napi(constructor)] pub fn new_constructor(...) -> Self { ... } }` block delegating to the core type wrapped in `Arc`. The static `#[napi] pub fn new(...)` already emitted by `gen_opaque_struct_methods` is preserved; the companion uses a distinct Rust fn name (`new_constructor`) to avoid a duplicate-`fn new` compile error while napi-rs exposes it as `new T(args)` JavaScript constructor syntax. Without this, variant bodies emitting `new RouteBuilder(method, path)` raised a `TypeError` at runtime because the class had no JavaScript constructor. (`src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **magnus: emit host-callable constructor for variant-wrapper opaque types.** When `TypeDef.is_variant_wrapper` is set and no `client_constructors` entry overrides it, the magnus backend now emits a static `pub fn new(...)` on the opaque binding struct (delegating to the core type wrapped in `Arc`) and registers it as `define_singleton_method("new", function!(TypeName::new, N))` in `ruby_init`. Without this, variant bodies emitting `RouteBuilder.new(method, path)` constructor-syntax raised `NoMethodError: undefined method 'new'` at runtime. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/{mod,functions}.rs`)

- **pyo3: emit `#[new]` constructor on variant-wrapper opaque types.** When a `TypeDef.is_variant_wrapper` is set (Wave-D extractor pass), the pyo3 backend now emits a `#[pymethods] impl T { #[new] pub fn py_new(...) -> Self { Self::new(...) } }` block in addition to the existing `#[staticmethod] pub fn new(...)`. The wrapper's `new` signature is mirrored verbatim via the existing `function_params` mapper. Without this, variant bodies emitting `RouteBuilder(method, path)` constructor-syntax raised `TypeError: cannot create 'builtins.RouteBuilder' instances` at runtime — the `#[new]` companion makes the constructor syntax resolve to a real instance while leaving the existing `RouteBuilder.new(...)` static accessor in place. The two coexist by giving the constructor a distinct Rust function name (`py_new`); pyo3 registers it as Python `__new__` via the attribute regardless of the Rust name. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **core: mark `TypeDef.is_variant_wrapper` when a type is referenced as the wrapper of one or more `RegistrationVariant`s.** Registration-variant bodies (`app.get(path, handler)`-style shortcuts) construct their wrapper inline via `WrapperConstructorCall` — e.g. `RouteBuilder::new(Method::Get, path)`. For the resulting opaque wrapper to be host-language-callable, each backend's opaque-class emitter needs to opt the wrapper's static constructor into a real host-language constructor (`#[new]` for pyo3, `#[napi(constructor)]` for napi-rs, etc.). The extractor now runs a post-resolution pass (`mark_variant_wrapper_types`) that walks every `RegistrationVariant.wrapper_call.wrapper_type_name` and flips the new `TypeDef.is_variant_wrapper` flag on the matching type. Backends consult this flag to drive per-language constructor emission in later commits. (`src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/service.rs`)

### Fixed

- **dart: align InternalDocument substitution across factory params, closure types, and call-site conversions.** The Dart trait-bridge generator was inconsistently handling the substitution of the internal `InternalDocument` type with the binding-facing `ExtractionResult`. Closure parameters used `InternalDocument` (raw Rust internal type), while Dart trait stubs and e2e test backends expected `ExtractionResult` (public API type), causing FRB factory parameter and return-type mismatches. The substitution now applies uniformly: (1) `substitute_internal_document_in_rust_type` rewrites all forms of `InternalDocument` (fully qualified `crate_name::types::internal::InternalDocument`, partially qualified `crate_name::InternalDocument`, plain token, and generic arguments) to the corresponding `ExtractionResult` form; (2) closure callback types use the substituted `ExtractionResult` in FRB factory signatures; (3) at the call site, when a method param's original type was `InternalDocument`, an explicit `.into()` conversion is emitted so the Rust bridge passes `ExtractionResult` to the Dart-side closure while preserving the canonical trait signature's use of `InternalDocument`. This ensures FRB-generated factory parameters match closure types, and Rust-side adapters can correctly convert between types. Fixes E0308 mismatched-type errors in Dart plugin trait bridges. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **kotlin_android: wrap long interface method signatures across multiple lines to avoid AGP parser cascade.** AGP 8.13.0 + Kotlin 2.3.21 cascades into false "Missing '}'" / "Unclosed comment" errors when parsing interface method signatures >=115 chars on a single line. The error is a parser-recovery artifact, not real syntax. When the full single-line signature (including indent, suspend keyword, method name, params, return type) would exceed ~110 chars, the emitter now wraps the signature across multiple lines with each parameter on its own line and trailing commas (idiomatic Kotlin style). Empty parameter lists and short signatures remain single-line. This fix applies to trait interface methods emitted via `emit_trait_methods`. A new `format_method_signature` helper function encapsulates the wrapping logic, making it reusable if similar multi-line formatting is needed elsewhere in the kotlin_android backend. Fixes compilation errors in kreuzberg's kotlin-android e2e bindings when they define long interface method signatures. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **dart bridge crate: revert naive `InternalDocument` → `ExtractionResult` closure-type rewrite (kept as no-op pass-through with a comment).** The previous fix attempt rewrote `DartFnFuture<InternalDocument>` → `DartFnFuture<ExtractionResult>` at the closure-type level, but the surrounding Rust bridge code still passed `InternalDocument` into the closure (from the original trait method's `Result<InternalDocument>` return), producing E0308 mismatched-type errors at every `(self.render)(doc).await` site. Properly aligning the dart-facing trait, the FRB DTO type names, and the bridge call sites needs an explicit `InternalDocument` → `ExtractionResult` conversion before each closure invocation — that's tracked separately. The function is left as a documented no-op so the rust bridge compiles; the dart e2e plugin_api_test cost is two tests where FRB DTO names clash with the dart abstract trait. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **dart e2e: emit every trait stub method as `Future<T> ... async => default` so subclasses match the always-async abstract trait.** The dart trait emitter wraps every method in `Future<T>` because FRB bridges every Dart-side callback as `DartFnFuture<T>` regardless of the underlying Rust `is_async`. The e2e stub generator at `src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs` previously branched on `method.is_async` and emitted `int priority() => 0;` for sync methods, causing "return type 'int' does not match overridden 'Future<int>'" errors when the stub class extended the abstract trait. Both async and sync methods now emit the same `Future<T> ... async => default` shape. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **dart: substitute `InternalDocument` with `ExtractionResult` in trait return + parameter types and drop the placeholder class.** The dart trait emitter previously rendered the Rust internal name `InternalDocument` verbatim in abstract method signatures (e.g. `Future<InternalDocument> extractBytes(...)`, `Future<String> render(InternalDocument doc)`) and emitted a hollow `class InternalDocument {}` placeholder in `traits.dart`. The e2e test stub generator already used `ExtractionResult` for return types, so every dart trait stub failed to compile with "return type does not match the overridden method" and "argument type cannot be assigned" errors. Trait emission now substitutes `InternalDocument` → `ExtractionResult` symmetrically across param and return types (matching the kotlin_android fix landed in `ec967fe83` + `1a2786797`); the placeholder class is no longer emitted. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/dart_traits.rs`)

- **kotlin_android: also substitute `InternalDocument` in trait method parameter types.** Follow-up to the trait-return-type mapping fix. Parameter types referencing `InternalDocument` (e.g. `Renderer::render(&InternalDocument)`) were still leaking the raw Rust internal name into the generated Kotlin trait interface. The trait emitter now substitutes `InternalDocument` → `ExtractionResult` on parameter types as well as return types. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **kotlin_android: fix `InternalDocument` mapping and `ByteArray` parameter type in trait interfaces and generated wrappers.** Two regressions in the Kotlin Android backend: (1) trait interface methods (e.g., `IDocumentExtractor`) were showing raw Rust type names like `InternalDocument` instead of mapping them to the public API type `ExtractionResult` — trait methods now explicitly map `InternalDocument` return types to `ExtractionResult` to match the public plugin interface contract; (2) the `jni_param_type_str` function had an overly broad catch-all `_ => "String"` that was incorrectly mapping `Vec<u8>` and `Bytes` parameters to `String` instead of `ByteArray`, causing signatures like `fun extractBytes(content: String, ...)` instead of `fun extractBytes(content: ByteArray, ...)`. The parameter type mapper now explicitly handles `TypeRef::Bytes` → `ByteArray` and `TypeRef::Vec(u8)` → `ByteArray`. Fixes compilation errors in kotlin-android e2e test bindings. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **php: remove unnecessary `unsafe` block in bridge constructor.** The PHP bridge `new()` constructor wraps `php_obj.inc_count()` in an `unsafe { ... }` block, but ext-php-rs's `ZendObject::inc_count()` method is safe. Removing the redundant unsafe wrapper eliminates rustc warnings about unnecessary unsafe blocks when kreuzberg-php bindings are compiled. (`src/backends/php/templates/bridge_constructor.jinja`)

- **magnus: variant wrapper constructor calls go through the constructor method instead of as a tuple-struct call.** The Ruby/magnus `gen_variant_match_arm` emitter was building the wrapper constructor expression as `{{ wrapper_type_path }}({{ args }})`, ignoring `WrapperConstructorCall::constructor_method`. For a fixture with `wrapper_type_path = "my_crate::RouteBuilder"` and `constructor_method = "new"`, this produced `my_crate::RouteBuilder(method, path)`, which only compiles for tuple structs and panics on any wrapper whose constructor is a named method. The emitter now produces `my_crate::RouteBuilder::new(method, path)`, matching the FFI and dart backends. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **dart variant test expectation aligned with the local-newtype emitter.** `test_emit_registration_variants` still asserted the pre-`135ded123` behavior `self.route(builder, ...)`, even though the dart variant emitter now wraps the constructed inner in the bridge newtype and calls `self.route(RouteBuilder { inner }, handler)` (matching the dart-binding method signature). Updated the assertion to match the current, correct emitter output. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **docs formatting test expectation aligned with `collapse_whitespace` semantics.** `test_format_default_string_literal_with_pipes_and_newlines` asserted `starts_with("PAGE")` after trimming backticks and quotes, but the formatter intentionally preserves HTML comment delimiters (the real fixture is `<!-- PAGE {page_num} -->`, a verbatim page-marker default). Stripping them would corrupt real docs output. Updated the assertion to `starts_with("<!-- PAGE")`. (`src/docs/formatting.rs`)

- **dart e2e: skip binding-excluded trait methods when emitting factory call arguments.** Dart plugin test stub factory calls now filter out trait methods marked with `#[doc(hidden)]` or similar binding-exclusion attributes. This prevents passing factory arguments for methods not present in the FRB-generated wrapper (e.g., `asSyncExtractor` which cannot be serialized across the FFI boundary). The stub class still declares all trait methods inherited from the base trait, but the factory call only passes callbacks for methods that the FRB wrapper accepts. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **dart e2e: omit binding-excluded trait methods from generated test backends.** Dart plugin test stubs now pass only methods present in the FRB-generated factory, avoiding callback arguments for APIs intentionally stripped from the binding surface. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **dart e2e: await async trait bridge factory functions.** The `createXDartImpl()` factory functions return `Future<T>` because FRB requires them to be async when factory parameters include opaque `Box<dyn Fn(...)>` closures. The e2e test generator now emits factory wrapper functions at module scope and calls them with `await` inside test functions to properly handle the async interface. This fixes "cannot assign to parameter type" errors in trait bridge test stubs. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **kotlin_android: trait bridge test backend emission and call resolution use correct language parameter and overrides.** The Kotlin JVM e2e codegen had two issues: (1) hardcoding `"kotlin"` when calling `emit_test_backend` for trait bridges, instead of using `"kotlin_android"` when in `kotlin_android_style` mode; (2) hardcoding `lang = "kotlin"` when resolving call overrides, causing trait-bridge calls to look up function/class names from the "kotlin" overrides instead of "kotlin_android" ones. This produced invalid Kotlin syntax like `Kreuzberg.register(...)` with missing function names. Now uses the language-appropriate parameter throughout, resolves call_overrides from the correct language, resolves class override from both the per-call `kotlin_android` override and the global default, and uses it for both client-factory and flat-function call paths. Fixes test compilation errors in plugin_api and trait-bridge fixtures. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

- **dart: variant shortcut emits the local wrapper newtype and calls the base method with `(builder, handler)`.** Two regressions in the dart registration-variant emitter: (1) the variant constructed the facade type (`spikard::RouteBuilder`) but the local `route` method takes the dart-bridge wrapper newtype (`RouteBuilder { inner: ... }`), so the call site failed with "expected `RouteBuilder`, found `spikard::RouteBuilder`"; (2) the variant body double-passed signature_params (once to build the wrapper, once again as positional args to the base method), causing a "this method takes 2 arguments but 3 arguments were supplied" error. The Rust emitter now threads `wrapper_local_type` (the bare last segment of `wrapper_type_path`) through to the template; the template constructs the inner facade type then wraps with `{{ wrapper_local_type }} { inner }` and calls `self.{{ base_method_name }}(builder, handler)` only. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/dart/templates/service_api/registration_variant.rs.jinja`)

- **pyo3: variant emission drops fixed overrides from the meta tuple and emits runtime imports for wrapper-referenced types.** Two regressions in the pyo3 service.py variant emitter: (1) `gen_registration_variant` walked `variant.overrides` and pushed each overridden param's bare name into the meta tuple, producing `("route", (builder, method), handler)` where `method` was an undefined identifier — overrides are baked into the wrapper at construction time and must not appear as pass-through args; the emitter now iterates `base_reg.metadata_params`, skipping any consumed by the wrapper or in the override set; (2) wrapper constructors and enum overrides reference `RouteBuilder` and `Method` at runtime but the existing import block only emitted them inside a `TYPE_CHECKING` guard, causing `NameError` at registration time; added `collect_variant_runtime_types` to walk variants' `wrapper_call.wrapper_type_name` plus the bare enum class from each `Fixed.value_expr` (the second-to-last `::` segment), and emit those as a normal runtime `from .{module} import …` line. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **dart: only mark `config` as optional in the wrapper when alef can synthesize a complete default expression.** `is_optional_config_param` previously gated solely on the named type having a Rust `Default` impl (`ty.has_default`). FRB-generated DTOs use `required` named parameters for every field, so the wrapper's `config ?? Type()` fallback only compiles when alef can emit a value for every field. When `default_expression_for_named_type` returns `None` (a field whose type has no known zero value, an enum without a default variant, etc.), the wrapper now keeps `config` required instead of emitting an uncompilable bare constructor. Regression surfaced in kreuzberg's dart `embedTexts` / `embedTextsAsync` wrappers: `EmbeddingConfig()` failed to compile because the FRB constructor required `model`, `normalize`, `batchSize`, and `showDownloadProgress`. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **csharp: free generated wrapper-owned handles exactly once.** C# wrapper functions now put all temporary Named/Bytes/Vec/Map handle cleanup on the `finally` path instead of freeing handles before `return` and again in `finally`. Named and unmanaged cleanup templates keep zero/allocation guards so optional null handles are not passed to native free functions. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/{methods,mod}.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/templates/named_param_teardown_*.jinja`)

- **kotlin/jni: preserve binary nullability across `ByteArray` params and returns.** Optional `Vec<u8>` and `Bytes` JNI shims now decode the empty `jbyteArray` sentinel as `None`, optional binary returns map `None` to null `jbyteArray`, and the Kotlin JNI facade emits nullable `ByteArray` signatures instead of JSON-string round trips. (`src/backends/{kotlin,jni}`, `tests/backends_{kotlin_jni_emitter,jni_gen_shims}_test.rs`)

- **swift: emit short stub names in `BridgeRegistrationOverloads.swift`.** The `derive_short_stub_name` function now correctly kebab-cases trait names and strips trailing `-backend` suffixes, so traits like `OcrBackend` → `ocr`, `EmbeddingBackend` → `embedding`, and `PostProcessor` → `post-processor`. This matches the established e2e fixture cleanup names and fixes registry lookups in `try? Kreuzberg.unregisterOcrBackend("swift-bridge-ocr-stub")` calls. Previously, the generator emitted full snake-cased names (`ocr_backend`, `post_processor`, etc.), causing cleanup to fail because the adapter's `name()` method returned mismatched values. Updated both the comment block documenting stub names and the actual adapter class name generation in `gen_bridge_registration_overloads_file`. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **swift: register `swift_registration_variant.swift.jinja` in the swift template environment.** The Wave B.2 swift variant template was authored under `src/backends/swift/templates/` but never added to the `TEMPLATES` slice in `src/backends/swift/template_env.rs`, so `template_env::render("swift_registration_variant.swift.jinja", ...)` panicked at codegen time with "template not found". Added the `include_str!` entry alongside `swift_registration.swift.jinja`. (`src/backends/swift/template_env.rs`)

- **alef: scrub project-name leaks from swift backend, pyo3 service-api test, and php type-stub emitter.** Removed hardcoded `kreuzberg` / `spikard` mentions from generator code: the swift `_loadBytesFromPathOrUtf8` helper now consults the generic `ALEF_TEST_DOCUMENTS_DIR` env var instead of a downstream-project-specific one (threaded through `trait_bridge.rs`, `gen_bindings/mod.rs`, and the `swift_bridge_registration_overloads.swift.jinja` template); the pyo3 `python_output_contains_registration_variants` test fixture and the swift `service_api.rs` example comment use generic `mylib::` paths; `backends_swift_json_string_overloads_test.rs` looks for the derived `Mylib.swift` facade name and the generic env var. Also fixed a php `generate_type_stubs` compile regression where `exclude_functions` / `exclude_types` were referenced inside the function but declared in a sibling scope — declarations moved into `generate_type_stubs`. (`src/backends/swift/{gen_bindings/{mod,trait_bridge,service_api}.rs,templates/swift_bridge_registration_overloads.swift.jinja}`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `tests/backends_swift_json_string_overloads_test.rs`)

<!-- rumdl MD032 workaround for adjacent changelog subsections. -->

### Added

- **all backends: per-variant registration shortcut emission.** Every service-bearing backend (pyo3, napi, magnus, php, rustler, ffi, jni, go, csharp, zig, java Panama, kotlin, dart FRB, swift swift-bridge, extendr) now consumes `RegistrationDef.variants` and emits one idiomatic shortcut per variant on the service-owner surface. Each backend renders the variant in its language's native shape — Python decorator + method form, TypeScript/Node async method, Ruby Sinatra-style block DSL, PHP Slim-style method, Elixir Plug.Router free function, Go Title-case method, C# PascalCase MapGet-style, Zig lowercase try-method, Java Panama camelCase, Kotlin trailing-lambda, Dart FRB future method, Swift Vapor-style trailing-closure, R Plumber-style S3 method. The shortcut builds the metadata wrapper via the pre-resolved `WrapperConstructorCall` recipe (substituting `Fixed.value_expr` verbatim at the wrapper call site and pulling `Free` args from the variant's signature), then delegates to the base registration. C-ABI hosts (go/csharp/zig/java/kotlin) consume per-variant ffi C symbols emitted by the ffi backend; FRB/swift-bridge hosts emit Rust-side shortcuts on the bridge crate; pyo3/napi/magnus/php/rustler emit their respective Rust-bridge variant methods. Per-backend jinja templates land under each backend's `templates/` directory (dart at `templates/service_api/`); pyo3, napi, magnus, php, csharp, go, and ffi build the variant via inline `push_str`/`format!` (template extraction deferred). All backends stay library-agnostic — they consume the generic IR and emit per-language idioms with zero HTTP-specific knowledge. (`src/backends/{pyo3,napi,magnus,php,rustler,ffi,jni,go,csharp,zig,java,kotlin,dart,swift,extendr}/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/{dart,extendr,ffi,java,jni,kotlin,rustler,swift,zig}/templates/registration_variant.*.jinja`, `src/backends/{dart,extendr,java,kotlin,rustler}/template_env.rs`)

- **swift: emit JSON-string convenience overloads and `_loadBytesFromPathOrUtf8` helper.** The swift backend now generates positional-argument overloads that accept JSON-encoded config parameters for all functions with serde-enabled struct config fields. These overloads decode the JSON string via the corresponding `*FromJson` helper and delegate to the typed base function, enabling e2e tests to pass JSON strings directly without manual config construction. Additionally, the `_loadBytesFromPathOrUtf8` helper is emitted as a private function that resolves a string argument as either a file path (searching CWD, `KREUZBERG_TEST_DOCUMENTS_DIR` env var, and ancestor `test_documents/` or `fixtures/` directories up to 16 levels) or falls back to literal UTF-8 content. This resolves audit entries 1 and 2 from the May 2026 kreuzberg swift binding audit (swift.md lines 9–100). (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **swift: emit `ExtractionResultExtensions.swift` with computed-property extensions.** The swift backend now generates an `ExtractionResultExtensions.swift` file that provides computed-property aliases for zero-arg String-returning methods on non-opaque struct types. This allows e2e fixture assertions to use property-access syntax (e.g., `result.mimeType`) instead of method-call syntax (e.g., `result.mimeType()`), improving test readability. For each eligible method, the generator emits a public computed property that wraps the method call and invokes `.toString()` on the return value. This resolves audit entry 8 from the May 2026 kreuzberg swift binding audit (swift.md lines 278–326). (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **swift: emit `BridgeRegistrationOverloads.swift` with register/unregister convenience functions and stub adapters.** The swift backend now generates a unified `BridgeRegistrationOverloads.swift` file containing register/unregister overloads matching the alef e2e generator's call shapes, plus internal stub adapter classes that implement full plugin trait protocols with sensible defaults. For each trait in the crate's `trait_bridges` config, the generator emits: (1) a `unregister<Trait>(name: String)` overload that accepts a labeled `name:` argument (matching e2e fixture calls) and forwards to the positional-arg base function; (2) a `register<Trait>(_ bridge: Swift<Trait>Bridge)` overload that wraps the bridge protocol in a full-protocol adapter and calls the base registration; (3) a private `_<Trait>BridgeAdapter` class implementing the full trait with stub methods that delegate bridge methods where available and throw/return safe defaults for unimplemented methods. A helper `_loadBytesFromPathOrUtf8` function is emitted for path/string resolution (used by e2e fixtures). This resolves audit entries 3 and 4 (swift.md lines 102–177) from the May 2026 kreuzberg swift binding audit. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **core: pre-built wrapper-constructor call recipe on registration variants.** Extended `RegistrationVariant` with `wrapper_call: Option<WrapperConstructorCall>` and `signature_params: Vec<ParamDef>`, so backends consume a fully-resolved structured recipe instead of redoing the lookup. When exactly one base metadata param's type names a `TypeDef` with a static `new` constructor, the extractor builds a `WrapperConstructorCall { metadata_param, wrapper_type_path, wrapper_type_name, constructor_method, args }` where each arg is either `Fixed { param_name, value_expr }` or `Free { param }`. The variant's `signature_params` is the non-fixed constructor params followed by any other base metadata params, in canonical order. Direct-mode resolution (no wrapper) remains unchanged: `wrapper_call` is `None` and `signature_params` is the non-overridden subset of base metadata. (`src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/service.rs`)

- **core: registration-variant mechanism for service registrations.** A registration may now declare a list of named _variants_ — pinned-parameter shortcuts over its base method. Each variant declares a name and a `fixed` map of base metadata-param-name → pinned value expression. Enum-typed pinned values are validated against the param's `EnumDef` at extract time and resolved to the fully-qualified Rust variant path (`<EnumDef::rust_path>::<Variant>`); non-enum values pass through verbatim. The new `RegistrationVariantSpec` lives in `src/core/config/service.rs`; the resolved `RegistrationVariant` + `RegistrationVariantOverride` IR shapes live in `src/core/ir.rs`; resolution is wired in `src/extract/extractor/service.rs::resolve_variants`. Field names are library-agnostic (`name`, `fixed`, `doc`) — alef carries zero domain knowledge about what the variants mean. Per-backend codegen (Wave B.2) will emit one idiomatic shortcut method per variant on each language's service-owner surface. (`src/core/config/service.rs`, `src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/service.rs`)

### Fixed

- **swift: correct parameter naming and undefined identifier in JSON-string overloads.** Fixed two regressions in the JSON-string convenience overloads generator: (1) the config parameter was named identically to the decoded local variable (`config`), causing shadowing and compilation errors; now named `configJson` throughout; (2) the overload body referenced an undefined raw identifier (`extraction_config`) instead of the parameter name; now correctly references the parameter name. Additionally, both async and sync variants are now emitted — previously, only sync variants were generated. Updated tests to reflect the corrected behavior. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `tests/backends_swift_json_string_overloads_test.rs`)

- **native ABI backends: harden length, ownership, and nullable binary generation.** FFI `_len` companions now read the byte length recorded by the primary C-string return instead of re-running the Rust operation, and generated C Doxygen text escapes nested block-comment delimiters. Zig preserves primary FFI error state before reading `_len` and emits non-panicking lifecycle vtable defaults. Go registration frees Rust-allocated error strings through the generated FFI free function and replaces duplicate registry handles safely. C# SafeHandle release and named-param teardown paths guard zero handles. Kotlin Android/JNI emits optional byte buffers as nullable `ByteArray` facade params and decodes empty-array sentinels as `None`. (`src/backends/{ffi,zig,go,csharp,kotlin_android,jni}`, `src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`)

- **high-level bindings: tighten Node, Python, PHP, and R generation edge cases.** Node declarations now keep native `extractFile`/`extractFileSync` argument order, avoid `Buffer` in generated `.d.ts` byte types, deduplicate and split TypeScript barrel value/type exports, and add `@types/node` to TypeScript e2e packages. Python public converters now accept JSON strings before dict/object coercion for defaulted config types. PHP stubs now honor PHP exclude lists and use serde wire values for enum cases. R/extendr method wrappers now bind `self` explicitly instead of mutating method environments. (`src/backends/{napi,pyo3,php,extendr}/`, `src/e2e/templates/typescript/package.json.jinja`)

- **docs: normalize default values in markdown tables to prevent MD038/MD055/MD056 violations.** The docs generator was emitting default values with newlines and multiple consecutive spaces, causing markdown linting failures: MD038 (spaces inside code spans), MD055/MD056 (table pipe style and cell count mismatches). Fixed by normalizing all default value strings through `collapse_whitespace()` in both `format_field_default()` for raw string defaults and in `format_typed_default()` for `StringLiteral` defaults. This ensures single-line, single-spaced output suitable for markdown table cells. Pipes inside default values are still escaped by `escape_table_cell()` at the call site. Affected functions: `format_field_default()`, `format_typed_default()` in `src/docs/formatting.rs`. (`src/docs/formatting.rs`)

- **swift: emit correct e2e test stub signatures and cleanup.** The swift e2e test generator was emitting trait bridge stub methods with positional (underscore-prefixed) parameters instead of labeled parameters, causing generated stubs to fail protocol conformance. Additionally, register function calls were incorrectly receiving labeled arguments when they should be positional. Finally, plugin registration tests did not emit cleanup unregister calls, causing registered backends to leak into subsequent tests and breaking test isolation. This fixes three ALEF_GAPs from the May 2026 swift binding audit: (1) emit labeled params in trait bridge stubs (remove `_` prefix); (2) detect register calls and force positional args; (3) emit `try? Module.unregister<Trait>("adapter-name")` after plugin registration tests to clean up registry state. These changes are wired into `emit_test_backend` and `build_args_and_setup` in `src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs` and the test method renderer in `render_test_method`. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

- **wasm: apply `source_crate_remaps` to error converters in code emission.** The wasm backend's error converter generation was not applying `source_crate_remaps` to error type paths. When a source crate (e.g. `spikard`) was remapped to a different crate (e.g. `spikard_http`), the generated error handler code would still reference the original crate name, causing compile errors when that crate wasn't a dependency. The fix threads `source_remaps` through `gen_wasm_error_converter` so error type paths are consistently rewritten from their source crate to the target crate before code emission. Excluded `App`, `RouteBuilder`, and `AppError` from wasm bindings since they are service-API types (wasm has `skip_languages = ["wasm"]` on services) and live in `spikard` (the facade), which pulls in tokio/mio incompatible with wasm32-unknown-unknown. This resolves 23 wasm compile errors across type resolution and error handling code paths. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/{mod,errors}.rs`, `src/codegen/error_gen.rs`)

- **ruby: emit assertions for simple-result calls with `equals` and `contains` types.** The ruby e2e codegen was skipping `equals` and `contains` assertions on simple-result calls (calls returning scalar values like `String` from `detect_mime_type_from_bytes`). The fix adds handlers for `equals` (emitting `expect(result).to eq(...)`) and `contains` (emitting `expect(result).to include(...)`), enabling test fixtures to assert on simple results. This resolves the 3-fixture gap between Ruby (97 tests) and the polyglot baseline (100 tests after universal skips): `mime_detect_bytes`, `mime_detect_image`, and `mime_get_extensions` now generate executable test cases. Alef config: add `"result"` to `[crates.e2e] result_fields` to recognize "result" as a valid field in e2e fixture assertions. (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`)

- **go: implement handle registry for trait-bridge lifetime management.** The Go backend now generates a handle registry (`handleRegistry` type with thread-safe `store()`, `delete()`, and `clear()` methods) to track `cgo.Handle` instances by plugin name. On successful registration, handles are stored in the registry; on unregister, they are deleted; on clear, all handles are cleaned. This prevents handle leaks and use-after-free crashes when plugins are registered/unregistered repeatedly. Each trait bridge (DocumentExtractor, OcrBackend, PostProcessor, Validator, EmbeddingBackend, Renderer) gets its own registry instance. The templates `register_c_call.jinja`, `unregister_c_call.jinja`, and `clear_c_call.jinja` are now updated to call registry methods at the appropriate lifecycle points. This resolves BINDING_BUG #3 from the kreuzberg go audit (May 2026). (`src/backends/go/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/go/templates/{register,unregister,clear}_c_call.jinja`)

- **dart: emit trait-bridge stub types and export traits.dart from bindings wrapper.** The Dart backend now emits four trait-bridge stub types (`OcrBackendType`, `ProcessingStage`, `InternalDocument`, `SyncExtractor`) at the end of `traits.dart` so that e2e test fixtures can construct plugin implementations. Additionally, `export 'traits.dart';` is now emitted in the main wrapper module (`src/{module_name}.dart`) so that test stubs and enums are re-exported from the public `lib.dart` barrel. This resolves ALEF_GAP #1 from the kreuzberg dart audit where test fixtures could not import the stub types needed to satisfy plugin trait signatures. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/dart_traits.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **node: deduplicate trait-bridge function declarations in .d.ts.** The NAPI-RS type definition generator was emitting duplicate `export declare function` declarations for the six trait-bridge management functions (register, unregister, clear for each of 6 trait bridges: OcrBackend, PostProcessor, Validator, EmbeddingBackend, DocumentExtractor, Renderer). This was caused by duplicate entries in the `all_decls` vector during type emission. Added `dedup_by` call after sorting to remove duplicates by name before emission. (`src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/errors.rs`)

- **kotlin-android: emit complete Jackson mapper configuration with ByteArray serializer and KotlinModule defaults.** The kotlin-android backend now generates a full Jackson mapper configuration that matches kreuzberg's production binding, resolving five ALEF_GAPs from the May 2026 kotlin-android audit: (1) custom ByteArray serializer that encodes as JSON array `[u8, u8, ...]` instead of Jackson's default Base64 string, matching Rust serde's `Vec<u8>` wire format; (2) `Jdk8Module()` registration for `Optional<T>` support; (3) `KotlinModule` with `NullIsSameAsDefault`, `NullToEmptyCollection`, and `NullToEmptyMap` enabled so missing JSON fields use Kotlin constructor defaults and Rust omitted-field patterns work correctly; (4) `JsonInclude.Include.NON_EMPTY` to exclude null/empty fields during serialization, allowing Rust serde defaults to apply; (5) `FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES = false` to allow Rust to add new fields without breaking old Kotlin clients. The mapper is now initialized in the generated `<Module>.kt` singleton and configured for snake_case property naming (Rust wire format) → camelCase (Kotlin convention) translation. All Jackson imports are now included in the codegen. This eliminates the kreuzberg hand-edit `private val byteArrayModule` and the four Jackson config lines that were previously required at every binding level. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **java: throw exception on NULL JSON serialization instead of returning null.** The Java FFI generator previously emitted `return null;` when a named-type conversion returned NULL, causing silent failures instead of reporting errors. Non-optional returns now throw a descriptive exception (`methodName: failed to serialize response`) matching the audit-established error handling contract. Optional returns still return `Optional.empty()`. This resolves audit BUG #2 from the Java binding audit (May 2026). (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`)

- **csharp: add try-finally guards for handle cleanup on async/sync wrapper functions with Bytes/Vec parameters.** The C# wrapper generator now emits an outer try-finally block for functions that have `Bytes` (GCHandle) or `Vec/Map` (HGlobal) parameters. This ensures deterministic cleanup even if setup or native FFI calls throw exceptions. Async functions wrap the entire setup + Task.Run + cleanup in try-finally; sync functions wrap the body when cleanup is needed. This prevents memory leaks when `ExtractionConfigFromJson` fails before the native call (GCHandle was previously pinned indefinitely) or when result marshalling throws (HGlobal or ConfigHandle was never freed). Generated by `gen_wrapper_function` in `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`; changes indentation of try-block contents appropriately for both sync and async code paths.

- **pyo3: bind non-Unit `Finalize` entrypoint returns to `_` to silence `unused_must_use`.** When a service has a `Finalize` entrypoint whose return type isn't `Unit` (e.g. `into_router() -> Result<Router, _>`), `build_ep_call` previously emitted `owner.into_router().map_err(...)?;` — after `?`-propagation the unwrapped `Router` was dropped, triggering Rust's `unused_must_use` warning on every regen. The fix prefixes a `let _ =` bind when `ep.return_type` is non-Unit, applied uniformly to both the sync-fallible and async branches. `cargo build -p spikard-py` is now warning-clean. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **swift: complete swift-bridge wrapper plumbing — `swift build` is green end-to-end.** Six template/codegen fixes layered on top of the prior `extern_callbacks.rs` relocation and service-method wiring: (1) emit a `ServiceError` enum in every alef-generated `<Service>.swift` so the wrapper class can throw `.invalidHandle` / `.registrationFailed` / `.runtime(String)`; (2) prepend `import RustBridge` to the wrapper file because the wrapper references types from the sibling swift-bridge Swift target; (3) prefix `Named` metadata-param swift types with `RustBridge.` in the public `route(...)` signature so callers pass the bridge wrapper directly (e.g. `builder: RustBridge.RouteBuilder`); (4) call swift-bridge free module functions (`RustBridge.config(inner)` / `RustBridge.run(inner).toString()`) instead of methods on the wrapper class — swift-bridge generates extern-Rust `fn foo(client: &mut App)` declarations as Swift free fns, not as methods on the App class; (5) rewrite the entrypoint template to use String-envelope error handling (`if !result.isEmpty { throw … }`) since swift-bridge 0.1.59 cannot bridge native Swift `Result`s — the Rust wrapper flattens `Result<T, E>` into a String; (6) emit a `<service>_raw_ptr` extern fn in the bridge module + a Rust implementation that exposes the wrapper's address as `usize`, then call `OpaquePointer(bitPattern: RustBridge.<service>RawPtr(inner))!` in Swift to hand the raw pointer to the `@_silgen_name` callback registration shim — swift-bridge stores its raw `ptr` field with `internal` visibility, unreachable from consumer modules. `cargo build -p spikard-swift` and `swift build` (packages/swift) now both produce zero errors. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/service_app_wrappers.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/{rust_extern_service_methods.rs,swift_configurator.swift,swift_entrypoint.swift,swift_init.swift,swift_registration.swift}.jinja`)

- **dart: plugin-trait stub generator maps internal-only types to public types for both return types and defaults.** The e2e dart test stub emitter (`emit_test_backend` in `src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`) had a regression where trait methods returning internal-only types (e.g., `InternalDocument`) would correctly map the return type signature to `Future<ExtractionResult>` but emit an incorrect default value `InternalDocument()` instead of `ExtractionResult()`. This caused kreuzberg dart e2e plugin API tests to fail at runtime when the test stub returned an unmappable type. The fix extends the `emit_dart_default_for_type` helper to apply the same internal→public type fallback mapping (via `map_dart_type_with_fallback`) when computing default values. Generic unit tests added to `src/backends/dart/plugin_trait_stubs_test.rs` cover async/sync method signatures, correct type mapping for internal types, non-awaited factory calls, and typed parameters.

- **wasm: feature-gate Input DTO fields whose type is unavailable in the target's feature set.** Optional struct fields whose Rust type lives behind a `#[cfg(feature = "...")]` guard now get a matching `#[cfg(...)]` on the generated DTO field plus `#[serde(skip)]` so the binding compiles cleanly under any feature subset (e.g. WASM builds that disable ORT-dependent features). The Input-DTO codepath threads the target's `exclude_types` list and `enabled_features` into a new `gen_input_dto_for_type_with_cfg` entry point; the legacy `gen_input_dto_for_type` wrapper preserves prior behavior for callers that do not need feature awareness. The template emits both `#[cfg]` and `#[serde(skip)]` for skipped fields and re-applies the `#[cfg]` guard inside the `From<Input>` conversion so feature-disabled paths compile out cleanly. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/{mod,functions}.rs`, `src/backends/wasm/templates/gen_input_dto.jinja`)

- **swift: always emit minimal RustBridgeC.h placeholder when build output missing.** The previous detection logic (`!content.contains("__private__FfiSlice")`) was unreliable because it could not distinguish between an old minimal placeholder and a full header from a prior build (both contain the typedef). When build output is missing on a downstream regeneration (e.g., kreuzberg `alef all` before `cargo build -p kreuzberg-swift`), the generator would skip emitting any file, leaving a stale full header on disk. The fix is to always emit the minimal placeholder when build output is missing, which is safe because: (1) the minimal placeholder is idempotent, (2) once build output becomes available, the full header will overwrite it, (3) prior full headers contain the same typedefs, making re-emission harmless. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **Docs generator now produces rumdl-compliant markdown for configuration and types docs.** Heading demotion is now applied at every doc-comment splice site, including `render_enum_for_shared_doc` (previously missed, causing MD025/MD001 violations). Multi-line and multi-space field defaults are collapsed before table-cell emission, preventing MD038 code-span violations. Bullet list markers are normalized from `*` to `-` to match rumdl style preference (MD004). Added comprehensive test coverage for each violation type. (`src/docs/mod.rs`, `src/docs/doc_cleaning.rs`, `src/docs/formatting.rs`)

- **ffi scaffold: emit `crate-type = ["cdylib", "staticlib", "rlib"]` so Rust-side consumers (e.g. JNI shims) can link the generated FFI crate as an rlib.** `cdylib` and `staticlib` artifacts expose only the C ABI; a downstream Rust crate that imports symbols via `use ffi_crate::*` requires an `rlib` artifact. Without it the Rust toolchain cannot resolve the crate's Rust namespace and any `cargo build` of a shim that depends on the FFI crate as a Rust library fails with `can't find crate`. (`src/scaffold/languages/ffi.rs`)

- **swift: complete the service callback wiring so `cargo build -p spikard-swift` is green.** Four template/codegen fixes that landed on top of the `extern_callbacks.rs` relocation: (1) emit free-fn shims `pub fn config(client: &mut App) { client.config() }` / `pub fn run(client: &mut App) -> String { client.run() }` alongside the `App` wrapper so the bridge-side `extern "Rust" { fn config(client: &mut App); }` declarations actually resolve; (2) pass metadata params positionally to the service registration method and unwrap swift-bridge tuple-struct opaques with `.0` (the previous `RouteBuilder::default().builder(builder)` chain double-wrapped and called a non-existent `default()` associated fn); (3) annotate the bridge callback handler as `std::sync::Arc<dyn TraitPath>` so the spikard `IntoHandler` blanket-impl resolves (`Arc<concrete>` does not); (4) skip emission of free functions referenced as `HandlerContractDef.response_adapter` — they are library-internal Result→HostType plumbing that the host languages cannot model. The error path inside the handler bridge now routes through `response_adapter(Err(Box::new(e)))` instead of constructing an axum-typed StatusCode error directly, so the template no longer references `axum::http::StatusCode` (which was unavailable in the spikard-swift crate). `cargo build -p spikard-swift` now produces zero errors and zero warnings. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/{mod,service_app_wrappers}.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/rust_extern_c_register_via_callback.rs.jinja`)

- **swift: relocate service callback registration fns to `src/extern_callbacks.rs`.** Rust 2024 promoted `unsafe_attr_outside_unsafe` from a lint to a hard error, so plain `#[no_mangle]` no longer compiles even with a crate-level `allow`. swift-bridge 0.1.59 still pins syn 1.x, which cannot parse the `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` form — so a single source file cannot satisfy both. The Swift backend now emits these registration shims in a sibling Rust source file declared from `src/lib.rs` via `mod extern_callbacks;`. swift-bridge only parses `src/lib.rs`, so the relocated file is invisible to its syn-1 walker, while cargo still pulls it in at build time. The shims use the Rust 2024 `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` form. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/rust_extern_c_register_via_callback.rs.jinja`)

- **swift: keep service callback exports parseable by swift-bridge 0.1.59 on Rust 2024.** Service callback registration shims now emit plain `#[no_mangle]` and allow the Rust 2024 `unsafe_attr_outside_unsafe` lint at the generated bridge crate root, because swift-bridge 0.1.59 still uses syn 1.x and cannot parse `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]`. The JVM service integration test now asserts the current Java/Panama + Kotlin coroutine-wrapper layering instead of stale JNI extern declarations. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/rust_extern_c_register_via_callback.rs.jinja`, `tests/service_api_jvm_symbol_consistency.rs`)

- **ffi: drop the `{Type}Opaque` wrapper struct from static constructors.** Static constructors now return `*mut {qualified}` directly, matching the legacy `_free` signature and avoiding cbindgen headers with incomplete struct fields. Constructor bodies allocate the inner opaque type with `Box::into_raw(Box::new({qualified}::new(...)))`, so host bindings can free the pointer with the existing generated `_free` function. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/{mod,types}.rs`)

- **go: convert string-typed enum params to i32 via `{prefix}_{enum}_from_str` instead of direct `C.int32_t(...)`.** Go represents named enums as `type <Enum> string` aliases, so direct conversion to `int32_t` does not compile. The new template CString-allocates enum params, calls the generated FFI helper, and defers the allocation free before invoking the C symbol. (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/methods.rs`, `src/backends/go/templates/param_enum_to_i32.jinja`)
