- **go: split Go-local FFI enum tracking for data enums vs unit-variant params.** `uses_ffi_enum_type` now allows unit-variant enum params through the i32 path while still skipping data enums that lack JSON helper handles. The new `ffi_param_enum_names` set is threaded through function, method, streaming, and parameter conversion codegen. (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/{mod,methods,functions}.rs`, `src/backends/go/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/go/templates/param_enum_to_i32.jinja` [new])

- **swift: remove the blank line after `extern "Rust" {` in `from_json` helper blocks.** swift-bridge 0.1.59 rejects blank lines inside these extern blocks, so the generator now emits the first helper declaration immediately after the opening brace. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/*.jinja`, plus formatter-only rewrites in other backends)

- **ffi: emit i32 for all unit-variant enums (not just `Copy`-typed), unblocking opaque static constructors with enum params for every C-ABI host.** The `enum_names` set in `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/mod.rs` was built with `.filter(|e| e.is_copy)` and reused for TWO unrelated concerns: (a) `.clone()` vs auto-deref decisions in value conversion (correctly Copy-only); (b) FFI param-type emission (`i32` discriminant vs `*const Type`) which should apply to ALL unit-variant enums regardless of `Copy`. Non-Copy enums like spikard's `Method` (which has a `Custom(String)` variant — actually data-bearing, but the unit variants ride the same discriminant convention) were excluded, so `RouteBuilder::new(method: Method, path: &str) -> Self` emitted `extern "C" fn ...(method: *const Method, ...)` and the only Method helpers (`spikard_method_from_i32` / `from_str`) couldn't produce that pointer. Fix: split the concept — added `ffi_param_enums` covering all unit-variant enums alongside the existing `enum_names`. Threaded through `gen_opaque_static_constructor`, `gen_method_wrapper`, `gen_free_function`, `gen_free_function_len_companion`. Added private `{enum_snake}_from_i32_rs()` helpers. Also added `gen_opaque_struct_def` emitting the previously-missing `#[repr(C)] pub struct {TypeName}Opaque { pub(crate) inner: ... }` (the `*mut RouteBuilderOpaque` returned by the constructor was referencing an undefined type). Tightened `is_static_constructor` to require `method.name == "new"` (previously `default()` also matched, producing duplicate `{prefix}_{type_snake}_new` symbols). Switched body enum reconstruction from `ok_or_else(...)?` to `match { Some(v) => v, None => { set_last_error(...); return null_mut(); } }` (the `?` operator is unavailable in `*mut T`-returning functions). Removed the dead `c_param_type_with_paths` wrapper. Verified: 121 ffi tests + clippy + stub guard + `cargo build -p spikard-ffi` all green. cbindgen-regenerated `crates/spikard-ffi/include/spikard.h` confirms `int32_t` for the method param. **ABI break** to the cbindgen-generated header — host backends previously emitting `(*C.SPIKARDMethod)(...)` now pass bare `int32_t`. Wave 2 host backend codegen (commits `bc49f62a7` + `525fd4068`) was already prepared for this. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/{mod,types,functions}.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/type_map.rs`)

- **swift: split per-service extern block into TWO blocks (type+constructor / methods-with-explicit-receivers) to work around swift-bridge 0.1.59 `expected path` parser limitation.** swift-bridge 0.1.59's parser rejects a specific combination: `type Foo;` declaration + `#[swift_bridge(init)] fn new() -> Foo;` constructor + instance methods with `&mut self` self-receiver all in the SAME `extern "Rust"` block. Split into two blocks: block 1 declares the type and the constructor; block 2 emits the instance methods using explicit `client: &mut <ServiceName>` parameters instead of `&mut self`. New templates: `rust_extern_service_type_and_constructor.rs.jinja` and `rust_extern_service_methods.rs.jinja`. Verified: alef tests + clippy + stub guard green. **Status: the persistent `expected path` parser error STILL fires post-split** — the swift agent that implemented the split-block isolated the error to a different part of lib.rs (suspected to be the blank-line cleanup in the JSON-helper `from_json` extern blocks, not the App service). This is a separate parser issue under task #34. The split-block structure is correct and lands as a prerequisite for whatever the next investigation reveals. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/{rust_extern_service_type_and_constructor,rust_extern_service_methods}.rs.jinja`)

- **java (Panama FFM): consume new ffi enum-i32 + opaque-`new` signatures + drop duplicate constructor-handle emission.** Wave 2 consumer for the Panama FFM Java binding. (1) Added `gen_ffi_layout_with_enums()` to distinguish enum types (emit `ValueLayout.JAVA_INT` discriminant) from regular Named types (emit `ValueLayout.ADDRESS`). (2) Threaded `enum_names: &AHashSet<String>` through `gen_static_factory_method()` and `gen_opaque_handle_class()` so static factory params get `.ordinal()` (i32) marshalling instead of JSON `_from_json`; optional enum params use `-1` sentinel when null. (3) The newly-added emission of `_NEW` constructor MethodHandles was REDUNDANT with the existing per-method-handle loop (line ~625) AND collided when a type had multiple static-returning-Self methods (e.g. `new()` + `default()` both resolved to the same `{PREFIX}_{TYPE}_NEW` handle name → duplicate-definition compile error). Removed the redundant emission. Verified: `cargo test --lib backends::java` + `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` + `cd packages/java && mvn compile` all green. The regenerated `NativeLib.java` now has exactly one `SPIKARD_GRAPH_QL_ROUTE_CONFIG_NEW` MethodHandle (emitted by the per-method-handle loop with proper method-name suffixing). (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/{marshal,native_lib,types,mod}.rs`)

- **kotlin (JNI): consume new ffi enum-i32 + opaque-`new` signatures.** Wave 2 consumer for the JNI-based Kotlin binding. Added `is_enum_param()` + `extract_named_type()` helpers and updated `emit_constructor_jni_external_funs()` + `emit_jni_client_factory()` so static-factory enum params map to `Int` (the i32 discriminant representation) instead of the default `Long`, and call sites convert via `.ordinal`. Verified: `cargo test --lib backends::kotlin` + `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` + `cd packages/kotlin && gradle compileKotlin` all green. Generated factory: `companion object { fun create(method: Method, path: String): RouteBuilder = RouteBuilder(DemoBridge.nativeNewRouteBuilder(method.ordinal, path)) }`. (`src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/jni_emitter.rs`)

- **zig: consume new ffi enum-i32 + opaque-`new` signatures via top-level static methods.** Wave 2 consumer for the Zig binding. Added `emit_opaque_static_method()` emitting top-level Zig functions for static constructors that (a) take Zig-idiomatic types — enum types directly, strings as `[]const u8`, structs as `[]const u8` JSON; (b) convert enums to i32 via `@intFromEnum`; (c) dupeZ strings to NUL-terminated C pointers with defer cleanup; (d) call the FFI constructor symbol; (e) wrap return in `error{OutOfMemory}!RouteBuilder`. Threaded `enum_names: &AHashSet<String>` through `emit_opaque_method`, `method_c_arg_names`, `emit_method_param_conversion` so instance methods also marshal enum params as i32 discriminants. Verified: `cargo test --lib backends::zig` (all 41 tests) + `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` + `cd packages/zig && zig build` all green. Generated: `pub fn new(method: Method, path: []const u8) error{OutOfMemory}!RouteBuilder { const method_i32: i32 = @intFromEnum(method); const path_z = try std.heap.c_allocator.dupeZ(u8, path); defer std.heap.c_allocator.free(path_z); const _handle = c.spikard_route_builder_new(method_i32, path_z.ptr); ... }`. (`src/backends/zig/gen_bindings/{opaque_handles,mod}.rs`)

- **swift: rename consolidated service init fn from `<service_snake>_new` to `new`.** The swift consolidated extern block previously emitted `#[swift_bridge(init)] fn app_new() -> App;` but swift-bridge 0.1.59's `#[swift_bridge(init)]` convention follows the rest of the bridge's opaque-type pattern of naming the constructor exactly `new` (matches every other init-annotated function in the same file). Note: this alone does NOT resolve the persistent `expected path` parse error in swift-bridge-build; the root cause remains elsewhere in the consolidated block. Tracking as part of task #34. (`src/backends/swift/templates/rust_extern_service_consolidated.rs.jinja`)

- **go: opaque static `new` constructors + enum_names threading for future i32 marshalling.** The go backend's opaque-method codegen blanket-skipped static methods returning a Named opaque type (citing "conversion pipeline not implemented"). This blocked emission of constructors like `RouteBuilder::new(Method, &str) -> Self`. Removed the blanket skip for static methods named `new` that return the owner type, and added a `gen_opaque_static_constructor`-style emission path. Threaded `enum_names: &HashSet<String>` through `gen_method_wrapper`, `gen_streaming_method_wrapper`, `gen_function_wrapper`, and `gen_param_to_c` to enable future i32 enum-param marshalling once the ffi backend emits i32 discriminants for non-Copy enums. Also fixed the static-vs-instance builder-pattern logic so static constructors create new struct wrappers instead of cloning a receiver. Verified: `cargo test --lib backends::go` + `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` + `cd packages/go && go build ./...` all green; the regenerated `binding.go` emits `func GraphQLRouteConfigNew() *GraphQLRouteConfig` (zero-arg constructor reachable). Methods with non-Copy enum params (e.g. `RouteBuilder.new(Method, path)`) still skipped pending ffi backend update to emit i32 for all enums. (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/{mod,methods,functions,service_api}.rs`)

- **csharp: opaque static constructors with params + enum_names threading for future i32 marshalling.** The csharp backend gained a public-constructor emission path for static methods with parameters that return the owner opaque type (e.g. `RouteBuilder::new(method: Method, path: &str) -> Self`). The new `gen_opaque_static_constructor` function marshals params, calls the FFI ctor symbol, wraps the returned IntPtr in the SafeHandle, and throws on null. Zero-arg `new()` remains handled by the existing `gen_opaque_factory_method` path (via `client_constructor` config), so `is_static_constructor` requires at least one param to avoid duplicate-constructor errors. Threaded `enum_names: &HashSet<String>` through `emit_named_param_setup`, `emit_named_param_teardown`, `emit_named_param_teardown_indented`, and `emit_record_methods` to enable future i32 enum-param marshalling once the ffi backend emits i32 discriminants for non-Copy enums. Verified: `cargo test --lib backends::csharp` + `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` + `cd packages/csharp/Spikard && dotnet build` all green (0 errors, 0 warnings). (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/{mod,methods,types,service_api}.rs`)

- **swift: consolidate per-method extern blocks into single per-service extern block (partial: cleaner emission but `expected path` parse error persists).** The swift consolidated-template approach replaces multiple `extern "Rust" { type App; … }` blocks (each re-declaring the type) with a single block emitting `type App;` once followed by all configurators + entrypoints + the `#[swift_bridge(init)] fn app_new() -> App` constructor. New template `rust_extern_service_consolidated.rs.jinja` (19 lines). `gen_service_rust_extern_blocks` refactored to collect configurators + entrypoints into `Vec<minijinja::Value>` and render the consolidated template once instead of one per method. Also collapsed an `if/else` with identical branches (clippy `if_same_then_else`) and simplified a boolean (`nonminimal_bool`). Verified: `cargo test --lib backends::swift` + `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` green. Spikard-swift build still hits `Error while parsing "src/lib.rs" expected path` — root cause now isolated to the bridge module's content, not the multi-block structure. Likely candidates for the next pass: `#[swift_bridge(init)]` may need to be on a function named exactly `new`, not `app_new`; or `fn run(&mut self) -> String` returning a non-Result `String` may need a different signature. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/swift/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/rust_extern_service_consolidated.rs.jinja`)

- **swift: callback redesign + App wrapper struct + sanitized-method gate + non-bridge service wrappers (partial: parses past `H`/`u8`/`>`/`RustString` errors, blocked on `expected path`).** Substantial swift backend progress unblocking the prior `swift-bridge 0.1.59` parser failures. (1) Moved the C callback registration OUTSIDE the `#[swift_bridge::bridge]` module — raw pointer types (`*const u8`, `*mut c_void`) and function-pointer types are not parseable inside `extern "Rust"` blocks. New template `rust_extern_c_register_via_callback.rs.jinja` emits a plain `#[no_mangle] extern "C" fn` symbol; new template `swift_silgen_callback.swift.jinja` emits Swift's `@_silgen_name + @convention(c)` wrapper to call it. (2) Added a new file `gen_rust_crate/service_app_wrappers.rs` emitting a wrapper struct `pub struct App { pub inner: tokio::sync::Mutex<Option<spikard::App>> }` plus `new()` / `config(&mut self)` / `run(&mut self) -> String` impl (Result/Error not bridgeable in swift-bridge 0.1.59 — return String envelope). (3) Updated `entrypoint_return_representable` predicate to take `&ServiceDef` and check `method.sanitized` (matching the dart fix), so finalize entrypoints whose source returns an unknown foreign type (`axum::Router → String` IR translation) are skipped from emission. (4) `RustString` is not accepted in `extern "Rust"` blocks — replace_all changed to `String`. (5) `swift_registration.swift.jinja` updated to match the C-callback ABI. **Known follow-up:** `swift-bridge-build` still emits `Error while parsing "src/lib.rs" expected path` after these fixes. The blocker is suspected to be either (a) multiple `type App;` declarations across separate `extern "Rust"` blocks not supported in 0.1.59 (each configurator/entrypoint currently emits its own block with `type App;`), or (b) a token swift-bridge cannot consume in one of the emitted bridge declarations. Resolution requires bisecting which emitted block produces the error and likely consolidating all `App` methods into a single `extern "Rust"` block (one `type App;` followed by all `fn` declarations). Deferred to a focused swift session. Tests: 3011 lib tests + 57 swift backend tests + clippy clean. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/{mod,cargo,shims,wrappers}.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/service_app_wrappers.rs`, `src/backends/swift/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/{rust_extern_configurator,rust_extern_entrypoint,rust_extern_c_register_via_callback,swift_silgen_callback,swift_registration}.jinja`)

- **swift gen_rust_crate: augment `exclude_types` with IR `binding_excluded`.** The swift Rust-crate emission (`packages/swift/rust/src/lib.rs`) was emitting a stale pre-pivot `extern "Rust" { type App; … fn app_route(client: &mut App, builder: RouteBuilder, handler: H) -> …; }` block in addition to the new service_api emission, causing swift-bridge-build to panic with `Type must be declared with \`type H\`` at build-time parse. The `gen_bindings/mod.rs` side already augmented its `exclude_types` set with `binding_excluded` IR types (line ~32), but `gen_rust_crate/mod.rs` did NOT — it only honored the `[crates.swift].exclude_types` config knob. Fixed: mirror the same augmentation in `gen_rust_crate/mod.rs` so service-owner types whose declaration is owned by the service_api codegen are excluded from regular opaque/extern-block emission. Note: this unblocks the `H`-generic panic but a second swift-bridge 0.1.59 limitation (raw pointer types `*const u8` / `*mut c_void` not parseable in `extern "Rust"` blocks) still blocks `cargo build -p spikard-swift`. The fix for that requires moving the C callback registration outside the `#[swift_bridge::bridge]` module — deferred to a follow-up. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`)

- **dart codegen: nine codegen bugs resolved end-to-end (`cargo build -p spikard-dart` + `dart analyze` now 0 errors).** The dart backend's service-API codegen had nine cumulative bugs preventing `cargo build -p spikard-dart` from compiling. (1) `App` service-owner type marked with `binding_excluded=true` (in the service extractor) was still being emitted to `lib.rs` alongside the new `mod service_api;` FRB-opaque form, causing 20 struct-name collisions and 17 unbound-type-variable errors. Fixed: added `&& !t.binding_excluded` filter to all type/enum emission loops in `emit_lib_rs` (lines ~229, ~282, ~303, ~317, ~336, ~407, ~490). (2) Generated `service_api.rs` referenced `std::pin::Pin`, `tokio::sync::Mutex`, `DartFnFuture`, `crate::ServerConfig`, and `RouteBuilder` without importing them. Fixed: added explicit use statements to `file_header.rs.jinja`. (3) `Finalize` entrypoint with `sanitized=true` source method (return type mapped from unknown foreign type to String) was emitted as an FRB method despite being unrepresentable. Fixed: updated `entrypoint_return_representable` predicate to accept `service: &ServiceDef` parameter, look up the source method, and return false if `method.sanitized==true`; updated the call site in `gen_entrypoint_method`; added `test_skip_sanitized_finalize`. (4) `entrypoint_method.rs.jinja` called `inner.run(self)` (consuming self), then attempted to restore the consumed value with `guard.replace(inner)`, producing E0382. Fixed: removed the restore block; both Run and Finalize are terminal so take-and-discard is correct. Changed `let mut inner` to `let inner`. (5) Doc comment template ended without a trailing newline, so the following struct declaration was concatenated onto the same line (`/// Spikard application builder.#[frb(opaque)]`). Fixed: added `out.push('\n')` before rendering the struct template, plus changed the doc template's `{%- if doc_lines %}` to plain `{% if doc_lines %}` so the inner blank-line separator survives. (6) `packages/dart/rust/Cargo.toml` did not pull `tokio` when only service-API codegen was active. Fixed: extended `gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs` `tokio_dep` selection to also depend on `!api.services.is_empty()`, and added the `sync` feature (for `tokio::sync::Mutex`) alongside `rt-multi-thread`. (7) `mirror_type_names` only covered `api.types`, not `api.enums`. Result: a mirror enum like `Method` was both `use spikard::Method;`-imported (by an opaque impl block referencing the type) and re-declared as `#[frb(mirror(Method))]`, causing E0255 redefinition AND E0117 orphan-rule on the auto-generated `impl From<spikard::Method> for Method` (the `use` shadowed the local mirror, making both sides of the impl foreign). Fixed: extended `mirror_type_names` to chain `api.enums` filtered by `!binding_excluded`. (8) Registration method passed `Arc<DartHandlerHandler>` to the inner service method, but spikard's `App::route<H: IntoHandler>` only accepts `Arc<dyn Handler>` via `IntoHandler`. Fixed: registration template now emits `let bridge: std::sync::Arc<dyn {{ trait_path }}> = std::sync::Arc::new(crate::{{ bridge_name }}::new(...));` so the explicit trait-object cast satisfies `IntoHandler`. `trait_path` is resolved from the contract's `rust_path` (or trait name fallback). Also: metadata params that resolve to local opaque mirrors (e.g. `RouteBuilder`) are unwrapped via `.inner` before being passed to the inner service method; non-opaque mirrors use `.into()`. (9) Configurator method called `inner.config(args)` via `&mut self`, but spikard's `App::config(mut self) -> Self` consumes self, producing E0507. Fixed: configurator template now uses a take-modify-replace pattern (`if let Some(inner) = guard.take() { *guard = Some(inner.config(...)); }`) for `Owned`-receiver configurators, with a parallel `&mut`-receiver branch that mutates in place then re-inserts. Threaded `is_owned` from `MethodDef.receiver` into the template context. End state: `cargo build -p spikard-dart` succeeds with only 3 cosmetic warnings about unused bare-name imports (the code uses fully-qualified paths); `dart pub get && dart analyze` reports `No issues found!`. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/{mod,cargo}.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/dart/templates/service_api/{file_header,entrypoint_method,registration_method,configurator_method,service_owner_doc}.rs.jinja`)

- **swift: instance-method call style + no empty leading comma + switch/case on own line + Named metadata typed correctly + skip non-representable Finalize.** The swift backend's service-API codegen had five bugs in the jinja templates. (1) Metadata params built by `gen_registration_method` added a leading comma in `meta_sig`, and the template `swift_registration.swift.jinja:3` added another, producing empty `„ "` between `handler` and `builder` params. Fixed by removing the leading comma from the Rust codegen side. (2) The configurator and entrypoint templates used `{%-` block-opener tags, stripping preceding whitespace and causing `switch` and `case` statements to collapse onto the previous line. Fixed by changing to `{% ` (plain brace + space, no leading minus) on block-openers that render control-flow statements. (3) Templates called rust-bridge methods via `RustBridge.{{ service_snake }}_{{ method_name }}(inner: &inner, …)` instead of using the swift-bridge instance-method convention. Fixed by changing to `inner.{{ method_name }}(…)` and adding `#[swift_bridge(swift_name = …)]` attributes to rust extern blocks so the swift names are explicit. (4) Named metadata types (e.g. `RouteBuilder`) were mapped to `"String"` by the catch-all `typeref_to_swift_type` fallback. Fixed by adding `TypeRef::Named(n) => n.clone()` branch to both `typeref_to_swift_type` and `typeref_to_rust_ffi_type`. (5) `Finalize` entrypoints with non-representable return types (e.g. `axum::Router → Vec<…>`) were emitted as `func intoRouter() -> String` instead of being skipped. Fixed by ensuring the existing `entrypoint_return_representable` predicate blocks generation. Updated in-file tests to verify instance-method calls, no double comma, switch/case on own lines, and Named types preserved; added `test_skip_non_representable_finalize` and `test_rust_extern_has_swift_bridge_names`. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/{swift_registration,swift_configurator,swift_entrypoint,rust_extern_configurator,rust_extern_register_via_callback,rust_extern_entrypoint}.jinja`)

- **dart: register handlers immediately + correct tokio Mutex API + emit `mod service_api;` instead of inline append + skip non-representable Finalize entrypoints.** The dart backend's service-API codegen had multiple bugs in the jinja templates. (1) Registration methods used the nonexistent `blocking_lock_mut()` method; changed to `blocking_lock()` + guard pattern. (2) Configurator methods emitted pseudo-code `inner.config(...)` with literal `(...)`; now pass real parameter names from the configurator's param list through the template context. (3) Entrypoint methods implemented a deferred-registration pattern that could not work — they drained pending registrations into a `registrations` vec, attempted runtime type dispatch, and emitted a stub comment acknowledging the impossibility. Redesigned: each `route()` / registration method now constructs the `DartHandlerX` bridge immediately and calls `inner.route(...)` synchronously (with `blocking_lock()` + `as_mut()`), storing no separate registrations vec; the `DartRegistration` struct is dead code and removed. (4) Entrypoints now simply acquire the inner via `blocking_lock()`, take it, call the method, and restore it — no registration draining, no match dispatch, no deferred work. (5) The service owner struct `registrations` field is removed. (6) Double-emit: lib.rs was being appended both in `gen_rust_crate/emit_lib_rs` and via a separate `service_api.rs` GeneratedFile — fixed by removing the emit_lib_rs append and adding `mod service_api; pub use service_api::*;` to lib.rs when services are present. (7) Non-representable return types: `Finalize` entrypoints with unrepresentable returns (e.g. `axum::Router`) are now properly skipped via the existing `entrypoint_return_representable` predicate. Updated in-file tests: `test_emit_service_owner_with_frb_opaque` now asserts no `registrations` field; added `test_registration_calls_inner_directly` asserting immediate dispatch and no match/drain logic. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/templates/service_api/{configurator_method,entrypoint_method,registration_method,service_owner_impl_open,service_owner_struct}.rs.jinja`)

### Added

- **Service handler contracts: generic dispatch-shape and response-adapter knobs.** `HandlerContractConfig` (and the corresponding `HandlerContractDef` IR) gained four optional fields so the generated async handler bridge can match a library's real dispatch signature and return type without the generator knowing anything library-specific: `dispatch_extra_params` (verbatim leading parameter declarations the bridge accepts and ignores — e.g. a foreign framework type the wire bridge does not consume), `wire_param_name` (name of the wire request parameter), `dispatch_return_type` (verbatim future `Output` type), and `response_adapter` (path to a library function converting the bridge's `Result<WireResponse, Box<dyn Error + Send + Sync>>` outcome into `dispatch_return_type`). When unset, behavior is unchanged (the bridge yields the wire response in a boxed-error `Result`). The pyo3 backend's `gen_handler_bridge` now drives its signature, future output, and body tail from these fields, replacing a hardcoded trait-name special case. (`src/core/config/service.rs`, `src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/service.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **Roll the generic dispatch-shape and response-adapter knobs across every Rust-impl handler-bridge backend.** The napi, magnus, php, rustler, extendr, ffi, and jni backends now drive their generated handler-bridge signature, future `Output`, and body tail from the same `dispatch_extra_params` / `wire_param_name` / `dispatch_return_type` / `response_adapter` knobs the pyo3 backend uses, so each can satisfy a library trait whose dispatch method receives extra framework parameters and returns a library-specific type. In the process all seven backends were standardized on emitting the canonical object-safe async-trait shape — a hand-written `fn dispatch(&self, ..) -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = ..> + Send + '_>>` returning `Box::pin(async move { .. })` — instead of the `#[async_trait]` macro, so the impl satisfies traits declared in that hand-written form (which `#[async_trait]` impls cannot). All fallible (`?`) work is kept inside the inner wire-result block so the only outer expression is the response-adapter tail. (`src/backends/{napi,magnus,php,rustler,extendr,ffi,jni}` service codegen)

### Changed

- **alef dart + swift service-API: pivot to bridge-based emission and refactor to minijinja templates (template content incomplete).** Two structural moves landed together. (1) Architecture pivot: the dart service backend now emits Rust source destined for the spikard-dart `packages/dart/rust/src/service_api.rs` (an `#[frb(opaque)]` owner + `DartFnFuture<String>` handler bridge implementing the contract trait via a manual `Pin<Box<dyn Future>>`); the swift service backend now emits a `#[swift_bridge::bridge]` `extern "Rust"` block plus a `Sources/Spikard/<Service>.swift` wrapper that boxes Swift closures via `Unmanaged.passRetained` and trampolines through a `@convention(c)` C callback (swift-bridge 0.1.59 lacks `extern "Swift"` / `Box<dyn Fn>` / `async fn`, hence the C-shim hybrid). Both backends discard their previous raw-C-symbol emission, which never linked because neither cdylib exports those symbols. (2) Refactor to minijinja: each backend now reads `static TEMPLATES: &[(&str, &str)] = &[ ("name.jinja", include_str!("templates/name.jinja")), … ]` and renders via `template_env::render(name, minijinja::context!{…})`, matching the rustler/go/zig convention. Dart emits 13 templates under `src/backends/dart/templates/service_api/`; swift emits 18 templates under `src/backends/swift/templates/` (split between `rust_extern_*.rs.jinja` for the bridge module and `swift_*.swift.jinja` for the wrapper). **Known follow-up — template content bugs:** the dart `entrypoint_method.rs.jinja` carries forward a stub-style dispatch comment ("// For now, we skip dispatch …") and incorrect `{%-` whitespace control (`match reg.method.as_str() { "route" => {` collapses onto one line); the dart `Finalize` entrypoint emits even when the return type is non-representable (e.g. `axum::Router`), and binds a `let result = inner.into_router()` for a non-async path then returns `Ok(result)` without the `let`. The swift `route(...)` emits an empty `,` from `meta_params` when the list is empty, the switch/case formatting for a unit-returning blocking entrypoint collapses onto one line, and the metadata param's `RouteBuilder` opaque type is mapped to `String` instead of its bridge-class form. The alef in-file tests pass (they check token presence, not syntactic correctness); spikard's `cargo build -p spikard-dart` and `swift build` still fail on the regen output. Verified post-this commit: `cargo test --lib` 3011/3011 green, `cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo test --test service_api_no_stub_markers` green. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/dart/templates/service_api/*.rs.jinja`, `src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/*.jinja`)

### Fixed

- **alef ffi: emit enum-typed FFI params by value as i32 and emit opaque static `new` constructors as C symbols.** The C-ABI host bindings (Go, C#, Zig, Java, Kotlin) need to construct a `RouteBuilder.new(method, path)` and pass a `Method` enum value across the boundary, but the ffi backend previously emitted `extern "C" fn <prefix>_route_builder_new(method: *const <core>::Method, path: ...)` — taking an opaque pointer to the enum — while the only available helper (`<prefix>_method_from_i32`) returns an `i32`, not a `*const Method`. No host could produce the required pointer. Two changes resolve this. (1) `type_map.rs`'s new `c_param_type_with_paths_and_enums` emits `Named` enum params as `i32` (the discriminant) instead of `*const Enum`; the Rust body reconstructs the enum value via the existing `<prefix>_<enum>_from_i32` helper. The `Optional<Enum>` form uses `i32` with a `-1` sentinel for `None`. The fallback path-lookup when `path_map` is empty now correctly preserves the `core_import::` prefix instead of dropping it. (2) `types.rs`'s new `gen_opaque_static_constructor` detects associated functions named `new` whose return type is the surface-wrapped opaque itself, and emits `#[no_mangle] pub unsafe extern "C" fn <prefix>_<typename_snake>_new(<params>) -> *mut <Typename>Opaque`, marshalling each param through the new enum-aware helper and wrapping the call in `Box::into_raw(Box::new(<Typename>Opaque { inner: ... }))`. `mod.rs` wires the new constructor emission into the per-type loop and `functions.rs::should_skip_method_wrapper` skips the static `new` in the general-method-wrapper pass so it isn't emitted twice. The cbindgen-driven C header reflects the new `int32_t` enum signature automatically on the next ffi crate build. (`src/backends/ffi/type_map.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/methods.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **alef dart, swift, rustler service-API: properly comment-prefix multi-line Rust docs (rustler also drops the redundant `Elixir.` defmodule prefix and emits real `@doc` attributes).** The dart and swift backends inlined a service method's Rust doc as `///\n/// {first-line}\n` — every continuation line below it was emitted as bare dart/swift source, with rust-style `# Errors` and prose bodies leaking outside the doc-comment scope. The rustler backend emitted the doc as a stray `"""…"""` heredoc inside each `def … do` body, so it was a no-op expression rather than an Elixir `@doc` attribute; it also declared `defmodule Elixir.<Name> do`, which compiles to a module under `Elixir.Elixir.<Name>` because Elixir already prepends `Elixir.` implicitly. Each backend now routes its docs through a per-language helper (`format_dart_comment` and `format_swift_comment` prefix every line with `///` and keep blank-line paragraph breaks as bare `///`; `elixir_heredoc_body` emits an indented heredoc body with blank-line paragraph breaks bare). The rustler backend additionally hoists every docstring out of the `def … do` body and into a preceding `@doc """ … """` attribute and emits `defmodule <Name> do` without the `Elixir.` prefix. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **alef magnus + php service-API: properly comment-prefix multi-line Rust docs.** Both backends used to inline a service method's Rust doc as `<comment-marker> {first-line}\n{rest}` — only the first line carried the language comment marker, so the Rust-doc `# Errors` / `Returns an error …` body lines below it were emitted as bare source. In Ruby that made `sig/service.rb` a `Lint/Syntax` failure (`unexpected token tIVAR` on the next instance-variable assignment); in PHP it would have parsed as ambient code (followed by a separate `Service.php` parse error). Each backend now routes every docstring through a per-language helper (`format_ruby_comment` prefixes every line with `# ` and keeps blank-line paragraph breaks as bare `#`; `format_php_comment` emits a single-line `// text` for one-line docs and a multi-line `/** ... */` docblock with ` * ` prefixes plus bare ` *` separators otherwise). The php backend additionally drops the parse-error-inducing `: void` return type on `__construct(...)` (PHP forbids return types on constructors) and emits the missing trailing `;` on each configurator's `$this->_<name> = $<name>;` instance-property assignment. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **alef pyo3 service-API Python emission: produce ruff/pydocstyle-clean output.** The generated `service.py` previously failed `ruff check` with `F821` on every `Named` metadata/configurator type (`ServerConfig`, `RouteBuilder`, …) because no annotation-target import was emitted; `D207`/`D209` on every Rust-style multi-line docstring (closing `"""` left on the content line, body lines flush-left at column 0 below the opening's column 8 indent); `PIE790` on the dead `pass` after `self._registrations = []` in `__init__`; `TC003` on the unconditional `from collections.abc import Callable`; `UP037` on each `"App"` self-class forward-ref annotation; and `ruff-format` reflowed import groups, the leading-blank-line gap before the first class, and the blank lines around nested `_decorator` functions. The pyo3 backend now (1) walks every `TypeRef` reachable through `ServiceDef`'s constructor / configurators / registration metadata / entrypoint params, gathering the referenced `Named` types into a sorted set, and emits a single `if TYPE_CHECKING:` block importing them (alongside `Callable` when any registration exists) from the `.{native_module}` package — keeping them in annotation scope without paying their runtime import cost; (2) routes every docstring through a `format_docstring(text, indent)` helper that single-lines simple docs and, for multi-line ones, indents every body line to the opening column and closes `"""` on its own line; (3) drops the unconditional trailing `pass` in `__init__` (the `_registrations` assignment is always a real statement); (4) renders self-class return-type annotations bare (`-> App`) because `from __future__ import annotations` is at the top; and (5) emits the PEP8 two-blank-lines gap before the first class plus the blank-line padding around the nested decorator-factory function. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **alef kotlin service-API: emit a coroutine wrapper that delegates to the JVM facade instead of a redeclaring JNI bridge.** When the service owner is `binding_excluded` from the generic Kotlin coroutine-wrapper path (so the host JVM-side facade is the sole owner type), the kotlin service backend used to emit a parallel JNI bridge for the same owner — `external fun nativeAppNew/_Free/_RegisterAppRoute/_Run` plus a `class App` constructed via that bridge — at `dev/spikard/App.kt`. That file's `class App` collided with the Java facade's `dev.spikard.App` (`Redeclaration`), and its `external fun` symbols did not exist (the jni backend exports `Java_…` symbols against the Java facade, not against this parallel kotlin class). The backend now emits, in the kotlin coroutine package, a coroutine-friendly wrapper class `App` that holds an `internal val inner: {java_pkg}.App`, exposes a no-arg secondary constructor (`constructor() : this({java_pkg}.App())`), forwards each registration as `fun {method}(handler: (String) -> String, …meta): Int = inner.register{Class}{Method}(Callable { request -> handler(request) }, …)` (the lambda is SAM-converted to the imported `{java_pkg}.Callable`), wraps each `Run` entrypoint in `suspend fun … = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) { inner.{method}(…) }`, and re-exposes each `Finalize` entrypoint whose return type is host-representable (`Unit` / a primitive / a surface-wrapped opaque) as a plain function; non-representable `Finalize` entrypoints (e.g. one returning `axum::Router`) are skipped. Surface-wrapped opaque metadata/entrypoint parameters take the Kotlin wrapper type and are unwrapped via `.inner` at the delegation site. (`src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **alef kotlin: serialize raw-JSON params to a JSON `String` when delegating to the JVM facade.** Kotlin deliberately maps `TypeRef::Json` to `Any` for caller ergonomics (the wrapper accepts arbitrary Jackson-serializable values), but the underlying Java facade method takes the already-serialized JSON `String` — so the previous direct forward (e.g. `inner.requestSchemaJson(schema)`) failed `compileKotlin` with `Type mismatch: required String!, found Any` on every `*SchemaJson`-style builder. `emit_client_method` now wraps any `TypeRef::Json` argument with `MAPPER.writeValueAsString(name)` at the call site, and `emit_client_type_file` emits `private companion object { private val MAPPER = com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper() }` once per class that has at least one such method, scoping the Jackson dependency to wrappers that actually need it. (`src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef dart frb_rewrite: remove leftover `[ALEF DEBUG]` eprintln instrumentation.** `make_struct_fields_with_defaults_optional` printed `[ALEF DEBUG]` lines to stderr on every Dart generation (struct detection, per-field rewrites, and a final MADE/NO CHANGES summary). The debug prints and the `changes_made` flag that only fed them are removed; generation output is now quiet. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

- **alef kotlin: honor the crate-level `[crates.exclude].types` when emitting coroutine-wrapper classes.** The Kotlin JVM coroutine-wrappers delegate to the sibling Java facade (`dev.<pkg>.<Type>`), so a wrapper for a crate-excluded type references a Java class that was never generated — e.g. `RequestContext` (a Rust-side handler-ergonomics wrapper pulled into the surface only because the service owner lives in the same module) produced a `dev.spikard.kt.RequestContext` wrapping the nonexistent `dev.spikard.RequestContext`, failing `compileKotlin`. `effective_kotlin_exclude_types` now folds in `config.exclude.types` alongside the ffi/java/per-binding excludes, keeping Kotlin in lockstep with the other JVM backends. (`src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef java: raw-JSON params map to `String`, and opaque-handle classes import collection types used in params.** The generated Java package (App service wrapper + opaque `RouteBuilder` etc.) now compiles (`mvn compile` succeeds). Two fixes: (1) the Java type mapping for raw JSON (`TypeRef::Json`) is now `String` rather than `Object` — raw JSON crosses the FFI as a JSON string allocated via `SegmentAllocator.allocateFrom(String)`, so an `Object` parameter produced `no suitable method found for allocateFrom(java.lang.Object)` at every `*SchemaJson` builder method. (2) `gen_opaque_handle_class` now derives its `java.util.{List,Map,Optional}` imports from the actual class body (params AND returns) instead of method return types only, so a builder method taking `List<String>` (e.g. `handlerDependencies`) no longer references an unimported `List`. (`src/backends/java/type_map.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef zig service codegen: emit compilable App/service wrappers via the `@cImport`ed C symbols.** The generated `*_service.zig` now compiles and links against `lib{ffi_lib_name}` (`zig build` succeeds). Previously it emitted hand-written `extern "C" fn … = undefined;` redeclarations that were invalid Zig (extern fns take no `= undefined` initializer), referenced an undefined bare `AppOpaque` type, used `*anyopaque builder` (reversed param syntax), stored the owner as a non-optional `*AppOpaque` then compared/assigned it to `null`, and returned a `[*:0]const u8`/`void` that mismatched the ffi entrypoint ABI. The backend now drops the manual externs entirely and calls the header's `c.<symbol>` functions directly: the owner is an `?*anyopaque` reached through `@ptrCast` at each call (so the cbindgen opaque type name is never named), entrypoints return `c_int` (the i32 status) — or `?*anyopaque` when the return is an opaque this surface wraps — and the static-callback/`builder._handle` arguments are `@ptrCast`/coerced to the C parameter types. (`src/backends/zig/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **alef csharp service codegen + Json type mapping: emit compilable App/service wrappers.** Five fixes let the generated C# `App.cs` plus the regular `RouteBuilder.cs`/`NativeMethods.cs` compile (`dotnet build` → 0 errors). (1) The native handler callback is now a normal static trampoline held in a `static readonly NativeMethods.HandlerCallback` field and passed to the register call, instead of an `[UnmanagedCallersOnly]` method passed where a delegate is expected (CS8902 — UnmanagedCallersOnly methods cannot become delegates). (2) The user handler is held with `GCHandleType.Normal` rather than `Pinned` (delegates are non-blittable and cannot be pinned — `Pinned` throws at runtime). (3) Entrypoint methods and their P/Invoke declarations mirror the C ABI: an `IntPtr` handle when the return is an opaque this surface wraps, otherwise an `int` status — and the method `return`s it, fixing `into_router`'s "not all code paths return a value" (CS0161) and the prior `void`/`IntPtr`/value-type mismatches. (4) The trampoline marshals its response with `Marshal.StringToCoTaskMemUTF8` (the nonexistent `StringToHGlobalUTF8` caused CS0117). (5) `using System.Linq;` is emitted for the `Dispose` cleanup's `.ToList()` (CS1061). Separately, the C# type mapping for raw JSON (`TypeRef::Json`) is now `string` rather than `object`: raw JSON always crosses the FFI as a JSON string (the P/Invoke param/return are `string` and nothing serialized a `Json` value), so passing an `object` produced CS1503 at every `*SchemaJson` call site. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/type_map.rs`)

- **alef go service codegen: emit compilable App/service wrappers.** Three fixes let the generated Go `service.go` plus the regular `binding.go` compile and link end-to-end (verified: `go build`, `go vet`, and a real `App.run()` serve smoke that binds and answers HTTP). (1) The registration call now casts the static cgo trampoline to the function-pointer type the register parameter expects (`(*[0]byte)(C.service_handler_trampoline)`) — cgo types an inline C function-pointer parameter as `*[0]byte`, which the bare trampoline value (an `unsafe.Pointer`) does not satisfy. (2) Entrypoint methods now mirror the C ABI: an entrypoint returns a value only when its return type is an opaque this surface wraps (yielding a `*T` wrapper), otherwise the call is status-only and is reported through `error` (capturing the `i32` status), instead of declaring a single value return whose body returned two values. (3) `json.RawMessage` (`TypeRef::Json`) method parameters now emit the JSON-marshal-to-C-string conversion preamble — previously the call site referenced an undeclared `c`-prefixed variable because `Json` fell through to the no-op wildcard arm. (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/go/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **Host backends (go, csharp, dart, swift, zig, java, kotlin) service-API: honor `binding_excluded` and marshal opaque metadata handles.** The host-marshaling backends (which call the C-ABI/JNI/Panama/swift-bridge layer rather than emitting a Rust `Handler` impl) had two service-API defects. (1) Their regular opaque/type codegen still emitted the service-owner type the service-API path now owns, referencing `_new`/`_free`/method C symbols the ffi backend no longer exports — each backend's type-exclusion set now also chains in the names of `binding_excluded` types, matching the ffi/pyo3 backends. (2) Their service registration passed a surface-wrapped `Named` metadata parameter (e.g. a route builder) by stringifying it instead of forwarding the opaque handle's native pointer — each backend now types such a parameter as its opaque-handle wrapper and forwards the underlying pointer (`unsafe.Pointer(.ptr)` for go, `.Handle` IntPtr for csharp, `ffi.Pointer<ffi.Void>` for dart, `._handle`/`*anyopaque` for zig, the opaque ref for swift, `.handle()` `MemorySegment` for java, `.inner` java-facade ref for kotlin). Each backend also gained the generic `entrypoint_return_representable` check and skips `finalize` entrypoints whose return type cannot cross its boundary. (`src/backends/{go,csharp,dart,swift,zig,java,kotlin}` service/type codegen)

- **ffi service-API: emit compilable Rust glue for the service owner, registration, and entrypoints.** The ffi `service.rs` had never been compiled (its module was orphaned), so its codegen carried several defects that broke once the module was wired in: the opaque owner struct used tuple-struct-plus-brace syntax (`pub struct AppOpaque({ .. })`); registration/entrypoint parameters used C declaration order (`Type name`) and C type names (`void`, `const char*`) inside a Rust `extern "C"` signature; the entrypoint body discarded its result through a stray paren; and the handler bridge moved non-`Send` raw pointers into `spawn_blocking` and freed the response via an undeclared `libc`. The generator now declares the opaque with normal struct syntax; binds each non-callback parameter through a `FfiParamBinding` helper (`String` → `*const c_char` rebound to an owned `String`; a surface-wrapped `Named` type → `*mut {core}::{name}` reconstructed via `Box::from_raw`; primitives by value); consumes the owner box in entrypoints (so a `self`-consuming `run` works), driving async ones on a fresh Tokio runtime and returning an `i32` status; skips `finalize` entrypoints whose return type cannot cross the C ABI; and crosses the callback context/result pointers through `spawn_blocking` as `usize`, freeing the response with an inline `extern \"C\" { fn free }` (no `libc` dependency). (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **ffi service-API: exclude `binding_excluded` types from plain opaque emission.** The ffi binding generator emitted a plain opaque wrapper (`_new`/`_free`/method exports) for every non-trait type, including the service owner the service-API path already emits, producing duplicate `_new`/`_free` C symbols. The opaque-emission exclude set now chains in the names of types flagged `binding_excluded` by the service extraction pass, matching the pyo3/napi/php/magnus/rustler backends. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **ffi service-API: declare the generated `service` module in the crate root.** When a surface has services, the ffi backend emits a `service.rs` (service owner constructor/registration/entrypoint wrappers) but the generated `lib.rs` never declared `pub mod service;`, leaving the module orphaned and uncompiled. The crate root now adds `pub mod service;` whenever `api.services` is non-empty, so the C-ABI service surface is actually built and exported. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **Service-API codegen: exclude `binding_excluded` types and fix service-owner handling in the magnus, rustler, and ffi backends.** These backends did not honor the `binding_excluded` flag (set on service-owner types and handler-contract traits by the service extraction pass), so they emitted the service owner as a regular opaque wrapper — cloning a non-`Clone` owner, wrapping it in `Arc<Mutex<…>>`, calling methods on the lock, and moving a `self`-consuming entrypoint out of a guard. The magnus and rustler type/opaque/re-export loops now chain the `binding_excluded` type names into their exclude sets (matching pyo3/napi/php), and the service run/registration codegen uses an owned owner with `Arc<dyn Contract>` handlers (no generic `<H>`). The ffi backend now skips emitting raw C wrappers for methods that have generic type parameters or return a borrow of the receiver (`&mut Self`/`&Self`) — they cannot be represented over the C ABI and are driven via the service-API path instead. The rustler backend additionally skips free functions with non-bindable (sanitized) signatures and converts `TypeRef::Json` (`serde_json::Value`) opaque-method parameters from their `String` wire form before the call. (`src/backends/{magnus,rustler,ffi}` service/type codegen)

- **Preserve `new`-returns-`Self` constructors for opaque types.** The extractor dropped every static `new` returning `Self`, assuming a field-based constructor would be generated instead — but opaque types (no public fields) have no field constructor, leaving them with no way to be constructed from a binding. `new` is now kept for opaque types (those with a non-generic impl block); field-based types still drop it in favor of the derived field constructor, and generic impls (e.g. `impl<T> Foo<T>`) never preserve it since their constructor cannot be lowered to a concrete binding. (`src/extract/extractor/functions.rs`)

- **pyo3 service run entrypoint: release the GIL during the blocking serve loop.** The generated run entrypoint held the GIL across `block_on(owner.run())`, so any host callback invoked from within the entrypoint (e.g. a request handler) deadlocked trying to re-acquire it. The blocking entrypoint call is now wrapped in `Python::detach`, releasing the GIL for its duration. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

## [0.20.15] - 2026-05-29

### Fixed

- **alef c e2e Makefile: declare `.DEFAULT_GOAL := all` so plain `make` builds run_tests instead of stopping after download_ffi.sh.** Without explicit default goal, Make picked the first explicit target rule encountered — `$(FFI_DIR)/include/ts_pack.h: download_ffi.sh` — causing `make` (no args) to only download FFI and exit instead of building the test binary. CI `E2E / Test C FFI` job failed with `sh: 1: ./run_tests: not found`. Fixed by adding `.DEFAULT_GOAL := all` after variable declarations (CC, FFI_DIR) and before the first file-target rule. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs:449-450`)

- **alef kotlin_android e2e: wrap nullable `System.getProperty` results with `?: ""` before string concatenation.** Generated test bodies for fixtures with mock URLs concatenated `System.getProperty(key, default) + "/fixtures/<id>"`. Under stricter Kotlin null-safety inference, `getProperty(String, String)` returns `String?`, and `String? + String` triggered "Only safe (?.) or non-null asserted (!!.) calls are allowed on a nullable receiver of type 'String?'" compile errors in test_apps/kotlin_android. Fixed by wrapping each `System.getProperty(...)` call in `(... ?: "")` and applying `?: ""` to all nested `System.getenv()` calls. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs:1368,1539,1545`)

- **alef swift e2e: move binaryTarget from dependencies to targets in Package.swift.** Swift Package manifests require `dependencies:` to contain `Package.Dependency` values (`.package(url:from:)`), not `.binaryTarget(...)`. The e2e Package.swift emitter incorrectly placed `.binaryTarget(url:checksum:)` in the dependencies block, causing `swift build/test` to fail with "type 'Target.Dependency' has no member 'binaryTarget'". Fixed by emitting binary targets under `targets:` and using `.target(name:)` in test target dependencies. Registry mode now emits: `targets: [.binaryTarget(...), .testTarget(dependencies: [.target(name:...)])]`. Local mode emits: `targets: [.testTarget(dependencies: [.product(...)])]` with no binary target block. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

- **alef FFI trait-bridge: add `out_error` parameter to callbacks returning complex types.** FFI trait bridge callbacks that return complex types (Named, Vec, Map, String, Json) without explicit error handling were missing `out_error` parameters, while C# P/Invoke delegates expected them for stack alignment. This caused NullReferenceException when C# callbacks executed. Now `c_return_convention()` and `gen_vtable_call_body()` automatically add `out_error` for all complex-return methods, regardless of error type. Generated C# delegates now correctly include both `out IntPtr outResult` and `out IntPtr outError`, and Rust FFI vtable callers pass both parameters. C# tests now pass all 100/100. (`src/backends/ffi/trait_bridge/mod.rs:101`, `src/backends/ffi/trait_bridge/call_body.rs:309`)

- **alef FFI trait-bridge: pass `out_error` from the constructor slice-cache initializer.** The constructor slice-cache init invoked the vtable function pointer with only `out_result`, but complex-return callbacks now take an `out_error` parameter (see above). The mismatched call dropped the trailing argument; the initializer now passes a null-initialized `out_error` so the call matches the ABI. (`src/backends/ffi/templates/constructor_slice_cache_init.jinja`)

- **alef rustler trait-bridge: use `block_on` when spawning outside tokio runtime context.** Trait bridge sync method body generation created a tokio runtime when not in an async context but never ran it, causing deadlock on `rx.blocking_recv()`. Now uses `rt.block_on()` to properly execute the spawn_blocking work and await the channel response. Elixir e2e tests no longer hang during plugin API test loading. (`src/backends/rustler/templates/sync_method_body.rs.jinja`)

- **alef csharp trait-bridge: pin the impl handle and harden callback result/error marshalling.** C# trait-bridge callbacks could crash with NullReferenceException or leak: the impl (plugin instance) handle was allocated with `GCHandle.Normal` rather than `GCHandle.Pinned`, and result/error marshalling had no guard against a failing `Marshal.StringToCoTaskMemUTF8`. The bridge constructor now allocates the impl handle as `GCHandle.Pinned`; the shared result-serialize block wraps marshalling in a try/catch that null-coalesces the value (`?? string.Empty`), zeroes `outResult` and rethrows on failure; and the callback error path defensively builds the message through nested try/catch, leaving `outError` null (so Rust sees return code 1) if marshalling fails for any reason. (`src/backends/csharp/templates/trait_bridge_class.jinja`, `src/backends/csharp/templates/callback_result_serialize.jinja`, `src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef swift bindings: convert `Vec<Named>` parameters and decode JSON-bridged async returns.** Free-function parameters typed `Vec<NamedType>`, where `NamedType` is an opaque swift-bridge class (not a first-class DTO), were passed through unconverted and failed to compile; and async/throwing functions returning JSON-bridged types (`Vec<Vec<_>>`, `Map`) returned the raw bridge string instead of a decoded value. The forwarder now builds a `RustVec<NamedType>` from the Swift array (handling optionals), applies the `.map` conversion suffix for `Vec<Named>` returns, and decodes JSON-bridged returns via `JSONDecoder` after the bridge call. This unblocks batch free functions that take or return arrays of opaque types. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef zig trait-bridge & e2e: wrap infallible complex returns in `out_result` and fix e2e stub defaults/error-var type.** Infallible trait methods returning a complex type (String/Bytes/Named/Vec/Map) were not wrapped at the C FFI boundary, so the vtable signature and thunk mishandled the return. A new `method_needs_out_result` predicate now routes both fallible and infallible-complex methods through an `out_result` pointer returning `i32`; the infallible-complex thunk calls the method directly and writes the returned C-string pointer to `out_result` via `@constCast`, returning 0. The e2e codegen now emits JSON-encoded default stub values (`"[]"`, `"{}"`, `""`) for infallible complex returns and declares the error out-variable as `?[*c]u8` (C pointer) instead of `?[*:0]u8`. (`src/backends/zig/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

## [0.20.14] - 2026-05-29

### Fixed

- **alef ruby scaffold: point gemspec `spec.extensions` to `ext/{name}/native/extconf.rb`.** After v0.20.13 moved extconf.rb to the `native/` subdir, the gemspec's `spec.extensions` array was still listing `ext/{name}/extconf.rb`. RubyGems uses this list to locate the build entrypoint; with the stale path, install-from-source failed to find extconf.rb. (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **alef ruby scaffold: filter out directories from gemspec `spec.files` glob.** The `Dir.glob("**/*")` expression in the generated gemspec included directory entries, which rake-compiler's copy task then tried to `copy_file` (file-only) → `Errno::EISDIR: Is a directory - read`. Added `.select { |f| File.file?(f) }` so only regular files are listed. (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **alef ruby scaffold: emit `ext.lib_dir = "lib"` (relative) instead of absolute path in ExtensionTask.** The previous template used `File.expand_path("lib", GEM_ROOT)`, but rake-compiler combines `lib_dir` with its own staging paths and absolute values caused incorrectly rooted copies. Switched to a relative `"lib"`, which rake-compiler resolves against the gem root. (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

## [0.20.13] - 2026-05-29

### Fixed

- **alef dart build: wire up config-aware BuildConfig so post-processors run during `alef build --lang dart`.** The `Backend::build_config()` trait method had no access to the crate `ResolvedCrateConfig`, so Dart's style-dependent post-processors (FrbDartOptionalFieldsWithDefaults for fields with Rust defaults) were never executed. Added optional trait method `build_config_with_config(&self, config: &ResolvedCrateConfig)` with a default implementation that calls the parameterless `build_config()`, allowing backends to override it with config-aware logic. `alef build` now calls `build_config_with_config()` instead of `build_config()`. DartBackend overrides the new method to delegate to `build_config_for(config)`, which already had the full set of post-processors including `FrbDartOptionalFieldsWithDefaults`. This fixes the kreuzberg Dart e2e: `EmbeddingConfig(...)` constructor now compiles because `required this.model` is correctly rewritten to `this.model` (optional). (`src/core/backend.rs`, `src/cli/pipeline/commands.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef swift e2e: respect per-language async overrides in test codegen.** Swift-bridge cannot natively expose async Rust functions — all Rust async calls are wrapped in `block_on` at the Rust FFI layer and exposed as synchronous functions to Swift. The e2e codegen was ignoring language-specific `async = false` overrides in `alef.toml` and always generating `async throws` test functions when the call's base `async = true`. This caused test compilation errors when a language's override explicitly set `async = false`. Fixed by checking `call_overrides.r#async` first, falling back to `call_config.r#async` only if the override is not set. Swift e2e tests for `extract_bytes` and `extract_file` now correctly generate as synchronous `throws` (not `async throws`). (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs:882-887`)

- **alef ruby scaffold: fix Rakefile task graph regression from Dir.chdir workaround.** The previous fix wrapped `RbSys::ExtensionTask.new()` in `Dir.chdir(native_dir)` to work around a `cargo metadata` lookup failure when the native crate is excluded from the workspace. However, rb_sys declares file prerequisites relative to the CWD at task-definition time, so the file tasks were registered at the native-dir context but then rake looked for them from the gem root, causing "Don't know how to build task 'Cargo.lock'" errors during `bundle exec rake compile`. Fixed by: (1) moving the extconf.rb file from `ext/{name}/extconf.rb` to `ext/{name}/native/extconf.rb` so it's at the cargo manifest directory where rb_sys expects it; (2) declaring Cargo.lock and Cargo.toml file prerequisites inside the `Dir.chdir` block so rake resolves them relative to the native dir where they actually exist. The Dir.chdir is kept for the entire task-definition scope (not exited afterwards), allowing all paths to be resolved correctly when tasks run. (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **alef codegen: remove all hardcoded downstream project-name references across 16 sites.** The PHP, Java, and Go e2e codegen emitted class names (`Kreuzberg::`, `Kreuzberg.`) in teardown scaffolding rather than deriving them from the configured `class_name`. The `emit_test_backend_with_context` (Java) and `emit_test_backend_with_ns` (PHP) functions now accept a `binding_class` parameter (passed as the resolved `class_name` at the call site) and use it for teardown method calls. The Go e2e test used `"kreuzberg"` as import alias; replaced with `"mylib"`. The Kotlin-Android version sync test used `"dev.kreuzberg"` as a fixture groupId; replaced with `"dev.example"`. Ten further occurrences in Rust doc comments across `dart/frb_rewrite.rs`, `swift/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs`, `php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `scaffold/languages/{ffi,node,php}.rs` were reworded to remove product-name examples. `prek run check-project-mentions --all-files` now exits 0. No new fields added to config; no behaviour change for existing consumers. (`src/e2e/codegen/{java,php,go}.rs`, `src/cli/pipeline/version.rs`, `src/backends/{dart/frb_rewrite,php/gen_bindings/mod,swift/gen_rust_crate/cargo}.rs`, `src/scaffold/languages/{ffi,node,php}.rs`)

- **alef rustler/elixir template: pre-wrap `RustlerPrecompiled` `base_url:` URL so downstream `mix format` doesn't have to.** The `native_module_header.jinja` template emitted the `base_url:` field and its URL on a single line, which exceeds `mix format`'s default ~98 char width whenever `repo_url + version` overflows. Every downstream consumer (e.g. tree-sitter-language-pack) had to hand-apply the `mix format` autofix after every regen. Fix pre-wraps the URL onto the next indented line in the template so the generated `native.ex` is already idempotent under `mix format`. (`src/backends/rustler/template_env.rs`)

- **alef C# trait-bridge: pin impl and delegates with GCHandle to prevent callback NRE.** C# trait bridge callbacks were crashing with NullReferenceException during callback execution. Root cause: the impl and delegates array could be garbage collected if the bridge was disposed or evicted from the registry while callbacks were in flight. Solution: create GCHandle.Pinned for the impl (to prevent collection of the plugin instance) and GCHandle.Normal for the _delegates array (to retain the delegate objects). Both are freed in Dispose(). The combination of GCHandles plus the registry refcount mechanism ensures objects remain valid throughout callback execution. C# e2e test suite now passes all 100 tests. (`src/backends/csharp/templates/trait_bridge_class.jinja`)

- **alef dart frb_rewrite: qualify Uri.parse() with _DartCore alias to avoid conflict with Kreuzberg Uri class.** The frb_rewrite external-library loader uses `Uri.parse()` but the Kreuzberg FFI bindings auto-generate a local `Uri` class. When the loader code calls `Uri.parse()`, the Dart type resolver selects the local `Uri` class instead of `dart:core.Uri`, causing type mismatch errors in the generated frb_generated.dart file. Fix imports `dart:core` with the `_DartCore` alias and replaces all `Uri.parse()` calls with `_DartCore.Uri.parse()` to ensure correct resolution. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

## [0.20.12] - 2026-05-29

### Fixed

- **alef R e2e: extract method return values from fixture.input instead of always using defaults.** The e2e stub generator for trait bridges was always emitting default values (0L for numbers) for method return values, which broke tests where methods must return non-zero values (e.g. `dimensions() > 0` for embedding backends). Now reads method values directly from `fixture.input.backend.<method_name>` if present; falls back to language defaults if not. Fixture `register_embedding_backend_trait_bridge.json` now specifies `"dimensions": 768` in the backend input. (`src/e2e/codegen/r.rs`)

- **alef R extendr: handle numeric doubles in primitive method returns.** The `sync_method_complex_return.jinja` template used `Robj::as_integer().unwrap_or(0)` to extract primitive return values from R callbacks, but R numeric literals (e.g. `768`) are **doubles**, not integers. `as_integer()` on a double returns `None`, causing the fallback to 0. This broke backends reporting `dimensions() > 0` (e.g. embedding backends). Fixed by trying `as_integer()` first, then falling back to `as_real()` with appropriate type casting. Both bool and numeric types now correctly handle R's default double type. (`src/backends/extendr/templates/sync_method_complex_return.jinja`)

- **alef R e2e: use fixture-extracted backend name in trait-bridge setup instead of hardcoded "test".** The R e2e codegen for trait-bridge plugin registration was creating backend stubs with hardcoded `name = "test"`, but emitting teardown calls with the actual extracted backend name from the fixture (e.g. `"test-backend"`). This caused the unregister call to fail, leaking the registered backend into subsequent tests and breaking OCR fixtures (e.g. `test_smoke.R:9:3` reports `OCR backend 'tesseract' not registered. Available backends: ["test"]`). Fixed by using the same extracted `backend_name` variable in both setup (Plugin `name` field) and teardown. All R e2e tests now pass (156 PASS). (`src/e2e/codegen/r.rs:1472-1523`)

- **alef PHP e2e: emit static `Kreuzberg::unregister*()` method calls instead of bare function imports.** The PHP e2e codegen for trait-bridge teardown was trying to call standalone `unregister_document_extractor()`, `unregister_ocr_backend()`, etc. functions imported via `use function Kreuzberg\unregister_*;` statements. However, these functions don't exist as standalone PHP functions — they're only available as static methods on the `Kreuzberg` class (`Kreuzberg::unregisterDocumentExtractor()`, etc.). Fixes the PHP e2e test suite by converting the snake_case function names to camelCase method names and emitting direct static method calls instead of bare function calls with use imports. All 100 PHP e2e tests now pass. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/php/test_file.jinja`)

- **alef ruby scaffold: wrap `RbSys::ExtensionTask.new` in `Dir.chdir(ext/<name>/native)` and restore `config.ext_dir = "native"` in extconf.rb.** The v0.20.11 fix changed `ext.ext_dir = "ext/<name>" → "ext/<name>/native"` and removed `config.ext_dir = "native"` from extconf.rb, but `RbSys::ExtensionTask.init()` (rb_sys 0.9.128) **overrides** the user-set `@ext_dir` with `Cargo::Metadata.new_or_inferred(name).manifest_directory`, and that metadata lookup runs `cargo metadata` from `Dir.pwd` (i.e. `packages/<lang>/`). When the binding native crate is in the parent workspace's `exclude` list — the default for kreuzcrawl, html-to-markdown, liter-llm, tree-sitter-language-pack, kreuzberg — the metadata lookup misses the crate and raises `RbSys::PackageNotFoundError: Could not find Cargo package metadata for "<name>-rb"`. Setting `ext.ext_dir` has no effect on this lookup. Real fix: run the `ExtensionTask.new` block inside `Dir.chdir(ext/<name>/native)` so cargo metadata finds the standalone `Cargo.toml`; restore `config.ext_dir = "native"` so the build-time `rb_sys/mkmf` path resolves identically. Absolute `lib_dir` keeps the rake task pointing at the gem root regardless of CWD. (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

## [0.20.11] - 2026-05-29

### Fixed

- **alef ruby scaffold: point `RbSys::ExtensionTask` at `ext/<name>/native` where Cargo.toml actually lives.** The Rakefile template set `ext.ext_dir = "ext/<name>"`, but the crate's `Cargo.toml` is one level deeper at `ext/<name>/native/Cargo.toml`. `RbSys::ExtensionTask.init()` invokes `Cargo::Metadata` lookup against `<ext_dir>/Cargo.toml` at task-construction time (before extconf.rb's `config.ext_dir = "native"` takes effect), so the lookup failed with `RbSys::PackageNotFoundError: Could not find Cargo package metadata for "<name>-rb"`. Fix sets `ext.ext_dir = "ext/<name>/native"` so both ExtensionTask init and the rb_sys/mkmf compile step resolve to the same manifest, and drops the now-redundant `config.ext_dir = "native"` from extconf.rb. Resolves liter-llm rc.46 publish failure across linux-aarch64, linux-x86_64, and macos-arm64 Ruby gem builds. (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **alef go embed_ffi template: import `embed` by name, not blank.** The template emitted `import _ "embed"` (blank import) but referenced `embed.FS` on the `var _ embed.FS` declaration. `goimports` auto-fixed the file in downstream repos, leaving both `"embed"` and `_ "embed"` (the blank import redundant once named is present), which then failed downstream `go-fmt` prek hooks. Fix changes the blank import to a named import, matching the existing use site. (`src/backends/go/templates/embed_ffi.go.jinja`)

- **alef go cmd/download_ffi/main.go template: drop extra blank line between functions.** The template had two blank lines separating `determinePaths()` from `libFilename()`. `gofmt` normalizes to one blank line, so downstream `go-fmt` hooks failed on freshly regenerated output. Fix removes the duplicated blank line. (`src/backends/go/templates/cmd_download_ffi_main.go.jinja`)

- **alef r extendr: emit wrapper functions for unit enums so e2e tests can call constructors.** ProcessingStage and other unit enums (enums with no data in any variant) were not being exported to R because they were generated as Rust enums but never wrapped in R functions or registered with extendr. Unit enums are now emitted as R functions that return a default instance with the proper class attribute (e.g., `ProcessingStage()` returns `list() |> structure(class = "ProcessingStage")`). This allows e2e trait-bridge tests to call enum constructors. (`src/backends/extendr/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef php e2e: add unregister functions to type_imports instead of bare calls.** PHP e2e test files are generated in the `Kreuzberg\E2e` namespace, but unregister functions live in the root `Kreuzberg` namespace. The original fix tried to fully-qualify calls as `\Kreuzberg\unregister_<trait>(...)`, but PHP doesn't allow calls to functions in other namespaces without explicit import. The real fix is to emit `use function Kreuzberg\unregister_<trait>;` at the top of the test file (via the type_imports field), allowing bare function calls. This mirrors PHP's standard pattern for importing functions from other namespaces. Fixes PluginApiTest trait-bridge teardown so test backends are properly unregistered between tests. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef ffi scaffold: remove unused `extra_deps_block` and `core_dep_line_block` locals.** After the FFI Cargo.toml generator was refactored to sort all deps into a single `dep_entries` vec, the two intermediate block-formatting variables were no longer interpolated into the template string but were still computed. Removed both dead assignments. (`src/scaffold/languages/ffi.rs`)

- **alef zig e2e: remove unused `supported_zig_platforms` helper.** The function was part of the old per-platform URL/hash generation path, which was replaced by a single generic tarball in `db268275f`. The function was left behind and only called by a test that was itself testing the dead multi-platform code path. Removed the function and updated the test to use a single `"generic"` map entry matching the current codegen. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef csharp: trait-bridge callbacks return error code instead of throwing when bridge is unregistered.** When Rust dispatches callbacks on bridges that were unregistered (FreeUserData removes registry entry, but callbacks may still be pending), the C# callback now returns error code 1 with an error message instead of throwing InvalidOperationException. This prevents cascade crashes through the registry lookup and makes the system more resilient to race conditions between unregistration and pending callbacks. (`src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`)
- **alef go: make FreeUserData no-op to avoid Go 1.26.3 cleanup-queue panic.** Go 1.26.3 introduced runtime cleanup-queue finalizers that panic when cgo.Handle.Delete() is called in finalizer context on invalid handles. The fix makes FreeUserData() a no-op; handles are only cleaned up in explicit Unregister() calls. Fixes go:e2e panic on exit. (`src/backends/go/trait_bridge.rs`)


- **alef csharp/zig: re-apply `cargo fmt` to long-line `push_str(&format!(...))` calls and zig codegen `format!` URL strings that drifted past rustfmt's width limit.** Resolves CI `cargo fmt --check` failures on v0.20.10. (`src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

## [0.20.10] - 2026-05-29

### Fixed

- **alef zig trait-bridge: emit C-pointer call args + error-union return signatures.** Bytes parameters are now passed directly as the underlying `[*c]const u8` C pointer instead of reconstructed as Zig slices, matching the vtable ABI. Error-unionable methods emit `!T` / `!void` so the vtable thunk can use `if (...) |r| ... else |e| ...` to handle results. (`src/backends/zig/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef kotlin object_wrapper: emit blank line between sealed-class variants for ktfmt parity.** Sealed-class variants (object/data class declarations) in the kotlin object-wrapper renderer were emitted back-to-back without blank lines, but `ktfmt` reflows the output to insert a blank line between consecutive variants. The committed file then drifted from alef's emission, triggering `alef-verify` failures on downstream polyglot repos. The fix inserts a single `\n` between variants (but not before the first or after the last). (`src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`)

- **alef csharp: fix NullReferenceException in trait-bridge callbacks caused by premature bridge disposal.** When Rust called `FreeUserDataCallback` (to notify C# that the bridge is no longer needed), the generated code immediately disposed the bridge by calling `bridge.Dispose()`. However, if this occurred while a reverse P/Invoke callback was still executing (e.g., in the middle of `ProcessingStageFnCallback`), the disposed bridge's `_impl` field became invalid, causing NRE when the callback tried to call `bridge._impl.ProcessingStage()`. The fix removes the `bridge.Dispose()` call from `FreeUserData()`; instead, the method only removes the bridge from the registry. The GC then collects the bridge after all callbacks complete, ensuring the `_impl` field remains valid throughout callback execution. Fixes all 100 C# e2e tests that were crashing after ~11 tests due to registry-based bridge NRE. (`src/backends/csharp/templates/trait_bridge_class.jinja`)

- **alef php + extendr: handle bool return-type in primitive-return path of trait-bridge sync methods.** A prior fix added a `is_primitive_return` branch that converts the Zval/Robj long value via `as {{ return_type }}`. For `usize`/`i32`/etc this works, but Rust forbids `as bool` (E0054), so methods returning `bool` failed to compile both `kreuzberg-php` and `kreuzberg-r`. The fix special-cases `return_type == "bool"` to use `!= 0` instead of `as bool`. (`src/backends/php/templates/sync_method_body.jinja`, `src/backends/extendr/templates/sync_method_complex_return.jinja`)

- **ffi: `Option<Bytes>` struct field accessors now emit the `out_len` companion parameter.** The `needs_len_out` predicate only matched non-optional `Bytes` fields; optional fields like `email_attachment_data` silently omitted `out_len: *mut usize`, making length-safe reads impossible for all C FFI consumers. Predicate now matches any `Bytes` field; the None arm writes 0. Fixes #118.

- **sync-versions: emit pre-formatted scaffold output (pyproject.toml, Package.swift).** `alef sync-versions` auto-regenerates scaffold seed files after bumping the version, but was emitting unformatted output that prek's post-gen formatters then rewrote, causing CI failures. Python: force `dependencies` array to multi-line and reorder to appear before `urls.*` keys, matching pyproject-fmt's canonical output. Swift: `.target()` and `.testTarget()` calls now emit multi-line shape (6-space indent continuation, 4-space base indent) that swift-format produces when wrapping >100-char lines. Fixes tslp v1.9.0-rc.15 CI failure. (`src/scaffold/languages/python.rs`, `src/scaffold/languages/swift.rs`)

- **alef e2e-codegen: emit teardown calls to unregister test backends in PHP/R/Java.** Trait-bridge tests (e.g., `register_ocr_backend`) register a test backend globally via the singleton registry. Without teardown, the test backend leaks into later smoke tests, which fail trying to load the default backend (e.g., `tesseract`) but find only the `test` stub in the registry. Python's codegen already emitted `unregister_<trait>()` calls; PHP, R, and Java were missing the emission. The fix adds teardown_block collection and emission in: (1) PHP `build_args_and_setup()` returns a 3-tuple with teardown_block; (2) R and Java use context structures (`RArgsContext`, `JavaArgsContext`) to pass teardown_block by reference; (3) all three languages emit teardown after assertions and before test-method closing. The teardown function name is converted to language idioms: PHP uses `{name}()`, R uses `{name}()`, Java converts snake_case to camelCase via `to_lower_camel_case()` (e.g., `unregister_ocr_backend` becomes `unregisterOcrBackend`). (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/r.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`, plus template updates)
