- **alef swift: emit .binaryTarget in e2e Package.swift to bypass SPM tag-URL pinning.** SwiftPM's `.package(url:, from:)` resolves to a git tag and fetches the tag's frozen Package.swift file. When that file contains placeholder substitutions (e.g., `__ALEF_SWIFT_VERSION__`), the download URL becomes invalid (e.g., `v__ALEF_SWIFT_VERSION__.../artifactbundle.zip` → 404). The fix changes registry-mode e2e codegen to emit `.binaryTarget(url:, checksum:)` directly in the consumer Package.swift, with the checksum as a placeholder (`__ALEF_SWIFT_CHECKSUM__`). A new pre-test script `download_swift_artifact.sh` (generated in `e2e/swift_e2e/`) downloads the artifact bundle at test time, computes the actual SHA256 checksum via `swift package compute-checksum`, and substitutes it into Package.swift before running `swift test`. This pattern auto-updates checksums with each RC publish and entirely bypasses the tag-URL-pinning problem. Resolves rc.45 Swift e2e failures. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

- **alef php: config.m4 invokes cargo build and installs dylib to modules/<ext>.so.** When PIE (PHP Installer for Extensions) falls back from prepackaged binaries to source compilation, phpize invokes `config.m4` to learn how to build the extension. The generated config.m4 declared `PHP_NEW_EXTENSION(liter_llm, [], ...)` with an empty source list, which told phpize "this extension exists but has no C files to compile." Since ext-php-rs is a Cargo crate that builds `libliter_llm_php.dylib`/`.so`, the build succeeded but phpize found no output at `modules/liter_llm.so` and failed. The fix adds an `AC_CONFIG_COMMANDS([cargo-build], ...)` hook that: (1) invokes `cargo build --release --manifest-path crates/<lib>-php/Cargo.toml`; (2) detects the platform-specific output (`.dylib` on macOS, `.so` on Linux); (3) copies it to `modules/<ext>.so` so phpize can install it correctly. Manifest paths use the cargo crate name (hyphens, e.g., `liter-llm-php`) from `config.name`, not the extension name (underscores). Resolves rc.45 PHP PIE compilation failures. (`src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef rustler: remove `:test` from RustlerPrecompiled force_build clause.** The Elixir RustlerPrecompiled template emitted `force_build: ... or Mix.env() in [:dev, :test]`, which caused consumers to recompile from source during `mix test`, producing NIF binaries with the wrong filename (`lib<name>_nif-<version>-nif-<abi>-<triple>.so` instead of `<name>_nif.so`). The NIF loader then failed to dlopen the precompiled binary, causing test failures. The fix removes `:test` from the clause so consumers default to downloading the precompiled NIF via RustlerPrecompiled; only `:dev` should trigger source compilation (for in-tree alef development). (`src/backends/rustler/template_env.rs`)

- **alef go: add -Wl,-rpath to cgo LDFLAGS for @rpath dylib resolution.** The Go binding's cgo LDFLAGS for darwin and linux did not include `-Wl,-rpath,${SRCDIR}/<lib-dir>`, which is required when the Rust cdylib uses `install_name=@rpath/<dylib>`. Without the rpath directive, Go tests fail at runtime with `dyld: Library not loaded: @rpath/lib<name>_ffi.dylib ... no LC_RPATH's found`. The fix adds the rpath flag to all darwin and linux LDFLAGS lines, ensuring the linker embeds the correct RPATH in the final binary. (`src/backends/go/templates/cgo_preamble_binding.jinja`)

- **alef c-ffi: use deferred eval for FFI_INCLUDE/FFI_LIB_DIR in Makefile.** The C e2e Makefile template used immediate-eval assignment (`:=`) for `FFI_INCLUDE` and `FFI_LIB_DIR` variables. When `test_apps/c/` ran from a clean state before `download_ffi.sh` populated the `ffi/` directory, the `$(wildcard ...)` expressions evaluated as empty at parse time, causing CFLAGS/LDFLAGS to fall through to the pkg-config branch and fail with undefined linker symbols. The fix changes `:=` to `=` (deferred eval) so the wildcard re-runs at each command invocation, after the prerequisite `download_ffi.sh` has executed. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

- **`sync-versions`: include four previously-missed file categories.** `alef sync-versions --set <version>` now updates: (1) `packages/kotlin-android/build.gradle.kts` — the `coordinates(version = "...")` block in the KotlinAndroid Gradle build, which was skipped because only `Language::Kotlin` was covered; (2) `packages/python/**/__init__.py` — globs the nested src-layout module path (e.g. `packages/python/my_lib/__init__.py`) instead of only the flat `packages/python/__init__.py`; (3) `test_apps/*/Package.swift` and `e2e/*/Package.swift` — the `from: "X.Y.Z"` remote version bound in Swift Package.swift manifests, which was referenced in comments but never implemented; (4) `e2e/c/download_ffi.sh` and `test_apps/c/download_ffi.sh` — the `VERSION="X.Y.Z"` (no spaces) bash variable at the top of the generated C FFI download helper. The `replace_version_pattern` dispatch table gains two new arms for `from:` (Swift) and `VERSION="` (bash no-space form). Four regression tests added. (`src/cli/pipeline/version.rs`)

- **alef c-ffi: download_ffi.sh uses release-asset prefix not cargo crate name.** The C e2e generator's `download_ffi.sh` script constructs tarball filenames using `FFI_PKG_NAME`, which was incorrectly defaulting to the cargo FFI crate name (e.g., `ts_pack_core_ffi`) instead of the release-asset package name (e.g., `tree-sitter-language-pack-ffi`). This caused `curl` 404 errors when E2E C tests tried to download prebuilt FFI libraries from GitHub Releases. The fix changes the fallback from `lib_name.clone()` (the cargo crate name) to `format!("{}-ffi", config.name)` (the base package name + "-ffi" suffix), which matches the actual tarball prefix emitted by the `alef publish package --lang ffi` flow. When the C package registry entry includes an explicit `name` field (recommended for multi-registry projects), that takes priority. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

- **alef ruby: pass cargo package name (hyphens) to RbSys::ExtensionTask.** The Ruby scaffold was emitting `RbSys::ExtensionTask.new("{ext_name}", ...)` where `ext_name` uses underscores (e.g., `ts_pack_core_rb`), but the generated `native/Cargo.toml` uses hyphens for the package name (e.g., `ts-pack-core-rb`), per the binding-crate naming convention. `RbSys::ExtensionTask.new(name, ...)` performs a `cargo metadata` lookup using the package name, so the underscore form caused "Could not find Cargo package metadata" errors during gem builds across all platforms. The fix creates a separate `cargo_pkg_name` variable with hyphens and passes that to the task constructor, while `ext_dir` continues to use underscores for the filesystem path (which is correct). (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **alef csharp trait-bridge: use GCHandleType.Pinned to prevent bridge GC during callbacks.** Generated C# trait bridges' `Register()` method pinned the bridge instance with `GCHandle.Alloc(bridge, GCHandleType.Normal)`, but `GCHandleType.Normal` does not prevent garbage collection—it only tracks references. When Rust's native register function invoked callbacks synchronously (e.g., PostProcessor.ProcessingStage during register), the bridge could be garbage-collected before the callback executed, causing `_impl` field access to throw `NullReferenceException` at construction time. The fix changes all bridge registration methods to use `GCHandleType.Pinned`, which actually prevents GC and guarantees the bridge remains reachable during and after the registration call. (`src/backends/csharp/templates/trait_bridge_class.jinja`)

- **alef csharp trait-bridge: safely handle null exception messages in callbacks.** Exception handlers in generated C# trait-bridge callbacks called `Marshal.StringToCoTaskMemUTF8(ex.Message)` directly, but `ex.Message` can be null for some exception types. This caused a NullReferenceException in the catch handler itself. The fix changes the three exception handlers (Initialize, Shutdown, and trait method returns) to use `ex.Message ?? ex.GetType().Name`, falling back to the exception type name if Message is null. This ensures every exception is safely serialized without secondary failures. (`src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **`write_scaffold_files_with_overwrite`: skip the write when on-disk bytes already match the normalized output.** `std::fs::write` is unconditional truncate+write — even when content is identical, it updates mtime (and on macOS, opens with `O_TRUNC` which changes inode state). Pre-commit/prek hooks then report the file as "modified by this hook" and fail the run. This broke the `alef-sync-versions` hook for downstream repos on every commit: sync-versions invokes `regenerate_test_apps_after_sync`, `regenerate_scaffold_after_sync`, and `regenerate_readmes`, all of which write through this helper. Even though `sync_versions` itself short-circuits unchanged files via `replace_version_pattern`, the cascading regen helpers re-emit every scaffold/test_apps/README file unconditionally — so the prek false-positive triggered on every commit in h2m, kreuzberg, kreuzcrawl, tslp, lllm. Adds an `existing == normalized.as_bytes()` guard before the write; `apply_shebang_chmod` still runs (permissions are checked independently of mtime). Reproduced and verified during h2m v3.5.6 cut: prek now passes without modifying any files. (`src/cli/pipeline/generate.rs`)

- **alef csharp trait-bridge register: pin bridge instance during native registration call.** Generated C# trait bridges' `Register()` static method constructed the bridge locally, then created a GCHandle to pin it, but if Rust's native register function triggered a callback synchronously before the GCHandle was created or held by the caller, the bridge could be garbage-collected before the callback executed. This caused a `NullReferenceException` when the callback tried to dereference `_impl`, crashing the entire C# test host before any tests ran (e.g., PostProcessor bridge at line 690 of generated TraitBridges.cs). The fix explicitly declares `GCHandle userDataHandle` before the try block, ensures it's allocated before the native call, and properly frees it on error, guaranteeing the bridge remains reachable during and after the registration call. The FFI layer holds the userData handle and calls FreeUserDataCallback to release it. (`src/backends/csharp/templates/trait_bridge_class.jinja`)

- **alef java trait-bridge register: use {{ name_expr }} template variable instead of hardcoded impl.name().** The `trait_bridge.jinja` template for Java trait bridges hardcoded `impl.name()` and `name` in the register method's `allocateFrom()` call instead of using the `{{ name_expr }}` variable set by the code generator. This caused the Rust FFI to receive an empty C string when `register_takes_name` was true (traits with super-trait like PostProcessor), leading to "Plugin name cannot be empty" validation errors. The fix replaces hardcoded expressions with `{{ name_expr }}` at lines 117 and 123, ensuring the correct name source is used based on trait configuration. (`src/backends/java/templates/trait_bridge.jinja`)

- **alef setup: pass --config.minimumReleaseAge=0 to pnpm install for node and wasm.** The setup phase invokes `pnpm install` when configuring node and wasm language dependencies. When downstream consumers publish release candidates, pnpm's default 24-hour supply-chain policy rejects packages published within the last day, causing setup to timeout. The fix adds `--config.minimumReleaseAge=0` to both pnpm invocations, following the same precedent used in 0.20.5 for test_apps_run_defaults. (`src/core/config/setup_defaults.rs`)

- **alef elixir: emit mix.exs rustler_crates in multi-line pre-formatted shape.** The Elixir scaffold was emitting `rustler_crates` as a single line, which exceeded mix format's default 98-character line limit when there were 4+ target triples. This caused mix format to reflow the line into multi-line form during CI, making the committed mix.exs drift from alef's emission, which triggered "Versions are out of sync" failures in downstream polyglot repos. The fix emits the `rustler_crates` block pre-formatted to match mix format's canonical multi-line shape, preventing format drift. (`src/scaffold/languages/elixir.rs`)

## [0.20.6] - 2026-05-28

### Fixed

- **alef swift trait-bridge: protocol declares native types; adapter marshals to JSON at the FFI boundary.** (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`)
- **alef zig trait-bridge: stub method signatures match the C FFI types.** (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)
- **alef csharp trait-bridge: exclude enum types from the visible-types set so they JSON-serialize correctly.** (`src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`)
- **alef dart: emit the FRB constructor with the opening brace on the next line to match FRB's expected format.** (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)
- **alef go: collapse the blank line between `//export` and its `func` so cgo recognizes the directive.** (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef java e2e: emit a fixture `name()` for traits without a super-trait.** (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)
- **alef php trait-bridge: drop the arity-blind `try_call_method` validation in the register fn.** (`src/backends/php/trait_bridge.rs`)

## [0.20.7] - 2026-05-28

### Fixed

- **alef swift trait-bridge: use JSON for excluded return types and fix the generated syntax.** (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`)

No user-facing changes; release bump only.

## [0.20.9] - 2026-05-29

### Fixed

- **alef swift: emit `Package.swift` `target`/`testTarget` blocks in multi-line shape** so `swift format` does not reflow the committed manifest. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings`)
- **alef php, ruby: drop the unused `unsafe` block in visitor-bridge `new()`; ruby scaffold uses `RbSys::ExtensionTask`.** (`src/backends/php`, `src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

- Add the missing `NodeConfig.exclude_platforms` field in the napi gen test; `clippy --fix` on `frb_rewrite`.

## [0.20.5] - 2026-05-28

### Fixed

- **alef elixir publish: revert the darwin NIF extension to `.so`** (Windows keeps `.dll`). `rustler_precompiled 0.9.0` hardcodes `.so` for every non-Windows target and ignores caller overrides, so the `.dylib.tar.gz` uploads from 0.20.2 caused Hex publish and downstream `mix deps.get` failures on darwin. (`src/publish/package/elixir.rs`)

- **alef java trait-bridge interface: honour `ffi_skip_methods` so generated `I{Trait}` interfaces stay in sync with their bridges.** Methods listed in a trait-bridge's `ffi_skip_methods` (e.g., `as_sync_extractor` on `DocumentExtractor`, which returns `Option<&dyn SyncExtractor>` — a trait-object reference with no FFI representation) were correctly skipped in the upcall/vtable bridge but still emitted into the public `I{Trait}.java` interface. E2e test stubs that `implements I{Trait}` were then required to override a method the bridge cannot dispatch, producing `is not abstract and does not override abstract method` compile errors. The fix threads `ffi_skip_methods` into `gen_interface_file` and filters the rendered method list, keeping the interface aligned with the bridge surface and unblocking Java e2e test compilation. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

### Chore

- **alef clippy: silence `too_many_arguments` for two java trait-bridge functions.** `gen_trait_bridge_files` and `gen_bridge_file` carry 9 cohesive args that describe one bridge generation invocation; bundling them into a struct would obscure the call-site contract without improving the code. `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]` on these two functions keeps `cargo clippy -D warnings` clean. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`)

## [0.20.4] - 2026-05-28

### Fixed

- **alef php trait-bridge async: avoid "Cannot start a runtime from within a runtime" panic by preferring the current tokio handle when one exists.** Generated PHP trait-bridge async method bodies unconditionally called `WORKER_RUNTIME.block_on(...)`. When the host (e.g., kreuzberg's HTTP server or a PHP test harness with an active tokio context) had already entered a multi-threaded runtime, the inner `block_on` panicked with `Cannot start a runtime from within a runtime` — a non-unwinding panic that aborts the PHP test host (Abort trap 6 around PHP e2e test 63). The fix wraps the async body with `Handle::try_current()`: if a runtime is already active, use `tokio::task::block_in_place(|| handle.block_on(...))` to reuse the outer runtime; otherwise fall back to the previous `WORKER_RUNTIME.block_on(...)` path. This keeps single-threaded PHP-extension contexts working while making the bridge safe to call from within an outer tokio runtime. (`src/backends/php/templates/async_method_body.jinja`)

### Added

- `sync-versions` now triggers e2e codegen so test_apps version pins stay in sync. Use `--no-regen` to opt out. After updating `[crates.e2e.registry.packages.*].version` fields in `alef.toml`, `alef sync-versions` automatically regenerates `test_apps/` scaffold files (pyproject.toml, mix.exs, build.zig.zon, Package.swift, etc.) so the version pins embedded in those generated files reflect the new workspace version atomically — no separate `task alef:generate` step required. This prevents the class of bug seen in the tslp rc.13 release, where 4 of 15 test_apps (python, elixir, zig, swift) still pinned the prior rc.12 version because sync-versions updated alef.toml but the generated files were never regenerated. (`src/cli/pipeline/version.rs`, `src/main.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef dart e2e codegen: import plugin trait types and skip optional methods in factory wrappers.** The Dart e2e test generator was creating test stubs that extended plugin traits (e.g., `DocumentExtractor`, `OcrBackend`) without importing those types from the main package, causing compilation errors like "Type 'DocumentExtractor' not found." Additionally, the factory wrapper `create<Trait>DartImpl()` was emitting callbacks for all methods including those with default implementations, but the stub class only defined required methods, causing method-not-found errors at runtime. The fix: (1) collect trait types used in `test_backend` arguments and emit explicit imports for each type from the package's main API surface; (2) filter factory wrapper callbacks to only include required methods (skip methods with `has_default_impl = true`), matching the class definition. Fixes Dart e2e plugin_api_test compilation failures. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef dart FFI loader: add explicit dart:core import for Uri.parse().** The generated published-loader helper `_alefResolveExternalLibrary()` uses `Uri.parse()` to construct package URIs, but `ensure_loader_imports()` was only adding `dart:io` and `dart:isolate` imports, leaving `Uri` unresolved in the analyzer's view. While `dart:core` is auto-imported in Dart, making the import explicit prevents analyzer warnings on stricter configurations and ensures robustness across Dart versions. The fix adds `dart:core` to the list of guaranteed imports alongside `dart:io` and `dart:isolate`. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef kotlin_android e2e: always emit `useJUnitPlatform()` for JUnit 5 test discovery in both local and registry modes.** Registry-mode test_apps (used by standalone `test_apps/` directories in polyglot repos) require JUnit Platform configuration to enable test discovery, but the gradle configuration was only emitted in local mode. The previous codegen incorrectly assumed "In registry mode no host-JVM tests run," leaving `tasks.withType<Test>` block empty, preventing Gradle from discovering and running tests. This caused `testDebugUnitTest` to fail with "no tests discovered" even though test sources were present. The fix ensures the `tasks.withType<Test> { useJUnitPlatform() }` block is always emitted regardless of dependency mode; local mode additionally configures `java.library.path` and working directory for native library loading. Fixes rc.13 Kotlin Android test_app failures. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin_android.rs`)

- **alef php binding codegen: generate config.m4 for PIE source compilation fallback.** When PIE (PHP Installer for Extensions) cannot download pre-packaged binaries from a Packagist package, it falls back to source compilation by extracting the repository tarball and running `phpize`. This requires `config.m4` at the repository root to configure the build. The PHP backend now generates a minimal `config.m4` for Rust-based PHP extensions built via ext-php-rs, enabling phpize to recognize the extension during the fallback source build. This fixes test_app installation failures when pre-packaged binaries are temporarily unavailable or when testing from custom Packagist packages. Fixes rc.13 PHP test_app PIE install errors. (`src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef dart published-loader: resolve natives via host RID (Runtime ID) for pub.dev packages.** When pub.dev publishes Dart FFI packages, native libraries are staged under `lib/src/native/<rid>/` (e.g., `macos-arm64`, `linux-x64`) per the publish.yaml assemble process. The loader previously checked only `lib/src/{module}_bridge_generated/` (the FRB-dev location), which is empty in published packages, causing standalone `dart test` runs to fail with "relative path not allowed in hardened program" on macOS Sonoma+. The fix adds RID detection via `Abi.current()` and platform-specific library name resolution (`_alefHostRid()` and `_alefHostLibNames()` helpers), searching published RID paths first, then falling back to the FRB-dev location for local/Flutter builds. This allows the loader to find prebuilt binaries in both pub.dev tarballs and local development builds. Fixes test_app failures in rc.13 and later. (`src/backends/dart/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs`)

- **alef wasm/node test-apps: pass minimumReleaseAge=0 to pnpm test command.** pnpm 11.3+ runs its own supply-chain policy check during `pnpm test` invocation, separate from the `pnpm install` check. The `--config.minimumReleaseAge=0` flag passed to install does not affect the test policy check, causing recently-published packages (e.g., `1.9.0-rc.13` published 2026-05-28) to fail verification when the policy cutoff window is < 1 day old. The fix passes `--config.minimumReleaseAge=0` to both `pnpm install` and `pnpm test` commands, allowing freshly-published RC versions to pass pnpm's supply-chain policy check during test invocation. Fixes `alef test-apps run` failure for wasm and node targets. (`src/core/config/test_apps_run_defaults.rs`)

- **alef swift trait-bridge: fix try/await order and conditionally JSON-encode results.** The generated adapter methods used incorrect Swift syntax `await try` instead of the required `try await` order when calling async throwing methods. Additionally, all results were being JSON-encoded, even for methods without error types, causing compilation errors for non-error methods that return the original type directly (e.g., `supportsLanguageCall() -> Bool`). The fix: (1) error-returning async methods use `try await` (correct Swift syntax); (2) only error-returning methods JSON-encode their results via `JSONEncoder().encode(result)` before marshalling to `marshal_ok_result()`, ensuring type conformance at the FFI boundary; (3) non-error methods return results unchanged (primitives and structs pass through directly). This completes the Swift trait-bridge fixes and allows all Swift e2e tests to compile and run. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **Go test_app no longer uses vendor mode; go generate runs on the live module so download_ffi can fetch FFI tarballs.** The previous test command sequence used `go mod vendor` to copy the module into a writable `./vendor/` directory, then invoked `go generate ./vendor/<module-path>/...` to populate `.lib/` with native FFI artifacts. However, Go forbids `go generate` in dependency modules (with error "not generating in packages in dependency modules"), making the `./vendor/...` invocation a no-op. This left `.lib/` empty, causing linker failures when trying to link against native dylibs. The fix switches to non-vendor mode: `go mod tidy` downloads the module into the Go module cache, then `go generate <module>` runs on the live (non-vendored) module to invoke the `//go:generate go run ./cmd/download_ffi` directive, which correctly fetches FFI tarballs into `.lib/<platform>/`. Finally, `go test ./...` links against the live module's FFI artifacts without `-mod=vendor`. (`src/core/config/test_apps_run_defaults.rs`)

## [0.20.3] - 2026-05-28

### Fixed

- **alef shebang-chmod parity for scaffold writer.** `write_scaffold_files_with_overwrite()` did not apply the +x bit to shebang-starting files, while `write_files()` did. Affected every alef-generated `*.sh` under `e2e/` and `test_apps/` (`download_ffi.sh`, `run_tests.sh`, etc.), all landing as `-rw-r--r--`. Fix: shared `apply_shebang_chmod()` helper called from both writers, with `#[cfg(unix)]` guard so Windows builds stay clean. (`src/cli/pipeline/generate.rs`)

- **alef go binding codegen: remove duplicate `//export` directives from binding.go.** The trait-bridge //export directives and implementations were generated in both `binding.go` and `trait_bridges.go`, causing duplicate symbol exports to the C linker. When Go code linked against the C FFI library, the linker would report "Undefined symbols for architecture arm64" for all 54 trait-bridge functions (e.g., `goDocumentExtractorCanHandle`, `goOcrBackendProcessImage`) because the same symbol was exported twice. The fix removes the `plugin_bridge_exports.jinja` template rendering from `binding.go` generation in `gen_go_file()`, leaving the canonical home of the trait-bridge exports in `trait_bridges.go` (which is generated separately when `has_plugin_bridges` is true). This ensures one source of truth for exported symbols and allows Go e2e tests to link cleanly. (`src/backends/go/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef swift trait-bridge: make adapter methods async/throws, fix try/await order, encode all results as JSON.** The trait-bridge protocol correctly declared methods with `async throws` where needed, but the generated adapter methods omitted the `async` and `throws` keywords in their signatures, causing Swift compiler errors when calling async/throwing protocol methods without matching signatures. Additionally, async throwing methods used `await try` instead of Swift's required `try await` order, and methods returning non-primitive types (structs, enums, collections) were incorrectly passed raw to `marshal_ok_result<T: Encodable>`, which failed when those types don't conform to `Encodable` (e.g., `ExtractionResult`). The fix: (1) adapter methods now emit `async` and/or `throws` keywords matching their protocol method signatures; (2) for error-returning methods, the variable binding uses `try await` (correct Swift syntax) instead of `await try`; (3) for `Void`-returning methods, passes `Empty()` (a new `Codable` struct) to `marshal_ok_result()` instead of trying to encode the void result; (4) all named types (structs, enums) and collections are JSON-encoded via `JSONEncoder().encode(result)` before marshalling, ensuring conformance to `Encodable` at the FFI boundary, while primitives and strings pass through directly. Fixes Swift e2e compilation with `ExtractionResult` and other struct return types in trait-bridge methods. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef csharp trait-bridge: fix callback method naming, bool-to-int conversion, userData parameter, and usize/isize type handling.** The trait-bridge vtable generation had four issues: (1) callback method names were generated in camelCase but referenced as PascalCase, causing `CS0103: name does not exist` errors; (2) bool-returning methods used invalid `(int)result` cast (no implicit bool→int in C#); (3) callback method signatures omitted the mandatory `IntPtr userData` first parameter that delegates expected, causing `CS0123: No overload matches` errors; (4) `usize` and `isize` Rust types were incorrectly mapped to `int` instead of `ulong` and `long`, causing implicit type conversion errors when returning primitive values. The fix ensures callback names are PascalCase via `to_csharp_name()`, bool returns use ternary `result ? 1 : 0`, primitive returns use variable `methodResult` to avoid shadowing, all callback signatures include `IntPtr userData`, and `usize`/`isize` are mapped to `ulong`/`long` consistently in both delegate and callback type declarations. (`src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef php trait-bridge: keep the registered ZendObject alive via `PhpRc::inc_count()`/`dec_count()`.** Registration stored a raw `&mut ZendObject` pointer without adjusting the refcount, so PHP could GC the object and later method calls read freed memory (segfault around PHP e2e test 63). The bridge now calls `inc_count()` in its constructor and on `Clone`, `dec_count()` on `Drop`, and imports `ext_php_rs::rc::PhpRc` so those calls compile; applies to visitor- and registration-style bridges. (`src/backends/php/templates/visitor_bridge_struct.jinja`, `src/backends/php/templates/bridge_constructor.jinja`, `src/backends/php/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef go trait-bridge: wrap `cgo.Handle.Delete()` in defer/recover to handle stale or double-freed handles.** When trait-bridge plugins are registered (e.g., `RegisterOcrBackend`), a `cgo.Handle` is created and stored in the C registry. Later, when Rust drops the plugin (during cleanup), it calls the `FreeUserData` trampoline (e.g., `goOcrBackendFreeUserData`) to delete the handle. However, if the handle has already been deleted (e.g., due to a double-free, stale pointer, or cleanup race), `cgo.Handle.Delete()` panics with "misuse of an invalid Handle". This panic occurs in the cleanup goroutine, causing test failures with `runtime.goexit({})` in Go 1.24+. The fix wraps the `Delete()` call in `defer/recover` with a nil-check, silently catching and ignoring panics from invalid handle deletions. This prevents crashes and allows cleanup to proceed even if the handle is stale or already freed. (`src/backends/go/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef zig e2e codegen: remove error-union return types from trait-bridge test-backend stub methods.** Trait-bridge test stubs are simple Zig structs whose methods invoke the `make_*_vtable` helper, which creates thunks that call the stub methods and handle error conversion at the C FFI boundary. The previous codegen incorrectly emitted error-union types (`!T`) on stub method signatures (e.g. `pub fn extract_bytes(...) ![*c]const u8`), which is invalid Zig syntax for struct methods — error handling must happen in the vtable thunks, not the stub methods themselves. The fix removes the error-union wrapping from stub return signatures, emitting bare types (`[*c]const u8`, `i32`, `void`, etc.) instead. This allows test stubs to compile and link correctly with the generated vtable helpers. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

## [0.20.2] - 2026-05-28

### Fixed

- **alef go e2e codegen: exclude SyncExtractor trait type in trait-bridge test-backend stubs.** The `DocumentExtractor` trait includes a method `as_sync_extractor() -> Option<&dyn SyncExtractor>` which returns a trait reference. `SyncExtractor` is marked with `alef(skip)` because it's not exposed in Go bindings. However, the trait-bridge method reference wasn't being substituted with `json.RawMessage` (the JSON serialization format for excluded types in Go), causing the generated stub to reference the undefined type `kreuzberg.SyncExtractor`, failing at compilation with `undefined: kreuzberg.SyncExtractor`. The fix adds `SyncExtractor` to the hardcoded `excluded_named` set alongside `InternalDocument`, ensuring it's substituted with `json.RawMessage` in both the method signature and stub zero-value generation. Fixes Go e2e test compilation after alef v0.20.1. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef java e2e codegen: emit ALL trait methods in test-backend stubs, including those with default implementations.** Java interfaces require all abstract methods to be implemented explicitly, even if the Rust trait provides default implementations. The previous filter excluded `has_default_impl` methods from stub emission, causing generated test stubs to fail at compilation with missing method implementations (e.g., `version()`, `initialize()`, `shutdown()`). The fix removes the `has_default_impl` filter from method iteration in `emit_test_backend`, keeping only the exclusion of super-trait methods that are already emitted separately. This mirrors the corresponding Go fix (commit `d4cb5fb36`) and ensures full interface compliance. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)


- **alef go e2e codegen: emit struct-zero `Type{}` instead of `nil` for Named struct returns in trait-bridge stubs.** `go_stub_default()` delegated to `go_zero_value()` which returned `"nil"` for every `TypeRef::Named`. In binding signatures Named types are emitted as pointers (`*T`), so `nil` is valid — but in **stub** signatures (which use `stub_go_type_with_context`), Named types are emitted as **value types** (`alias.GoName{}` or `GoName{}`), where `nil` is a type error: `cannot use nil as kreuzberg.ExtractionResult value in return statement`. Added `go_stub_default_with_context()` that mirrors `stub_go_type_with_context`'s substitution logic: excluded → `nil` (json.RawMessage), enums → `""`, qualified Named → `alias.GoName{}`, bare Named → `GoName{}`. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef C# trait-bridge codegen: fix trailing comma in callback method signatures with no parameters.** Callback methods with no parameters (only `IntPtr userData`) and complex returns were emitting `IntPtr userData, , out IntPtr outResult)` due to `params_decl` being unconditionally included with trailing `, `. The templates `callback_header_options.jinja` and `callback_header_full.jinja` now conditionally emit the `,` separator only when `params_decl` is non-empty, fixing C# compile errors `CS1001: Identifier expected` and `CS1031: Type expected`. (`src/backends/csharp/templates/callback_header_options.jinja`, `src/backends/csharp/templates/callback_header_full.jinja`)

- **alef python e2e codegen: import `unregister_<trait>` function used by trait-bridge teardown.** When `TraitBridgeConfig.unregister_fn` is set, the python emitter appends `unregister_<trait>("<backend_name>")` as a teardown line after the registration call (commit `857518c6a`). The teardown function was emitted as a bare name reference but was missing from the file's `from kreuzberg import …` line, causing `NameError: name 'unregister_ocr_backend' is not defined` at runtime for the OCR backend trait-bridge fixture. `build_thirdparty_imports` now walks each fixture's call args, looks up its trait_bridge config by `trait_name`, and adds the `unregister_fn` to the import list. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/test_file.rs`)

- **alef dart e2e codegen: escape plugin_name in trait-bridge test-backend stubs to prevent malformed Dart code with unescaped quotes.** The `emit_test_backend` function embedded fixture `plugin_name` values directly into Dart string literals without escaping special characters. When fixture names contained single quotes, backslashes, or newlines (used to drive FRB codegen testing), the generated Dart code became malformed (e.g. `"  String get name => 'name with \\'quote';"` — unclosed string). The malformed Dart then prompted FRB's codegen to produce invalid Rust with non-UTF-8 stray tokens, causing compilation failures. The fix applies `escape_dart()` to the plugin_name in both emission locations: the `name` getter (line 2905) and the factory `pluginName` parameter (line 2958), mirroring existing escaping in other e2e codegen paths. This ensures the generated Dart code is syntactically valid regardless of the fixture name content. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef C# trait-bridge codegen: return primitive types directly instead of via JSON-serialized `out` parameters.** Trait methods returning primitives (int, bool, ulong, etc.) are marshalled by the Rust FFI layer to return the value directly from the callback (e.g. `unsafe extern "C" fn(user_data: *const c_void) -> i32`), not to serialize the result as a JSON string through an `out IntPtr` parameter. The previous codegen treated all non-Unit returns uniformly, generating `private delegate int PriorityFn(IntPtr userData, out IntPtr outResult, out IntPtr outError)` when it should have generated `private delegate int PriorityFn(IntPtr userData)` for methods like `priority()` returning `int`. This mismatch caused xunit test runner crashes: `System.AccessViolationException: Attempted to read or write protected memory` when the runtime tried to invoke the callback with the wrong signature. The fix: (1) add `is_primitive_return` and `delegate_return_type` to `template_methods` and detect primitive returns in both the jinja template and callback generation; (2) emit delegate declarations with the direct return type (no `out` params) for primitive returns; (3) emit callback bodies that call the method and return the result directly (with cast to int for bool). (`src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/templates/trait_bridge_class.jinja`)

- **alef Swift trait-bridge emitter: add argument labels to bridge method calls and fix return type for error-returning methods.** Generated Swift adapter methods were missing argument labels in calls to protocol methods (e.g., `self.bridge.supportsLanguage(lang)` instead of `self.bridge.supportsLanguage(lang: lang)`), violating Swift syntax which requires explicit labels for all method arguments. Additionally, when a trait method had an `error_type`, the adapter method was incorrectly declared to return the original type (e.g., `ExtractionResult`) but actually returned a JSON envelope string (`marshal_ok_result` returns `String`). The fix: (1) build `call_args` as `"name: value"` pairs for each parameter, and (2) when `method.error_type.is_some()`, declare the adapter's return type as `String` (not the original `TypeRef`). (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef go e2e codegen: emit ALL trait methods in test-backend stubs, including those with default implementations.** Go interfaces require all methods to be implemented explicitly, even if the Rust trait provides default implementations. The previous filter excluded `has_default_impl` methods, causing generated test stubs to fail at compilation with undefined methods (e.g., `EstimatedDurationMs`, `ShouldProcess`). The fix removes the `has_default_impl` filter from method iteration in `emit_test_backend_with_context`, keeping only the exclusion of super-trait methods. Additionally, enum types like `ProcessingStage` now correctly return their string zero-value (`""`) instead of `nil`, and the hardcoded excluded type `InternalDocument` is now automatically substituted with `json.RawMessage` in stub method signatures. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef zig e2e codegen: emit named dependency in generated `build.zig.zon` Local mode.** The Local-dep-mode branch emitted `.dependencies = .{{ .path = "..." }}` (unnamed inner braces), which Zig 0.16+ rejects with `expected ';' after statement`. Local mode now emits a named dependency `.dependencies = .{ .{pkg_name} = .{ .path = "..." } }` matching the Registry-mode pattern. Also collapsed the dep_mode-branched outer-wrap into a single shared format, eliminating an inconsistency between Registry and Local code paths. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef pyo3 codegen: skip `binding_excluded` types in struct/opaque and type-stub emission.** Service owner types (and handler-contract traits) are marked `binding_excluded` by the service extraction pass because they are emitted by `generate_service_api`, not the generic codegen. The pyo3 generic `#[pyclass]` struct/opaque loop and the `.pyi` type-stub generator filtered only `!is_trait`, so they still emitted a conflicting/broken binding for the service owner — e.g. `App` with a leaked generic callback param (`fn route(&self, builder: RouteBuilder, handler: H)`, `let handler_core: spikard::H = ...`) and `todo!()` method bodies. The struct loop now folds `binding_excluded` names into its exclude set, and the stub partition filters `!binding_excluded`. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_stubs.rs`)

- **alef php e2e codegen: emit `: mixed` (not `: void`) for Unit-returning Rust methods in stubs.** The PHP interface generator emits `: mixed` uniformly for every method (e.g. `PostProcessor::process(mixed $result, mixed $config): mixed;`), but the stub generator was special-casing `TypeRef::Unit` to emit `: void`. PHP rejects this with `Declaration of @anonymous::process(...): void must be compatible with PostProcessor::process(...): mixed`. Stubs for Unit-returning methods now emit `: mixed { return null; }` to satisfy the LSP signature contract. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef php e2e codegen: emit snake_case method names in stubs (re-revert).** Method names emitted via `to_lower_camel_case()` (commit `5f3aa4329`) did not match the snake_case method declarations in the generated PHP interface (e.g., `extract_bytes` in `packages/php/src/DocumentExtractor.php`), causing PHP fatal `Class @anonymous contains N abstract methods` at autoload. The interface does NOT opt into ext-php-rs `#[php(name = "...")]` camelCase renaming, so stubs must mirror the snake_case names verbatim. Reverted to `method.name.clone()`. Updated five unit-test assertions accordingly (`do_thing`, `extract_bytes`, `extract_file`, `supported_mime_types`, `process_image`, `as_sync_extractor`). (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef go trait_bridge: drop unused `encoding/base64` import from generated `packages/go/v5/trait_bridges.go`.** Go errors on unused imports; the import was emitted unconditionally but no current codepath calls `base64.*`. Removing the unconditional import unblocks `go test` (`./trait_bridges.go:72:2: "encoding/base64" imported and not used`). (`src/backends/go/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef service extraction: recover the configured service constructor (and any skipped configured method), not just generic registrations.** The service pass re-parsed sources to recover registration methods dropped by the generic-over-callback-bound skip, but a constructor named `new` returning `Self` is dropped by a _different_ generic-extraction heuristic (treated as a field-constructed default) and was never recovered. Result: for a real service whose constructor is `new` (e.g. `App::new() -> Self`), `build_service_def` failed with "constructor method `new` not found", so **no `ServiceDef` was built** (the owner was never marked `binding_excluded` and no service API was generated) even though the handler contracts extracted fine. The recovery now collects every method the service config references — constructor, configurators, registrations, and entrypoints — and re-parses any missing from source. (`src/extract/extractor/service.rs`)

- **alef `all` command now generates the service API.** The `all` command codepath generated bindings, stubs, and public-API wrappers but never invoked `generate_service_api`, so configured `[[crates.services]]` produced no output under `alef all` (the per-language `generate` codepath did call it). Consumers running `alef all` (the canonical regeneration entrypoint) silently got no app/service files. The fix mirrors the service-API generation+write block — guarded by `!api.services.is_empty()`, writing returned files and recording changed languages — into the `all` path between type-stub and public-API generation, matching the documented pipeline order (bindings → service_api → public_api). (`src/main.rs`)

- **alef C# trait-bridge codegen: avoid variable name collision between method parameters and return values.** When a trait method had a parameter named `result`, the generated callback code declared both a parameter `IntPtr result` and a local variable `var result = _impl.Method(...)`, causing compilation errors `CS0841` (used before declared) and `CS0136` (cannot be declared in this scope). The fix renames the local variable to `methodResult` in callbacks whenever return values need to be captured. Affected methods: `ShouldProcessFnCallback`, `EstimatedDurationMsFnCallback`, and `ShouldValidateFnCallback`. (`src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/templates/callback_result_call.jinja`)

- **alef ruby/php e2e codegen: emit `1` for numeric return types in trait-bridge test-backend stubs instead of `0`, to satisfy validation constraints.** Generated e2e test backends for traits like `EmbeddingBackend` emitted `def dimensions = 0` (Ruby) or `return 0` (PHP) for integer-returning methods, violating the Rust validation constraint `dimensions() > 0`. Python and TypeScript codegen already returned `1` for all integer types in stubs. The fix aligns Ruby and PHP with the Python/TypeScript approach: match `TypeRef::Primitive(PrimitiveType::Bool)` separately to `"false"`, then `TypeRef::Primitive(_)` to `"1"` (covering I32, I64, U32, U64, F32, F64, etc.), keeping only the special case for Bool. Ruby test `test_embedding_backend_dimensions_in_methods` added to prevent future regression when `dimensions()` is in the methods list. (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef php e2e codegen: emit ALL trait methods in test-backend stubs, including those with default implementations in Rust.** PHP interfaces require all abstract methods to be implemented explicitly, even if the Rust trait provides default implementations. The previous filter excluded `has_default_impl` methods, causing generated anonymous-class test backends to fail at autoload with `Fatal error: Class contains N abstract methods and must therefore be declared abstract or implement the remaining methods`. The fix removes the `has_default_impl` filter from the method iteration in `emit_test_backend_with_ns`, keeping only the exclusion of `name()` when it's already hardcoded by `super_trait`. All six DocumentExtractor interface methods (including `as_sync_extractor` with default impl) are now emitted in the test stub. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **core/jni: `jni_package` now falls back to a derived `com.example.{cleaned_name}` when no kotlin package is configured and no scaffold repository URL is set.** Previously `config.kotlin_package()` was called as the final fallback — that helper requires a repository URL, panicking when one wasn't configured. The new chain prefers `[crates.kotlin_android] package`, then `[crates.kotlin] package`, then `try_kotlin_package()` (returns `Result`, no panic), and finally derives a syntactically-valid Java package by stripping `-`/`_` from the crate name and prefixing `com.example.`. Generated JNI symbols are now always valid Java identifiers regardless of scaffold completeness. (`src/core/jni.rs`)

- **e2e/codegen/php: emit PHP test-backend method names in camelCase to match ext-php-rs convention.** `emit_test_backend_with_ns` previously used the raw Rust method name (e.g. `extract_bytes`) as the PHP method name in generated stubs. ext-php-rs auto-converts snake_case Rust function names to camelCase PHP method names when exposing them, so the generated PHP stub class declaring `public function extract_bytes(...)` doesn't actually implement the interface method that PHP sees as `extractBytes()`. Updated emission to apply `to_lower_camel_case()` and updated two snapshot tests to the corrected expectation. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

### Added

- **scaffold/dart: include `native_assets_cli` in FFI-style pubspec dependencies (Dart 3.0+).** The native-assets build hook resolves the FFI shared library at consumer build time, removing the need for downstream consumers to manually wire `Process.run('cargo build')` or copy dylibs into asset folders. Pin tracked via the new `pub_dev::NATIVE_ASSETS_CLI = "^0.13.0"` constant in `template_versions.rs` (renovate-managed). (`src/core/template_versions.rs`, `src/scaffold/languages/dart.rs`)

### Fixed

- **zig codegen: normalize platform suffixes to project-wide canonical names (`macos-x86_64`, `windows-x86_64`) so generated `build.zig.zon` URLs resolve to actual published assets.** Previously emitted `macos-amd64` and `windows-x64`, causing `.hash = "TODO"` placeholders because those platform-suffixed release assets don't exist in tslp's artifact uploads. The project-wide canonical names come from the publish workflow (shared across all language bindings). (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef ruby e2e codegen: emit `<Module>.<unregister_fn>('<name>')` teardown after trait-bridge `it` blocks.** RSpec runs every generated `it` example in a single ruby process, so `register_ocr_backend_trait_bridge` registered the magnus stub named `test-backend` into kreuzberg's global OCR registry and never removed it; combined with sibling `*_clear` tests that ran earlier and emptied the registry of defaults, the next OCR-using fixture (e.g. `smoke_spec.rb` `ocr_image_png`) failed because the registry contained only the test stub and the core's `ensure_ocr_backends_initialized` self-heal only triggers when the registry is empty. The ruby fix mirrors the python one and threads through ruby's existing template: (1) `build_args_and_setup` now returns `(setup_lines, args_str, teardown_lines)` — a 3-tuple — and the `test_backend` arm pushes `{call_receiver}.{unregister_fn}('{backend_name}')` when the trait bridge declares both `register_fn` and `unregister_fn`. (2) Both `render_test_function` and the streaming `render_chat_stream_example` caller destructure the third tuple element. (3) The standard `ruby/test_function.jinja` template gets a new `teardown_lines` context var rendered as `    {{ line }}` after assertions (or the post-clear list check) and before `end`; the streaming branch writes teardown directly with the same per-line indentation. Result: `ruby/spec/plugin_api_spec.rb`'s `*_trait_bridge` examples now unregister their magnus stubs before the next example loads, restoring shared registry state across the whole spec file. (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/ruby/test_function.jinja`)

- **alef python e2e codegen: emit `unregister_<trait>("<name>")` teardown after trait-bridge fixtures.** Pytest runs every generated test in a single python process, so `test_register_ocr_backend_trait_bridge` (which registers a stub named `test-backend` via `register_ocr_backend`) leaked into later fixtures: when a destructive sibling like `test_ocr_backends_clear` ran first, the registry contained only `test-backend` afterwards, and the core's `ensure_ocr_backends_initialized` self-heal could not re-seed defaults (it triggers only when the registry is empty), so the next OCR-using fixture (e.g. `test_ocr_image_png` in `test_smoke.py`) failed with `OCR backend 'tesseract' not registered. Available backends: ["test-backend"]`. (1) Added `teardown_block` to `TestBackendEmission` with `Default` derive so existing backends keep compiling. (2) Python `emit_test_backend` now populates `teardown_block` with `<unregister_fn>("<backend_name>")` whenever the `TraitBridgeConfig` declares `unregister_fn` — the language-native function name is taken straight from the config so no kreuzberg-specific knowledge leaks into alef. (3) `render_test_function` (python) threads the teardown out of `build_args_and_setup` (now returns a 3-tuple) and appends it to `result_assertions` so it runs after the call and its assertions in the pytest body. Languages that already isolate per test (rust integration tests, go) emit an empty teardown. Added two unit tests covering `unregister_fn` set/unset paths. (`src/e2e/codegen/mod.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/mod.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/test_function.rs`, plus one-line `teardown_block: String::new()` additions to every other language's `emit_test_backend` for forward compatibility.)

- **alef csharp service API: fix AccessViolationException in callback marshaling by pinning GCHandles and maintaining lifetime registry.** C# service-API registration methods would crash all e2e tests with `System.AccessViolationException: Attempted to read or write protected memory` during constructor initialization. The root cause: `GCHandle.Alloc(handler)` without pinning allowed the GC to move or collect the callback delegate, and the GCHandle was immediately freed on success, so the native callback trampoline would try to recover an invalid handle in `UnmanagedCallersOnlyHandler`. The fix pins the GCHandle with `GCHandleType.Pinned` and maintains a static thread-safe dictionary `_registeredCallbacks` to keep handles alive for the registration lifetime, freed only in `Dispose()`. All pinned handles are cleaned up atomically in the service class destructor. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **alef elixir: emit `.dll` NIF extension for Windows and `.dylib` for macOS targets** (previously hardcoded `.so` for all non-Windows OSes), matching publish-workflow asset names. The macOS `.dylib` choice was later reverted in 0.20.5. (`src/publish/package/elixir.rs`)

- **alef JVM service-API native-symbol linking: align jni↔kotlin symbols and rewrite java service to Panama FFM.** The JVM service-API codegen would not link. (1) The jni backend emitted entry points with a hardcoded `Java_com_example_*` prefix and ad-hoc method names while the kotlin backend declared unrelated `external fun` names — these can never resolve. Both now use the shared `src/core/jni.rs` convention: a config-driven package (`jni_package`), the per-service bridge class `service_bridge_class_name` (new shared helper, e.g. `AppServiceBridge`), `bridge_method_name` for native method names, and `jni_symbol` for proper JNI §5.11.3 mangling — so the Rust `Java_*` symbols and the Kotlin `external fun`s in `object <Service>ServiceBridge` line up exactly. The Kotlin `System.loadLibrary` now uses `config.jni_lib_name()` instead of a hardcoded library name. (2) The java service backend was wrongly built on JNI `native` methods, but alef's java backend is **Panama FFM** (no `loadLibrary`, binds the C ABI). It is rewritten to bind the ffi backend's C service symbols (`{ffi_prefix}_{service}_new/_free/_register_{m}/_ep_{e}`) via `Linker.downcallHandle`, wrapping the host handler as a Panama `upcallStub` (mirroring the java trait-bridge pattern), with `Arena`-managed callback lifetime. (3) New `tests/service_api_jvm_symbol_consistency.rs` structurally proves every Kotlin `external fun` has the matching jni `Java_*` symbol, preventing future drift. (`src/core/jni.rs`, `src/backends/jni/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `tests/service_api_jvm_symbol_consistency.rs`)

- **alef elixir rustler e2e codegen: emit trait-bridge GenServer module definitions at module level, not inside test functions.** Generated Elixir e2e test files for trait-bridge fixtures defined `TestStub*GenServer` modules inside `test` function blocks with zero indentation, breaking the Elixir AST and causing `UndefinedFunctionError: start_link/1 not available` when tests tried to instantiate the GenServer. The GenServer modules are Elixir `defmodule` declarations, which cannot be nested inside functions — they must be at module level. The fix splits `emit_test_backend`'s output into two parts: (1) module-level definitions (trait stub + GenServer wrapper) marked with a `__TRAIT_BRIDGE_MODULE_DEFS_END__` sentinel, and (2) test-function-level setup (`.start_link(nil)` call). In `render_test_case`, the extraction logic emits module definitions before the `describe` block (at module scope), then inserts only the `.start_link` call inside the test function with proper indentation. All five trait-bridge e2e tests (document_extractor, embedding_backend, ocr_backend, post_processor, renderer, validator) now compile and run successfully. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **alef php e2e codegen: use snake_case method names in trait-bridge test-backend stubs to match interface declarations.** Generated PHP e2e test files emitted anonymous class methods in camelCase (e.g., `extractBytes()`, `supportedMimeTypes()`) while the interface declarations required snake_case (e.g., `extract_bytes()`, `supported_mime_types()`), causing `Class contains abstract methods and must therefore be declared abstract` fatal errors at test load time. The mismatch arose because ext-php-rs bindings use camelCase for native PHP method names (via `#[php(name = "camelCase")]`) but trait interfaces are declared with Rust's snake_case method names (the canonical source). Test stub methods must match the interface contract, not the native binding convention. The fix removes the `.to_lower_camel_case()` transformation on method names in `emit_test_backend`, using the Rust method name directly (snake_case). Updated snapshot and unit-test expectations accordingly. Generated PHP e2e tests now instantiate valid anonymous classes implementing the trait interfaces. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef r e2e codegen: emit `initialize` and `shutdown` no-op functions in trait-bridge test-backend stubs.** The R extendr trait bridge calls `initialize` and `shutdown` on every registered plugin (mirroring the python/ruby bridges), but `emit_test_backend` for R only emitted `name = "test"` under `super_trait`, so registering an R `list` stub failed with `Plugin 'test' missing method 'initialize'` / `'shutdown'` at runtime. Generated e2e suites for R (e.g. kreuzberg `tests/test_plugin_api.R`, `tests/test_renderer_management.R`) couldn't pass any trait-bridge fixture. The fix emits `initialize = function() invisible(NULL)` and `shutdown = function() invisible(NULL)` alongside `name = "test"` when `super_trait` is set, and re-flows the trailing-comma logic to keep the R `list(...)` syntactically valid as entries are added or removed. Extended `test_emit_test_backend_is_generic_no_domain_names` to assert both methods appear when `super_trait` is `Some`. (`src/e2e/codegen/r.rs`)

- **alef swift: fix `InternalDocument`-in-scope and Adapter/Box type mismatch in generated trait bridge files.** Generated `Swift{Trait}Bridge.swift` files had two bugs: (1) IR `binding_excluded` types (e.g. `InternalDocument`) were not in the `effective_exclude_types` set — only config-level `exclude_types` was consulted — so `InternalDocument` appeared raw in protocol/adapter method signatures instead of being marshalled as `String`. The Swift compiler then failed with `cannot find type 'InternalDocument' in scope`. (2) `gen_single_trait_bridge_file` emitted a `public func register{Trait}(_ bridge: any Swift{Trait}Bridge) throws` function that called `try RustBridge.{fn}(adapter)` where `adapter` is `Swift{Trait}Adapter` — but `RustBridge.{fn}` expects `Swift{Trait}Box` (defined in `Sources/RustBridge/Plugins.swift`). These are distinct, incompatible types. The duplicate registration function was also redundant because `emit_trait_bridge_forwarders` in `Kreuzberg.swift` already emits the correct Box-based forwarder. The fixes: (1) in `generate_bindings`, augment `exclude_types` with IR `binding_excluded` names before calling `gen_trait_bridge_files`; (2) remove the `register_fn` emission block from `gen_single_trait_bridge_file` entirely — the bridge file now contains only the protocol and adapter, not the registration function. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef java e2e codegen: use actual binding package (e.g. `dev.kreuzberg`) instead of hardcoded `dev.sample_crate` for interface names and named-type qualification in trait-bridge stubs.** Generated Java e2e test files (e.g. `e2e/java/.../PluginApiTest.java`) declared stub classes implementing `dev.sample_crate.IDocumentExtractor` with method signatures like `dev.sample_crate.ExtractionConfig config` — but the actual binding jar exposes `dev.kreuzberg.IDocumentExtractor` with `dev.kreuzberg.ExtractionConfig`. The mismatch caused compilation failures (`cannot find symbol: class IDocumentExtractor`). The root cause: `java_stub_type_fqn` and `emit_test_backend` hardcoded `dev.sample_crate.` as the package qualifier. The fix adds a `binding_pkg` parameter (the Java package from `config.java_package()`) to `java_stub_type_fqn`, `java_boxed_stub_type_fqn`, `emit_java_stub_method`, and `emit_test_backend`. The `build_args_and_setup` call site now bypasses the generic dispatch and calls `java::emit_test_backend` directly with `config.java_package()`. The generic dispatch in `mod.rs` passes `""` (unqualified) for the rare case where no config context is available. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/mod.rs`)

- **alef go e2e codegen: qualify named types with import alias and substitute binding-excluded types with `json.RawMessage` in trait-bridge stubs.** Generated Go e2e test files (e.g. `e2e/go/plugin_api_test.go`) are in `package e2e_test` which imports the binding package under an alias (e.g. `kreuzberg "github.com/kreuzberg-dev/kreuzberg/v5"`). The previous `emit_test_backend` emitted bare type names (e.g. `ExtractionConfig` instead of `kreuzberg.ExtractionConfig`) causing `undefined` compile errors for every type used in trait-bridge stub method signatures. Additionally, binding-excluded types like `InternalDocument` (which are never emitted as Go structs — the trait-bridge serialises them to JSON) mapped to the non-existent `InternalDocument` Go type instead of `json.RawMessage`. The fix introduces `emit_test_backend_with_context` and `stub_go_type_with_context` which accept an `excluded_types` set (names of IR `binding_excluded` types) and an `import_alias`. The `build_args_and_setup` call site now computes the excluded set from `type_defs` and passes the file-level `import_alias`, so generated stubs compile correctly. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef ffi/csharp/go trait-bridge: pass companion `_len` for `Bytes` params so NUL bytes do not truncate payloads (#114).** The vtable signature, C trampoline signature, and host-side delegate signature for every `Bytes` parameter now carry a companion `{name}_len: usize` (or `UIntPtr`/`C.size_t`) argument immediately after the pointer. Without it, the callee's C-string conversion (`C.GoString`, `Marshal.PtrToStringUTF8`, etc.) silently truncates the payload at the first `0x00` byte — a real-world hit for binary fixtures (PDFs, images) flowing through trait-bridge stubs. The fix is uniform across the three FFI-shaped backends (ffi `vtable.rs`/`call_body.rs`, csharp `trait_bridge.rs` + `callback_bytes_param.jinja`, go `trait_bridge.rs`) and is paired with a regression unit test in `call_body.rs`. (`src/backends/ffi/trait_bridge/{call_body,vtable}.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/templates/{callback_bytes_param,trait_bridge_class}.jinja`, `src/backends/go/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef extendr: emit Rust-side `options.rs` with `decode_options` for `ExternalPtr`-wrapped binding types.** The R extendr backend exposes `ConversionOptions$default()` as an `ExternalPtr` to the underlying Rust struct, but downstream binding functions had no way to unwrap that pointer back into the core type. The new `gen_options_rs` emits a `decode_options` function that downcasts the `ExternalPtr<ConversionOptions>` to `&ConversionOptions` so all R functions taking a `ConversionOptions` argument get a typed reference — without it, generated R bindings could not consume their own option-constructor outputs. Output path: `packages/r/src/rust/src/options.rs` under the resolved R `output_paths` root. (`src/backends/extendr/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef cleanup pipeline: skip well-known consumer dependency cache dirs so installed alef-hash files in `.venv`/`node_modules`/`vendor`/`deps`/`target`/etc. are never swept.** `cleanup_orphaned_files` walks the workspace looking for alef-marked files outside the current emission set and deletes them. That gate accidentally caught alef-generated files that consumers had installed from registries (e.g. a wheel inside `e2e/python/.venv/lib/python3.*/site-packages/kreuzberg/`) — those have legitimate `alef:hash:` headers from the published package but belong to the consumer's install cache, not the current generator output. Added a `DEPENDENCY_CACHE_DIRS` allow-list (`.venv`, `venv`, `__pypackages__`, `node_modules`, `deps`, `_build`, `vendor`, `target`, `.cargo`, `pkg`, `.gradle`, `.m2`, `.cache`) and an unconditional `continue` in `cleanup_dir_recursive` when the walker encounters any of them. Prevents cross-language e2e suites from getting their installed bindings nuked between regenerations. (`src/cli/pipeline/cleanup.rs`)

- **alef generate pipeline: chmod 755 generated shebang scripts on Unix.** `write_files` writes every emitted file with default permissions (0644), but generated shell scripts (e.g. Java's `mvnw`, and any future shell wrappers) need executable bits or the consumer can't invoke them. The fix detects `#!` at file start on Unix targets and applies `Permissions::from_mode(0o755)` immediately after the write. Windows is unaffected (no POSIX permission bits). (`src/cli/pipeline/generate.rs`)

- **alef typescript e2e: depth-index the `_u` builder variable so nested IFEs do not shadow each other.** Builder expressions for nested option types render as nested IFEs (`(() => { const _u = WasmConversionOptions.default(); _u.preprocessing = (() => { const _u = WasmPreprocessingOptions.default(); ... })(); return _u; })()`), and oxlint's `no-shadow` flagged every inner `_u` as shadowing the outer. The fix threads a `depth` counter through `ts_builder_expression_inner` and renders the variable as `_u{depth}`, so each nested level gets a unique name (`_u0`, `_u1`, `_u2`, …). No runtime behaviour change; lint clean across all wasm + napi e2e builder outputs. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`)

- **alef go e2e codegen: add `emit_test_backend_with_context` so trait-bridge stubs in `package e2e_test` qualify named types and substitute binding-excluded types with `json.RawMessage`.** Generated Go e2e suites live in an external `package e2e_test` that imports the binding under an alias (e.g. `kreuzberg`). The old `emit_test_backend` returned `Named` types unqualified, so stub method signatures referencing e.g. `Document` did not compile from the test package. It also did not know about binding-excluded types (those exist in the Rust IR but are never emitted as Go structs — trait-bridge serialises them as JSON) and would emit references to types that don't exist in the Go binding. The new `emit_test_backend_with_context(trait_bridge, methods, fixture, excluded_types, import_alias)` accepts both bits of context and (a) prefixes `Named` types with `{alias}.` when `import_alias` is non-empty, (b) substitutes any type in `excluded_types` with `json.RawMessage`. Backwards-compatible wrapper `emit_test_backend` calls through with empty defaults. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef java e2e codegen: emit Maven wrapper (`mvnw` + `mvnw.cmd` + `.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties`) so generated test_app builds on any host without a pre-installed Maven.** Previously the generated Java e2e `test_app` directory had a `pom.xml` but no Maven wrapper, forcing consumers to install Apache Maven globally or have CI bring its own. The new `java_mvnw` module embeds the Maven 3.9.x wrapper scripts (shell + cmd) and a `maven-wrapper.properties` pointing at the official wrapper distribution; `JavaCodegen::generate` writes all three into the test_app root and `.mvn/wrapper/`. The generate pipeline's new shebang-detect chmod step (see above) sets `mvnw` to 0755 automatically. The wrapper bootstraps `maven-wrapper.jar` from the URL on first invocation, so alef does not vendor the binary jar. New integration test `tests/e2e_java_mvnw_wrapper_test.rs` asserts the three artefacts appear in the generated file set. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/java_mvnw.rs` [new], `src/e2e/codegen/mod.rs`, `tests/e2e_java_mvnw_wrapper_test.rs` [new])

## [0.20.1] - 2026-05-28

### Fixed

- **alef swift/java/kotlin service API + workspace stub guard: complete the remaining `generate_service_api` backends with real dispatch.** Finishes the four Phase-B service backends not covered by the napi/csharp fix. (1) swift (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`): the trampoline conflated one `contextPtr` as both the service instance (`Unmanaged<AnyObject>.fromOpaque`) and a registry index (`Int(bitPattern:)`), so the `handlerRegistry[handlerIndex]` lookup could never match; replaced with a single model that boxes the handler closure in a `HandlerBox`, retains it via `Unmanaged.passRetained` as the context pointer, recovers and invokes it in the trampoline, and releases every box in `deinit`. Also moved swift's service emission from a fold inside `generate_bindings` to the canonical `generate_service_api` hook, matching all other backends. (2) java (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, new): generates a Java service class wrapping the jni opaque owner handle with registration methods that forward a functional handler to native code and run/finalize entrypoint methods; `supports_service_api` flipped to `true`. (3) kotlin (`src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, new): same jni contract wrapped with idiomatic Kotlin lambdas; `supports_service_api` flipped to `true`. (4) rustler (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`): `gen_run_nif` now decodes the Elixir registration tuples (with correct 1-element-tuple arity), constructs the owner, registers the message-passing handler bridges, and actually calls `owner.run(...)` / `owner.finalize(...)` instead of returning a placeholder `Ok(atoms::ok())`; the `no_stub_responses_in_generated_code` guard is un-ignored. (5) A new workspace test `tests/service_api_no_stub_markers.rs` drives every service-API-capable backend's `generate_service_api` over one realistic service surface and asserts no output contains a forbidden stub marker (`TODO`, `unimplemented!`, "would be called here", un-interpolated `{placeholder}`, …), closing the blind spot where substring-only golden tests let stubbed dispatch ship. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/{service_api,mod}.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/{service_api,mod}.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/gen_bindings/{service_api,mod}.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `tests/service_api_no_stub_markers.rs`)

- **alef zig codegen: emit platform-suffixed URLs in generated `build.zig.zon` for published packages.** When tree-sitter-language-pack publishes Zig release artifacts, the tarballs are named `{crate}-zig-v{version}-{platform}.tar.gz` (e.g. `tree-sitter-language-pack-zig-v1.9.0-rc.12-macos-arm64.tar.gz`), one per supported platform. The generated e2e test `build.zig.zon` was emitting a single dependency with a non-suffixed URL (`{crate}-zig-v{version}.tar.gz`), producing 404s when consumers tried to fetch. The fix emits one dependency entry per supported platform (linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, macos-amd64, macos-arm64, windows-x64) in the `.dependencies` section, each with its own platform-suffixed `.url` and `.hash`. The distributed `build.zig` script selects the correct dependency at build time using `@import("builtin").target.os.tag` and `.cpu.arch`, resolving to the host's platform tarball. Registry-mode test_apps now fetch and link the correct prebuilt FFI library for their build host. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef kotlin e2e: handle nullable bare results in string assertions.** When a Kotlin function returns an optional type (T?) and an e2e fixture calls it without specifying a field path, the generated assertion operates on the bare result. If `result_is_option=true`, string operations like `.trim()` fail with a Kotlin compile error ("Only safe (?.) or non-null asserted (!!.) calls are allowed on a nullable receiver"). The fix detects bare nullable results in `render_assertion` and applies `.orEmpty()` to convert them to non-null empty strings, allowing string operations to proceed safely. This preserves assertion semantics for both kotlin and kotlin_android targets. Generated tests now compile successfully when calling nullable-returning functions like `detectLanguageFromContent()`. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

- **alef php e2e codegen: dedup super-trait `name()` method in anonymous-class test backend emission.** Generated PHP e2e test files emitted anonymous classes that declared `name()` twice — once hardcoded to return the backend name (phase A, when `super_trait` is set), and again iterating all methods from the methods vec that had no default impl (phase B). This caused a PHP fatal `Cannot redeclare Kreuzberg\DocumentExtractor@anonymous::name()` at autoload time. The fix excludes `name()` from phase B's method iteration when phase A has already hardcoded it (`super_trait.is_some() && m.name == "name"`). Added unit test `test_backend_no_duplicate_name_with_super_trait` to verify exactly one `function name()` declaration in the output. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef fmt: apply `cargo fmt` to napi `service_api.rs` and php e2e codegen.** `cargo fmt --all -- --check` (CI "Validate" step) failed on two files: `src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs` had a multi-line `format!()` call that rustfmt collapsed to one line; `src/e2e/codegen/php.rs` had a chained `.iter().filter()` call and a multi-line assertion that rustfmt reflows. Applied `cargo fmt --all` to fix both. No semantic change. (`src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef napi + csharp service API: emit real handler dispatch instead of stubs.** Two `generate_service_api` backends produced non-functional handler dispatch that passed CI only because the golden tests assert substring presence, not compilation/behavior. (1) napi (`src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`): registration metadata arguments were emitted as the literal comment `/* TODO: extract metadata */` inside the `owner.{method}(...)` call, producing uncompilable Rust whenever a registration carried metadata params (the common case — a key/path plus the handler callback); each metadata param is now extracted from the received `_metadata` values with its real NAPI coercion (string/bool/number/optional/serde-roundtrip per `TypeRef`) and passed by name into the registration call. (2) csharp (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`): the `[UnmanagedCallersOnly]` trampoline recovered the `GCHandle` and unmarshalled the request but returned a hardcoded `"{}"` without ever invoking the delegate; it now casts `handle.Target` to `Func<string, string>`, invokes it with the request JSON, and marshals the returned response back to native memory (caller-frees, matching the ffi backend's `char* (*)(void*, const char*)` callback contract). Both backends gained invocation-asserting unit tests that also assert no `TODO`/`stub`/hardcoded-`"{}"` markers remain. (`src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)
- **alef rustler service_api: silence unused-variable lints and gate stub-detection test as `#[ignore]` until owner-method dispatch is implemented.** Two `-D warnings` violations in `src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`: `api: &ApiSurface` parameter in `gen_run_nif` is currently unused (the run entrypoint only references `service` and `ep`); renamed to `_api`. `let contract_name = &reg.callback_contract` inside the registration dispatch loop is also unused (the wire dispatch keys off `method_name` not `contract_name` in the current implementation); renamed to `_contract_name`. Both are intentional placeholders — when the rustler emitter is completed to call `owner.run(...)` / `owner.finalize(...)` properly (mirroring the pyo3 native-fn lookup pattern in `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs:448+`), these will become real reads. The matching `no_stub_responses_in_generated_code` test, which asserts no `Ok(atoms::ok())\n}\n\n/// Drive` pattern in the generated rust output, is gated with `#[ignore = "rustler entrypoint emitter still emits stub Ok(atoms::ok()); see gen_run_nif TODO"]` as a permanent regression-target marker — remove the `#[ignore]` once `gen_run_nif` properly invokes the owner method. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`)

- **alef kotlin / kotlin_android / swift snapshot tests: accept new baselines for files emitted under the `dev.sample_crate` package path.** After the `feat(csharp,dart,go,swift,zig): generate_service_api for five more backends` commit (`972fd27fe`) and downstream emitter refinements, the kotlin/kotlin_android/swift snapshot tests discover files at `dev/sample_crate/{Config,Demo,DemoBridge,DemoBridgeException,DemoError,Status}.kt` and `Sources/Demo/SwiftOcrBackendBridge.swift` that previously did not exist (or lived under different package paths). Ran `cargo insta accept` (and manually moved residual `.snap.new` files to canonical `.snap`) to capture the new outputs as baselines. 7 new files + 1 updated swift baseline (the swift trait-bridge inbound output gained adapter helpers). (`tests/snapshots/backends_kotlin_*.snap`, `tests/snapshots/backends_swift_*.snap`)

- **alef integration tests: align test expectations + insta snapshots with spec-compliant JNI symbol encoding, updated dart FRB post-build step count, and gate jni service_api doc-tests as Java.** Three orthogonal test-side fixes surfaced when the CI integration suite ran post-`v0.20.0` cycle: (1) `tests/backends_jni_gen_shims_test.rs` and 8 paired insta `.snap` files asserted `Java_dev_sample_crate_demo_*` symbols on the demo fixture (package `dev.sample_crate.demo`), but the production `jni_symbol` function correctly encodes `_` → `_1` per JNI spec §5.11.3 (so the actual emitted symbol is `Java_dev_sample_1crate_demo_*`). Bulk-replaced `dev_sample_crate_` → `dev_sample_1crate_` across `tests/` (12 files). (2) `tests/backends_dart_gen_bindings_test.rs::build_config_for_frb_emits_post_process_file_step` and `..._run_command_precedes_post_process_file` asserted three PostProcessFile steps; the dart backend now emits five (added `FrbDartOptionalFieldsWithDefaults` on lib.dart, and a separate `FrbDartExcludeFunctions` pass on frb_generated.dart for the `crateCalculateQualityScore` filter). Updated test expectations to match — full per-step processor/path assertions retained. (3) Two doc-tests in `src/backends/jni/service_api.rs` (`gen_register_jni_function`, `gen_entrypoint_jni_function`) contained un-annotated code fences with Java pseudo-signatures (`public native void register{ServiceName}{MethodName}(Object handler);`) that rustdoc tried to compile as Rust. Marked both as `java,ignore`. (`tests/backends_jni_gen_shims_test.rs`, `tests/backends_dart_gen_bindings_test.rs`, `tests/snapshots/backends_jni_gen_shims_test__*.snap`, `src/backends/jni/service_api.rs`)

## [0.20.0] - 2026-05-27

### Changed

- **alef hash: compute per-file `alef:hash:` over generation inputs, not emitted content.** Previously `finalize_hashes` embedded `blake3(sources_hash || file_content)`, so any post-generation formatter (rumdl-fmt, oxfmt, cargo fmt, ruff, etc.) that rewrote whitespace or trailing newlines in a generated file would invalidate its embedded hash. `alef verify --exit-code` would then report the file as stale even though no inputs had changed, causing false-positive CI failures on every regen+lint cycle.
