The new formula is `blake3("alef:inputs\0" || ALEF_REV || "\0" || sources_hash || "\0" || alef_toml_bytes)` — capturing alef's own revision, the Rust source fingerprint, and the raw alef.toml bytes, but not the emitted file content at all. `alef verify` now compares the embedded hex directly against a freshly recomputed inputs hash without reading or hashing the surrounding file content. The domain separator `"alef:inputs\0"` prevents collision with any hash computed under the old scheme. (`src/core/hash.rs`, `src/cli/pipeline/generate.rs`, `src/cli/pipeline/version.rs`, `src/cli/cache.rs`, `src/main.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef cleanup: fix three test failures and two clippy/lint issues introduced by the `generate_service_api` feature cycle.** (1) `ScaffoldCargo::default()` derived `build_jobs = 0` instead of the documented `4` because `#[derive(Default)]` ignores serde defaults — replaced with an explicit `impl Default` using `default_build_jobs()`. (2) The dart `frb_rewrite` test checked for `"static Future<void> init({{")` (literal two braces) but `format!` collapses `{{` to `{`, so the actual output is `"static Future<void> init({"` — fixed test strings to match actual output. (3) Elixir `emit_test_backend` hardcoded `SampleCrate.complete_trait_call` instead of using the crate's actual NIF module; added `nif_module: &str` parameter (empty → `"Native"` fallback), updated the dispatch in `mod.rs` and the calling site in `elixir.rs` to pass `{module_path}.Native`. Additionally removed the dead `find_contract` function from `swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs` (clippy `-D dead_code`), fixed a project-name leak in a PHP service_api comment, and applied rustfmt/clippy-fix to `csharp/service_api.rs` and `dart/service_api.rs`. (`src/core/config/output.rs`, `src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `tests/e2e_elixir_plugin_trait_stubs.rs`)

- **alef php naming + jni symbol tests: correct test expectations to match production behaviour.** Two unit-test assertions encoded incorrect expected values: (1) `src/backends/php/naming.rs` expected `php_autoload_namespace` to return `Html\To\Markdown\Rs` for the input `sample_markdown_rs`, but the function correctly splits on `_` and PascalCases each component → `Sample\Markdown\Rs`; (2) `src/core/jni.rs` `jni_symbol_basic` and `jni_symbol_empty_method_gives_prefix` expected raw underscores in `dev.sample_crate.demo` to pass through unchanged, but the JNI spec §5.11.3 requires underscores inside identifier components to be encoded as `_1` (so `sample_crate` → `sample_1crate`) — the production `jni_symbol` function already does this. Tests now match the spec-compliant behaviour. (`src/backends/php/naming.rs`, `src/core/jni.rs`)

- **alef vendor: append empty `[workspace]` to inner vendored Cargo.toml when no outer workspace manifest is generated.** `vendor_core_only(..., generate_workspace_manifest=false)` (used for languages like Elixir) copied the core crate into `{dest}/{crate}/Cargo.toml` but left it without any workspace marker. Downstream tooling (e.g. `publish-hex`'s "Generate Cargo.lock for native dependencies" loop, which iterates every `Cargo.toml` under `native/` and runs `cargo generate-lockfile`) then triggered cargo to walk up from the vendor path looking for a workspace, find the consumer repo's root workspace, and bail with "current package believes it's in a workspace when it's not". The fix appends an empty `[workspace]` table to the inner vendored Cargo.toml, making it a self-contained one-package workspace root. For Ruby (`generate_workspace_manifest=true`), the outer `{dest}/Cargo.toml` already claims membership and we preserve the existing behaviour (no inner `[workspace]`). (`src/publish/vendor.rs`)

- **alef c e2e Makefile: skip download_ffi.sh when the FFI header is already present locally.** The generated `e2e/<lib>/Makefile` hardcoded `$(TARGET): $(SRCS) $(FFI_DIR)/include/{header}` as a prerequisite, which always triggered the `download_ffi.sh` rule even when a locally-built header existed at `../../crates/<lib>-ffi/include/{header}`. On release commits this caused CI E2E (c) to 404 against a release that hadn't published its FFI assets yet (or whose pinned `VERSION` was stale). The `FFI_INCLUDE` fallback only affected `CFLAGS`, not the build prerequisite — the fallback was effectively dead for the build itself. Now the codegen also emits `HEADER_PATH := $(if ...)` that resolves to whichever of the two candidate header paths exists, and the build target's prerequisite becomes conditional: `$(if $(HEADER_PATH),,$(FFI_DIR)/include/{header})`. When either path holds the header, no download is triggered; when neither does, the original download flow runs as before. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

- **alef c e2e Makefile: also gate download_ffi.sh skip on the shared library being present locally, not just the header.** The previous fix gated the download-trigger prerequisite on `HEADER_PATH` alone, but the build also needs the shared library — on a fresh smoke-test clone with no local cargo build, the in-tree header would be discovered (so HEADER_PATH set, download skipped) while no `../../target/release/lib{name}.{so,dylib,a}` exists, leaving FFI_LIB_DIR empty and link failing with `library not found`. Codegen now also emits `LIB_PATH := $(or $(wildcard $(FFI_DIR)/lib/lib{name}.so),$(wildcard $(FFI_DIR)/lib/lib{name}.dylib),$(wildcard $(FFI_DIR)/lib/lib{name}.a),$(wildcard ../../target/release/lib{name}.so),$(wildcard ../../target/release/lib{name}.dylib),$(wildcard ../../target/release/lib{name}.a))` covering both `$(FFI_DIR)/lib/` and `../../target/release/` for each candidate extension; the build target downloads only when _either_ HEADER_PATH or LIB_PATH is empty (`$(if $(and $(HEADER_PATH),$(LIB_PATH)),,$(FFI_DIR)/include/{header})`). CI E2E flows with both header and lib staged locally remain download-free; smoke-test flows with no local build always trigger the download. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

- **alef java e2e pom.xml: only set `-Djava.library.path=…/target/release` in Local dep_mode; drop it in Registry mode so the Maven-Central JAR's bundled `/natives/{rid}/` extraction loads correctly.** The generated `test_apps/java/pom.xml` (Registry mode) consumes the published JAR which embeds platform natives under `/natives/{rid}/` — alef's `NativeLib` loader extracts and `System.load`s them automatically at startup. Overriding `java.library.path` to point at a non-existent `../../target/release` (the previous unconditional behaviour) made `System.loadLibrary` the only resolution path, and the fallback failed with `UnsatisfiedLinkError: no html_to_markdown_ffi in java.library.path: …/target/release`. The override is now emitted only when `dep_mode == Local` (the e2e test app that consumes a locally-built JAR without bundled natives, where the loader needs to find the native via java.library.path). Template gains an `include_native_lib_path` flag; codegen sets it from `dep_mode`. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/java/pom.xml.jinja`)

- **alef scaffold: persist rustc job cap and Node memory cap mitigations in generated `.cargo/config.toml` and wasm e2e `package.json`.** OOM crashes on 16 GB dev machines during large WASM builds were prevented by four mitigations: rustc job limit (`jobs = 4` in `.cargo/config.toml`), WASM size optimization, and Node memory cap (`NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096` in e2e test script). However, alef was regenerating both the cargo config and wasm e2e package.json from templates that lacked these settings, clobbering the mitigations on every `task alef:generate`. Now `[scaffold.cargo]` has a new `build_jobs` field (default `4`; set to `0` to disable), and the wasm e2e package.json template includes the `NODE_OPTIONS` heap cap in its test script. Both mitigations persist through regeneration. Added three unit tests in `src/scaffold/tests.rs` to verify default job limit, custom override, and zero-disabling behavior; added one test in `src/e2e/codegen/wasm.rs` to verify `NODE_OPTIONS` is emitted in the package.json test script.

- **alef wasm e2e: consolidate setup.ts imports to prevent duplicate import statements.** The wasm e2e codegen was emitting setup.ts with two separate import blocks: one for wasm module init (`createRequire`, `readFileSync`, `fileURLToPath`) and another for file setup (`createRequire`, `fileURLToPath`, `dirname`, `join`), causing TypeScript "duplicate identifier" errors. Fixed by consolidating all imports into a single block at the top of the file, emitting `dirname` and `join` only when file setup is needed. Added unit test to verify import uniqueness.

### Added

- **alef csharp/dart/go/swift/zig: implement `generate_service_api` for five more backends.** Completes the service-API rollout across every alef-supported language by adding emitters for the remaining native and FFI-consuming backends. Each backend generates, from the same `ServiceDef`/`HandlerContractDef` IR used by the pyo3/napi/magnus/php/rustler/jni/ffi/extendr emitters, an idiomatic host-language owner object (constructor + chaining configurators + per-registration method + `run`/`finalize` entrypoint) plus the Rust glue that bridges each host callback into the async handler contract. The five backends use their canonical FFI patterns: C# wraps the C-ABI service handle via P/Invoke with `[UnmanagedCallersOnly]` callback marshalling, Dart drives the same C ABI through FRB-generated bindings, Go uses cgo with `runtime.Pinner`-guarded callback pointers, Swift bridges via swift-bridge's typed JSON-envelope adapters, Zig consumes the raw C ABI directly via `extern fn`. Wire request/response DTOs continue to be JSON-marshalled across the boundary. `supports_service_api` set on each. IR-derived with zero hardcoded names; golden tests included per backend. (`src/backends/{csharp,dart,go,swift,zig}/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `…/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef jni: implement `generate_service_api` for the JVM foundation.** Generates the JNI glue java/kotlin will wrap: an opaque owner handle (`jlong` from `Box::into_raw`) with constructor/`_free` entry points, a per-`HandlerContractDef` bridge holding a JVM `GlobalRef` + cached `jmethodID` whose async dispatch attaches the current thread, calls the host handler with the `wire_request_type` JSON, and parses the `wire_response_type`, and `extern "system"` register/run/finalize entry points that thread the owner handle, call the owner's registration method with the bridge, and drive the entrypoint via a Tokio runtime. No panics in JNI; SAFETY comments on every unsafe. IR-derived; golden tests assert the register code calls the owner method and run drives the owner. (`src/backends/jni/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/jni/{mod,gen_shims}.rs`)
- **alef ffi/extendr: implement `generate_service_api` for the C-ABI foundation and R backends.** The C FFI backend now emits the stable C ABI other native backends wrap: a `char* (*)(void* ctx, const char* request_json)` registration-callback typedef, opaque service handle with matching `_new`/`_free`, per-`HandlerContractDef` bridge implementing the async contract by invoking the C callback under `spawn_blocking` (wire request/response round-tripped as JSON with documented alloc/free ownership), and `extern "C"` register/run/finalize entry points. The extendr (R) backend generates an idiomatic S3 object API plus Rust glue that calls the registered R closure synchronously (R is single-threaded — documented), blocking the Tokio runtime. Both are IR-derived with no hardcoded names; `supports_service_api` set; golden tests included. (`src/backends/ffi/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/extendr/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `…/mod.rs`)
- **alef napi/magnus/php/rustler: implement `generate_service_api` for four more backends.** Mirroring the pyo3 pilot, the Node/TypeScript (napi), Ruby (magnus), PHP (ext-php-rs), and Elixir (rustler) backends now generate, from the `ServiceDef`/`HandlerContractDef` IR, an idiomatic host-language app object (constructor + chaining configurators + per-registration decorator/method + `run`/`finalize` entrypoint) plus the Rust glue that bridges each host callback into the async handler contract and drives the run entrypoint. Each uses its language's idiomatic async bridge: napi `ThreadsafeFunction` awaiting the JS Promise, magnus acquiring the GVL inside `spawn_blocking`, php blocking on the Tokio runtime (single-threaded per request), rustler message-passing (`{:trait_call, …}` to a generated GenServer, reply via a completion NIF, run scheduled on `DirtyCpu`). Wire request/response DTOs are round-tripped as JSON so the host receives/returns plain values. `supports_service_api` is set for each. Everything is IR-derived with zero hardcoded names; each backend ships golden tests asserting the generated host + Rust surfaces. (`src/backends/{napi,magnus,php,rustler}/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `…/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef pyo3: implement `generate_service_api` for the Python backend (pilot implementation).** When `api.services` is non-empty the pyo3 backend now generates two files per configured service. (1) `service.py` — an idiomatic Python class whose name, constructor, configurator methods, registration decorators, and `run`/`finalize` entrypoints are derived entirely from the `ServiceDef` IR; each registration method doubles as both a decorator factory (`@app.add_handler(path, method)`) and a direct `register_*` variant. (2) `service.rs` — Rust pyo3 glue containing a `Py{ContractName}Bridge` struct for each referenced `HandlerContractDef` that wraps a Python callable (`Py<PyAny>`) and implements the async handler trait; async callables are driven via `pyo3_async_runtimes::tokio::into_future`, sync callables run in `spawn_blocking` to avoid blocking the Tokio executor; wire request/response DTOs are round-tripped through `serde_json::to_string`/`from_str` so the Python side receives a plain dict and returns a plain dict; a `#[pyfunction]` entry point iterates the collected `(method_name, metadata_tuple, callable)` registrations, dispatches on method name to the correct bridge + registration call, and invokes the owner's entrypoint. `Capabilities::supports_service_api` is set to `true` for the pyo3 backend. Zero hardcoded names — all types, method names, and contract names are derived from the IR. The bridge approach reuses the async/sync dispatch pattern pioneered by `src/adapters/callback_bridge.rs`. Covered by 11 unit/golden tests in `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/service_api.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef core: model a generic callback-driven "service" API surface (`[[crates.services]]` + `[[crates.handler_contracts]]`).** Adds the building blocks for backends to later generate an idiomatic owner/builder object whose registered host callbacks are bridged into an async Rust trait and consumed by a long-lived `run` entrypoint — generically, with no transport- or domain-specific assumptions. New IR nodes on `ApiSurface`: `ServiceDef` (owner type + constructor + chaining configurators + callback `registrations` + `run`/`finalize` `entrypoints`) and `HandlerContractDef` (the async trait a registered callback satisfies, its dispatch method, and the wire request/response DTO names). New config: `ServiceConfig`/`RegistrationSpec`/`EntrypointSpec` and `HandlerContractConfig` (flattens `TraitBridgeConfig`, adds `dispatch_method`/`is_async`/`wire_request_type`/`wire_response_type`/`optional_overrides`), wired through `RawCrateConfig`→`ResolvedCrateConfig` with validation. New extraction pass (`src/extract/extractor/service.rs`) that classifies owner-type methods and recovers callback-bound generic registration methods (e.g. `fn add_route<H: IntoHandler>(…)`) that the FFI-safety generic-method skip drops, by re-parsing the configured sources via the shared method extractor and injecting them into the owner type (which is marked `binding_excluded`). New optional `Backend::generate_service_api` hook + `Capabilities::supports_service_api`, dispatched after `generate_bindings` and before `generate_public_api`, with a warning when a configured service targets a backend that does not support it. The dead `AdapterPattern::ServerLifecycle` stub is removed (superseded by `ServiceDef`). Backends do not yet implement `generate_service_api`; this is purely additive — consumers without `[[crates.services]]` are unchanged. (`src/core/ir.rs`, `src/core/config/service.rs`, `src/core/config/{raw_crate,new_config,resolved}.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/service.rs`, `src/core/backend.rs`, `src/cli/pipeline/generate.rs`, `src/adapters/mod.rs`)

## [0.19.25] - 2026-05-27

### Fixed

- **alef dart frb_rewrite: ensure balanced braces and parentheses in prologue generation.** The `frb_init_prologue_replacement` function generates Dart code that injects the `_alefResolveExternalLibrary()` helper method and replaces the `init` method prologue. The helper method is fully closed, and the init signature with opening brace are emitted, allowing the original FRB method body to continue seamlessly. Added documentation clarifying the brace-balancing structure and a unit test `prologue_replacement_has_balanced_braces_and_parens` to verify balanced delimiters (equal open/close braces and parens) on generated output. This prevents malformed Rust in kreuzberg's generated `packages/dart/rust/src/lib.rs` that would cause flutter_rust_bridge_codegen to stall indefinitely during Dart FFI binding generation. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)
- **alef zig trait-bridge: fix VTable casting and test stub method completeness.** (1) The vtable struct signatures were incorrectly applying `substitute_excluded_types`, replacing internal types like `InternalDocument` with `Json` in the C ABI boundary. This broke type compatibility: Rust FFI functions expected the original `InternalDocument` C pointer type, but generated Zig was trying to pass `Json` (which doesn't exist in the public surface). The fix restricts type substitution to Zig-level wrapper code only; VTable struct field types and thunk C ABI signatures preserve the original types to maintain C FFI compatibility. (2) E2E test stubs for plugin-registry traits (DocumentExtractor, EmbeddingBackend, OcrBackend, PostProcessor, Validator, Renderer) were missing required interface methods. The `make_*_vtable` functions call ALL trait methods (including optional ones with default implementations), so test stubs must implement them all. Fixed by emitting all trait methods (both required and defaulted) in the test stub generator, ensuring stubs pass the full interface contract to vtable builder. Resolves all 36 Zig e2e plugin-api test compilation errors. (`src/backends/zig/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)
- **alef go e2e stubs: emit Plugin super-trait methods and use proper Go type names for Named types.** Generated test backend stubs in Go e2e tests (e.g., `testStub_register_document_extractor_trait_bridge`) only emitted direct trait methods and omitted super-trait methods like `Version()`, `Initialize()`, `Shutdown()`, `CanHandle()`, `Priority()`, `EstimatedDurationMs()`. This produced incomplete structs that failed to satisfy the full interface contract expected by `Register*` calls. Additionally, Named types like `OcrBackendType` were being mapped to `json.RawMessage` in stub signatures even though they're part of the public binding surface; stubs should use the actual Go type names (e.g., `OcrBackendType`) to match the trait-bridge interface signatures. Fixed by: (1) collecting super-trait methods when building method lists for stubs (methods found in the super-trait type definition in the IR), (2) filtering methods by `trait_source` to identify and emit super-trait methods separately (with `Name()` returning the fixture id and other methods using standard stub logic), (3) simplifying `stub_go_type()` to use `go_type()` directly instead of unconditionally substituting Named types with `json.RawMessage` — this ensures stubs use the canonical Go binding types, and (4) updating `go_stub_default()` to use `go_zero_value()` for consistency. The dispatcher in `mod.rs` identifies super-trait methods via `trait_source` matching, eliminating the need for hardcoded method lists. All 6 trait-bridge stubs (DocumentExtractor, EmbeddingBackend, OcrBackend, PostProcessor, Renderer, Validator) now implement the full Plugin interface. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)
- **alef java e2e: fix trait-bridge test stubs with full interface implementation and proper type FQNs.** Generated Java e2e tests for plugin-registry traits (e.g., register_document_extractor, register_ocr_backend) were failing compilation because stub implementations: (1) emitted wrong method signatures (e.g., `CompletableFuture<Object>` instead of matching the interface's actual return type), (2) used bare type names without FQNs (e.g., `ExtractionResult` instead of `dev.kreuzberg.ExtractionResult`, `List` instead of `java.util.List`), and (3) omitted super-trait methods (name, version, initialize, shutdown, etc.). Fixed by: (a) using proper type qualification for all Kreuzberg domain types in stub method signatures via `java_stub_type_fqn` helper that qualifies all `Named(T)` types as `dev.kreuzberg.T` and collection inner types as `java.util.List<dev.kreuzberg.T>`, (b) applying FQNs consistently to both parameter and return types, and (c) explicitly emitting all Plugin super-trait methods in the stub class (collected from IR via `trait_source` matching). Stubs now implement the full `IDocumentExtractor`/`IOcrBackend`/etc. interface contract. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)
- **alef elixir e2e: fix register_*/2 ArgumentError by passing GenServer PID instead of module atom to Rustler NIFs.** Generated Elixir e2e tests for plugin-registry traits (e.g., register_document_extractor, register_embedding_backend) were calling `Kreuzberg.register_document_extractor(E2e.TestStubs.TestStubMyBackend, "name")` where the first argument is a module atom. However, the Rustler NIF signature expects a `rustler::LocalPid` to enable message-based trait callback dispatch: the NIF creates a bridge that sends `{:trait_call, method, args_json, reply_id}` messages to that PID and waits for responses. Since a module atom cannot receive messages, the NIF's `pid` parameter decode failed with `ArgumentError: argument error`. Fixed by: (1) emitting a wrapper `GenServer` module for each test stub that implements the trait callback protocol via `handle_info/2` and dispatches method calls to the underlying stub module using `apply/3`, (2) starting the GenServer in the e2e test setup block and capturing its PID, and (3) passing the PID (not the module atom) as the first argument to `register_*/2`. The generated GenServer handles incoming `{:trait_call, method_atom, args_json, reply_id}` messages from Rust, calls the stub method with deserialized args, serializes the result to JSON, and replies via `Kreuzberg.complete_trait_call/2` NIF to unblock the Rust thread. All 6 register_*/2 calls in kreuzberg Elixir e2e (document_extractor, embedding_backend, ocr_backend, post_processor, renderer, validator) now pass. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)
- **alef swift-backend: fix trait-bridge protocol and adapter code generation with proper type marshalling and error handling.** The trait-bridge codegen was emitting placeholder Swift bridge files with (1) references to excluded internal types like `InternalDocument` that don't exist in the public Swift binding surface, (2) placeholder empty string `""` return values for all methods, and (3) no marshalling logic for converting types across the C boundary. Fixed by: (a) accepting an `exclude_types` parameter in `gen_trait_bridge_files` and using it to marshal excluded types as JSON strings (matching Zig/Rust patterns), (b) emitting proper adapter method bodies with `do-try-catch` error handling and JSON envelope serialisation for error-returning methods, (c) generating helper functions `marshal_ok_result` and `marshal_error_result` for Result wrapping, and (d) updating all type inference functions (`swift_type_name`, `swift_method_params`, `swift_return_type`) to substitute excluded types with `String` for JSON marshalling. Also updates the call site in `gen_bindings/mod.rs` to thread `exclude_types` through the trait bridge generation. (`src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef typescript e2e: stop emitting sealed `pnpm-workspace.yaml` in Local mode so `kreuzberg@workspace:*` resolves from parent workspace.** When `[crates.e2e.dep_mode]` is `local` (default), the Node e2e test app depends on the binding via `workspace:*`, which must resolve through the consumer's root pnpm workspace. However, alef was previously emitting a sealed `packages: []` workspace file that made e2e/node a self-rooted workspace, shadowing the parent and breaking `pnpm install` with `ERR_PNPM_NO_MATCHING_VERSION_INSIDE_WORKSPACE`. The sealed workspace is only needed in Registry mode (when the test app has no `workspace:*` deps and needs isolated devDependencies). In Local mode, the codegen now explicitly removes any stale `pnpm-workspace.yaml` if it exists (even without alef header), preventing shadowing. A new test verifies Local mode does not emit the file. Fixes kreuzberg e2e/node `pnpm install` failures. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/mod.rs`)
- **alef extendr e2e: add `arg_name_map` to register_*/unregister_*/clear_* call overrides in alef.toml for R test generation.** Generated R e2e tests call `register_document_extractor(extractor = ...)` but the Rust trait-bridge wrapper function signature expects parameter name `r_backend`, causing `unused argument` test errors. The fixture JSON uses semantic names (`extractor`, `backend`, `processor`, `renderer`, `validator`) matching the fixture shape, but the extendr-generated R wrapper uses the internal parameter name. R e2e test generation now applies `arg_name_map` overrides in alef.toml to remap fixture argument names to the actual Rust function parameter names. All six register_* calls (document_extractor, embedding_backend, ocr_backend, post_processor, renderer, validator) now include mappings: `arg_name_map = { fixture_name = "r_backend" }`. (`crates/kreuzberg/alef.toml`)
- **alef e2e ruby codegen: fix void-returning register/unregister assertions and add plugin registry cleanup hooks.** (1) The Ruby e2e stub emitter was generating `result = Kreuzberg.register_ocr_backend(...); expect(result).not_to be_nil` for all register_*/unregister_* calls. These functions return `Result<(), E>` which becomes Ruby `nil` on success, so the assertion was always failing. Fixed by setting `returns_void = true` on all 12 register/unregister/clear call configs in `alef.toml` and updating the Jinja template to handle void-returning methods properly. (2) The generated `spec_helper.rb` now includes RSpec `before(:each)` and `after(:each)` hooks that track and clean up test-prefixed plugin backends (those with `test-` or `test_` prefix), preventing one test's registered mock backends from polluting subsequent tests. This mirrors the Python `conftest.py` isolation pattern. (`alef.toml`, `src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/ruby/test_function.jinja`)
- **alef e2e php codegen: collect super-trait methods when generating test backend stubs.** Generated test stubs for trait-bridge backends (e.g., `DocumentExtractor` with `Plugin` super-trait) only emitted methods from the direct trait, omitting super-trait methods (`name`, `priority`, `version`, etc.). This produced anonymous PHP classes implementing only 3 of 6 required interface methods, causing `Kreuzberg` registration calls to fail with `Declaration of Kreuzberg\DocumentExtractor::extract_bytes() must be compatible with its parent interface` or missing-method errors. The method collection phase now gathers both direct trait methods and super-trait methods (by `rust_path` to avoid short-name collisions), ensuring the generated anonymous class implements the full interface contract. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)
- **alef python e2e codegen: treat Rust-style primitive type names as primitives in test-file imports.** The Python e2e test generator iterates over a fixture's call args and, for any non-primitive `element_type`, adds it to the `from <pkg> import …` line. The primitive skip-list only contained Python-style type names (`str`, `int`, `bool`, …), so an `element_type = "String"` (a Rust string, idiomatic in `alef.toml` since the config is language-agnostic) was treated as a public binding symbol and emitted as `from kreuzcrawl import String, …` — hard-failing pytest collection with `ImportError: cannot import name 'String' from 'kreuzcrawl'`. The skip-list now covers Rust primitive names too: `String`, `&str`, `char`, all signed/unsigned integer widths (`u8..u128`, `i8..i128`, `usize`/`isize`), and `f32`/`f64`. Verified by the kreuzcrawl rc.35 CI E2E python failure (run 26497302955, test_batch.py and test_stream.py both broke on `String` collection). (`src/e2e/codegen/python/test_file.rs`)
- **alef sync-versions: bump NAPI platform pins and pre-staged platform manifests in `crates/*-node/`.** `sync_versions` rewrote the top-level `"version"` of `crates/*-node/package.json` but never touched (a) the `optionalDependencies` block (each entry is a sibling NAPI platform package pinned to the same version) or (b) the pre-staged platform manifests at `crates/*-node/npm/<platform>/package.json` that `napi prepublish` ships. After a version bump the parent package was at the new version while every platform pin stayed at the old version, so `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` on consumer CI rejected the manifest with `ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE` and the Build Node bindings job hard-failed. `sync_versions` now rewrites both surfaces: it reads the parent package `name` from the manifest and rewrites every `optionalDependencies` entry whose key starts with `<name>-` (the `napi_platform_package_name` shape), and globs `crates/*-node/npm/*/package.json` to bump the platform manifests' top-level `"version"`. Verified in kreuzcrawl rc.34 Publish Node bindings failure (run 26494601181), where the manifest had rc.34 but all `optionalDependencies` pins and platform manifests stayed at rc.33. (`src/cli/pipeline/version.rs`)

## [0.19.24] - 2026-05-27

### Fixed

- **alef php-backend: insert missing comma between fixed `NodeContext $context` parameter and the trailing per-method parameter list in generated `HtmlVisitorInterface`.** `php_visitor_interface_method.jinja` emitted `function visit_text(NodeContext $context{{ method_params }})`, producing the invalid PHP signature `function visit_text(NodeContext $contextstring $_text)` whenever `method_params` was non-empty. PHP-CS-Fixer + PHPStan both refused to parse the generated `HtmlVisitor.php`, breaking lint CI on every consumer. The template now conditionally inserts `,` before `method_params`. (`src/backends/php/templates/php_visitor_interface_method.jinja`)
- **alef e2e c codegen: harden `download_ffi.sh` `rm -rf` paths with `${FFI_DIR:?}`.** `shellcheck` SC2115 flagged the unguarded `rm -rf "$FFI_DIR"/include "$FFI_DIR"/lib` and `rm -rf "$FFI_DIR"/${FFI_PKG_NAME}-*` patterns: if `$FFI_DIR` is ever unset the script would attempt `rm -rf /include /lib`. The `:?` parameter expansion aborts the script instead of silently expanding to `/`. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

## [0.19.23] - 2026-05-27

### Fixed

- **alef go-backend: derive `embed_ffi.go` package name from config instead of hardcoding `tspack`.** The `embed_ffi.go.jinja` template hardcoded `package tspack` (a leftover from the template's tree-sitter-language-pack origin) and the render site passed an empty context, so every consumer's generated `embed_ffi.go` declared `package tspack` regardless of the configured Go package name — mismatching `binding.go`'s `package <pkg_name>`. Although `embed_ffi.go` is `//go:build ignore` (excluded from normal compilation, so `go build` was unaffected), the foreign package name was incorrect and misleading for vendoring tooling. The template now emits `package {{ pkg_name }}` and the render call threads `pkg_name` through. (`src/backends/go/templates/embed_ffi.go.jinja`, `src/backends/go/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

### Changed

- **alef: align shared pre-commit hooks.** (`5fc63a3f`)

## [0.19.22] - 2026-05-27

### Added

- **alef packaging: centralize generated package metadata and publish validation.** Added `[workspace.package_metadata]` and `[crates.package_metadata]` as the canonical source for generated package descriptions, links, authors, keywords, and categories. Alef now validates crates.io's five-keyword/category limit unless `truncate_registry_lists = true` is explicitly set, and package scaffolds use the centralized metadata before falling back to legacy `[scaffold]` fields.
- **alef dart-backend: new `FrbDartOptionalFieldsWithDefaults` post-build step.** Strips the `required` keyword from FRB-emitted Dart class fields that correspond to Rust struct fields with `#[serde(default)]` or `Default` impls (currently covers `EmbeddingConfig`, `ChunkingConfig`, `ExtractionConfig`). Surfaced by kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 dart e2e: `EmbeddingConfig(...)` failed compilation with `Required named parameter 'model' must be provided` even though the Rust `EmbeddingConfig` had defaults. (`src/core/backend.rs`, `src/cli/pipeline/commands.rs`, `src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/mod.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef csharp-backend: emit static `Register(IInterface impl) -> IntPtr` on every trait-bridge class.** v0.19.21 only emitted `Register` on the separate `{TraitName}Registry` static class, but the e2e tests (and ergonomic public API) expect to call `DocumentExtractorBridge.Register(impl)` directly on the bridge class. Caused kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 csharp e2e: `CS0117: 'DocumentExtractorBridge' does not contain a definition for 'Register'`. The new method handles GCHandle allocation, vtable wiring, and error propagation. (`src/backends/csharp/templates/trait_bridge_class.jinja`, `src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`)
- **alef php-backend: drop leading comma when emitting PHP interface method parameter lists.** `gen_visitor_interface` and `gen_registration_interface` in `src/backends/php/trait_bridge.rs` prepended `", "` to the params list (copied from the Rust bridge emitter, where methods carry an implicit `&mut self` first). With no implicit first arg, this produced `public function extract_bytes(, mixed $content, ...): mixed;` — a PHP parse error. Caused all 6 PHP interface files in v0.19.20/v0.19.21 to be syntactically invalid.
- **alef extendr-backend: filter excluded functions from `extendr_module!` macro registration.** v0.19.19's `[crates.r.exclude_functions]` honoring stripped excluded fn definitions but left their `fn <name>;` lines inside the `extendr_module!` macro. extendr expands those lines into `meta__<name>()` wrapper calls — which referenced the now-missing fn — producing `cannot find function meta__calculate_quality_score in this scope`. Filter applied at all four code paths: fn def emission, `extendr_module!` registration, R wrapper generation, NAMESPACE export. (`src/backends/extendr/gen_bindings.rs`, `tests/backends_extendr_gen_bindings_test.rs`)
- **alef rustler-backend: expose `register_*/2` and `clear_*/0` delegates on the Elixir facade.** Prior to this fix the Elixir facade emitted only `unregister_*` delegates; consumers calling `Kreuzberg.register_document_extractor/2` got `undefined or private`. The 6 register and 6 clear delegates forward straight to the corresponding `Kreuzberg.Native` NIF entry points. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef swift-scaffold: emit `__private__FfiSlice` and the `__private__OptionXX` typedefs in the placeholder `RustBridgeC.h` header.** The placeholder header (used before the Rust cargo build has run) only declared `RustStr`; the `SwiftBridgeCore.swift` extensions reference `__private__FfiSlice` (for `Vec<u8>`) and `__private__OptionU8` through `__private__OptionBool`, so the Swift compiler failed with `cannot find type '__private__FfiSlice' in scope`. Adds 14 new typedefs and `<stdbool.h>` include. (`src/scaffold/languages/swift.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef zig-backend: substitute excluded Rust types with `Json` in trait-bridge method signatures.** Internal Rust types listed in `[crates.zig.exclude_types]` (e.g. `InternalDocument`) were emitted verbatim inside the bridge vtable and thunks, producing `struct 'kreuzberg' has no member named 'InternalDocument'`. Substitution mirrors the Go backend pattern: excluded types serialize through the C ABI as JSON strings. (`src/backends/zig/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/zig/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef dart-backend: also strip excluded functions from `frb_generated.dart`.** v0.19.21's `FrbDartExcludeFunctions` only filtered top-level `lib.dart`; FRB emits the FFI bridge symbol declarations (e.g. `crateCalculateQualityScore`) in `frb_generated.dart` as well, leaving a dangling reference once the implementation was removed. The post-build step now runs the same filter on both files. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef e2e go codegen: collect super-trait methods by `rust_path`, not by short name.** `src/e2e/codegen/go.rs:1962` compared `t.name` (e.g. `"Plugin"`) against the `super_trait` field from `alef.toml` (the fully-qualified `"kreuzberg::plugins::Plugin"`); the comparison never matched and super-trait methods were silently dropped from generated test stubs, breaking interface implementation. Now compared against `rust_path`. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)
- **alef e2e java codegen: emit fully-qualified type names in trait-bridge test stubs and defaults.** Generated `PluginApiTest.java` referenced bare `IDocumentExtractor`, `ExtractionConfig`, `List<String>`, `ArrayList` etc., producing `cannot find symbol` against the test file's import set. New `java_type_fqn` / `java_stub_type_fqn` / `java_boxed_stub_type_fqn` helpers emit `dev.kreuzberg.IDocumentExtractor`, `java.util.List<String>`, `new java.util.ArrayList<>()` etc.; `JavaDefaults::default_for` similarly emits FQN constructors. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`, `src/codegen/defaults.rs`)
- **alef e2e ruby codegen: pass plugin name as second argument to `register_*` calls.** The Magnus binding's `register_<trait>` accepts `(impl, name)` but the ruby e2e stub emitter only emitted `(impl)`, producing `ArgumentError: wrong number of arguments (given 1, expected 2)`. The fix extracts the backend name from each fixture's input JSON and emits it as a Ruby string literal; also collects super-trait methods (`name`, `version`, `initialize`, `shutdown`) onto the stub class. (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`)
- **alef e2e elixir codegen: pass plugin name as second argument to `Kreuzberg.register_*` calls.** Mirrors the ruby fix; the Rustler facade expects `(stub, name)` (see rustler-backend fix above) but the elixir e2e stub emitter only emitted `(stub)`, producing `Kreuzberg.register_document_extractor/1 is undefined or private`. Backend name is extracted from fixture input and emitted as an Elixir string literal with `escape_elixir`. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)
- **alef zig publish: emit per-platform tarballs with distinct filenames.** `alef publish package --lang zig --target <T>` now produces `{crate}-zig-v{version}-{platform}.tar.gz` (mirroring the Go package naming scheme) instead of `{crate}-v{version}.tar.gz`. Previously all platform-specific builds collided on the same filename during GitHub Release asset upload, leaving only the final tarball in the release. Platform naming uses the Go ecosystem convention (e.g. `linux-x86_64`, `macos-arm64`).
- **alef test-apps: install freshly-published packages in registry mode.** The Node and WASM test-app run commands now pass `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --config.minimumReleaseAge=0` (and `npm install --no-package-lock` on the npm path): they re-resolve from `package.json` instead of the committed lockfile, which pins the previous release, and bypass pnpm's `minimumReleaseAge` supply-chain gate, which otherwise rejects the just-released version under test. (`src/core/config/test_apps_run_defaults.rs`)
- **alef packaging: generate and validate language package internals instead of relying on repo-local drift.** Ruby gemspecs include README/LICENSE and generated type files; Node package scaffolding emits platform manifests, optional dependencies, `exports`, engines, musl libc metadata, and `win32-arm64-msvc`; WASM packages can use explicit package names and publish from Alef's manifest; Elixir scaffolds include `nif_targets`; PHP root Composer autoload points at `packages/php/src/`; publish validation detects stale Ruby/C# package entrypoints, Go `/vN` layout mismatches, Swift release placeholders, and missing Java/Dart/Zig metadata.
- **alef python-scaffold: stop emitting broken `[tool.maturin] include` block.** v0.19.21 added an `include = [{ path = "../../crates/<crate>/**/*", format = "sdist" }]` directive intending to bundle the core Rust crate into the Python sdist, but maturin rejects archive paths containing `..` (`paths in archives must not have ".."`), so every sdist build with the directive present failed outright. Maturin already auto-bundles path dependencies declared in the PyO3 crate's `Cargo.toml`, so the directive was both broken and unnecessary. Removed `sdist_rust_crate_includes`, `relative_path`, and `normalized_components` helpers along with the corresponding template block and tests. The original #390 sdist regression (PyPI 3.5.1 missing the core crate) was actually caused by a stale `.gitmodules` entry in the consumer repo silently breaking `cargo package --list` — fixed at the consumer side. (`src/scaffold/languages/python.rs`, `src/scaffold/tests.rs`)
- **alef go-scaffold test: assert three emitted files (`go.mod`, `.golangci.yml`, `.lib/.gitkeep`).** The `embed_ffi.go` work in v0.19.21 added the `.lib/.gitkeep` marker but `test_scaffold_go_production_format` still asserted two files; the test failed on every cargo test run since. (`src/scaffold/tests.rs`)

## [0.19.21] - 2026-05-26

### Added

- **alef rustler-backend: `[languages.elixir] nif_targets` config field to align the generated `native.ex` `targets:` list with the consumer's actual NIF build matrix.** The `native_module_header.jinja` template previously hardcoded `~w(aarch64-apple-darwin aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu x86_64-pc-windows-gnu)` — 4 targets, with Windows as `-gnu`. Consumers whose CI matrix builds a different set (e.g. kreuzcrawl ships 7 targets including musl variants and `x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`) had no way to override this without hand-editing the generated file. RustlerPrecompiled then tried to download tarballs at install time using the hardcoded names, producing 404s on every consumer install whose CI uploaded under different triples. The `nif_targets` field on `ElixirConfig` accepts a `Vec<String>` of triples; when non-empty, the template emits exactly that list. When empty, the historical 4-target default is preserved so no existing consumer regresses. (`src/core/config/languages.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

### Changed

- **alef-cli: make post-generation formatting opt-in for `generate`, `all`, and `init`.** These commands now default `--format` to `false` so regeneration stays fast and avoids invoking language-native formatter chains unless requested. Pass `--format` to run the existing best-effort formatter pipeline before hash finalisation. (`src/main.rs`, `tests/cli_generate_format_flag.rs`, `skills/alef/references/cli-reference.md`)

### Fixed

- **kotlin e2e: stop the Gradle test worker from failing to fork.** The generated `e2e/kotlin/build.gradle.kts` set the test `workingDir` to `../../test_documents` unconditionally and passed no `jvmArgs`. When that directory is absent (consumers without document fixtures), Gradle's test worker could not `chdir` and aborted before JUnit started, surfacing as the misleading `Cannot abort process 'Gradle Test Executor N' because it is not in started or detached state`. The `workingDir` is now guarded on `isDirectory`, and the test fork sets `--enable-preview --enable-native-access=ALL-UNNAMED` to mirror the Panama Maven surefire argLine (the Java FFI bindings are preview-compiled). (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

## [0.19.20] - 2026-05-26

### Fixed

- **alef php template_env: register `php_interface_start.jinja` and `php_interface_method.jinja` in the embedded `TEMPLATES` array.** The v0.19.19 PHP trait-bridge interface emission added the two new Jinja templates on disk and referenced them in `src/backends/php/trait_bridge.rs:960`, but forgot to register them in `src/backends/php/template_env.rs::TEMPLATES`. The template loader uses `include_str!` to embed templates at compile time, so the on-disk files are never read at runtime — only entries in `TEMPLATES` are visible to `make_env()`. As shipped, `alef 0.19.19` panicked on every PHP binding generation with `template php_interface_start.jinja not found`, blocking all consumers from upgrading. The fix adds both templates to the `TEMPLATES` array. (`src/backends/php/template_env.rs`)

## [0.19.19] - 2026-05-26

### Fixed

- **alef python scaffold: bundle the core Rust crate in the maturin sdist.** Generated `packages/python/pyproject.toml` now emits `[tool.maturin] include = [{ path = "../../crates/<core>/**/*", format = "sdist" }]` so the published sdist tarball contains the workspace's core crate alongside the binding crate. Without this, `pip install <package>` on platforms without a precompiled wheel (Alpine/musl, less-common architectures) falls back to building from sdist and fails — maturin strips the binding's `path = "../<core>"` clause from the published manifest, and the workspace `[patch.crates-io]` (when present) is left pointing at a missing path. Surfaced by `html-to-markdown` issue #390 (Alpine/musl source build broken on 3.5.1). (`src/scaffold/languages/python.rs`)

- **alef r-backend: honor [crates.r.exclude_functions] and [crates.r.exclude_types] to prevent incompatible functions from being emitted.** The R backend (extendr) did not respect the `exclude_functions` and `exclude_types` configuration fields that have been supported by other backends (Python, Ruby, etc.) since v0.19.10. This caused kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 R CI job (run 26452244660, job 77885220796) to fail with type-mismatch compilation errors when binding `calculate_quality_score`, which returns `AHashMap<Cow<'_, str>, serde_json::Value>` — a type that cannot be mapped to R's standard types. The function was correctly listed in kreuzberg's `alef.toml` `[crates.exclude_functions]` (task #70) but the R backend had not yet implemented filtering. Fixes: (1) add `exclude_functions: Vec<String>` and `exclude_types: Vec<String>` fields to `RConfig` struct (alongside existing RubyConfig, PythonConfig patterns); (2) integrate exclusion filtering into three code generation paths: Rust-side function emission (in `generate_bindings`), R-wrapper generation (in `gen_extendr_wrappers_r`), and NAMESPACE export (in `gen_namespace`); (3) add snapshot test `test_exclude_functions_honored` verifying functions in the exclusion list are not emitted in Rust, R wrappers, or NAMESPACE. (`src/core/config/languages.rs`, `src/backends/extendr/gen_bindings.rs`, `tests/backends_extendr_gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef dart-e2e-codegen: fix 5 trait-bridge stub generation bugs causing kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI failures.** Dart E2E test stub emitter (emit_test_backend in `src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`) had multiple regressions in kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI (job 77885221080, 23/23 tests failed). Bug 1: internal-only return types like `InternalDocument` were emitted directly instead of being mapped to public types like `ExtractionResult`. Bug 2: `Uint8List` import was not added to generated test files when trait-bridge stubs used byte array parameters. Bug 3: enum return types like `OcrBackendType` and `ProcessingStage` were emitted as constructor calls `OcrBackendType()` instead of with default enum values `OcrBackendType.tesseract`. Bug 4: trait-bridge factory wrapper was marked `await` (async) but registration API expects sync `T` not `Future<T>`. Bug 5: excluded functions referenced in FRB-generated `lib.dart` (e.g., `crateCalculateQualityScore` at line 281) were not being filtered; the filter function was correct but not integrated into the emit path. Fixes: (1) add `map_dart_type_with_fallback()` helper that converts unmapped internal types to `ExtractionResult`; (2) detect test_backend args during import scanning and unconditionally emit `dart:typed_data` import; (3) add `emit_dart_default_for_type()` helper that special-cases enum types to return valid default variants; (4) remove `await` keyword from factory wrapper emission; (5) add snapshot test `filter_excluded_functions_removes_multiline_functions` to verify FRB post-processor filters excluded functions correctly. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`, `src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

- **alef java-facade: wrap trait-bridge wrapper method exceptions in try-catch blocks.** Java facade class (`Kreuzberg.java`) trait-bridge wrapper methods (e.g., `registerOcrBackend`, `unregisterOcrBackend`, `registerPostProcessor`) at lines 484, 488, 492, 528 called into Bridge classes that declare `throws Exception`, but the wrapper methods only declared `throws KreuzbergRsException`. This caused compilation errors `unreported exception java.lang.Exception; must be caught or declared to be thrown` in kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI (java e2e job 77885220931). The fix wraps each Bridge call in a try-catch block that catches `Exception e` and re-throws as `throw new KreuzbergRsException(e.getMessage(), e)` to match the method's declared exception type. This preserves exception context while converting checked exceptions to the expected KreuzbergRsException. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`)

- **alef ruby-e2e-codegen: emit super-trait methods and nonzero dimensions on test stubs.** Generated Ruby test stubs for trait-bridge fixtures (plugin API tests) were missing super-trait methods (`initialize`, `shutdown`, `version`) because the emitter skipped all methods with `has_default_impl = true`. However, the Magnus bridge unconditionally calls these methods on every registered plugin object regardless of whether Rust has a default implementation. This caused 12 test failures in kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI (ruby e2e job 77885220965) with `ArgumentError` on `Kreuzberg.register_*(impl)` calls. Additionally, `EmbeddingBackend.dimensions()` was not returning a value, violating validation that requires `dimensions() > 0`. The fixes: (1) emit `initialize` and `shutdown` as no-op methods (returning `nil`) unconditionally when `super_trait` is set; (2) emit `version = '1.0.0'` to complete the super-trait API; (3) special-case `EmbeddingBackend` to emit `dimensions = 384` if not already present in the method list. Snapshot test updated, new test `test_embedding_backend_dimensions` added. This mirrors analogous fixes for Python (v0.19.14) and PHP (v0.19.14). (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`)

- **alef elixir-e2e-codegen: emit complete plugin trait stubs with super-trait methods and nonzero dimensions.** Generated Elixir test stubs for trait-bridge fixtures (plugin API tests) were missing super-trait methods (`initialize/0`) because the fixture processor only collected the primary trait's methods, not the Plugin super-trait. This caused 6 test failures in kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI when Rustler unconditionally called `call_fn0("initialize")` on registered plugin objects during lifecycle events. Additionally, `EmbeddingBackend.dimensions/0` was returning 0 instead of 1, violating backend validation that requires `dimensions() > 0`. The fixes: (1) extend the fixture processor to also collect super-trait methods when present, preventing duplicates; (2) emit `initialize/0` unconditionally when `super_trait` is set in the trait bridge config; (3) special-case `EmbeddingBackend.dimensions` to return 1 instead of 0. Snapshot test added in `tests/e2e_elixir_plugin_trait_stubs.rs`. This mirrors analogous fixes for Python (v0.19.14), C# (v0.19.13), and Node.js. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **alef e2e c: emit `e2e/c/.gitignore` so locally-built binaries aren't committed.** Caused Linux CI `Exec format error` failures when a developer ran `make` on macOS and the resulting Mach-O `run_tests` binary got checked in (the C backend previously emitted Makefile, download_ffi.sh, test_runner.h, main.c, and per-category `test_*.c` files but no `.gitignore`). The emitted `.gitignore` covers `run_tests`, `*.o`, and the `mock_server.stdin`/`mock_server.stdout` named-pipe artifacts created by fixtures that mock HTTP traffic. Generated without an alef-managed header so the file is treated as developer-owned (matching the kotlin_android `.gitignore` pattern). Snapshot test in `tests/e2e_c_gitignore_emission.rs` locks in emission and required entries. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

- **alef kotlin e2e codegen: gate the `as List<String>` cast on `contains` / `contains_all` / `not_contains` assertions to actual collection fields.** The earlier "cast receiver in collection-contains assertions to fix type inference" change applied the cast unconditionally, so assertions on plain `String` result fields — e.g. `result.text` on the liter-llm `TranscribeTest` — emitted `(result.text as List<String>).contains("…")` and threw `ClassCastException: String cannot be cast to List` at runtime in kotlin_android CI. The fix introduces `field_is_collection` (true when `field_resolver.is_array` or `is_collection_root` is true for the assertion field on either the alias or the resolved name) and only emits the cast when the field really is a `List<String>`; non-collection fields fall through to `field.contains(value)`, which resolves correctly against `kotlin.String.contains(CharSequence)`. Regression tests in `tests/e2e_kotlin_contains_string_field.rs` cover both branches. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

- **alef-swift (trait bridges): add comprehensive test to verify Vec<RendererBox> and sibling trait-box FFI symbols are emitted.** Swift binding compilation was failing with "cannot find `__swift_bridge__$Vec_RendererBox$pop` in scope" errors during kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI, despite the Rust glue code correctly declaring the phantom function. Root cause could not be determined from code inspection (the phantom function declarations and implementations were present and correct), so the fix adds a snapshot test (`swift_trait_bridge_vec_symbols.rs`) that validates: (1) every trait bridge emits the phantom declaration in its extern "Rust" block, (2) every trait bridge emits the phantom implementation at module scope, (3) the phantom is specifically emitted for Renderer to prevent regression. The test passes with current alef main code, confirming the phantom logic is intact. Any future regression in phantom emission will be caught immediately by test failure. (`tests/swift_trait_bridge_vec_symbols.rs`

- **alef-php: emit trait-bridge interface files for all bridges, not just visitor-style ones.** PHP e2e tests declare anonymous classes implementing trait-bridge interfaces (e.g. `new class implements \Kreuzberg\DocumentExtractor { … }`), but the PHP backend only generated interface stubs for visitor-style bridges (those with `type_alias` and no `register_fn`). Registration-style bridges (DocumentExtractor, OcrBackend, PostProcessor, EmbeddingBackend, Renderer, Validator) were missing their interface files, causing runtime `Error: Interface "Kreuzberg\{TraitName}" not found` during test setup. The fix extends interface generation to emit files for all trait bridges: visitor-style via `gen_visitor_interface()` as before, and registration-style via a new `gen_registration_interface()` function that includes all trait methods (including super-trait methods from Plugin). Interface filenames changed from `{TraitName}Interface.php` to `{TraitName}.php` for consistency with Java/C# conventions. This resolves all 6 PHP e2e interface-lookup errors in kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI. (`src/backends/php/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, new templates `php_interface_start.jinja`, `php_interface_method.jinja`)

- **alef zig-e2e-codegen: mark unused parameters as discard in stubs; emit `undefined` for enum/struct defaults instead of invalid `.{}`** Zig e2e test stub emitter (emit_test_backend) was generating functions with unused parameters that did not follow Zig's pattern-based parameter discard syntax (leading `_:`). Additionally, ZigDefaults was emitting `.{}` for all Named types (enums and structs alike), which is invalid syntax for enums that need a discriminant. The fixes: (1) change parameter emission from `name: type` to `_: type` to comply with Zig's unused binding convention; (2) change ZigDefaults::Named from `.{}` to `undefined`, which is valid for both stub enums and structs in test contexts. This resolves 50+ compilation errors in generated `e2e/zig/src/plugin_api_test.zig` from kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`, `src/codegen/defaults.rs`)

- **alef-csharp(trait bridges): Bridge.Register() now returns IntPtr instead of void.** C# e2e test generator emits `var result = RendererBridge.Register(new TestStub_...())` to capture the native handle for plugin assertions. Previously, the generated Register method returned void, causing `CS0815: Cannot assign void to an implicitly-typed variable` compilation errors on Renderer and Validator trait bridges (along with 6 other errors for other trait bridge registrations). The fix changes the return type of the public `Register(ITraitName impl)` registry method from void to IntPtr, returning the userData handle after successful FFI registration. This allows e2e test stubs to capture and use the registration result without compilation failure. (`src/backends/csharp/templates/trait_registry_class.jinja`)

- **alef python-e2e-codegen: emit `shutdown()` stub in plugin test backends and use non-zero defaults for integer return types.** Generated Python test stubs for trait-bridge fixtures (plugin API tests) were missing `def shutdown(self): pass` because the emitter skipped all methods with `has_default_impl = true`. However, the PyO3 bridge unconditionally calls `call_method0("shutdown")` on every registered plugin object during cleanup regardless of whether Rust has a default implementation. At runtime this produced `AttributeError: 'method 'shutdown' failed'`. Additionally, integer-returning methods (like `dimensions()` on EmbeddingBackend) were returning 0, which violated backend validation that requires `dimensions() > 0`. The fixes: (1) emit `shutdown(self): pass` unconditionally alongside `name(self)` and `initialize(self)` whenever `super_trait` is set; (2) special-case numeric types in stub method emission to return 1 for integers and 0.0 for floats instead of delegating to defaults. This mirrors the Node.js fix in v0.19.13. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/mod.rs`)

- Go E2E test stub emitter now collects both main-trait and super-trait (Plugin)
  methods so stubs implement all required interface members. Previously, stubs
  were missing `Initialize`, `Shutdown` and other super-trait lifecycle methods,
  causing compilation failures in generated `plugin_api_test.go`.

- **alef-e2e-rust: prefix auxiliary metadata unwrap bindings with `_` to suppress unused-variable warnings.** The Rust e2e emitter generated `let metadata_output_format = result.metadata.output_format.as_ref().map(|v| v.to_string()).unwrap_or_default();` (and similar bindings for other optional fields) before assertions. When no assertion actually referenced the binding — because the field was not valid on the result type and the renderer emitted a `// skipped:` comment instead — the binding was dead, causing 4 `unused variable` errors under `-D warnings` in kreuzberg v5.0.0-rc.3 CI (run 26452244660, job 77885220855). Fix: `rust_unwrap_binding` now prefixes both the binding name and the returned local-var identifier with `_`, suppressing the warning while keeping the variable fully accessible when an assertion does reference it. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `tests/e2e_codegen_rust_unused_var.rs`)

## [0.19.18] - 2026-05-26

### Fixed

- Napi streaming-adapter codegen no longer emits a redundant `..Default::default()`
  spread when the synthesized request struct has exactly one field. Clippy's
  `needless_update` lint (denied under `-D warnings` in downstream CI) was
  failing on the generated `Js{Type}Request { field: …, ..Default::default() }`
  literals.

## [0.19.17] - 2026-05-26

### Fixed

- Wasm input DTO codegen now filters out fields excluded from bindings
  (`binding_excluded`). Trait-object and other non-marshalable fields were
  leaking into the generated `{TypeName}Input` struct and producing
  uncompilable `serde_json::from_str` calls in `From` impls.

## [0.19.16] - 2026-05-26

### Fixed

- Auto-exclude fields whose type contains a trait object (`dyn Trait`) from generated bindings. Trait objects cannot be marshaled through serde or constructed from binding languages, so emitting them caused compile failures in downstream backends (swift, dart). This applies regardless of any `#[serde(skip)]` annotation.

## [0.19.15] - 2026-05-26

### Fixed

- **alef setup R: route R install command through the package root (`packages/r/`) instead of the extendr Rust source path.** `package_dir(Language::R)` was falling through to the `[crates.output]` first-priority branch and returning the configured Rust source path (e.g. `packages/r/src/rust/src/`). `Rscript -e "remotes::install_deps()"` then ran from the wrong directory and failed with `cannot open the connection ... 'packages/r/src/rust/src/DESCRIPTION' ... probable reason 'No such file or directory'`, because DESCRIPTION lives at the R package root. The fix adds `Language::R` to the source-output-override exclusion list in `package_dir`, alongside Node/Wasm/Elixir/Ruby/Java/Swift/Zig/Dart/Kotlin/KotlinAndroid which all suffer the same root-vs-source-tree mismatch. A regression test (`package_dir_r_ignores_source_output_override`) locks in the behavior. (`src/core/config/resolved/lookups.rs`)

- **alef go e2e test-apps: use `go mod vendor` + `go generate` to populate cgo `.lib/` directories from downloaded FFI tarballs.** Published Go modules with cgo bindings include `.lib/<platform>/libts_pack_core_ffi.{so,dylib,dll}` artifacts which `binding.go` references via `${SRCDIR}/.lib/<platform>/...`. After `go mod download`, the module sits in the read-only Go module cache (`~/go/pkg/mod/...`), and the `go:generate` directive (`//go:generate go run ./cmd/download_ffi`) cannot write into it. The fix chains `go mod tidy && go mod vendor && go generate ./vendor/<module-path>/... && go test -mod=vendor` so `go generate` runs against a writable vendored copy and populates `.lib/` before linking. This matches the Go ecosystem norm for cgo with bundled natives. The change is transparent to consumers who already use `go mod vendor`. (`src/core/config/test_apps_run_defaults.rs`)

- **alef setup Ruby: split `--add-checksums` into a tolerant `bundle lock` step so older bundlers don't fail the whole setup.** Embedding `--add-checksums` in `bundle install` made the entire setup non-recoverable on bundler versions that don't recognise the flag — older bundlers (<2.5.0) and some 2.7+ revisions print `Unknown switches "--add-checksums"` and exit non-zero. The GH-runner-default bundler on Ubuntu 24.04 ARM64 falls into that gap. The fix runs `bundle install` first (always supported), then `bundle lock --add-checksums 2>/dev/null || true` as a separate, tolerant pass that still populates the CHECKSUMS section when supported but no-ops cleanly when not. The bundler-4.x frozen-mode protection is preserved on supported bundlers without breaking anyone else. (`src/core/config/setup_defaults.rs`)

- **alef all: write `.public_api` generation hashes so `alef verify` does not report stale public-api wrappers after a full regen (matches the pattern already used in the per-lang bindings codepath).** (`src/main.rs`)

- **alef e2e/{node,wasm} codegen: mark `pnpm-workspace.yaml` as alef-owned so the cleanup pass can sweep stale Registry-mode emits when consumers flip to Local mode.** The typescript node generator conditionally emits an empty `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (with `packages: []`) only in Registry mode — in Local mode the test app depends on the binding via `workspace:*`, which can only resolve through the consumer's root pnpm workspace, so the empty marker would shadow it. However, the file was emitted with `generated_header: false`, which means the orphan-file cleanup pass (which only deletes files carrying the `alef:hash:` header) skipped it. Consumers who flipped `[crates.e2e.dep_mode]` from Registry → Local kept the stale empty marker on disk, breaking `cd e2e/node && pnpm install` with `ERR_PNPM_WORKSPACE_PKG_NOT_FOUND` because the nested workspace claims no members. Setting `generated_header: true` lets the cleanup pass recognize and remove the file when the codegen stops emitting it. The wasm codegen's `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (which always emits with `packages: - "."`) gets the same treatment for symmetry. YAML's `#` comments are preserved by pnpm's parser. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/mod.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/wasm.rs`)

## [0.19.14] - 2026-05-26

### Fixed

- **alef homebrew e2e codegen: remove unused `CLI_FORMULA_QUALIFIED` variable from `run_tests.sh`.** The generated `run_tests.sh` declared `CLI_FORMULA_QUALIFIED="$TAP/$CLI_FORMULA"` alongside its FFI counterpart, but only `FFI_FORMULA_QUALIFIED` was actually used downstream — CLI invocations use the short `$CLI_FORMULA` since the installed `brew` symlink resolves to it directly. `shellcheck` flagged the unused variable as SC2034, breaking the consumer's pre-commit run. Now only the FFI fully-qualified name is emitted, with the comment clarifying why CLI doesn't need the same. (`src/e2e/codegen/homebrew.rs`)

## [0.19.13] - 2026-05-26

### Fixed

- **alef kotlin-android e2e codegen: infer actual config class name from type_defs for optional json_object args.** When a fixture had no config value for an optional argument like `config`, the emitter inferred the type from the argument name via `arg.name.to_upper_camel_case()`, producing `Config()` instead of `ExtractionConfig()`. The fix checks the `type_defs` passed to the emitter, looking for `ExtractionConfig` or the fully qualified type (e.g., `EmbeddingConfig`) before falling back to simple name derivation. For `EmbeddingConfig`, which has a required `model` parameter, the emitter now provides a sensible default: `EmbeddingConfig(model = EmbeddingModelType.Preset(name = "balanced"))`. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs:1630–1661`)

- **alef kotlin-android e2e codegen: use `.size` instead of `.length` for ByteArray assertions on simple result types.** When a fixture's result type is `ByteArray` (simple result, not a struct), assertions for `min_length` and `max_length` incorrectly emitted `.length` (which is valid for `String` only). Kotlin's `ByteArray.length` is a property, but the test called `.length` as if accessing it on a type without that property, failing with `unresolved reference`. The fix checks whether the result is simple and the field is the raw result; if so, uses `.size` (valid for `ByteArray` collections) instead of `.length`. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs:2227–2246`)

- **alef dart codegen: emit RID-aware loader in `frb_generated.dart` `_alefResolveExternalLibrary`.** The native-library loader now computes the runtime identifier (RID) from `Platform.operatingSystem` and `Platform.version` architecture detection, then checks `lib/src/native/<rid>/` (e.g., `lib/src/native/linux-x64/`) _before_ falling back to the legacy flat `lib/src/<module>_bridge_generated/` path. This allows pub.dev tarballs built with per-platform matrix jobs to ship architecture-specific natives in RID-keyed subdirectories, while maintaining backward compatibility for dev builds and packages without per-RID structure. RID variants supported: `linux-x64`, `linux-arm64`, `macos-x64`, `macos-arm64`, `windows-x64`. (`src/backends/dart/frb_rewrite.rs`)

- **alef binding-helpers: emit `.as_ref()` instead of `.as_deref()` for `Option<HashMap<_,_>>` call arguments.** The `gen_call_args` and `gen_call_args_with_let_bindings` helpers fell through to the `_ =>` catch-all branch for `TypeRef::Map`, which emitted `name.as_deref()` for optional reference parameters. `HashMap` does not implement `Deref`, so the generated code failed with `E0599: the method 'as_deref' ... doesn't satisfy 'Deref'`. The fix adds an explicit `TypeRef::Map(_, _)` arm in both catch-all branches that emits `.as_ref()` (yielding `Option<&HashMap<_,_>>`). (`src/codegen/generators/binding_helpers.rs`)

- **alef dart codegen: emit `()` for unit-variant defaults on freezed sealed-class enums.** `render_enum_variant_default` emitted `EnumName.variant` (no call parens) for any variant whose `fields` list was empty. For `@freezed sealed class` enums (where any variant carries data), every variant — including unit ones — is a `const factory` constructor and requires `()` to be invoked. Without parens the expression is a function tear-off (`OutputFormat Function()`) rather than an `OutputFormat` value, causing a Dart type error. The fix checks whether ALL variants of the enum are unit variants (flat Dart `enum`); only that case omits parens. When any variant has fields (freezed sealed class), all variants emit `variant()`. (`src/backends/dart/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef swift scaffold/generate: include `RustStr` typedef in the `RustBridgeC.h` placeholder.** The committed placeholder `RustBridgeC.h` contained only guard macros and a comment; `SwiftBridgeCore.swift` references `extension RustStr` which requires the type to be in scope. When the C header was empty, the Swift compiler reported "cannot find type 'RustStr' in scope" at every `extension RustStr` block. The scaffold placeholder now includes the canonical `typedef struct RustStr { uint8_t* const start; uintptr_t len; } RustStr;` declaration. The `generate` command also upgrades existing old-form (RustStr-less) placeholders on disk when no swift-bridge build output is present. (`src/scaffold/languages/swift.rs`, `src/backends/swift/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef elixir scaffold: always include `:jason` in mix.exs deps for Elixir bindings.** The mix.exs generator was gating `:jason` dependency on the presence of visitor bridges (trait bridges in rustler mode). However, generated data-class modules (pack_config.ex, code_chunk.ex, process_config.ex, etc.) unconditionally call `Jason.encode!` and `Jason.decode!` for JSON serialization, so `:jason` must always be present when any Elixir binding is generated. The fix removes the trait-bridge gate and unconditionally emits `{:jason, ...}` in the deps list. This resolves compilation failures with `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` reporting "Jason.decode/1 is undefined (module Jason is not available)". (`src/scaffold/languages/elixir.rs`)

- **alef csharp codegen (trait bridges): use per-binding exception class instead of hardcoded `KreuzbergException`.** The C# register/unregister facade methods emitted for trait bridges were hardcoded to throw `KreuzbergException` — a stale reference to the kreuzberg core lib's exception class. Bindings for other crates (e.g. `HtmlToMarkdownRsException` in html-to-markdown) fail to compile with `CS0246: The type or namespace name 'KreuzbergException' could not be found`. The fix threads the per-binding `exception_name` (already in scope in `gen_wrapper_class`) into the two emission sites, matching the rest of the C# backend's exception-name derivation. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **alef go backend (download_ffi): fix arch name mismatch between cgo LDFLAGS directories and release asset URLs.** `cmd_download_ffi_main.go.jinja` was constructing asset URLs using `runtime.GOARCH` directly (e.g. `linux-amd64.tar.gz`, `linux-arm64.tar.gz`), but `alef publish package --lang go` names tarballs with its own platform labels (`linux-x86_64.tar.gz`, `linux-aarch64.tar.gz`). Only macOS arm64 happened to match. The fix maps Go arch names to alef arch names in the download URL only: `amd64→x86_64` everywhere, `arm64→aarch64` on non-Darwin. The local `.lib/<os>-<goarch>/` directory names are unchanged (they match the `#cgo LDFLAGS` in the generated binding). Also removed the unused `regexp` import. (`src/backends/go/templates/cmd_download_ffi_main.go.jinja`)

- **alef kotlin e2e codegen: gate streaming virtual fields on `is_streaming` flag to fix chunk-count assertions in non-streaming fixtures.** The Kotlin codegen intercepted `chunks`-field assertions in both streaming and non-streaming contexts via `is_streaming_virtual_field()`, but the virtual field accessor unconditionally resolved `chunks` as a local variable (populated only in streaming mode), causing unresolved reference errors in non-streaming fixtures. For example, `assertTrue(chunks.size >= 2)` was emitted instead of `assertTrue(result.chunks.size >= 2)` on non-streaming test functions. The fix adds the `is_streaming &&` gate before the streaming virtual field interception, matching the Java codegen; non-streaming fixtures now fall through to the normal field resolver and correctly emit `result.chunks`. Verified: regenerated Kotlin Android test suite compiles with zero unresolved-reference errors. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

- **alef ruby scaffold: add `[package.metadata.cargo-machete] ignored` section to generated Cargo.toml.** `scaffold_ruby_cargo` emitted `ahash`, `tokio`, and `async-trait` as Cargo deps when needed by the generated Magnus wrapper, but did not include the matching `[package.metadata.cargo-machete] ignored = [...]` table. The `cargo-machete` pre-commit hook flagged these entries as unused (they are referenced only transitively through the Rust core, not directly in the binding crate). The fix mirrors the Elixir scaffold pattern: after sorting `[dependencies]`, build a `machete_ignored` list from the same conditions used to add the deps and emit the `[package.metadata.cargo-machete]` section when non-empty. (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **alef java facade codegen: deduplicate trait-bridge wrapper methods already emitted via `api.functions`.** The `gen_facade_class` function emitted static facade methods for every `register_fn`/`unregister_fn`/`clear_fn` in `config.trait_bridges` without checking whether the same function name was already emitted from the `api.functions` loop. When those helpers are also exported as top-level Rust API functions (as in kreuzberg, where `clear_ocr_backends`, `register_renderer`, etc. are part of the public surface), the facade class ended up with two identically-named static methods — e.g. `clearOcrBackends()` at lines 308 and 491 — causing `method clearOcrBackends() is already defined in class dev.kreuzberg.Kreuzberg`. The fix builds a `HashSet<String>` of names already emitted from `api.functions` and skips any trait-bridge wrapper whose Java name collides. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`)

- **alef kotlin e2e codegen: cast receiver in collection-contains assertions to fix type inference.** Collection assertions (contains, contains_all, not_contains) on `List<String>` fields now emit `(receiver as List<String>).contains(...)` instead of `receiver.contains(...)`. Kotlin's `@OnlyInputTypes` annotation on `List.contains()` refuses inference when the type parameter `T` is not fully constrained at the call site, causing compile error "Type inference failed. The value of the type parameter 'T' must be mentioned in input types" on lines with `result.structure.contains("Function")`. The cast fixes type inference for both streaming (result field) and non-streaming (intermediate fields) assertion paths. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

- **alef rust e2e codegen: fix unused-variable warning for `result_is_simple` calls whose field is not in `result_fields`.** When a call sets `result_is_simple = true` but the global `result_fields` set does not contain the fixture's assertion field (e.g. field `"result"` on `detect_mime_type_from_bytes`), the assertion renderer prematurely skipped the assertion with a `// skipped: field 'result' not available on result type` comment, yet still bound the return value to a named `result` variable. Under `-D warnings` this is a hard error. The fix adds `!result_is_simple &&` to the skip guard in `render_assertion`: for `result_is_simple` calls, `field_access` maps every field to `effective_result_var` regardless of name, so the skip must not fire. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/assertions.rs`)

- **alef python-e2e-codegen: emit `initialize()` stub in plugin test backends.** Generated Python test stubs for trait-bridge fixtures (plugin API tests) were missing `def initialize(self): pass` because the emitter skipped all methods with `has_default_impl = true`. However, the PyO3 bridge unconditionally calls `call_method0("initialize")` on every registered plugin object regardless of whether Rust has a default implementation. At runtime this produced `AttributeError: 'method 'initialize' failed'`. The fix emits `initialize(self)` unconditionally alongside `name(self)` whenever `super_trait` is set in the trait bridge config. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/mod.rs`)

- **alef php-e2e-codegen: qualify interface name with binding namespace in anonymous class stubs.** Generated PHP test stubs emitted `new class implements DocumentExtractor` with an unqualified interface name. In a test file under `namespace Kreuzberg\E2e`, PHP resolves this as `Kreuzberg\E2e\DocumentExtractor`, which does not exist. The real interface lives in `\Kreuzberg\DocumentExtractor`. The fix adds a namespace-aware `emit_test_backend_with_ns` variant and calls it from the PHP e2e renderer (which has the binding namespace in scope), emitting `new class implements \Kreuzberg\DocumentExtractor` instead. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef csharp-e2e-codegen: use `Bridge.Register(impl)` instead of `Bridge.RegisterXxx(impl)`.** Generated C# test stubs called `DocumentExtractorBridge.RegisterDocumentExtractor(new ...)` (derived via `reg_fn.to_upper_camel_case()`), but that overload returns `IntPtr` (the low-level FFI handle) and is not the public registration API. The correct high-level factory is `DocumentExtractorBridge.Register(IDocumentExtractor impl)`, which performs the FFI call and discards the handle. Compilation failed with `CS0117: 'DocumentExtractorBridge' does not contain a definition for 'RegisterDocumentExtractor'`. The fix always emits `{Trait}Bridge.Register(new {Stub}())` regardless of the `register_fn` config field. (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`)

- **alef elixir binding emitter: expose `unregister_*` delegates in the public wrapper module.** All trait-bridge function names (register, unregister, clear) were added to `exclude_functions` to prevent duplication with the trait-bridge generator. This was correct for register and clear (which have dedicated bridge handlers), but it accidentally suppressed the unregister delegates, leaving `Kreuzberg.unregister_document_extractor/1` and siblings undefined. E2e tests calling these functions received `undefined or private` warnings.
