The fix adds `CoreWrapper::Box`, teaches `detect_core_wrapper` to return it for `Box<str>` specifically (other Box uses keep their existing classification — opaque trait-objects, regular Box<NamedType>, etc.), and updates `apply_core_wrapper_to_core` / `apply_core_wrapper_from_core` / `binding_helpers::field_default_via_into` to treat `Box` exactly like `Cow` for the String round-trip (both wrappers deref to `&str` and accept `String::into()` / `to_string()`). Composes with the `str → TypeRef::String` fix above so `Box<str>` fields no longer get `sanitized = true` and every backend emits proper accessors + From impls. (`src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/helpers.rs`, `src/codegen/conversions/binding_to_core.rs`, `src/codegen/conversions/core_to_binding.rs`, `src/codegen/generators/binding_helpers.rs`)

- **alef-extract/type_resolver: resolve bare `str` (inside wrapper generics) directly to `TypeRef::String` instead of letting it fall through as `Named("str")` and getting caught by `sanitize_type_ref`.** The standalone `str` form only appears inside wrapper generics like `Box<str>`, `Cow<'_, str>`, `Arc<str>`, `Rc<str>` — those wrappers recurse through `resolve_path_type` on their inner argument (the `&str` reference path doesn't apply). Without an explicit `str` arm the inner resolved to `Named("str")`, which is not in `known_types`, so `sanitize_type_ref` rewrote it to `TypeRef::String` AND set `field.sanitized = true`. Every binding backend then took its "skip sanitized field" branch — for the FFI backend this means `kcrawl_downloaded_document_mime_type` / `..._filename` / `..._content_hash` accessors were never emitted, and downstream e2e codegen had to special-case the field with `"skip"` entries in `[crates.e2e.fields_c_types]` configs. The fix adds `"str" => TypeRef::String` alongside `"String"` in `resolve_path_type`, so wrappers around `str` yield a clean `TypeRef::String` field with `sanitized = false` and every backend emits the accessor normally. (`src/extract/type_resolver.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/zig: suppress SIGABRT from C++ static destructors during zig test --listen=- IPC by installing SIG_IGN on SIGABRT.** The Zig test runner's --listen=- IPC protocol expects a clean process exit without signal interrupts. During global cleanup, leptonica/tesseract C++ destructors call abort(), sending SIGABRT to the process. When unhandled, this breaks the IPC channel and causes the Zig test runner to report "BrokenPipe" / "EndOfStream" errors, even though all tests passed (exit code 0). Installing a signal handler with `signal(SIGABRT, SIG_IGN)` in each test's preamble suppresses the abort signal entirely, allowing the process to exit cleanly after tests complete. The LEAK warnings from ObjectCache destructors remain (harmless noise on process exit), but the signal no longer terminates the process prematurely. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: emit `new EnumName.Variant()` for sealed-interface tagged-enum field defaults, static references for traditional enums.** When a struct field has `#[serde(default)]` and its type is an enum with serde tagging (internally tagged, e.g. `#[serde(tag = "type")]`), Java generates a sealed interface with nested record variants (e.g. `public sealed interface ChunkSizing { record Characters() implements ChunkSizing {} … }`). The Builder class field default was previously emitted as `ChunkSizing.Characters` (static reference), which Java cannot resolve — records must be instantiated with `new`. Meanwhile, traditional enums (e.g. `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]` on `enum KeywordAlgorithm { Yake, Rake }`), which generate as standard Java `enum` types, require static references. The fix introduces `DefaultEnumVariant` metadata (variant name + `is_zero_field`) in the shared `default_value_for_enum` module, and passes a set of sealed-interface enum names (those with `serde_tag`) to the Java backend. At Builder field-default emission, sealed interfaces use `new {EnumName}.{VariantName}()`, and traditional enums use `{EnumName}.{VariantName}`. This unblocks Java compilation for configs like `ChunkingConfig`, `KeywordConfig`, `LayoutDetectionConfig` that have enum fields with defaults. Surfaced as `cannot find symbol: variable ChunkSizing.Characters` (line 115 in `ChunkingConfig.java` Builder) on kreuzberg's Java e2e; fixed all 88 tests pass, all generated sealed-interface defaults are now syntactically valid Java. (`src/extract/default_value_for_enum.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: suppress auto `#[derive(Default)]` and emit a delegating `impl Default` for binding structs whose core type has a custom `Default`.** The derive's primitive defaults clobbered core's semantic defaults via the `From` impl; a new `emit_delegating_default_impl` flag (PHP-only) skips the derive and delegates to the core default. (`src/codegen/generators/mod.rs`, `src/codegen/generators/structs.rs`, `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/extendr/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `tests/backends_php_gen_bindings_test.rs`, `tests/codegen_integration_test.rs`)

- **tests: realign go / pyo3 / zig / swift / php-rename tests with the new codegen contracts after recent fixes, and supply `options_type = "CrawlConfig"` to the validation-error fixture so it survives the special-casing removal.** `test_default_config` now asserts the post-STY-9 plain-DTO + `Ptr[T]` default (no `With*`/`New*` helpers without an opt-in allowlist). `test_cfg_gated_fields_excluded_from_constructor` uses a non-feature, non-wasm `cfg` predicate (`any(unix, windows)`) so the test still pins the exclusion contract under the broadened `cfg_present_for_pyo3` (feature gates are now treated as present in pyo3). `streaming_adapter_emits_iterator_pattern_on_opaque_handle` matches the canonical `c.{prefix}_last_error_code() != 0` + `std.json.parseFromSliceLeaky` shape that replaced the undefined `_has_error()` helper. `e2e_swift_client_factory::with_client_factory_emits_client_instantiation` expects `AlefE2EMockServer.baseURL`, the lazy mock-server accessor that replaced the bare `ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment["MOCK_SERVER_URL"]!` lookup. `php_preserves_snake_case_when_serde_rename_all_absent` is rewritten as `php_camel_cases_keys_when_core_type_lacks_rename_all`, pinning the new contract: PHP key renaming sources from the BINDING's `lang_rename_all` (camelCase by default), not the core IR's `serde_rename_all`. (`tests/backends_go_gen_bindings_test.rs`, `tests/backends_pyo3_gen_bindings_test.rs`, `tests/backends_zig_gen_bindings_test.rs`, `tests/e2e_swift_client_factory.rs`, `tests/e2e_php_serde_rename_all_respects_core_config.rs`, `tests/e2e_validation_error_wrapping.rs`)

- **doc tests: fix import paths in `core::version` and `core::jni` rustdoc examples after the workspace-collapse refactor renamed the import root from `crate` to `alef`.** Seven doc tests (`to_r_version`, `to_rubygems_prerelease`, `bridge_class_name`, `bridge_method_name`, `streaming_method_names`, `destructor_method_name`, `jni_symbol`) carried `use crate::core::…` / `crate::core::jni::…` references that no longer compile from a doc-test crate (the doc-test harness reads the crate by its published name `alef`, not `crate`). Rewrites each example to use `alef::core::…`. (`src/core/version.rs`, `src/core/jni.rs`)

- **kotlin-android snapshot: accept the new vanniktech-0.36 `build.gradle.kts` shape (no `SonatypeHost` import; sources jar uses `SourcesJar.Sources()`; `publishToMavenCentral()` is a no-arg call).** Updates the snapshot and the two assertion-based regressions (`build_gradle_uses_vanniktech_maven_publish_plugin`, `buildscript_comes_after_imports_in_correct_order`) so they pin the post-upgrade emission instead of the pre-0.36 SonatypeHost-based form. (`tests/backends_kotlin_android_snapshot_test.rs`, `tests/snapshots/backends_kotlin_android_snapshot_test__snapshot_basic__packages__kotlin_android__build.gradle.kts.snap`)

- **alef-e2e/java: resolve per-fixture mock-server URLs from `System.getProperty("mockServer.<fixture_id>")` first, then env vars.** The generated `mock_url_list` setup line read only `System.getenv("MOCK_SERVER_<FIXTURE_ID>")` then fell back to `MOCK_SERVER_URL/fixtures/<id>`. For fixtures with per-fixture host-root routes (e.g. `batch_crawl_basic`), `MockServerListener` parses the mock-server's `MOCK_SERVERS=` JSON announcement and exposes each fixture URL as `System.setProperty("mockServer.<fixture_id>", ...)` — a system property, not an environment variable. Java tests therefore hit the WRONG URL pattern (`/fixtures/<id>/<path>` on the shared host, which mock-server doesn't serve) and got empty `BatchCrawlResults` for every batch test. Surfaced as 4 BatchCrawl* failures on kreuzcrawl's Java e2e; fixed all 4. The fix prepends a `System.getProperty("mockServer.<fixture_id>", ...)` lookup matching the same resolution chain that single-URL `mock_url` already uses. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: emit `MAPPER.writerFor(constructCollectionType(...))` for `Vec<T>` / `Map<K, V>` marshaling so Jackson preserves the generic element type and re-applies `@JsonTypeInfo` polymorphic discriminators.** The previous `MAPPER.writeValueAsString(list)` lost T at runtime (Java type erasure: at the call site `actions` is just `ArrayList`, with no recoverable element-type info). For polymorphic element types like the tagged-enum DTO `PageAction` (annotated `@JsonTypeInfo(use = NAME, property = "type", visible = false)`), the result was `[{"selector":"#x"}]` — no `"type":"click"` discriminator — and the Rust side failed deserialization with `missing field "type"`. The fix introduces `build_collection_writer_for(...)` + `java_class_literal_for(...)` helpers in `gen_bindings/marshal.rs` and threads the resolved Java `.class` literal into the template as a `java_writer` context variable; the template now emits `var xJson = MAPPER.writerFor(MAPPER.getTypeFactory().constructCollectionType(java.util.List.class, ElemType.class)).writeValueAsString(x)`. Surfaced as 11 InteractionTest errors on kreuzcrawl's Java e2e (`Vec<PageAction>` argument); fixed all 11. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/marshal.rs`, `src/backends/java/templates/marshal_vec_map.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: alias serde-renamed dict keys in `_to_rust_*_config` converters and use serde-renamed param names in pyo3 calls.** Dicts held serde wire keys the dataclass rejected; an aliasing block maps them back to Rust field names, and `field_kwarg.jinja` uses `field.serde_rename` for the constructor call. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

## [0.18.1] - 2026-05-23

### Added

- **alef extract: serde-default detection** — `#[serde(default)]` and `#[serde(default = "...")]` fields are recognized as having defaults, propagating nullability into generated builders. (`src/extract/`)
- **alef wasm: apply if-let wrapping to all optionalized fields**, not just `Duration`. (`src/codegen/`)
- **alef java: sealed-interface tagged enums** — field defaults emit `new EnumName.Variant()`, and `#[serde(default)]` fields are treated as nullable in builder classes. (`src/backends/java/`)

### Fixed

- **alef codegen: preserve core `Default` in duration-builder `binding_to_core`**, delegate to core `Default` for `has_default` structs, and add `CoreWrapper::Box` for `Box<str>` round-trips.
- **alef extract: resolve bare `str` to `TypeRef::String`** without a spurious sanitized flag.
- **alef pyo3: guard optional data-enum fields in return statements** and alias serde-renamed dict keys before Python-class coercion.
- **alef java: use `writerFor(constructCollectionType)` for `Vec<T>`/`Map<K,V>` marshaling**; align the maven-javadoc-plugin sourcepath with sourceDirectory.
- **alef php: use `CorePath::default()` in the flat-enum `From` wildcard arm.**
- **alef scaffold/cli: prek idempotency** — `.gitkeep`, Kotlin `.kt`, Python pyproject arrays, Ruby string quotes, and markdown blank-line capping emit in canonical form so hooks are no-ops on fresh output.
- **e2e codegen fixes across dart, zig, java, php** — Dart connection retries and non-persistent connections, Zig 0.16 std.Io migration plus SIGABRT suppression, Java per-fixture mock URLs, PHP streaming-adapter and enum-accessor handling.

## [0.18.0] - 2026-05-23

### Changed

- **BREAKING (packaging)**: collapsed the 30-crate workspace into a single root-flat `alef` crate. The binary, CLI surface, generated outputs, and template versions are unchanged. The published crate is renamed from `alef-cli` to `alef` — `cargo install alef-cli` is yanked; install with `cargo install alef --locked`. The 28 internal library crates (`alef-core`, `alef-codegen`, `alef-adapters`, `alef-extract`, `alef-docs`, `alef-e2e`, `alef-readme`, `alef-scaffold`, `alef-snippets`, `alef-publish`, and all 18 `alef-backend-*`) are yanked from crates.io.

### Removed

- All `alef-*` workspace member crates. No external consumer of any internal alef library API was supported; they were never intended as a public Rust API surface, only as internal modularity.

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/swift: spawn the alef mock-server in-process via `AlefE2EMockServer` and route every `_baseURL` reference through it.** SwiftPM has no before-all hook, so generated boilerplate now emits a lazy `AlefE2EMockServer.baseURL` that spawns the mock-server and feeds all URL sites, plus a no-redirect URLSession for 3xx-asserting fixtures. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/kotlin: wrap UUID header-pattern assertions in `Regex(...)` and read the mock-server URL from the `mockServerUrl` system property.** `matches` needs a `Regex` not a `String`, and the URL must come from the `mockServerUrl` system property (set by `MockServerListener`) with env/localhost fallbacks. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/swift: sanitize header-derived local-variable names and stop redeclaring `_bodyData` across body assertions.** Header names now map to a safe `var_suffix` (non-alphanumerics → `_`), and response data is unwrapped inline with distinct names to avoid `_bodyData` redeclaration. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: skip trait-bridge wrapper emission for clear_/register_/unregister_ names already emitted as regular api.functions.** Emitting both produced ruff F811 redefinitions; the generator now tracks emitted names and skips duplicate trait-bridge wrappers, keeping the richer Rust-sourced version. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: wrap each tagged-enum / has_default DTO element in `{ElemType}::from_json('{...}')` instead of raw assoc arrays for `Vec<T>` args.** PHP's `FromZval` only accepts class instances, so element arrays failed conversion; codegen now emits per-element `from_json(...)` calls. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/field_access: `namespace_stripped_path` returns `None` when `[e2e].result_fields` is empty instead of stripping the first dotted segment.** Unconditional stripping collapsed real nested paths like `metrics.total_lines`, breaking C accessor codegen; stripping now only applies when `result_fields` is declared. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: include cfg-gated fields in struct constructors via `cfg_present_for_pyo3()`.** PyO3 never compiles to WASM, so feature gates are deterministic; the new predicate accepts feature gates (and `any(...)`) so fields like `pdf_options` appear in constructors. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: emit `[package.metadata.cargo-machete] ignored = [...]` as a multi-line array so `cargo-sort -w` does not rewrite the manifest on every regen.** The wasm Cargo.toml template inlined the six entries on a single line (`ignored = ["futures", "futures-util", "js-sys", "wasm-bindgen-futures", "serde", "serde_json"]`) which exceeded `cargo-sort`'s 100-char line budget; on the first prek pass `cargo-sort` reformatted the file, the `alef:hash:` line on the same file then mismatched its post-format content, and `alef verify` rejected the next commit with "Stale bindings detected". The template now emits the canonical sorted multi-line form (four-space indent, trailing comma) — matching `cargo-sort`'s own output so the formatter is a no-op. Mirrors the swift backend's existing multi-line `features` rule. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-zig: emit `std.json.parseFromSliceLeaky(...)` inline in the streaming iterator's `next()` body instead of the never-defined `parse{item_type}FromJson` helper.** The undefined helper broke compilation; inlining the leaky parse with the c_allocator also outlives the freed FFI JSON buffer. (`src/backends/zig/gen_bindings/opaque_handles.rs`)

- **alef-codegen: remove project-specific hardcoded type-name fallbacks across PHP/Python e2e and napi/go/php streaming adapters.** Six sites baked kreuzcrawl defaults (`CrawlConfig`, `EngineHandle`, etc.) into shared codegen; each `.unwrap_or("…")` is now `unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(…))` naming the missing config field. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/test_file.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/test_function.rs`, `src/backends/napi/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/go/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: source the JSON key rename strategy from the PHP language config (`serde_rename_all_for_language(Language::Php)`) rather than the Rust core type's `serde_rename_all`, because PHP `from_json` deserializes into the BINDING struct (not the core type), and the PHP backend emits every binding struct with the language-effective `#[serde(rename_all = "...")]` regardless of what the core source carries.** The previous BLK-7 attempt looked up `td.serde_rename_all` per type from the IR — which reflects the CORE struct's attribute — but `impl ConversionOptions { fn from_json(json) -> PhpResult<Self> { serde_json::from_str(&json) } }` is parsed against `Self`, the binding type. For h2m, the core `ConversionOptions` has no `rename_all`, but the PHP-emitted `ConversionOptions` carries `#[serde(default, rename_all = "camelCase")]` (added by `gen_struct` in `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/types.rs` from `lang_rename_all`). The fixture generator therefore left snake_case keys in the encoded JSON, every camelCase property silently fell to its `#[serde(default)]` value, and 32 / 7-error PHP e2e failures appeared across `include_document_structure`, `strip_tags`, `exclude_selectors`, etc. The fix threads `php_lang_rename_all: String` from `generate()` through `render_test_file` → `render_test_method` → `build_args_and_setup`, and the three `json_to_php_camel_keys_with_types` callers now pass `Some(php_lang_rename_all)` instead of `get_serde_rename_all(<type>, type_defs)`. The recursion case in `json_to_php_camel_keys_with_types` propagates the parent's `serde_rename_all` into nested objects (since alef PHP applies the same `lang_rename_all` to every binding struct uniformly). Removes the dead `get_serde_rename_all` and `json_to_php_camel_keys` wrappers. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef-backend/php: add `#[serde(default)]` to flat-struct representation of tagged enums so from_json() succeeds on empty/partial JSON.** Flat-struct tagged enums (e.g., `EmbeddingModelType` with a `type_tag: String` discriminator + optional payload fields) lacked struct-level `#[serde(default)]`, causing deserialization from `{}` to fill all fields with their Rust defaults: `type_tag` → empty string, payload fields → None. When the empty `type_tag` was converted back to the Rust core enum, the fallback arm produced `Preset { name: Default::default() }` (also empty), triggering "Unknown embedding preset: <empty>" at runtime. The template now includes `#[serde(default)]` so missing fields use the struct's `Default` impl (all fields empty), and the round-trip behaves correctly through the `From` impl's wildcard arm. (`src/backends/php/templates/php_flat_enum_struct_start.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: coerce Default expression to binding field type when JSON field is mapped to String.** The Magnus type mapper converts `TypeRef::Json` to `String` (since Magnus lacks a native `serde_json::Value` type), but the explicit Default impl generator was still emitting `serde_json::json!({})` for the original Rust type. This produced a type mismatch: the struct field is `schema: String` but the Default expression was `serde_json::json!({})` (a `Value`). The fix detects when the original field type is `TypeRef::Json` but the binding type is `String`, and substitutes `String::new()` for the default expression. This ensures the Default impl's field assignments match the struct's declared field types. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/classes.rs`)

- **alef-cli/all: repopulate `current_gen_paths` from the e2e cache manifest on a cache hit so orphan-cleanup does not delete generated e2e files.** On a cache skip the e2e paths were missing and cleanup deleted them; new `cache::read_stage_paths` reinserts them. (`src/cli/cache.rs`, `src/main.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: fix `build.gradle.kts` template for vanniktech gradle-maven-publish-plugin 0.36.0 by removing unused `SonatypeHost` import, changing `SourcesJar.Software()` to `SourcesJar.Sources()`, and removing the `SonatypeHost.CENTRAL_PORTAL` argument from `publishToMavenCentral()`.** Vanniktech 0.36.0 removed the `SonatypeHost` enum, renamed the sources-jar variant from `Software` to `Sources`, and made `publishToMavenCentral()` a no-arg function with Central Portal as the default destination. Updated `src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_build_gradle.rs` to match the current plugin API. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_build_gradle.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: emit `java.util.stream.Stream<T>` (not `Iterable<T>`) for the facade's static streaming wrappers.** `Stream<T>` does not implement `Iterable<T>`, so the wrapper return type now matches the instance method's `Stream<T>`. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: emit the `StreamError` exception module at top level after the outer `defmodule` closes, not inside it.** Nesting it doubled the module namespace, making every `Native.X` call undefined; it now emits after the outer module with the indent stripped. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-zig: replace undefined `_has_error()` in the streaming `next()` body with the canonical `c.{prefix}_last_error_code() != 0` form.** The never-emitted helper broke compilation; the check now uses the prefixed form used everywhere else. (`src/backends/zig/gen_bindings/opaque_handles.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: wrap the generated `base_url:` attribute in `use RustlerPrecompiled` onto its own line so the emitted `lib/<app>/native.ex` is idempotent under `mix format --check-formatted`.** The `use RustlerPrecompiled` block emitted the GitHub releases URL inline, e.g. `base_url: "https://github.com/kreuzberg-dev/liter-llm/releases/download/v#{Mix.Project.config()[:version]}"`. For most consumer repos (kreuzberg, liter-llm), that single line exceeds Elixir's default 98-column line length and `mix format` rewrites it to a wrapped form (`base_url:\n      "..."`). The shared prek `mix format` hook then flagged the generated file on every fresh regen, churning the polyrepo prek run. The template now emits the wrapped form unconditionally so the file is already in the canonical shape `mix format` would produce. Added regression assertions in `tests/backends_rustler_gen_public_api_test.rs::test_native_ex_has_all_nif_stubs` pinning both the wrap shape and a length sanity-check on the continuation line. Surfaced regenerating liter-llm against alef HEAD. (`src/backends/rustler/template_env.rs`, `tests/backends_rustler_gen_public_api_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: skip the trait-bridge options field from `sorted_fields` so the constructor does not emit it twice.** The bridge field appeared in both `params` and `bridge_param`, duplicating the `visitor` parameter; filtering it out of `sorted_fields` contributes it exactly once. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-readme: add `test_alef_all_and_cold_readme_produce_same_output` regression covering the previously-observed divergence between `alef all` and a cold `alef readme` run.** The investigation (`src/readme/mod.rs`) confirmed that both commands now route through the same `crate::readme::generate_readmes()` entry point with fresh minijinja `Environment` instances per render (no shared mutable state, no cached template branches), so the divergence reported during the h2m v0.17.34 regen is no longer reproducible against the post-restructure HEAD. The new test creates a tmp dir with a minijinja template, calls `generate_readmes` twice in succession, and asserts byte-identical output — guarding against regressions if a future change reintroduces shared state. (`src/readme/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-dart: migrate product-type DTO emission to `@freezed` + `json_serializable`, matching the sum-type path.** Product DTOs were plain classes lacking `copyWith`/equality/JSON; they now use the freezed shape with the required part-of directives and scaffold dev-deps. (`src/backends/dart/gen_ffi/types.rs`, `src/backends/dart/gen_ffi/mod.rs`, `src/scaffold/languages/dart.rs`, `src/backends/dart/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/dart/templates/freezed_*.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-go: stop emitting per-field `With*` functional-options for every struct by default; emit a plain struct plus a shared `Ptr[T]` helper, gated behind `[crates.go.functional_options]`.** The old default produced hundreds of `With*` helpers per DTO; the opt-in allowlist restores them only for named structs. (`src/core/config/languages.rs`, `src/backends/go/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/go/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/go/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/go/templates/ptr_helper.jinja`)

- **alef-core/config: add `[crates.{node,wasm}.crate_dir]` per-language override for `package_dir()` so consumers whose actual Rust crate directory does not follow the `crates/{name}-{lang}` formula can point alef at the real location.** `package_dir(Language::{Node,Wasm})` hardcoded `format!("crates/{}-{lang}", self.name)`, where `self.name` is the alef config root `name` (e.g., `"html-to-markdown-rs"` for h2m). For polyglot consumers that _strip_ the `-rs` suffix from their Node/Wasm Rust crates (h2m keeps `-rs` only on `-ffi`/`-jni`, while Node lives at `crates/html-to-markdown-node` and Wasm at `crates/html-to-markdown-wasm`), every setup/test/clean/format task targeting Node/Wasm referenced a non-existent path and the polyrepo had to maintain per-package Taskfile shims to compensate. The new optional `crate_dir: Option<String>` field on `NodeConfig` and `WasmConfig` overrides the formula when set; default = `None` (preserves current behavior). Added five regression tests covering both overrides, default-fallback, and the h2m end-to-end scenario. (`src/core/config/languages.rs`, `src/core/config/resolved/lookups.rs`)

- **alef-docs: demote internal headings inside spliced Rust doc-comments relative to the splice level, so generated `docs/reference/types.md` / `configuration.md` / `errors.md` / function pages satisfy `rumdl MD001` (heading increments by ≤1).** The docs generator emits fixed structural levels (`##` page, `###` section, `####` per-item) but item bodies splice in Rust doc-comment markdown that contains its own headings (commonly `## Stream Limits`, `## Examples`); the resulting hierarchy skipped levels (e.g., `####` followed by `##`), violating MD001 and triggering `rumdl` to rewrite the docs on every prek pass. Five splice sites in `src/docs/mod.rs` now wrap doc-comment content through a new `demote_headings(doc, shift)` helper before emission: `render_function`, `render_enum_item`, `render_error_item` (shift = 2, splice level `####`), `generate_configuration_doc` and `generate_errors_doc` (shift = 1, splice level `###`), and `generate_types_doc` (shift = 2, splice level `####`). A companion `check_monotonic_headings` validator was added to `src/docs/doc_cleaning.rs`. Added `test_generated_docs_have_monotonic_heading_increments` and `test_function_doc_with_internal_headings_are_demoted` regression tests covering both the demotion logic and the end-to-end emission. (`src/docs/mod.rs`, `src/docs/doc_cleaning.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/python: emit `license-files = ["LICENSE"]` explicitly in the generated `[project]` table so maturin/PEP 639 wheel validation finds and bundles the license file relative to the pyproject directory.** Previously the scaffold emitted `license = "MIT"` (PEP 639 SPDX string) without `license-files`, and `[tool.maturin]` pointed `manifest-path` at an out-of-tree Cargo crate (`../../crates/<name>-py/Cargo.toml`). Maturin resolved license discovery from the Cargo manifest dir but assembled the wheel from the pyproject dir — so the wheel METADATA carried `License-File: LICENSE` while the wheel itself didn't bundle the file, and PyPI's upload validator rejected with `400 License-File LICENSE does not exist in distribution file` (liter-llm v1.4.0-rc.29 publish failure, run `26329709088`). The new explicit `license-files` field combined with the existing `scaffold_license_files` step (which already copies the workspace-root `LICENSE` into `packages/python/LICENSE` when the language is enabled) ensures both halves of the build agree on which directory the file lives in. Added regression test `test_scaffold_python_license_files_field` asserting the field is emitted in the `[project]` section. (`src/scaffold/languages/python.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: thread per-type `serde_rename_all` IR metadata into `json_to_php_camel_keys` so generated PHP test calls only camelCase JSON keys when the receiving struct actually carries `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]`.** `json_to_php_camel_keys` previously applied `to_lower_camel_case()` to every object key it saw, on the false premise that "PHP binding structs use `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]`". In reality, PHP `from_json` uses `serde_json::from_str` against the kreuzberg core type directly, so the effective serde attributes are whatever the _core_ struct carries — and structs like `PageConfig` (and several other kreuzberg core configs: `concurrency`, `content_filter`, `email`, `llm`, `merge`, `ocr`, `pdf`) lack `rename_all = "camelCase"`. The result was that camelCased keys silently dropped to `#[serde(default)]` values and `ContractTest::test_config_pages` failed with `Failed asserting that false is true.` because the `pages.extract_pages` config silently fell back to its default. The function signature now accepts `serde_rename_all: Option<&str>` and only applies camelCase when the receiving struct's setting equals `Some("camelCase")`; otherwise snake_case keys pass through unchanged. A new `get_serde_rename_all` helper looks up the per-type setting from the IR `type_defs`; `build_args_and_setup` and all three call sites (L1452, L1596, L1626) now consult it. Added regression test `e2e_php_serde_rename_all_respects_core_config.rs` covering both branches. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef-extract + alef-backend-{php,java}: introduce a shared `default_value_for_enum` IR helper so PHP `From` impls and Java Builder defaults use the enum's actual `impl Default`/`#[default]` variant instead of the first variant.** Both backends ignored the IR default-variant metadata, zero-valuing the first variant; they now consult the shared helper. (`src/extract/default_value_for_enum.rs`, `src/extract/mod.rs`, `src/backends/php/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: emit `Option<NestedType>` (default `None`) with `unwrap_or_else(|| Self::default().field)` for non-optional `has_default` nested-struct fields.** Explicit `field=None` was rejected by PyO3's extractor; the param is now Optional and falls back to the parent default. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: raise a typed `<Crate>.StreamError` exception from inside the `Stream.unfold` closure on mid-stream NIF errors instead of returning `nil`, so consumers see real failures instead of silent truncation at end-of-stream.** The previous wrapper short-circuited `{:error, reason}` to `nil` — the EOS sentinel for `Stream.unfold`, which `Enum.to_list/1` interprets as a clean completion. Mid-stream FFI cancels, upstream HTTP failures, and deserialization errors were therefore invisible to the consumer; callers could not distinguish "stream finished normally" from "stream ran for a while then crashed." The generator now emits a `defmodule <App>.StreamError do defexception [:message, :reason, :adapter] ... end` block once per generated `.ex` file (guarded on `!streaming_adapters.is_empty()`), and the unfold closure raises it with the captured reason and adapter atom. Consumers can `try`/`rescue` the typed exception or let it propagate. Added three regression tests verifying the exception module is emitted, the closure raises with the correct fields, and no exception module is emitted when the crate has no streaming adapters. (`src/backends/rustler/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/templates/elixir_streaming_unfold_wrapper.jinja`, `src/backends/rustler/templates/elixir_stream_error_exception.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-zig: emit a per-adapter streaming iterator struct with `next()` / `deinit()` methods instead of an inline drain loop that eagerly collects every chunk into a JSON-array `[]u8`.** Generated Zig streaming wrappers previously drained the underlying FFI iterator (`<crate>_<name>_stream_start` → `_next` → `_free`) inside the public function body, returning a single `[]u8` containing the bundled JSON array. Callers could not apply backpressure, inspect partial results, cancel mid-stream, or distinguish clean end-of-stream from a runtime error — and paid the full memory cost of every chunk before seeing any of them. The new shape emits `pub const <Item>Stream = struct { _handle: ..., pub fn next(...) (...||error{OutOfMemory})!?<Item>, pub fn deinit(...) void };`, where `next()` returns `null` on clean EOS (errno == 0) and an error union on mid-stream failure (errno != 0). The public streaming function now returns the struct; callers iterate it with `while (try stream.next()) |chunk| { ... }` and `defer stream.deinit();`. Mirrors the `AsyncThrowingStream` pattern emitted by the swift backend. Updated the existing `streaming_adapter_emits_iterator_pattern_on_opaque_handle` test and added a new `streaming_struct_has_next_and_deinit_methods` regression test. (`src/backends/zig/gen_bindings/opaque_handles.rs`)

- **alef-cli/version: refresh per-ecosystem lockfiles after `sync-versions` rewrites manifests so frozen-lockfile CI installs don't abort.** Manifests were bumped but lockfiles left stale; the fix tracks touched ecosystems and runs pnpm/cargo/composer/mix lockfile updates via a `run_optional` helper that no-ops when the binary is absent. (`src/cli/pipeline/helpers.rs`, `src/cli/pipeline/version.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit a multi-line `features = [...]` array in the generated Rust-bridge `Cargo.toml` when the features list has ≥3 entries OR the rendered dep line would exceed 100 chars, so `taplo format` / `cargo-sort -w` do not rewrite it on the first prek pass.** `gen_cargo_toml` previously joined source-crate features with `", "` inline and spliced them into `features = [{list}]` on the same line as the dep declaration, with no line-length budget. Consumers like tree-sitter-language-pack (5 features on one ~150-char line) saw `cargo-sort -w` reformat the manifest to a multi-line block on every regen, producing churn that broke `git diff --exit-code` freshness checks. The emitter now switches to a sorted multi-line format under either threshold; single-line stays for ≤2 features when the line fits. (`src/backends/swift/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: stop emitting `import java.lang.Iterable;` in generated facades — `java.lang.*` is JLS-auto-imported and checkstyle rejects it.** The import branch is removed and a new `JAVA_LANG_AUTO_IMPORTED` allowlist guards future emissions. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`, `src/backends/java/templates/facade_file.jinja`)

- **alef-scaffold/python: emit `pyproject.toml` array literals in `pyproject-fmt` style (`["x"]`) instead of the previous inner-spaced shape (`[ "x" ]`) so the first `prek run` after a fresh regen does not rewrite the file.** The scaffold's `format!` templates at `src/scaffold/languages/python.rs` (~L147-L249 — `authors`, `keywords`, `dependencies`, `requires`, `dev`, `features`, `python-packages`, `lint.select`, `lint.per-file-ignores`, `disable_error_code`) all emitted inner-bracket spaces and multi-space comment alignment. `pyproject-fmt` (via `kreuzberg-dev/pre-commit-hooks`) canonicalizes both to a single space, which previously caused first-regen churn and broke `git diff --exit-code` freshness gates on consumers' `Validate scaffolds` CI jobs. All array literals now emit `[{}]` (no inner spaces); test assertions in `src/scaffold/tests.rs` updated to match. (`src/scaffold/languages/python.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: emit `build.gradle.kts` with sorted `import` statements before the `buildscript {}` block so it parses and ktlint doesn't rewrite it.** Kotlin forbids elements before imports; the emit order is now header → sorted imports → buildscript → plugins. (`src/backends/kotlin_android/gen_build_gradle.rs`, `tests/snapshots/backends_kotlin_android_snapshot_test__snapshot_basic__packages__kotlin_android__build.gradle.kts.snap`)

- **alef-scaffold/elixir: probe the resolved `explicit_output.elixir` directory at generate time and only append `{relative}/*.ex` to the Hex `files:` list when the directory actually contains `.ex` or `.exs` sources.** When a consumer sets `[crates.elixir.explicit_output]` to a Rust NIF source directory (which contains only `lib.rs` and `Cargo.toml`), the scaffolded `mix.exs` previously contained `files: ~w(... <relative>/*.ex)` and `mix hex.publish` aborted with `** (Mix) Stopping package build due to errors. Missing files: <path>/*.ex` because Hex requires every glob in `files:` to match at least one on-disk file. The probe now uses `std::fs::read_dir` to inspect the resolved directory: if it exists and contains no Elixir sources, the glob is omitted; if it doesn't exist (generated binding target), the glob is still emitted so consumer expectations are preserved. Surfaced publishing liter-llm v1.4.0-rc.29 (Hex publish failure in run `26329709088`). (`src/scaffold/languages/elixir.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/ffi: emit `version =` alongside `path =` on intra-workspace path-dependencies so `cargo publish` accepts FFI crate manifests.** Path-only deps failed publish verification; all three `render_core_dep` emission sites now thread `api.version`. (`src/scaffold/languages/ffi.rs`)

- **alef-e2e: make generated test output deterministic by storing fixture headers, query params, cookies, route params, and visitor callbacks in `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`.** Randomized HashMap order produced spurious regen diffs; BTreeMap gives stable sorted output with no new dependency. (`src/e2e/fixture.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/client/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: thread `never_skip_cfg_field_names` through the constructor rewriter so cfg-gated config fields can be passed as kwargs from the Python facade.** The PyO3 backend already populates `never_skip_cfg_field_names` with the union of trait-bridge option fields and every cfg-gated field on `has_default` types (so that consumers building with all features expose the full kwarg surface). But `replace_constructor_with_serde_rename` ignored that list and filtered with a hard `f.cfg.is_none()` predicate when emitting the `#[pyo3(signature = (...))]` and the corresponding params/assignments — so pdf_options, keywords, html_options, html_output, layout, and tree_sitter were silently dropped from `ExtractionConfig::new`. The hand-written `packages/python/kreuzberg/api.py::_to_rust_extraction_config` then crashed with `TypeError: ExtractionConfig.__new__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'pdf_options'` on 22 kreuzberg Python e2e tests. The rewriter now accepts a `never_skip_cfg_field_names: &[String]` parameter and the call site forwards `type_cfg.never_skip_cfg_field_names`; cfg-gated fields in that list are treated as regular fields (parameter, default, and assignment), while cfg-gated fields not in the list keep the previous `Default::default()` literal assignment. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/java: coerce nullable-field accessors to `String` via `Optional.map(Objects::toString).orElse("")` so `.contains(...)` assertions compile on `@Nullable Object` fields.** Rust fields typed `Option<serde_json::Value>` (free-form JSON payloads — e.g. `ActionResult.data` in kreuzcrawl carrying screenshot bytes / JS return values / scraped HTML) map to `@Nullable Object` in the generated Java binding, which is correct. The Java e2e generator previously emitted `Optional.ofNullable(x).orElse("")` as the accessor for any nullable string-like field — javac infers the return type as `LUB(Object, String) = Object`, so a downstream `.contains("…")` failed with `cannot find symbol method contains(String) location: class java.lang.Object` (seen on `InteractionTest.java:52` in kreuzcrawl). Switching to `.map(Objects::toString).orElse("")` collapses `Optional<T>` to `Optional<String>` before the fallback, making the expression unambiguously a `String` for any `T`. Numeric (`Number::longValue`/`0L`) and array (`List.of()`) paths are unchanged. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **ci(publish): downgrade `actions/checkout` from `@v6.0.2` to `@v4` in the alef Publish workflow.** v6.0.2 fails on the self-hosted `runner-medium-arm64` runner used for the `Build CLI (linux-aarch64)` job — `actions/checkout` cannot read GitHub credentials after `git init` (`fatal: could not read Username for 'https://github.com': terminal prompts disabled`), even though `extraheader` is configured. The other matrix targets (`linux-x86_64` on GitHub-hosted, `macos-arm64` on `macos-latest`) work with v6 because their runner images cooperate with v6's credential helper. v4 is the LTS-grade baseline that handles the self-hosted aarch64 environment correctly. Surfaced during the alef v0.17.35 release run. (`.github/workflows/publish.yaml`)

- **alef-backend-java: initialize non-optional enum Builder fields with their Rust default variant instead of null when `#[serde(default)]` is present.** When a Rust struct field is a non-optional enum with `#[serde(default)]` (e.g., `KeywordConfig.algorithm: KeywordAlgorithm`), the field defaults to its Rust `impl Default` variant (e.g., `KeywordAlgorithm::Yake`). The Java Builder previously initialized the field to `null`, which caused Jackson's `NON_NULL` serialization filter to omit the field from the JSON string passed to Rust. Rust's serde deserializer then rejected the config with `"Null pointer passed for parameter 'config'"` because the field is required. The fix detects when a field has the `/* serde(default) */` marker and is a non-optional enum (TypeRef::Named), and initializes the Builder field to the corresponding Java enum constant (e.g., `KeywordAlgorithm.Yake`). Currently handles known enums by name; a future enhancement will extract the default variant from Rust's `impl Default` block in the IR. Added regression test `src/e2e/tests/java_enum_serde_default.rs` verifying that partial JSON configs (omitting enum fields) deserialize and serialize correctly. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: avoid double-wrapping `Option<Option<T>>` constructor params, and use the matching struct field's type for options-field bridge params so `Self { visitor }` compiles.** Two fixes for the rewritten PyO3 `__new__` constructor. (1) When the IR field type is already `TypeRef::Optional(_)` (which happens for fields like `Update::max_depth: Option<Option<usize>>` peeled to `f.optional=true, f.ty=Optional(usize)`), the constructor param emission was wrapping again to `Option<Option<usize>>`, but the matching pyclass struct field is `Option<usize>` — so the bare `Self { max_depth }` line failed with `expected Option<usize>, found Option<Option<usize>>` (and PyO3's `=None` macro expansion picked up the resulting ambiguity). The param emission now mirrors the guard already in `gen_struct_with_per_field_attrs` and skips re-wrapping when `field.ty` is already optional. (2) The options-field bridge parameter (e.g. `ConversionOptions.visitor`) was typed as `Option<{type_alias}>` (user-facing `VisitorHandle`), but the struct field is `Option<{wrapper_type}>` (the binding-internal `PyVisitorRef`). The bare assignment `visitor: visitor` therefore failed with `expected Option<PyVisitorRef>, found Option<VisitorHandle>`. The bridge param now uses the matching field's actual mapped type. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: emit RuboCop-clean tagged-enum marker modules so generated Ruby stays stable.** Generated Ruby lives under `lib/**`, which a gem's `.rubocop.yml` typically excludes — so alef's own `bundle exec rubocop -A` pass (no file args) never reformats it and reports "no offenses". But a pre-commit `rubocop` hook that passes the file path explicitly overrides that `Exclude` and reformats it, changing the file after alef computed its hash and tripping `alef verify` with "Stale bindings detected". Three offenses are now avoided at generation time: (1) the sum-type `from_hash` dispatcher emitted double-quoted literals (`hash["type"]`, `when "http"`) while the rest of the file used single quotes — the discriminator read, `when` arms, and per-variant field reads now use single quotes (`Style/StringLiterals`), while the interpolated `raise "Unknown discriminator: #{...}"` stays double-quoted; (2) the marker module lacked a blank line after its `extend` inclusion group before `interface!` (`Layout/EmptyLinesAfterModuleInclusion`); (3) both the marker module and the enclosing module body ended with a trailing blank line (`Layout/EmptyLinesAroundModuleBody`). Added a regression test asserting all three. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: de-duplicate WASM `Cargo.toml` dependencies so `[crates.<lang>.extra_dependencies]` overrides a built-in instead of emitting a duplicate key.** `gen_cargo_toml` seeds a built-in dependency list (`serde`, `serde_json`, `js-sys`, …), then appended consumer `extra_dependencies` and only sorted — so a consumer that re-declared a built-in (e.g. `serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }`) produced two `serde` keys and `cargo` rejected the manifest with `error: duplicate key ... in dependencies`. The backend now drops any built-in whose name also appears in `extra_dependencies` (the consumer override wins) before sorting. Added a regression test asserting a single `serde` key, that the override's extra feature is preserved, and that the emitted manifest parses as valid TOML. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/elixir: emit Rustler `NifTaggedEnum` tuples for internally-tagged enum array arguments.** Calls like `interact(engine, url, actions)` where `actions: Vec<PageAction>` (`#[serde(tag = "type")]`) previously emitted raw Elixir maps (`[%{"type" => "click", "selector" => "..."}]`), causing `(ErlangError) :invalid_variant` / `(ArgumentError) argument error` at the NIF boundary because the `NifTaggedEnum` decoder expects `:atom` (unit variants) or `{:atom, %{field_atom: val}}` (struct variants). The codegen now threads the IR `enums` slice through the rendering call chain and, when an arg's `element_type` matches an enum with `serde_tag.is_some()`, emits a list of tuples driven entirely from the IR: variant atoms use `variant.name.to_snake_case()` (Rustler ignores serde rename for atoms), field atoms use the Rust field name as-is, and nested `Named` references to unit-only enums convert input strings to atoms via that enum's `serde_rename_all`. Added `src/e2e/tests/elixir_tagged_enum.rs` covering unit, struct, and nested-unit-enum variants. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: expose canonical streaming facade methods on the static `<Crate>` class.** Streaming adapters (e.g. `crawl_stream`, `batch_crawl_stream`) previously emitted only instance methods on the opaque handle class. The e2e generator and external callers expected the same module-level surface that Go, Python, Ruby, and other backends provide: `Kreuzcrawl.crawlStream(engine, req)` returning `Iterable<Event>`. Java compile failed with `cannot find symbol method crawlStream(CrawlEngineHandle, CrawlStreamRequest)` across `EngineTest`/`StreamTest`. The Java facade emitter now iterates configured streaming adapters with an `owner_type`, generates a static wrapper that delegates to the owner handle's instance method, and adds the `java.lang.Iterable` import. Driven from the IR adapter configuration — no consumer-specific literals. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`, `src/backends/java/templates/facade_class.jinja`, `src/backends/java/templates/facade_file.jinja`)

- **alef-core: add `unsupported_in` per-backend call configuration to mark calls unsupported in specific backends with documented rationale.** Previously, when a backend structurally could not support a call (e.g., brew CLI lacking PageAction enum serialization for interact), the only mechanism was `skip_languages`, which silently omitted tests with no visible rationale. The fix adds `unsupported_in: HashMap<String, String>` to `CallConfig`, mapping backend names to rationale strings (e.g., `unsupported_in = { brew = "interact requires..." }`). The brew e2e codegen now detects this field and emits a skip comment with the documented reason (`# SKIP [brew unsupported]: ...`) before returning success, so fixtures remain testable in other languages and users can understand why the test was skipped. This is a generic, backend-agnostic mechanism: any backend can check `call_config.unsupported_in.get("backend_name")` to skip with documented rationale. Added `src/e2e/codegen/brew.rs` logic to check and skip unsupported calls, and `src/e2e/tests/brew_unsupported_in_call.rs` test verifying the skip comment is emitted. (`src/core/config/e2e.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/brew.rs`)

- **alef-codegen + alef-backend-java: drop the `/* serde(default) */` placeholder marker before emission so generated code compiles.** The extractor sets `field.default = Some("/* serde(default) */")` to signal that a field carries `#[serde(default)]` but has no explicit value (the marker tells C# to skip its `required` modifier). Java's Jackson POJO builder and the shared `alef_codegen::config_gen::default_value_for_field` helper used to clone `field.default` directly into the emitted code, so generated Java contained `private List<String> excludeSelectors = /* serde(default) */;` (invalid Java) and generated Ruby contained `children: /* serde(default) */` (invalid Rust). Both call sites now filter the marker and fall through to the type-driven default (`List.of()`, `false`, `0`, etc.). (`src/codegen/config_gen.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: add buildscript classpath for vanniktech plugin and use typed jar/sources parameters in AndroidSingleVariantLibrary.** The generated `build.gradle.kts` imports and calls `SonatypeHost.CENTRAL_PORTAL` and `AndroidSingleVariantLibrary(..., sourcesJar = true, publishJavadocJar = true)`, but these classes were not on the Gradle script classpath, causing "Unresolved reference 'SonatypeHost'" compile errors when running `gradle test`. Additionally, the Boolean constructor parameters are deprecated in vanniktech maven-publish 0.36.0; the current API expects `sourcesJar = SourcesJar.Software()` and `javadocJar = JavadocJar.Javadoc()`. The fixes: (1) prepend a `buildscript { dependencies { classpath("com.vanniktech:gradle-maven-publish-plugin:{version}") } }` block before plugins to load the plugin classes onto the script classpath; (2) replace `sourcesJar = true` with `sourcesJar = com.vanniktech.maven.publish.SourcesJar.Software()` and `publishJavadocJar = true` with `javadocJar = com.vanniktech.maven.publish.JavadocJar.Javadoc()` to use the non-deprecated API. Updated snapshot test expectations to match. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_build_gradle.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/tests/snapshots/...`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: disable ktlint's `trailing-comma-on-call-site` / `-on-declaration-site` rules in the generated `.editorconfig` so ktfmt and ktlint stop fighting.** ktfmt in `--kotlinlang-style` strips trailing commas before `)` on multiline call sites; ktlint then re-reports the missing comma. After the kotlin-android `vanniktech` maven-publish migration, this trapped repos in an unfixable ktfmt↔ktlint loop on `build.gradle.kts`. Defer to ktfmt — the new `.editorconfig` block disables both ktlint rules under `[*.kt]` and `[*.gradle.kts]`. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_editorconfig.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: map sanitized Vec fields to `null` and sanitized enum-variant payloads to `object` so external Rust types round-trip through System.Text.Json.** Empty-list and string-coerced defaults broke serde deserialization; sanitized fields now emit a null default (dropped via WhenWritingNull) and `object` payloads. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/enums.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

## [0.17.36] - 2026-05-23

### Added

- **alef core: per-backend `unsupported_in` call configuration** with documented rationale — marks calls that structurally cannot be supported on a backend, emitting visible skip comments in generated tests. (`src/core/config/e2e.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef pyo3: correct constructor param types for `Optional` fields and bridge params**, plus cfg-gated constructor kwargs. (`src/backends/pyo3`)
- **alef magnus: emit rubocop-clean blank-line layout and single-quoted `from_hash` dispatcher literals** in tagged-enum modules. (`src/backends/magnus`)
- **alef java: expose the canonical streaming facade method** and fix the enum-builder default. (`src/backends/java`)
- **alef csharp: fix variant struct payload and sanitize the tuple default.** (`src/backends/csharp`)
- **alef wasm: dedup `Cargo.toml` deps so `extra_dependencies` override built-ins.** (`src/backends/wasm`)
- **alef kotlin-android: add the `SonatypeHost` classpath and typed `sourcesJar`/`javadocJar` API**, plus an AGP bump. (`src/backends/kotlin-android`)
- **alef publish: hoist `alef-scaffold` above backends in publish order.** (`src/publish`)
- **e2e codegen fixes across java, elixir** — deterministic fixture-map output via `BTreeMap`, Java nullable-accessor `String` coercion for `.contains()`, and Elixir Rustler `NifTaggedEnum` tuples for array args. (`src/e2e/codegen`)

## [0.17.35] - 2026-05-23

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/dart: construct a default options instance for absent optional `json_object` config args when the Dart facade declares the arg as required-positional.** Calls like `embed_texts_async(texts, config)` have `config` marked `optional = true` in the alef IR but the Dart facade signature is `embedTextsAsync(List<String>, EmbeddingConfig)` — non-nullable. When the fixture omitted the `config` field, the e2e codegen previously skipped emitting any value (relying on a hypothetical wrapper-side default), so the generated test compiled to `KreuzbergBridge.embedTextsAsync(<String>[])` and failed with `Too few positional arguments: 2 required, 1 given`. The codegen now mirrors the existing empty-object branch: when `arg_value` is absent and the call has a configured `options_type`, emit `final _config = await create<Type>FromJson(json: '{}');` and pass `_config` positionally. Calls without an `options_type` (e.g. the `ExtractionConfig?` nullable facade path) still omit the arg. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: only emit `_parsePageAction()` helper when fixtures use PageAction arrays.** The Dart e2e codegen unconditionally emitted the `_parsePageAction()` function and `ScrollDirection` references into every generated test file. Test files without any PageAction-array fixture (e.g. `code_test.dart`, `async_test.dart`) failed to compile with `Type 'PageAction' not found` / `Undefined name 'ScrollDirection'`. The helper is now emitted only when at least one fixture in the file has a `json_object` arg with `element_type = "PageAction"`, mirroring the existing `has_handle_args` / `has_http_fixtures` conditional-emit pattern. Added `src/e2e/tests/dart_page_action_conditional.rs`. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/kotlin-android: emit default constructor for optional `json_object` config args instead of `null`.** The kotlin-android binding declares its config parameter as non-nullable `ExtractionConfig`, but the e2e codegen passed `null` whenever the fixture omitted the optional `config` arg, causing Kotlin compile errors (`Null cannot be a value of a non-null type 'ExtractionConfig'`) in `AsyncTest.kt` / `BatchTest.kt`. The codegen now emits `ExtractionConfig()` (using the data-class default constructor) for json_object args with `options_type` set, and infers the type from the arg name otherwise (e.g. `config` → `ExtractionConfig()`). Non-`json_object` optional args still emit `null` as before. Added `src/e2e/tests/kotlin_android_optional_config_arg.rs`. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

- **alef-extract: detect `#[serde(default)]` on struct fields and mark them as having a default value in the IR.** Without this, downstream codegen (notably C#) treated `serde(default)` fields as required JSON properties, emitting a `required` modifier on record properties like `DocumentNode.content_layer`. When Rust serialized the field with `skip_serializing_if`, the JSON arrived without the property and C# deserialization rejected the payload with `JSON property required`. The extractor now scans field attributes for `#[serde(default)]` (with or without an explicit value path) and sets `field.default = Some(...)` accordingly, which downstream backends already consume via `field.default.is_some()`. Fixes `Test_ConfigDocumentStructureWithHeadings` in kreuzberg C# e2e. Added `src/e2e/tests/csharp_serde_default_field.rs`. (`src/extract/extractor/helpers.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/java: inherit `options_type` from non-Java overrides when Java omits one.** Java e2e codegen previously read `options_type` only from `[crates.e2e.call.overrides.java]`, so calls that declared `options_type = "ExtractionConfig"` in their C#/Go/PHP/Python override but not Java fell back to emitting raw JSON literals (or `null` for null-bodied configs) instead of deserializing via `JsonUtil.fromJson(..., ExtractionConfig.class)`. Java e2e tests like `testConfigKeywords` consequently passed a null `MemorySegment` for the config handle, hitting a Panama `WrongMethodTypeException` against a non-null parameter. The fallback walks `[csharp, c, go, php, python]` (in that order) and inherits the first available `options_type`. Added `src/e2e/tests/java_json_object_arg_with_value.rs` to lock the inheritance path. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: restore cfg-gated fields to dict coercion and e2e kwargs (reverts 9228bb6f filtering).** PyO3 builds with all features, so cfg-gated fields are present; the filtering predicates and `is_cfg_gated_field` helper are removed so all fields reach the constructor. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/{mod,test_file,test_function}.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: wrap Option<NonOpaqueNamed> getter returns with .map(Into::into).** ext-php-rs's IntoZval for Option<#[php_class]> returned null; getters now emit `self.field.clone().map(Into::into)` to convert the inner object. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: apply serde-rename constructor rewriting to all struct types, not just has_default.** The `replace_constructor_with_serde_rename` function was only called when `typ.has_default` was true, leaving non-has_default types like `ChunkingConfig` with positional parameters bearing the Rust field names instead of serde-rename wire names. The fix extends the rewrite to all struct types: for `has_default` types, non-optional fields get default values in the signature (`field=Self::default().field`); for non-has_default types, required fields remain required (no signature default) while optional fields still default to `None`. This ensures all types expose the wire-name API (matching JSON serialization and other language bindings) while preserving the semantic difference between types that can be partially constructed with defaults vs. those requiring all parameters. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/python: skip cfg-gated fields when emitting constructor kwargs.** The Python e2e codegen was emitting constructor kwargs for all fields in fixture JSON objects, including cfg-gated fields like `pdf_options` that are excluded from the PyO3 constructor signature by the `#[cfg(...)]` guards in the core Rust type. When a fixture included a cfg-gated field, the e2e test failed with `TypeError: ExtractionConfig.__new__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'pdf_options'`. The fix threads `type_defs` from `generate_e2e` through `render_test_file` → `render_test_function` → `build_args_and_setup` → `emit_json_object_arg`, and adds a helper `is_cfg_gated_field` that checks the IR to detect cfg-gated fields. When emitting kwargs for a config type in the "kwargs" options_via mode, cfg-gated fields are filtered out before mapping to the constructor call. This restores Python e2e test success when fixtures include conditional features. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/mod.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/test_file.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/python/test_function.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: exclude cfg-gated fields from dict coercion functions.** The Python wrapper's `_coerce_dict_*` functions were attempting to coerce cfg-gated fields (like `pdf_options`, `html_options`, `keywords`, `layout`, `tree_sitter`) that are excluded from the PyO3 constructor signature by `#[cfg(...)]` guards in the Rust core. When a user passed these fields in a dict to the wrapper, the coercion function would try to include them in the constructor arguments, causing `TypeError: ExtractionConfig.__new__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'pdf_options'`. The fix adds `f.cfg.is_none() &&` filters to the three field-collection lists (`struct_coercible`, `simple_enum_coercible`, `data_enum_coercible`) in the dict coercion helper generator, matching the same cfg-gate checks already applied to constructor parameter generation. Now only non-cfg-gated fields are included in the `_coerce_dict_*` helper, and the wrapper only passes fields that the PyO3 constructor actually accepts. This is the final layer of the three-part cfg-gating fix (constructor signature + e2e kwargs emission + wrapper coercion). (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: JSON-encode the streaming request struct before passing it to the NIF.** The NIF expects `Option<String>` but the wrapper forwarded the struct, crashing with ArgumentError; it now passes `nil`/binaries through and `Jason.encode!`s other terms. (`src/backends/rustler/templates/elixir_streaming_unfold_wrapper.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: remove invalid uppercase RBS type alias.** RBS type aliases must use lowercase identifiers. The uppercase `JsonValue` alias was invalid RBS syntax, causing `steep` and RBS tooling to reject the generated `.rbs` files with a lint error. The lowercase `json_value` on line 11 serves the same purpose and is valid syntax. Removed the invalid alias from stub generation and updated the type mapper to reference `json_value` instead of `JsonValue` in method signatures, fixing h2m Ruby e2e test startup. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_stubs.rs`, `src/backends/magnus/type_map.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: include options-field bridge parameters in PyO3 constructor signatures.** When a `has_default` struct has an options-field bridge (e.g., `ConversionOptions.visitor` for the `visitor` trait bridge), the PyO3 binding constructor was not accepting the bridge parameter in its `#[pyo3(signature)]` and function parameters, causing the Python wrapper's `_to_rust_*()` converter to fail with `TypeError: ConversionOptions.__new__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'visitor'` when passing `visitor=` to the constructor. The fix detects options-field bridges matching the struct type and adds the bridge's `param_name` and `type_alias` to the constructor parameter list and signature defaults. When a bridge field is present, it is excluded from the regular field iteration to avoid duplicate assignments in the struct literal. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: restore camelCase key transformation in `from_json()` calls.** A prior commit removed the `json_to_php_camel_keys()` helper from PHP e2e codegen, replacing it with `json_to_php()` under the assumption that fixtures' snake_case keys should be passed verbatim to `from_json()`. This was incorrect: the PHP binding's config struct definitions use `#[serde(default, rename_all = "camelCase")]`, which means serde expects camelCase JSON keys. When snake_case keys were passed, serde silently ignored them due to `#[serde(default)]`, leaving configuration values in their default state instead of using the fixture's intent. This caused the test `test_error_extract_bytes_conflicting_ocr` to fail because `allow_ocr_conflicts` was not being set, resulting in a different error than expected. The fix restores the `json_to_php_camel_keys()` helper and switches the three affected call sites (CrawlConfig, PageConfig, and ExtractionConfig construction) back to it. The regression test file documenting the incorrect behavior is removed. This fixes 1 test regression in the PHP e2e suite (`test_error_extract_bytes_conflicting_ocr`). (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: invoke streaming owner_type adapters as instance methods and wrap url-list args in the request type.** The PHP streaming facade exposes `crawlStream`/`batchCrawlStream` only as instance methods on the owner handle class (`$engine->crawlStream($req)`), but the PHP e2e codegen emitted `<FacadeClass>::crawlStream($engine, $req)` — a non-existent static call. Additionally, `mock_url_list` arguments were passed as a raw array instead of being wrapped in the adapter's request type (`BatchCrawlStreamRequest`), while `mock_url` args were already wrapped. The codegen now detects a streaming adapter with an `owner_type`, captures the owner handle variable (`owner_handle_is_receiver` flag suppresses adding it to the positional argument list), renders the call as `$<handle>-><method>(<args>)`, and wraps `mock_url_list` args in the adapter request type the same way `mock_url` args are wrapped. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/java: invoke streaming owner_type adapters as instance methods on the owner handle.** The Java facade deliberately emits no static streaming methods — a streaming iterator is bound to a live owner handle, so `crawlStream`/`batchCrawlStream` exist only as instance methods on the owner's opaque-handle class (e.g. `engine.crawlStream(req)`). The Java e2e codegen was emitting `<FacadeClass>.crawlStream(engine, req)` — a static call that does not exist — causing `cannot find symbol` compile errors in `StreamTest`/`EngineTest`. The codegen now detects a streaming adapter with an `owner_type`, captures the owner handle variable, emits its construction via `build_args_and_setup` (new `owner_handle_is_receiver` flag suppresses adding the handle to the positional argument list), and renders the call as an instance-method invocation `<handle>.<method>(<args>)`. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: generate streaming adapter facade methods.** The PHP backend was missing static facade methods for streaming adapters, causing `Class "Kreuzcrawl\E2e\CrawlStreamRequest" not found` and `Call to undefined method Kreuzcrawl\Kreuzcrawl::batchCrawlStream()` errors in PHP e2e tests. The request-type classes (`CrawlStreamRequest`, `BatchCrawlStreamRequest`) were already being generated as part of the API surface, but the streaming adapter facade methods that expose them via static methods on the main API class were never being emitted. The fix adds logic to iterate over streaming adapters in the facade class generation section and emit a static method for each one: `public static function crawlStream(engine: &CrawlEngineHandle, req: &CrawlStreamRequest) -> Result<Vec<String>>` that delegates to the instance method on the engine handle. This mirrors the existing pattern in alef-backend-go where streaming adapters receive module-level wrapper functions. The request types are passed by reference and the facade methods return the streaming result directly (Vec<String> of JSON chunks, wrapped by the PHP Generator infrastructure at the opaque type level). (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/go: decode sum-type argument elements via the generated `Unmarshal<Type>` discriminator.** Bare `json.Unmarshal` cannot init interface values, so data-enum array args now decode each element through the discriminator function. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: emit `#[pyo3(signature)]` defaults for non-optional constructor parameters.** `replace_constructor_with_serde_rename` emitted non-optional fields of `has_default` config types as bare parameters with no signature default, so the generated `__new__` required them as positional arguments — `TypeError: ExtractionConfig.__new__() missing 9 required positional arguments: 'use_cache', ...`. Since these constructors are only generated for `has_default` types, whose pyclass struct derives `Default`, each non-optional parameter now defaults to `Self::default().<field>`, restoring the all-keyword-argument-optional constructor. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: preserve snake_case fixture keys when emitting `from_json(json_encode(...))` calls.** The PHP e2e codegen was applying camelCase transformation to JSON object keys before passing them to `from_json()`, converting fixture keys like `extract_pages` to `extractPages`. The PHP binding's serde configuration includes `#[serde(default, rename_all = "camelCase")]`, but fixtures are authored in Rust wire format (snake_case). When the transformed keys were deserialized, serde silently ignored them due to `#[serde(default)]`, leaving configuration values in their default state instead of using the fixture's intent. This caused tests like `test_config_pages` to fail because page markers were not inserted. The fix removes the `json_to_php_camel_keys()` function and replaces all three call sites (lines 1429, 1567, 1597) with `json_to_php()`, which preserves the fixture's original keys. The binding's serde deserializer now receives the fixture's snake_case keys and correctly deserializes them. This fixes 1 runtime failure in the PHP e2e suite (`ContractTest::test_config_pages`). Added regression test `php_snake_case_fixture_keys` documenting the fix. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`, `src/e2e/tests/php_snake_case_fixture_keys.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/c: select correct FFI free function for opaque struct-returning getter results.** Opaque struct handles were freed with `_free_string`; `emit_nested_accessor` now returns the type name so callers register them in `opaque_handle_locals` and free with the struct-specific function. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/zig: fix format string escaping in allocPrint wrapper for interact() calls.** The Zig e2e codegen was generating an invalid format string for `std.fmt.allocPrint` when wrapping interact result: `"{{\\\"interaction\\\"{{s}}}}"` which became the Zig string `{\"interaction\"{s}}` — an unterminated placeholder due to the doubled `{{s}}` being interpreted as a literal `{s}` instead of the string placeholder. The format string in Zig should be `"{{\"interaction\":{s}}}"` where `{{` and `}}` are literal braces and `{s}` is the placeholder. In Rust source, this requires double-escaping: `"{{{{\\\"interaction\\\"{{s}}}}}}"` (each `{` → `{{` → `{{{{` in Rust source). The fix updates the writeln! invocation to emit the correct format string, allowing Zig 0.16 to correctly parse and compile the allocPrint call. This fixes `error: expected . or }, found '{'` compilation errors at the allocPrint line in kreuzcrawl Zig e2e tests. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/csharp: emit plain `.ToString()` instead of null-conditional `?.ToString()` for non-nullable value types.** `?.` is invalid on non-nullable types; the codegen now passes a `field_is_nullable` flag so only nullable fields use `?.ToString()`. (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/csharp/assertion.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: escape Rust keywords in serde-rename constructor parameters using raw-identifier syntax.** When a struct field carries `#[serde(rename = "type")]` (or any Rust keyword as the wire name), `replace_constructor_with_serde_rename` was emitting the bare keyword as a function parameter name and struct-literal RHS — e.g. `pub fn new(type: String, ...)` and `Self { item_type: type }`. Both are invalid Rust because `type` is a reserved keyword. The fix calls `alef_core::keywords::rust_raw_ident` on every resolved parameter name, emitting `r#type` wherever the wire name collides with a Rust keyword. PyO3 strips the `r#` prefix when deriving the Python-facing kwarg name, so the Python API remains unchanged (`type`). A new `RUST_KEYWORDS` constant and `rust_raw_ident`/`rust_raw_ident_safe` helpers have been added to `alef-core::keywords` and re-exported via `alef-codegen::keywords`. Added regression test `test_serde_rename_rust_keyword_emitted_as_raw_ident` that verifies the emitted constructor uses `r#type`, the struct literal uses `item_type: r#type`, and no bare `type` token appears. (`src/core/keywords.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: emit non-optional Duration struct fields as `Option<u64>` in constructor parameters to match the struct field.** The struct wrapped Duration in `Option<u64>` but the constructor param stayed `u64`, causing E0308; the param emitter now applies the same `option_duration_on_defaults` wrapping. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-cli/version: sync `.version` in `packages/zig/build.zig.zon`.** `alef sync-versions` updated every other per-language manifest (pyproject.toml, package.json, pom.xml, mix.exs, composer.json, pubspec.yaml, etc.) but never `packages/zig/build.zig.zon`, so the Zig package version drifted from the workspace version every release. tslp's `1.9.0-rc.1` release shipped with `.version = "1.8.1"` still committed in the zon, and the `publish-zig` job consumed it. Both the validate path and the sync-all path now match the `.version = "X.Y.Z"` field, anchored to start-of-line with `(?m)^\s*\.version` so the `.minimum_zig_version = "..."` field on the same file is not accidentally matched. Added regression test `zon_version_regex_anchors_to_dot_version_only` covering exactly that anchor case. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/version.rs`)

### Added

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: apply com.vanniktech.maven.publish plugin so `publishAndReleaseToMavenCentral` resolves.** The shared `kreuzberg-dev/actions/publish-maven-gradle@v1` action invokes `gradle publishAndReleaseToMavenCentral` by default — a task provided by the **vanniktech maven-publish** plugin, not the vanilla `maven-publish` plugin alef previously emitted. Releases failed with `Task 'publishAndReleaseToMavenCentral' not found in root project` because the generated `build.gradle.kts` only declared `id("maven-publish")` plus a manual `publishing { publications { register<MavenPublication>("release") { ... } } }` block. The emitter now applies `id("com.vanniktech.maven.publish") version "0.36.0"` and replaces the manual publishing/singleVariant blocks with vanniktech's `mavenPublishing { configure(AndroidSingleVariantLibrary("release", sourcesJar = true, publishJavadocJar = true)); publishToMavenCentral(SonatypeHost.CENTRAL_PORTAL); signAllPublications(); coordinates(...); pom { name, description, url, licenses, developers, scm } }`. Pom metadata is sourced from `config.scaffold` via the now-public `scaffold_meta`/`parse_author`/`xml_escape` helpers re-exported from `alef-scaffold`, falling back to the same defaults `scaffold_meta` uses elsewhere (license `MIT`, repository `https://github.com/example/{name}`, no developers when authors is empty). Added `VANNIKTECH_MAVEN_PUBLISH = "0.36.0"` to `src/core/template_versions.rs::maven`. Snapshot updated; regression test `build_gradle_uses_vanniktech_maven_publish_plugin` asserts the plugin id, the `mavenPublishing {` block, and the absence of the old `register<MavenPublication>("release")` and `android { publishing { } }` blocks. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_build_gradle.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/Cargo.toml`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/tests/snapshot_test.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/tests/snapshots/...build.gradle.kts.snap`, `src/core/template_versions.rs`, `crates/alef-scaffold/src/lib.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/php: emit root composer.json mirroring packages/php for Packagist/PIE.** Packagist indexes the repo-root `composer.json` and PIE reads its `extra.pie.binary.url-template` from the indexed manifest to download prebuilt extension binaries from GitHub Releases. Until now alef only emitted `packages/php/composer.json`, leaving each polyglot repo's root manifest as a hand-written file that drifted from the alef-generated one — most notably missing the `extra.pie` block, which forced PIE to fall back to source builds. The PHP scaffold now also emits a root `composer.json` byte-identical to `packages/php/composer.json` except that the PSR-4 autoload src path is repointed from `src/` to `packages/php/src/`, so the same classes resolve when consumers install the package from the repo root. `alef sync-versions` already updated the root file's `version` field when present (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/version.rs`); generation closes the gap and keeps the two manifests in sync. Verified across all five polyglot repos (kreuzberg, html-to-markdown, liter-llm, kreuzcrawl, tree-sitter-language-pack), each of which previously had a hand-written root `composer.json` divergent from its alef-generated `packages/php/composer.json` in vendor name, extension name, namespace, and `extra.pie` presence. Added regression test `test_scaffold_php_emits_root_composer_json_mirroring_package` that asserts the root manifest equals the package manifest with the autoload src repointed, carries the `extra.pie.binary.url-template`, and uses `<owner>/<repo>` as the Packagist package name. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/php.rs`, `crates/alef-scaffold/src/tests.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: copy workspace-root LICENSE into every per-language package directory.** Ecosystems like pub.dev (Dart) require a `LICENSE` file in the package root and reject uploads without one. The scaffold step now reads `<workspace_root>/LICENSE` and emits it into each per-language package directory (e.g. `packages/dart/LICENSE`, `packages/python/LICENSE`).
