When no LICENSE file is present the step warns and continues without error. Internal-only languages (Rust, C, FFI, JNI) are excluded. The copy is idempotent — `generated_header: false` means the file is created once and not overwritten on subsequent `alef scaffold` runs, keeping `alef verify` happy. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/lib.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/dart: add `repository:` field to pubspec.yaml and emit CHANGELOG.md.** `dart pub publish` flags packages missing a `repository:` field in `pubspec.yaml` and requires a `CHANGELOG.md` at the package root. The generated `pubspec.yaml` now includes `repository: <configured-url>` (and `homepage:` when set). A minimal `CHANGELOG.md` seed keyed to the current version is emitted as a create-once file so consumers update it before publishing. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/dart.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-pyo3: fix regression in cfg-gated field struct literals in constructors.** Commit fe570c1c added support for `serde(rename)` in constructor parameters for has_default config types, but introduced a regression: cfg-gated fields (e.g., `#[cfg(feature = "pdf")]`) were excluded from the constructor parameter list (correct) but still referenced in struct literal shorthand without being defined (incorrect), causing `error[E0425]: cannot find value` compilation errors in kreuzberg-py for fields like `pdf_options`, `keywords`, `html_options`, etc. The fix correctly handles cfg-gated fields in struct literals: they are excluded from constructor parameters (Python doesn't know about Rust feature gates) and initialized with `None` for optional cfg-gated fields or `Default::default()` for required ones. This ensures all struct fields are properly initialized regardless of feature gate status. Affected types: `ExtractionConfig`, `FileExtractionConfig`, `ExtractionResult`, `KeywordConfig`. Added regression test `test_cfg_gated_fields_excluded_from_constructor` verifying cfg-gated fields are omitted from parameters but correctly initialized in struct literals. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: fully qualify config class names to the binding namespace.** Config classes like `EmbeddingConfig` were being emitted as bare names (e.g., `EmbeddingConfig::from_json('{}')`) in generated test code, causing PHP to look for them in the test namespace (`Kreuzberg\E2e`) instead of the binding namespace (`Kreuzberg`), resulting in `Class "Kreuzberg\E2e\EmbeddingConfig" not found` errors. Config references are now fully qualified to the binding namespace (e.g., `\Kreuzberg\EmbeddingConfig::from_json('{}')`). This fixes 3 runtime failures in the PHP e2e suite for the `embed_texts_async` call. The fix also requires passing the `namespace` parameter through `build_args_and_setup()` to enable qualification at the codegen point. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/kotlin_android: emit plain string paths instead of `java.nio.file.Path.of()` wrappers.** The Kotlin e2e codegen was always wrapping file path arguments with `java.nio.file.Path.of(...)` because the Kotlin/JVM binding re-exports the Java facade (which takes `Path`), but kotlin_android has a different signature that takes a plain `String`. The fix detects kotlin_android mode via the existing `kotlin_android_style` flag and emits plain string paths for kotlin_android while keeping the `Path.of()` wrapping for Kotlin/JVM. This fixes 5 compilation errors in the kotlin_android e2e suite where `extractFile()` received a `Path` when a `String` was expected. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: fix clear_fn error handling to throw exception with (int code, String message) constructor.** A prior fix added error-code checking to trait-bridge clear_fn functions, but the generated exception throw used a single-String constructor (`new KreuzbergRsException("FFI call failed: " + msg)`) when `KreuzbergRsException` only has constructors `(int code, String message)` or `(String message, Throwable cause)`. The generated code now throws the correct two-argument form `new KreuzbergRsException(primitiveResult, msg)`, matching how all other fallible FFI calls construct the exception. This fixes compilation errors in the Java binding: `no suitable constructor found for KreuzbergRsException(java.lang.String)`. Added regression test `clear_fn_error_throws_exception_with_code_and_message` to verify the exact throw statement format. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`)

- **alef-cli: prepend the native-library directory to the search-path env var instead of replacing it.** The e2e/test runner inlines an `export <VAR>='<dir>'; <cmd>` prefix so freshly-built native libraries are found at runtime — `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` on Linux, `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH` on macOS, and `PATH` on Windows (Windows resolves DLLs via `PATH`). The export _replaced_ the variable's entire value, which is catastrophic on Windows: `export PATH='...target\release'` wiped out every tool on the path, so `uv: command not found` killed every Windows e2e job. On Linux/macOS the bug was masked because `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`/`DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH` are normally empty, so replace happened to equal set. The fix emits `export <VAR>='<dir>'"${<VAR>:+:$<VAR>}"` so the directory is prepended to any existing value, with the `${VAR:+:$VAR}` guard avoiding a stray `:` when the variable was unset. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/helpers.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/ruby: exclude generated binding code from RuboCop linting.** The `.rubocop.yml` generated for Ruby packages was excluding build artifacts (`ext/**/*`, `tmp/**/*`) but not the generated binding sources (`lib/**/*.rb`), causing RuboCop to lint alef-generated code (`native.rb`, `version.rb`) against project style rules it was never written to satisfy. This resulted in 7 RuboCop offenses across Metrics and Style rules (ModuleLength, OneClassPerFile, CyclomaticComplexity, AbcSize). The fix adds `lib/**/*.rb` to the `AllCops: Exclude:` list, keeping hand-written spec files linted while exempting all generated binding code. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: decode streaming sum-type items via the generated `Unmarshal<Type>` discriminator.** A streaming adapter whose item type is a data enum (a Rust enum with fielded variants) is emitted as a Go sealed interface. The streaming-consumer goroutine decoded each event with a bare `json.Unmarshal` into a named-interface variable, which fails at runtime (`json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type <Interface>`) — the goroutine bailed after the first event and delivered zero events to the channel. The generator now calls the generated `Unmarshal<Type>` discriminator function when the stream item type is a data enum. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/methods.rs`, `src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/go/templates/streaming_method_body.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: honor `serde(rename)` in constructor parameter names and PyO3 signatures.** PyO3 binding constructors for `has_default` config types were using bare Rust field names as parameter names (e.g. `max_characters`) instead of serde-renamed names (e.g. `max_chars`), causing `TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'max_chars'` when e2e tests passed the wire name. This broke consistency with other language bindings (Node, Ruby, PHP, Go, etc.) which all expose the serde-renamed names as the public API. The fix generates a custom constructor that honors `field.serde_rename` when available, deriving parameter names from the renamed values while keeping struct literal field names as bare Rust identifiers. The fix validates that config-based keyword renames take precedence over serde_rename (e.g. when `class` is keyword-escaped to `class_`). Added regression test `test_serde_rename_in_constructor_and_properties` that verifies constructor parameters, PyO3 signatures, and struct literals are all correct. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-napi: add regression test for TypeScript declaration formatting (no trailing semicolons on interface members).** NAPI-RS generates `.d.ts` files without trailing semicolons on interface member declarations. Alef must emit the same format to avoid churn when `napi build` regenerates the file. Added explicit test assertions to `test_dts_dto_fields_are_readonly` that verify interface fields do NOT have trailing semicolons, ensuring the formatting remains stable across regenerations. (`src/backends/napi/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: trait-bridge clear_fn functions invoke with out-error parameter and error code checking.** Trait-bridge clear functions (e.g. `clear_ocr_backends`) return `Result<()>` in Rust, which export as `int kreuzberg_clear_X(char **out_error)` in the C FFI. The Java codegen was treating them as `Unit` return types (void) and invoking them with no parameters, producing `java.lang.invoke.WrongMethodTypeException: cannot convert MethodHandle(MemorySegment)int to ()void`. The fix detects trait-bridge clear_fn functions via the computed `clear_fn_handles` map and generates the same error-code-checking pattern as other fallible FFI functions: allocate an out-error buffer, pass it to the FFI invocation, and throw an exception if the return code is non-zero. This fixes 7 runtime failures in the Java e2e suite (`clearValidators`, `clearOcrBackends`, `clearRenderers`, etc.). (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: use `Mix.env()` instead of `System.get_env("MIX_ENV")` for the force_build predicate in native_module_header.** The `rustler_precompiled` configuration's `force_build` clause was checking `System.get_env("MIX_ENV") in ["test", "dev"]`, which returns `nil` when the environment variable is unset, causing the predicate to always be false. However, `mix compile` and `mix test` run in the `:dev` and `:test` environments internally (set by Mix) even when `MIX_ENV` is not exported. This caused `rustler_precompiled` to attempt downloading precompiled NIFs from a GitHub release URL that does not exist for unreleased versions (e.g. `v5.0.0-rc.1`), resulting in HTTP 404 and build failure. The fix uses `Mix.env() in [:dev, :test]` to check the actual Mix environment atom instead. (`src/backends/rustler/src/template_env.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: fix trait-bridge clear facade method naming — use `clear_fn` name (plural) not trait name (singular).** A prior fix added `Clear*` facade methods to the wrapper class to forward to trait-bridge registries' `Clear()` methods, but named them from the trait name: `ClearOcrBackend` (singular, from trait `OcrBackend`). The public API and e2e tests expect the plural name from the Rust free-function: `ClearOcrBackends` (from `clear_ocr_backends`). The method name derivation now uses `bridge_cfg.clear_fn` in PascalCase instead of the trait name, generating the correct plural names: `ClearOcrBackends`, `ClearPostProcessors`, `ClearValidators`, `ClearEmbeddingBackends`, `ClearDocumentExtractors`, `ClearRenderers`. This fixes `CS0117: 'KreuzbergLib' does not contain a definition for 'ClearOcrBackends'` compilation errors in C# e2e suites. Added regression test `test_trait_bridge_clear_method_uses_clear_fn_name_not_trait_name` to verify the exact method signatures. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/ruby: emit specific `raise_error(RuntimeError)` matchers instead of bare `raise_error`.** RSpec emits a false-positive warning for every bare `raise_error` matcher because it can match NoMethodError, NameError, or ArgumentError unintentionally. The Ruby e2e generator now emits `raise_error(RuntimeError)` for error fixtures, matching the Kreuzberg binding's exception type. (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/typescript: bind `result` variable for calls returning a plain type when an assertion field is not a struct member.** For calls returning a plain type (e.g. `embedTexts`, `detectMimeTypeFromBytes`), assertions on synthetic fields (e.g. `embeddings` on an `Array<Array<number>>`) render against the result directly, but the binding predicate gated solely on `is_valid_for_result(field)`, leaving undefined `result` references in generated test code. Mirrors the prior Rust/Go fix. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/typescript: emit extended timeout for embedding e2e tests.** Embedding fixture calls trigger a multi-minute HuggingFace model download on first run. Generated tests now pass a 600-second timeout (e.g. `it(name, fn, 600_000)`) for fixtures tagged with the `embeddings` call name, gating the timeout on the call type rather than duplicating per-fixture configuration. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/go: do not emit unused `metadataOutputFormat` bindings for contract calls.** The Go e2e generator emitted `metadataOutputFormat := ...` for certain fixtures but did not consume it in the resulting test, producing `declared and not used` errors. The binding is now only emitted when the output format is actually applied in an assertion. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: honor `options_type` for async embed calls, and emit defensive null checks for optional metadata properties.** The `embedTextsAsync` call now resolves the config type from the call-level `options_type` (`EmbeddingConfig`) instead of falling back to `ExtractionConfig`. Additionally, smoke tests now emit null-coalescing for optional/nullable metadata properties (e.g. `$metadata->excel ?? null`) to prevent PHP "Attempt to read property" warnings. Finally, the generated test for `embed_texts_different_preset` now declares at least one assertion to satisfy PHPUnit's strict assertion checking. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/r: emit non-empty test bodies for mime_utilities fixtures.** The R e2e generator was emitting empty `test_that` blocks for some mime_utilities fixtures, causing testthat to skip them with a "Reason: empty test" warning. Test bodies now always contain at least one meaningful assertion or call. (`src/e2e/codegen/r.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: keep the system php.ini when re-execing the PHPUnit harness.** The generated `run_tests.php` re-execs PHP with `-n` (no ini) plus `-d extension=<local build>` so the freshly-built extension is loaded without a stale system copy. But `-n` also drops every _shared_ PHP module, so on distributions that ship `dom`/`mbstring`/`tokenizer`/`xml`/`xmlwriter` as shared extensions (Debian/Ubuntu, hence CI) PHPUnit 13 aborts at startup with "PHPUnit requires the dom, json, libxml, mbstring, tokenizer, xml, xmlwriter extensions … not available". It only passed where those modules are compiled statically (macOS Homebrew). The harness now re-execs without `-n`, keeping the system ini, and still loads the local build via `-d extension=`. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

### Added

- **alef-core: `CallConfig.options_type`.** The binding-agnostic config parameter type for an e2e call can now be declared once at call level (`[e2e.calls.<name>].options_type`) instead of being duplicated across every per-language override. The field was already present in `kreuzberg`'s `alef.toml` but silently discarded because the model lacked it. (`src/core/config/e2e.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/java: keep engine handle in streaming adapter calls.** Streaming adapters expose free-function facades (`Kreuzcrawl.crawlStream(engine, req)`) that take the engine handle as the first positional parameter, not methods on the handle itself. The Java e2e generator was filtering out handle-type args whenever an adapter had an `owner_type`, but streaming adapters are special: they still need the handle. The filter now checks the adapter's `pattern` and only drops the handle for non-streaming adapters with `owner_type`. This fixes `cannot find symbol` errors for `crawl_stream` and `batch_crawl_stream` streaming calls. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: emit `mock_url_list` arguments for batch streaming calls.** The `batch_crawl_stream` call requires a `urls: List<String>` parameter, but the Dart e2e generator had no handler for the `mock_url_list` arg type — the argument list fell through without emitting the URLs. Added a `mock_url_list` case that builds a Dart list literal with the same mock-server base-URL resolution as `mock_url` (per-fixture env var, then global `MOCK_SERVER_URL`). This fixes `too few positional arguments` errors for batch streaming calls. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: emit scalar integer and numeric arguments positionally.** The Dart e2e generator had no handler for `int`, `float`, `bool`, and other scalar arg types — they fell through to the catch-all `_ => {}` pattern without being added to the argument list. This caused calls with mixed positional args (e.g. `pdf_bytes: bytes`, `page_index: int`) to emit the first arg only, producing "too few positional arguments" errors at Dart compile time. Added handlers for `"int"`, `"integer"`, `"i64"`, `"float"`, `"number"`, `"bool"`, and `"boolean"` types that emit scalar values positionally as Dart literals, with appropriate defaults for missing/null required args. Added regression test `dart_int_positional_arg` that verifies both bytes and int args are emitted in order. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`, `src/e2e/tests/dart_int_positional_arg.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-swift: trait-bridge result enums are emitted as first-class Swift enums without FFI from_json functions.** Trait-bridge result types (e.g. `VisitResult`) are first-class Swift enums that JSON-decode locally and never cross the FFI boundary. Previously, the Rust-crate emitter declared them in the swift-bridge `extern` block, causing swift-bridge to auto-generate a second opaque `class VisitResult` that collided with the enum. The fix skips both the `extern` type declaration and the `from_json` function declaration for result-type enums, while the Swift-side emitter now emits them as native Swift enums without the `intoRust()` extension (since they decode locally, not via a Rust-side function). This resolves "ambiguous type lookup" errors in consumer Swift code. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e: bind `result_is_simple`/`result_is_tree` calls to the result variable, and emit Go `fmt`/`strings` imports, when an assertion's field is not a real struct member of the plain result type.** For calls returning a plain type (e.g. `embed_texts`, `detect_mime_type_from_bytes`), `count_equals`/`contains` assertions naming a non-member field (e.g. `embeddings` on a `Vec`) render against the result variable directly — but the binding/import predicates gated solely on `is_valid_for_result(field)`, so the call was bound to `_` (Rust) or the imports were omitted (Go), leaving `result.len()` / `fmt.Sprint(result)` referencing undefined names. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/test_file.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: keep an explicit `null` for an omitted optional middle argument, and honor the call-level `options_type`.** When a fixture omitted an optional positional arg (e.g. `extractFile`'s `mime_type`) followed by a `json_object` config arg that always emits a default, the config object was shifted into the wrong positional slot, causing a PHP `TypeError`. Separately, embed calls now resolve the config type from the call-level `options_type` (`EmbeddingConfig`) instead of falling back to the `ExtractionConfig` heuristic. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: emit `mime_type` positionally for facade extract methods.** `extractBytes`/`extractBytesSync`/`extractFile`/`extractFileSync` declare `mime_type` as a positional parameter, but the e2e generator emitted it as a `mimeType:` named argument, producing "too few positional arguments" errors and invalid named-then-positional calls. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: trait-bridge P/Invoke `EntryPoint` names now match the FFI exports.** `register`/`unregister`/`clear` entry points are derived as `{prefix}_{verb}_{trait_snake}` (matching the go and java backends) instead of using the unprefixed/plural `alef.toml` aliases, which name only the host-language wrappers — fixing `EntryPointNotFoundException` for `kreuzberg_clear_renderers` and similar. (`src/backends/csharp/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: facade `clear_*` methods now reference the singular FFI-derived `NativeLib` handle constant.** The `KreuzbergRs` facade body derived a free function's `MethodHandle` constant from `func.name`, so a trait-bridge `clear_fn` (the plural core Rust name, e.g. `clear_ocr_backends`) produced `KREUZBERG_CLEAR_OCR_BACKENDS` — but `NativeLib.java` declares the constant from the trait name as the singular `KREUZBERG_CLEAR_OCR_BACKEND` (matching the C FFI export `kreuzberg_clear_ocr_backend`). The mismatch failed Java compilation. The main-class emitter now maps each trait bridge's `clear_fn` to its singular handle constant and the facade body looks it up. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: apply float-literal post-processing in the JNI-mode emitter.** The `kotlin-android` backend emits via its own `gen_bindings::emit` path and never ran the `fix_float_literals` post-processor, so a `Double` field default like `32.0` rendered as `32` — invalid Kotlin (`Initializer type mismatch: expected 'Double', actual 'Int'`). (`src/backends/kotlin-android/lib.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/ruby: stop the scaffold from clobbering the magnus-generated gem entry point.** The Ruby scaffold emitted `packages/ruby/lib/<gem>.rb` containing only `require '<ext>_rb'`, overwriting the correct entry point the magnus backend's `gen_bindings` emits — which `require_relative`s `<gem>/native` (the `module <Module>` re-export) and `<gem>/version`. The on-disk entry point therefore loaded the native extension (`<Module>Rs`) but never defined the public `<Module>` constant, so `require '<gem>'` left `<Module>.convert` undefined and every consumer e2e spec failed with `NameError: uninitialized constant <Module>`. The scaffold no longer emits `lib/<gem>.rb`; the magnus backend is its sole owner. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/ruby.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: import `kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.milliseconds` when a Duration default is emitted.** A `Duration`-typed field with an integer default is rendered as `<n>.milliseconds` to match the declared `kotlin.time.Duration` type, but the generated file only imported `kotlin.time.Duration` — so `.milliseconds` was an unresolved reference and the binding failed to compile. The data-class emitter now adds `import kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.milliseconds` whenever a rendered field default uses the `.milliseconds` extension. Applies to the `kotlin-android` backend too (shared `object_wrapper.rs`). (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit `@unchecked Sendable` for opaque result types returned across the bridge.** The catch-all `@unchecked Sendable` emission filtered `api.types` by the IR `is_opaque` flag, which is false for a result struct like `ScrapeResult` even though swift-bridge still bridges it as an opaque `RustBridge.ScrapeResult` class — and async forwarders return that class out of a `Task.detached`, so Swift 6 strict-concurrency rejected the binding with `type 'ScrapeResult' does not conform to the 'Sendable' protocol`. The emission now also covers `compute_handle_returned_types` (every type returned across the bridge as an opaque handle), and a shared `sendable_emitted` set prevents the streaming-specific emissions and the catch-all block from declaring the same conformance twice (Swift warns on a redundant conformance). (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

## [0.17.34] - 2026-05-22

### Added

- **alef php: generate streaming facade methods and request types**, giving PHP bindings a first-class streaming surface, and use snake_case adapter names for Rust method calls. (`src/backends/php/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef scaffold: LICENSE sync plus Dart `pubspec.yaml` and CHANGELOG fixes.** (`src/scaffold`)

### Fixed

- **alef pyo3: keyword-escape and apply signature defaults in serde-rename constructors**, and emit a non-optional duration struct field as `u64` in constructor params. (`src/backends/pyo3`)
- **alef csharp: use the plural `clear_fn` name (not the trait name) for facade methods.** (`src/backends/csharp`)
- **alef java: throw `clear_fn` errors via the `(int, String)` constructor.** (`src/backends/java`)
- **alef cli: prepend the lib dir to the search-path env var in the e2e runner.** (`src/cli`)
- **e2e codegen fixes across go, c, zig, csharp, php, java, dart** — Go sum-type/import-alias decoding, C/Zig/C# compile errors, PHP fixture-key and config-type qualification, Java streaming request-type wrapping and `.size()` assertions, Dart scalar positional args. (`src/e2e/codegen`)

## [0.17.33] - 2026-05-22

### Fixed

- **alef go: decode streaming sum-type items via `Unmarshal<Type>`.** (`src/backends/go`)
- **alef csharp: emit `Clear*` facade methods on the wrapper class for trait bridges.** (`src/backends/csharp`)
- **alef rustler: use `Mix.env()` for the `force_build` predicate.** (`src/backends/rustler`)
- **alef ruby scaffold: exclude generated binding code from RuboCop linting.** (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)
- **e2e codegen fixes across r, php, ruby, typescript, go** — corrections for simple types and optionals; keep the system `php.ini` in the PHPUnit harness. (`src/e2e/codegen`)

## [0.17.32] - 2026-05-22

### Fixed

- **alef swift trait-bridge: emit result enums as first-class Swift enums** and refresh opaque-result `Sendable` conformance. (`src/backends/swift`)
- **alef java: reference the singular FFI clear handle in facade method bodies.** (`src/backends/java`)
- **alef kotlin, swift: repair the `Duration` import and opaque-result `Sendable` conformance.**
- **alef ruby scaffold: stop clobbering the Magnus gem entry point.** (`src/scaffold/languages/ruby.rs`)
- **e2e codegen fixes across java, dart** — keep the engine handle in Java streaming calls and emit the Dart url-list argument. (`src/e2e/codegen`)

## [0.17.31] - 2026-05-22

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-swift: don't emit a swift-bridge opaque `extern` class for trait-bridge result enums.** A trait-bridge result type like `VisitResult` is a first-class Swift `enum` used only inside the visitor trait protocol — it never crosses the FFI boundary as an opaque handle. It was nonetheless declared as `type VisitResult;` in the swift-bridge `extern` block, so swift-bridge generated a second opaque `class VisitResult: VisitResultRefMut` that collided with the enum, making every `VisitResult` reference ambiguous and breaking compilation of consumer Swift code (e.g. html-to-markdown's Swift e2e suite). The Rust-crate emitter now collects trait-bridge result-enum names and skips their `extern`-block declaration. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-extendr: keep generated R within lintr's 120-character line limit.** Two emitters produced over-long lines that failed `lintr` in consumer repos: the `from_json` factory template emitted the whole `.Call(...)` invocation on one line (exceeding 120 chars for longer type names), and roxygen `@field` doc lines concatenated long field descriptions past the limit. The factory template now wraps the `.Call()` body onto its own indented line, and `r_field_one_liner()` truncates over-long `@field` descriptions at a word boundary. (`src/backends/extendr/templates/r_from_json_factory.jinja`, `src/backends/extendr/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/typescript: pin the e2e `vitest` devDependency to the shared `template_versions` constant.** The TypeScript e2e `package.json` template hardcoded `vitest ^3.0.0` while the WASM e2e template already used `template_versions::npm::VITEST` (`^4.1.5`). A regen therefore downgraded `e2e/<consumer>/package.json` out of sync with the WASM suite and with any committed `pnpm-lock.yaml` resolved against vitest 4, breaking `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in CI. Both e2e templates now source the same constant. (`src/e2e/templates/typescript/package.json.jinja`, `src/e2e/codegen/typescript/config.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: stop emitting unreachable duplicate clauses for functions with a trailing optional parameter.** A function with N required parameters plus one trailing optional parameter is emitted as two explicit arity clauses (`f(a)` and `f(a, b)`). The longer clause additionally carried a `\\ nil` positional default on the optional parameter, which makes Elixir generate an _implicit_ lower-arity head that collides with the explicitly-emitted shorter clause — `this clause cannot match because a previous clause always matches`, fatal under `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`. Positional defaults are now applied only when the function emits a single clause. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

## [0.17.30] - 2026-05-22

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-java: stop emitting broken duplicate streaming methods on the FFI and facade classes.** A streaming adapter with an `owner_type` is bound as an instance method on the owner's opaque-handle class (`types.rs::gen_streaming_method`) — that is the only class with the `handle` field and the `STREAM_MAPPER` / `checkLastFfiError` helpers in scope. The FFI class also emitted the same `streaming_iterator_method.jinja` body (referencing the absent `this.handle` / `STREAM_MAPPER` / `checkLastFfiError`), and the facade emitted a `static` method that delegated to that instance method — both uncompilable. Removed the streaming emission from `ffi_class.rs` and `facade.rs`; the opaque-handle class is the sole owner-typed streaming surface. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`, `src/backends/java/templates/facade_class.jinja`)

## [0.17.29] - 2026-05-22

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-wasm: add `serde` to the generated crate's `cargo-machete` ignore list.** The WASM `Cargo.toml` declares `serde` unconditionally, but `serde` is only referenced when a config type gets an `Input` DTO (`#[derive(serde::Deserialize)]`). Consumers whose config types do not trigger an Input DTO (e.g. types not ending in `Config`) therefore failed `cargo machete` with `serde` flagged as unused. Added `serde` to the `[package.metadata.cargo-machete] ignored` array alongside the other conditionally-used dependencies (`futures`, `serde_json`, …). (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: generate compilable `Vec<DTO>` contains assertions.** The Dart `contains` aggregator for `Vec<T>` fields emitted swift-bridge-style method-call accessors (`item.kind()`) and a hardcoded list of 17 candidate accessor names probed under `on NoSuchMethodError`. Dart is statically typed and flutter_rust_bridge renders struct DTOs as plain classes with `final` fields, so `item.kind()` (invoking a non-callable field) and `item.source()` (a field the type lacks) are compile errors, not runtime misses — `process_test.dart` failed to load entirely. Fixed by: (1) deriving the per-call root type from the call config `result_type` override (mirroring `swift_call_result_type`) so the IR-driven stringy-field path is selected instead of the catch-all fallback; (2) emitting property reads with no parens; (3) replacing the uncompilable hardcoded-accessor fallback with a whole-object stringification match; (4) making the substring match case-insensitive so enum/sealed-class fields (`StructureKind.function()`) match PascalCase fixture node-type values (`Function`). (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: treat C `<stdint.h>` field-type overrides as primitives, not opaque handles.** `is_primitive_c_type_override` only recognised Rust primitive spellings (`u64`, `i32`, …), so a `fields_c_types` override naming the C type directly (`uint16_t`, `uint32_t`, `int32_t`, …) was misclassified as an opaque handle: the field accessor emitted `-> *mut <crate>::uint16_t` and a bare `null` return, producing dozens of `cannot find type` / `cannot find value` errors in generated `*-ffi` crates. Added the C `<stdint.h>` / scalar spellings to the primitive set, and corrected the opaque-handle null sentinel from the invalid bare `null` to `std::ptr::null_mut()`. (`src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: fix the camelCase Input DTO for config types with `Named` fields or no `Default` impl.** The data-driven WASM Input DTO had two compile-breaking bugs. (1) `Named` field types were emitted with their bare core name (`AuthHeaderFormat`), unresolved in the binding crate — they are now core-qualified (`{core_import}::{name}`), matching the core type the DTO converts into. (2) The `From<Input>` body unconditionally started from `Self::default()`, so any config type that does not implement `Default` (`CustomProviderConfig`) failed with `no function ... named 'default'`. The body now branches on the IR `TypeDef::has_default` flag: `Self::default()` + field overrides when the core type is `Default`, otherwise a direct struct literal with per-field `.map(Into::into).unwrap_or_default()`. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/wasm/templates/gen_input_dto.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: emit opaque-handle field accessors when the field type is overridden to a struct.** When an e2e config maps a field accessor's return type to a non-scalar struct via `fields_c_types` (e.g. a getter yielding a `CitationResult`), the FFI backend set the C function's return type to `*mut CitationResult` but still generated the body from the original scalar field type — producing `obj.field as i32` against a pointer return, a C `incompatible integer to pointer conversion` error. The body generator now receives the override info and emits a handle-shaped body for opaque-handle overrides. (`src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: place the `async` keyword before the return type in free-function forwarders.** Async free-function forwarders were emitted as `func f(...) throws -> T async {`, which is invalid Swift — `async` must precede `throws` and the return type (`func f(...) async throws -> T {`). The generated Swift module failed to compile, so every `Kreuzcrawl.scrape(...)`-style call reported `module has no member named 'scrape'`. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/java: fix streaming-adapter call generation.** Java e2e tests for streaming adapters emitted `Module.crawlStream(engine, req)` — passing the engine handle separately even though the streaming facade method takes only the request object (the handle is encapsulated by the adapter) — and typed the collected-chunk `ArrayList` with the default `ChatCompletionChunk` instead of the adapter's declared `item_type`. Both caused `cannot find symbol` compile errors. The handle arg is now filtered out when an `owner_type` adapter is present, and the collected-chunk list is typed from `adapter.item_type`. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/csharp: probe a preset `MOCK_SERVER_URL` for reachability before trusting it.** The generated C# `TestSetup.cs` used a preset `MOCK_SERVER_URL` environment variable without checking the server was alive. When a test harness sourced a stale `.mock-server.env` from a previous run, every HTTP fixture request failed with `net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED`. `TestSetup.cs` now makes a short-timeout TCP connection to the preset URL and, if it is unreachable, spawns a fresh mock-server — staying backward-compatible with harnesses that pre-spawn a reachable server. (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: disable NuGet audit in the generated compile smoke-test project.** The
  regression test that builds generated C# bindings should verify generated source compatibility,
  not require network access to nuget.org vulnerability metadata. Setting `NuGetAudit=false` keeps
  the test stable in offline CI/local runs while preserving warnings-as-errors for generated code.
  (`src/backends/csharp/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: ignore the e2e `"skip"` sentinel in field-accessor C-type overrides.** The FFI field-accessor generator consults the e2e `fields_c_types` map for a C-type override. That map also carries a `"skip"` sentinel meaning "omit this field from generated C e2e assertions" — not a C type. The generator treated `"skip"` as an opaque-handle type name and emitted `-> *mut <crate>::skip` plus a bare `null` return, breaking compilation of every `*-ffi` crate whose e2e config skips any field. The sentinel is now filtered out before the override is applied, so the accessor falls through to normal IR-driven generation. (`src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: declare streaming-adapter native methods on the generated FFI class.** The Java facade emitted `crawlStream(...)` / `batchCrawlStream(...)` calls against the `*Rs` native-method class, but the `*Rs` class only declared `native` methods for plain functions — streaming adapters were skipped — so the binding failed to compile with `cannot find symbol`. The FFI-class generator now iterates `config.adapters` and emits a matching `native` method declaration (request DTO in, streaming handle out) for every streaming adapter, with all type names derived from `adapter.request_type` / `item_type` / `owner_type` / `name`. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: wrap integer defaults for `Duration`-typed fields.** A config field whose Rust core type is `std::time::Duration` is rendered as `kotlin.time.Duration`, but `render_kotlin_default` returned an `IntLiteral` default as a bare integer, producing `Initializer type mismatch: expected 'Duration', actual 'Int'`. The default for a `TypeRef::Duration` field is now wrapped with the `.milliseconds` extension so the value matches the declared type. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: make generated opaque wrapper classes conform to `Sendable`.** Async free-function forwarders wrap blocking Rust calls in `Task.detached { … }.value`, which requires the returned type to be `Sendable`. Generated opaque wrapper classes (`public class T: TRefMut`) did not conform, so Swift 6 strict-concurrency rejected the binding with `type 'T' does not conform to the 'Sendable' protocol`. The backend now emits an `extension RustBridge.T: @unchecked Sendable {}` for every generated opaque type — sound because the type wraps a `Send + Sync` Rust pointer. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: convert non-`Into`-bridged Input DTO fields with an explicit constructor.** The camelCase Input DTO's `From<Input>` body assigned every field with a blanket `v.into()`. That works for matching types and for `Option<T>: From<T>`, but not for core fields whose DTO spelling has no `From` impl — `Duration` (DTO `u64` milliseconds) and `PathBuf` (DTO `String`) failed with `the trait From<u64>/From<String> is not satisfied`. Each field's conversion expression is now derived from its IR type: `Duration` via `std::time::Duration::from_millis`, `Path` via `std::path::PathBuf::from`, both wrapped in `Into::into` so they remain valid whether the core field is `T` or `Option<T>`; all other types keep `v.into()`. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/wasm/templates/gen_input_dto.jinja`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: silence `clippy::too_many_arguments` on `render_test_file`.** The Dart e2e `render_test_file` helper grew to eight parameters, tripping the workspace `-D warnings` clippy gate. Annotated with `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`, matching the convention used elsewhere in the codegen crates. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

## [0.17.28] - 2026-05-22

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/go: emit the `testify/assert` import only when the rendered body uses it.** The Go e2e generator predicted whether `testify` was needed from a heuristic over fixture assertion shapes and categories. The heuristic produced false positives — importing `testify/assert` into generated test files whose tests only use `t.Errorf`/`t.Fatalf` — which fails Go's `imported and not used` compile check and breaks the entire Go e2e build. Removed the heuristic; the import is now decided purely empirically: the file body is rendered into a buffer first, then the import is emitted only if that buffer contains an `assert.` reference. (`src/e2e/codegen/go.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: name the public streaming wrapper after the adapter, not the owner type.** The high-level `Stream.unfold` wrapper for a streaming adapter was emitted as `{owner}_{adapter}` (e.g. `crawlenginehandle_crawl_stream`), so callers could not reach it as `Module.crawl_stream/2` the way every other binding exposes a streaming adapter — the e2e suite failed with `function Module.crawl_stream/2 is undefined`. The public wrapper is now named after the adapter (`crawl_stream`); the owner-prefixed form is retained only for the internal `_start`/`_next` NIF delegates. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/csharp: coerce nullable string expressions in `not_empty`/`is_empty` assertions.** `string.IsNullOrEmpty({expr})` was emitted for string assertions where `{expr}` may be a nullable chain such as `result.Markdown!.Citations?.ToString()`. The nullable result cannot be passed to `string.IsNullOrEmpty`, producing a C# compile error. Coerce the expression with `?? string.Empty`, matching the pattern already used for the `contains` assertion. (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/r: stop scaffolding from clobbering the generated R `NAMESPACE`.** `scaffold_r_cargo` unconditionally emitted a `packages/r/NAMESPACE` containing only the `useDynLib(...)` directive — no `export(...)` / `S3method(...)` entries. Because `alef all` runs scaffolding after the extendr backend's `generate_public_api` (which emits the full NAMESPACE via `gen_namespace`), the scaffold stub overwrote the real file on every run, leaving the installed R package with zero exported symbols — `library(htmltomarkdown)` exposed nothing and every call raised `could not find function`. Removed the NAMESPACE `GeneratedFile` from `scaffold_r_cargo`; the extendr backend is the sole owner of NAMESPACE and regenerates it in full each run. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/r.rs`)

- **alef workspace: restore CI test coverage after backend generator regressions.** Fixed C# enum/default-field emission, Java builder `@JsonProperty` annotations for single-word fields, Kotlin Android trait interface files, Rustler `force_build` line wrapping, FFI field-accessor type overrides, Swift snapshot drift, and Zig e2e JSON wrapping so the affected workspace tests compile and pass again. (`src/backends/csharp`, `src/backends/java`, `src/backends/kotlin-android`, `src/backends/rustler`, `src/backends/ffi`, `src/backends/swift`, `crates/alef-e2e`)

- **alef-backend-wasm/ffi/java: skip top-level emission of trait-bridge `clear_fn` functions to prevent duplicate-symbol errors.** `clear_fn` functions (e.g. `clear_ocr_backends`) have no `dyn Trait` parameter, so `find_bridge_param` returns `None` and the function fell through to the regular top-level emitter in each backend. The trait-bridge layer also emits these functions independently. In the WASM backend this produced two `#[wasm_bindgen(js_name = "clearXxx")]` exports in the same namespace (the bridge module is glob-re-exported via `pub use __alef_wasm_bridge_*::*`), causing wasm-bindgen to error with `"symbol already defined"`. The FFI backend emitted duplicate C symbols, and the Java backend's `trait_bridge_handles` skip-set omitted clear-function handle names, allowing a duplicate method handle through the dedup filter. Applied the `is_trait_bridge_managed_fn` guard at the top of the `api.functions` loop in alef-backend-wasm and alef-backend-ffi, and extended `trait_bridge_handles` in alef-backend-java to pre-register clear-function handle names. The extendr (R) backend was already protected — `collect_trait_bridge_fn_names` includes `clear_fn` names and the functions-loop early-continue covers them. pyo3 and csharp are unaffected. Fixes #100. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/native_lib.rs`)

- **publish workflow: make crates.io and CLI binary publishing parallel.** Removed `macos-x86_64` and `windows-x86_64` from the CLI matrix, and decoupled `build-cli` from `publish-crates` so crates.io publishing no longer gates binary builds. Homebrew bottle publishing already consumes only `cli-macos-arm64`, so the target removal drops only standalone release binaries and leaves the Homebrew path unchanged. (`.github/workflows/publish.yaml`)

- **alef-backend-go: resolve passthrough-enum struct fields to their generated named type instead of `*json.RawMessage`.** `gen_struct_type` classified any `Named` field type absent from `enum_names`, `data_enum_names`, and `struct_names` as an unresolved external type and emitted the fallback `*json.RawMessage`. Passthrough enums (untagged Rust unions like `StopSequence`, emitted as a `json.RawMessage`-backed named Go type) live only in `passthrough_enum_names`, so they were misclassified. Meanwhile the generated `UnmarshalJSON` raw-mirror struct resolves the same field via `go_optional_type`, which yields the proper `*StopSequence`. The two diverged and `s.Field = raw.Field` failed to type-check (`cannot use raw.Stop (variable of type *StopSequence) as *json.RawMessage value`). Fixed by threading `passthrough_enum_names` into `gen_struct_type` and its `is_unresolved_named` check, so every passthrough-union field resolves to its generated named type and matches the raw mirror. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: emit camelCase `#[php(name = ...)]` on static methods so PSR-12 callers resolve.** The static-method templates used a single `{{ name }}` placeholder for both the `#[php(name)]` attribute and the Rust `pub fn` ident, forcing the PHP-facing method name to the raw snake_case IR name. An explicit `#[php(name)]` overrides ext-php-rs's automatic snake→camel conversion, so the extension class registered e.g. `create_client` while the userland facade and stubs call `createClient` — `Call to undefined method` at runtime. Fixed by computing a separate camelCase `php_name` for the `#[php(name)]` attribute while keeping the Rust ident snake_case, mirroring the existing streaming-adapter pattern. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/php/templates/php_{,async_}static_method_definition_{no_,with_}params.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: suppress `clippy::await_holding_refcell_ref` in generated bindings.** The generated WASM streaming iterator holds a `RefCell` borrow of its `mpsc::Receiver` across the `.await` in `next()` — unavoidable, since `Receiver::next()` borrows the receiver, and sound because WASM is single-threaded. Under `-D warnings` this tripped `clippy::await_holding_refcell_ref` and failed the clippy gate in consumer crates. Added the lint to the crate-level `#![allow(...)]` block the wasm backend already emits. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/c: apply the cbindgen type-name prefix to streaming opaque-handle types.** The C streaming e2e test referenced the opaque handle as `{Pascal}DefaultClientChatStreamStreamHandle`, but cbindgen prepends the configured uppercase `prefix` (e.g. `LITERLLM`) to every exported type — as it already does for ordinary opaque handles. The streaming-handle emission omitted the prefix, producing `unknown type name` compile errors in `test_streaming.c` and `test_smoke.c`. Fixed by prepending `prefix_upper` to the streaming-handle type name. (`src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: pass named arguments for FRB-generated client methods.** FRB emits every non-`config` parameter of a generated client method as a Dart named-required parameter (e.g. `retrieveFile({required String fileId})`). The e2e codegen passed required string arguments positionally, which Dart rejects (`Too many positional arguments`), failing whole test files to load. Fixed by detecting the `client_factory` routing and emitting `name: value` for required string args on those calls, while keeping positional args for hand-written facade methods. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

## [0.17.27] - 2026-05-22

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-jni: drop redundant parentheses around optional-param call expressions.** The JNI shim emitter wrapped optional `String` and primitive call-site expressions in parentheses (`(if x.is_empty() { None } else { Some(x) })`). As a complete function argument these parentheses are unnecessary and trip rustc's `unused_parens` lint, which fails the `-D warnings` clippy gate in consumer crates' generated `*-jni` bindings. Emit the bare `if/else` expression instead. (`src/backends/jni/src/gen_shims.rs`)

- **alef workspace: restore clean clippy and pre-commit verification.** Removed an unused Kotlin Android backend dependency and adjusted the R e2e assertion renderer to avoid clippy's argument-count warning while preserving generated assertion behavior. This keeps `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` and `prek run --all-files` clean before release. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/Cargo.toml`, `src/e2e/codegen/r.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/python: fix F841 unused variable when all assertions are skipped.** Python e2e codegen was assigning the result variable even when every assertion was skipped (because the field didn't exist on the result type), causing ruff F841 "local variable assigned but never used" errors. The bug occurred because the detection of result variable usage was a naive substring search on the rendered assertion buffer, which would match the variable name inside skipped comment text like "# skipped: field 'result' not available on result type". Fixed by checking only non-comment lines when detecting if the result variable is actually used in code: `temp_assertions.lines().any(|line| !line.trim().starts_with('#') && line.contains(result_var))`. When no assertions actually use the variable, it is now assigned to `_` (underscore), which correctly indicates intentional discard. Fixes 8 Python e2e F841 linting errors across test_engine.py, test_filter.py, and test_redirect.py. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/test_function.rs`)

- **alef-e2e: enable `mock_url_list` argument aliasing in streaming call selection.** The `select_best_matching_call()` function was using only the primary field name (e.g., `batch_urls`) when checking if call arguments could be satisfied from fixture input. For `mock_url_list` arguments, fixtures often use aliased names (e.g., `urls` instead of `batch_urls`), so calls with `batch_urls` were marked as unsatisfiable even when the fixture provided `urls`. This caused streaming fixtures like `batch_crawl_stream_events` (which provide `input.urls`) to fail routing to `batch_crawl_stream` (which requires `batch_urls`) and incorrectly fall back to the default call. Fixed by using `resolve_urls_field()` (which already handles aliasing) in `select_best_matching_call()` when the argument type is `mock_url_list`. Now both direct matches and aliased field names are recognized, ensuring correct call routing for all streaming batch functions. (`src/e2e/codegen/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-dart: fix streaming adapter method resolution in generated facade.** The Dart facade's streaming methods (e.g., `crawlStream`, `batchCrawlStream`) were calling `rust_bridge.crawlStream(...)` instead of delegating to the correct instance method on the opaque handle parameter. This caused "isn't defined" analyzer errors because FRB emits streaming functions only as instance methods on the abstract API class, not as top-level bridge functions. Fixed by: (1) updating `dart_streaming_method.jinja` to call `{{ owner_param }}.{{ method_name }}(...)` instead of `rust_bridge.{{ method_name }}(...)`; (2) extracting `owner_type` and deriving `owner_param` in `emit_streaming_adapter_methods()` so the template context receives the correct parameter name. The fix is generic across all streaming adapters and does not hardcode language-specific types. (`src/backends/dart/templates/dart_streaming_method.jinja`, `src/backends/dart/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-extract: skip underscore-prefixed `pub fn`s when building the API surface.** A leading underscore on a `pub fn` is the Rust convention for "public for technical reasons but not part of the supported API" — for example test-only helpers like `_testing_extract_languages` that are gated behind a `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-internals"))]` attribute so integration tests in a separate crate can reach them. The extractor scans source syntactically and does not evaluate `#[cfg]`, so it picked these methods up as ordinary public API and generated bindings (and API-reference docs) for them across every backend. The generated callers then failed to compile because the underlying method does not exist without the test feature enabled. Fixed by skipping both inherent impl methods and free functions whose name starts with `_` during extraction, so they never reach any backend or the docs generator. (`src/extract/extractor/functions.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: stop emitting the broken `*Input` config DTO.** The WASM backend generated a camelCase `*Input` deserialization struct for config-like function parameters, but the implementation (`gen_input_dto_for_type`) hard-coded the field list per type instead of reading the real struct definition. For `ProcessConfig` this produced uncompilable code: `#[derive(serde::Deserialize)]` referencing a `serde` crate that is not a WASM-crate dependency, `Option<bool>` fields assigned directly into the core type's `bool` fields, and a `String` field assigned into a `Cow<'static, str>` — eleven compile errors. The Input DTO never compiled and never shipped. Disabled it (`should_have_input_dto` now returns `false`); config-like parameters are deserialized directly into the core type via `serde_wasm_bindgen`, the behavior that shipped before the stub was added. A data-driven Input DTO that reads fields from the `ApiSurface` can be reintroduced later. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: add `LongMethod` to the generated `@file:Suppress` list.** Enum type files emit `toWire()`/`fromWire()` conversion functions as exhaustive `when` expressions — one arm per variant. For wide enums (e.g. `NodeType` with ~70 variants) these exceed detekt's default `LongMethod` threshold of 60 lines (90+ lines observed), failing `detekt --build-upon-default-config` in consumer repos. These functions are inherently long machine-generated mappings that cannot be sensibly split, so `LongMethod` is suppressed alongside the existing `CyclomaticComplexMethod`/`MaxLineLength`/`TooManyFunctions` file-level suppressions. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/swift: qualify helper function calls with module name and inherit `options_type` from call config.** Swift e2e tests were calling unqualified helper functions like `extractionConfigFromJson(...)` in files that imported both Kreuzberg and RustBridge modules. Since both modules export the same functions, this caused "ambiguous use" compile errors. Additionally, Swift had no per-language override for `options_type`, so embedding tests were incorrectly using `extractionConfigFromJson` instead of `embeddingConfigFromJson`. Fixed by: (1) qualifying all helper function calls in test setup with the module name (e.g., `Kreuzberg.embeddingConfigFromJson(...)`), eliminating name collisions; (2) adding Swift-specific overrides for `embed_texts` and `embed_texts_async` calls that set `options_type = "EmbeddingConfig"`, matching configurations for other languages. Fixes Swift e2e compile errors: 5 "ambiguous use of extractionConfigFromJson", 4 "cannot convert value of type 'ExtractionConfig' to expected argument type 'EmbeddingConfig'". (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`, `alef.toml` in kreuzberg repo)

- **alef-backend-rustler: enable force_build in test/dev to skip artifact downloads for unpublished versions.** The rustler_precompiled library attempts downloading precompiled artifacts before falling back to local compilation. For unreleased versions (e.g., v5.0.0-rc.1), this causes repeated 404 errors and unnecessary network latency. Fixed by adding `System.get_env("MIX_ENV") in ["test", "dev"]` to the `force_build` condition, ensuring test/dev environments skip download attempts entirely and compile from source immediately. The `{{ build_env_var }}` env var override is still available for production use. Ensures fast, reliable e2e test builds even when release artifacts aren't published. (`src/backends/rustler/src/template_env.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: re-export JSON deserialization helpers and add ExtractionResult property accessors with String conversion.** Swift e2e test files were calling JSON helper functions (`extractionConfigFromJson()`, `batchBytesItemFromJson()`, `batchFileItemFromJson()`) without importing RustBridge, and accessing ExtractionResult fields as properties (`.mimeType`, `.content`) with String methods (`.contains()`, `.count`). The e2e codegen intentionally avoids `import RustBridge` to prevent name collisions with first-class Swift types like `VisitResult`. Fixed by: (1) adding public re-export forwarders in the Kreuzberg module for all three JSON helper functions; (2) adding computed property accessors on ExtractionResultRef (`var mimeType: String`, `var content: String`) that convert RustString to String via `.toString()`, enabling test code to use Swift String methods directly. Fixes Swift e2e compile errors: "cannot find 'extractionConfigFromJson' in scope", "cannot find 'RustBridge' in scope", "value of type 'RustString' has no member 'contains'", and "value of type 'RustString' has no member 'count'". (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

## [0.17.26] - 2026-05-21

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/python: fix NameError when assertions reference undeclared result variable.** When Python e2e test fixtures had assertions but the code generator computed `has_usable_assertion = false` (edge case due to upstream miscomputation of assertion usability), the result binding would be discarded into `_` while subsequent assertions still tried to read the `result` variable. This caused NameError: "name 'result' is not defined" at test runtime. Fixed by always binding to `result_var` when there are any assertions in the fixture, even if `has_usable_assertion` is false. The variable check is now: if `fixture.assertions` is non-empty, bind the result; only use `_` discard when there are truly no assertions. Fixes Python e2e NameError on 3 tests: test_embed_texts_async_*, test_embed_texts_different_preset. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/test_function.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: skip trait interface emission for Android AAR.** The Android backend was unconditionally calling `emit_trait_interfaces()`, generating Kotlin trait interface files (e.g., `IDocumentExtractor.kt`, `IRenderer.kt`) in the same package as Java trait interfaces (generated by a different backend or left from a previous build). This caused "Redeclaration" errors when the Gradle compiler encountered both the `.java` and `.kt` versions of the same interface with identical signatures. The Android AAR is a self-contained JNI library without a Java facade, so trait bridge interfaces are not needed — plugin implementations would be handled by a higher-level Kotlin/JVM layer outside the AAR. Fixed by removing the `emit_trait_interfaces()` call and replacing with a clarifying comment. Kotlin trait interfaces are emitted only by alef-backend-kotlin (JVM target). Fixes Kotlin Android e2e interface redeclaration errors for IDocumentExtractor, IRenderer, IOcrBackend, IEmbeddingBackend, IPostProcessor, and IValidator. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: fix `Vec<u8>` return type mapping to `ByteArray` in public facade functions.** The Android AAR's public wrapper functions were incorrectly mapping `Vec<u8>` (binary data) return types to `String` instead of `ByteArray`. This caused type mismatches at the Kotlin wrapper level when functions like `render_pdf_page_to_png` (which returns `Result<Vec<u8>>` for image bytes) were called. The root cause was that `jni_return_type_str()` didn't special-case `Vec<u8>` the way the JNI bridge emitter does. Fixed by adding explicit `Vec<u8>` → `ByteArray` handling in `jni_return_type_str()`, mirroring the JNI bridge's logic. Fixes Kotlin Android e2e compile error: "Return type mismatch: expected 'Long', actual 'ByteArray'" on renderPdfPageToPng. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/elixir: emit `func()` not `func(nil)` for zero-arg functions.** E2e test codegen was rendering `Kreuzberg.list_document_extractors(nil)` for registry functions that take no arguments, causing `FunctionClauseError` when the Rustler binding defines `def list_document_extractors()` without an optional parameter. The bug occurred because call configs (e.g., `[crates.e2e.calls.list_document_extractors]` with `args = []`) were falling back to the global `[crates.e2e.call]` args when the call's args were empty. Since `call_config.args = []` explicitly means "no args for this function," there should be no fallback. Fixed by always using the call config's args directly, never falling back to the global default args. Fixes 19 Elixir e2e test failures in registry operations (list_document_extractors, list_ocr_backends, list_validators, list_post_processors, list_renderers, list_embedding_backends, clear_* variants). (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: use `null` instead of `""` as builder default for Path-typed fields.** Builder field initialization was defaulting all non-optional String-like fields to `""`, including `java.nio.file.Path` fields. Since Path is an interface with no public constructor, assigning an empty string causes a compile error "incompatible types: java.lang.String cannot be converted to java.nio.file.Path". Fixed by special-casing `TypeRef::Path` to default to `null` — Jackson's builder only sets the field when present in deserialized JSON. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/csharp: fix sealed-union enum field assertions and chunk metadata navigation.** Three code generation bugs were emitting invalid C# assertions: (1) `FormatMetadata` (sealed union) was incorrectly configured as an `assert_enum_field` in alef.toml, causing `.Trim()` to be called on sealed union objects (which don't have `.Trim()`). Sealed unions should only be asserted via discriminated-union accessors (e.g., `metadata.format.excel.sheet_count`), not directly. Fixed by removing `assert_enum_fields = { "metadata.format" = "FormatMetadata" }` from alef.toml and removing the corresponding assertion from the smoke fixtures. (2) `chunks_have_heading_context` predicate was accessing `c.HeadingContext` directly instead of `c.Metadata?.HeadingContext`, causing "field not found" errors since HeadingContext lives on ChunkMetadata, not Chunk. (3) `first_chunk_starts_with_heading` used wrong property name; fixed to `c.Metadata?.HeadingContext`. Additionally enhanced `render_discriminated_union_assertion` to accept and apply `assert_enum_fields` for other enum fields within sealed unions. All assertions now correctly route through Metadata. Fixes C# e2e compile from 11 → 0 errors (81/88 tests now pass, runtime failures only). (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`, `alef.toml` in kreuzberg repo)

- **alef-e2e/python: inherit `options_type` from top-level call config.** The Python test function codegen resolved `options_type` only from per-call overrides, not from the base e2e.call config. This meant `[crates.e2e.calls.embed_texts] options_type = "EmbeddingConfig"` was ignored in Python, causing tests to emit ExtractionConfig instead of EmbeddingConfig. Fixed by falling back to the top-level `[crate] call.overrides.get("python")` options_type when the per-call override is absent, matching the C# codegen pattern. Also applied the same merge strategy to `options_via`. (`src/e2e/codegen/python/test_function.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: emit `#[php(name = ...)]` attribute on static methods in KreuzbergApi facade.** Methods generated in `#[php_impl]` facades were not being exposed to PHP because they lacked the required `#[php(...)]` annotation. Both sync (`php_static_method_definition_{no_,}with_params.jinja`) and async (`php_async_static_method_definition_{no_,}with_params.jinja`) method templates were updated to emit the annotation. Fixes all 14 PHP e2e method-not-found errors for `embed_texts_async`, `detect_mime_type_from_bytes`, `render_pdf_page_to_png`, and other KreuzbergApi methods. (`src/backends/php/templates/php_static_method_definition_{no_,}with_params.jinja`, `src/backends/php/templates/php_async_static_method_definition_{no_,}with_params.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin{,-android}: normalize integer-like float literals in Kotlin code.** Kotlin requires float literals to have a decimal point or exponent, but Rust's f64 `Display` trait drops trailing zeros, causing codegen to emit invalid Kotlin like `val field: Double = 32` instead of `val field: Double = 32.0`. Added post-processing in `generate_bindings()` for both JVM and Android backends to fix `: Double = <digit>` → `: Double = <digit>.0` and `: Float = <digit>f` → `: Float = <digit>.0f` via regex. Fixes kotlin_android e2e compile errors in OcrQualityThresholds, TesseractConfig, and other data classes with numeric defaults. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/lib.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/literal_normalizer.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: use `unwrap_or` instead of `unwrap_or_else(|_| …)` in default-construction string-like serde fallbacks.** Clippy's `redundant_closure` lint rejects `unwrap_or_else(|_| Value::String(s))` when the constructor is a cheap eager expression — under `-D warnings` this becomes a compile error in consumer crates' published builds. Switched both `default_field_string_like_optional_serde.jinja` and `default_field_string_like_serde.jinja` to the eager `unwrap_or(::serde_json::Value::String(...))` form. The borrow analysis is sound: `from_str(&s)` releases its borrow before the argument is evaluated. Fixes h2m CI clippy failures in alef-generated `packages/swift/rust/src/lib.rs`. (`src/backends/swift/templates/default_field_string_like_serde.jinja`, `src/backends/swift/templates/default_field_string_like_optional_serde.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-java: skip all streaming adapters in Java facade to prevent invalid method references.** Java streaming is not supported via the FFI layer (Panama FFM is inherently blocking), so the Java backend cannot implement streaming adapters. However, the facade codegen was unconditionally emitting wrapper methods like `crawlStream()` that tried to delegate to non-existent FFI layer implementations, causing compilation errors like "cannot find symbol: method crawlStream(...)". Fixed by replacing the adapter loop in the facade with an explicit skip of all streaming adapters, ensuring no wrapper methods are generated for Java bindings regardless of adapter configuration. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/csharp: inherit `assert_enum_fields` and `nested_types` from top-level call config when C# override is empty.** The C# codegen resolved `assert_enum_fields` (fields whose values are sealed unions requiring display-helper wrapping) and `nested_types` (fields whose values are custom types needing JsonSerializer.Deserialize) only from per-language overrides, not from the base e2e.call config. This caused (1) fields like `metadata.format` to skip display-helper wrapping, leading to `.Trim()` calls on non-string sealed union objects, and (2) fields like `embedding_model: "default"` to be treated as scalar strings instead of EmbeddingModelType instances. Fixed by falling back to the top-level config for both `assert_enum_fields` and `nested_types`, and building per-call effective mappings that merge top-level + C# overrides. (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/csharp: use `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>` for nested-type fields instead of non-existent `.FromJson()`.** When a fixture field was mapped to a `nested_type` (e.g., `embedding_model` → `EmbeddingModelType`), the codegen emitted `.FromJson(json_str)`, assuming a factory method. However, sealed records and custom JsonConverter types don't expose `.FromJson()`; deserialization must route through `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>`. Fixed by always emitting `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<{nested_type}>(json_str, ConfigOptions)!` for nested types, which works correctly with both custom converters and sealed unions. (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: collect types from internally-tagged enum variant fields.** The `collect_types_for_nif_derives` helper walks function/method/struct signatures to collect all types that need NifMap/NifStruct derives, then recursively walks their field types for the transitive closure. However, it did not walk enum variant fields, so types referenced only in enum variants (e.g., `TableGrid` in `NodeContent::Table { grid: TableGrid }`) were never included in `types_to_emit`. This caused 39+ `error[E0425]: cannot find type` errors when generating Elixir bindings with internally-tagged enums containing nested struct references. Fixed by seeding enum variant fields during initial type collection, before the transitive-closure loop. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

## [0.17.25] - 2026-05-21

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-rustler: add opaque types to NIF resource wrapper emission set.** The `collect_types_for_nif_derives` helper deliberately filters out opaque types (since they don't need NifMap/NifStruct derives), but the type-iteration loop that emits rustler::Resource wrappers expected them to be present in `types_to_emit`. This caused opaque structs like `CrawlEngineHandle` to never generate their `pub struct CrawlEngineHandle { inner: Arc<...> }` wrapper and `impl rustler::Resource for CrawlEngineHandle {}` impl block, resulting in `error[E0422]: cannot find struct "CrawlEngineHandle"` across all NIF functions that use them. Fixed by re-inserting opaque types into `types_to_emit` after the initial collection, before the type-iteration loop, so Resource wrappers are generated. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-adapters: qualify `owner_type` with `core_import` in streaming NIF + use `Deref` clone.** Streaming NIF wrappers referenced `owner_type` without qualifying it, causing `unresolved import` errors when the wrapper crate uses `core_import` re-exports. Now uses `{core_import}::{owner_type}` and dereferences resources via `&*resource` for the Clone path. (`crates/alef-adapters/src/streaming.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: qualify opaque-type refs with `core_import` in NIF signatures; use `Deref` instead of private `.inner` field.** Opaque types in NIF function signatures were emitted as bare identifiers (`fn foo(handle: &CrawlEngineHandle, …)`), failing to resolve when the wrapper crate uses `core_import`. Now emits `{core_import}::{type}` for the core-side type and `&*resource` for resource extraction (the `.inner` field is private to the wrapper crate). (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/`)

- **alef-e2e: prefix unused fn params with underscore to unblock `cargo install`.** Several test-helper functions in the codegen modules had unused params that triggered `-D warnings` only under `cargo install` (which builds release with deny-warnings). Prefixed with `_` to silence. (`src/e2e/codegen/{elixir,zig}.rs`)

## [0.17.24] - 2026-05-21

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/zig: use binding module name (not registry pkg name) for `root_source_file` path in test_apps build.zig.** The Zig test_app build.zig was referencing `../../packages/zig/src/{pkg_name}.zig` where `pkg_name` is the registry-display package name (e.g., `html_to_markdown`). But the alef-backend-zig emits the binding file at `src/{module_name}.zig` where `module_name` is the Rust crate's snake_case identifier (e.g., `html_to_markdown_rs`). When the two differ — which is the common case for crates whose Rust name carries a `-rs` suffix while the published package drops it — `zig build` fails with "no such file". Now uses `module_name` to match the actual emitted file path. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin/kotlin-android: synthesize `= Name()` default for non-nullable nested struct fields whose type has Rust `Default`.** Jackson's Kotlin module raises `MissingKotlinParameterException` when the wire JSON omits a non-nullable parameter (the common shape of partial-update payloads sent from test fixtures, e.g., `mapper.readValue("{\"autolinks\":false}", ConversionOptions::class.java)`). The previous emitter intentionally refused to synthesize `Name()` for `TypeRef::Named` fields with `DefaultValue::Empty` because it couldn't distinguish data classes from sealed classes and didn't know which data classes had all-default constructor params. Added a `default_constructible_types: &HashSet<String>` parameter threaded through `emit_type_with_imports → kotlin_field_default → render_kotlin_default`, populated by the kotlin-android backend with the set of non-trait, non-opaque types in the IR that have `has_default = true`. For named-type fields in this set the emitter now emits `= Name()`, which invokes the data class's synthesized no-arg constructor (every Kotlin field for these types also gets a default by the same logic). Sealed classes are still excluded — they're tracked in the existing `sealed_class_names` set. New public entry point `emit_type_pub_with_defaults_sealed_and_constructible`. Fixes html-to-markdown Kotlin Android e2e tests that pass partial-options JSON. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/src/lib.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/zig: emit local-path dependencies instead of invalid hashes for registry-mode e2e test apps.** Zig has no official package registry like PyPI/npm/Maven; when registry-mode test_apps were generated, the Zig codegen emitted a placeholder URL + dummy hash (`0000000...`) for the main dependency, which could never fetch. Fixed by always using local `.path` dependencies for Zig, regardless of `DependencyMode`, since Zig packages are resolved via filesystem paths or the Zig package manager (which is primarily git-based). Test apps continue to use relative paths that resolve correctly inside the generated directory structure. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: avoid double-caret in pubspec.yaml version constraints.** When `[crates.e2e.registry.packages.dart]` specifies a version constraint like `version = "^3.5.0"`, the Dart e2e codegen was unconditionally prepending another `^`, resulting in `^^3.5.0` in test_apps pubspec.yaml. The fix checks if the version already starts with a constraint operator (`^`, `~`, `>`, `<`, `=`) and only adds `^` if not. Downstream alef.toml configs can now express constraints directly; single-version specs still get `^` for flexible pinning. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: emit `@field:JsonSerialize(\`as\` = T::class)` (and `contentAs` for collections) for data-class fields whose declared type is a sealed class.** Jackson default-bean serialization dispatches serializer lookup by runtime variant subtype. Sealed-class variants (tagged or untagged) emit `@JsonSerialize(using = JsonSerializer.None::class)` to break deserializer recursion — which short-circuits to the default POJO serializer, bypassing the parent's custom `{Sealed}Serializer` and dropping the `"type"` discriminator. The new `@field:JsonSerialize(\`as\` = T::class)` annotation forces Jackson to use the declared static type, which still carries the custom serializer. Threaded a `sealed_class_names: &HashSet<String>` parameter through `emit_type_with_imports` via a new public helper `emit_type_pub_with_enum_defaults_and_sealed_classes`; the kotlin-android backend now builds the set from `serde_tag.is_some() || serde_untagged` enums and passes it. Fixes Rust-side `missing field type` failures for `OcrRequest.document`, `ChatCompletionRequest.responseFormat`/`messages`/`stop`/`toolChoice`, `RerankRequest.documents`, `UserMessage.content`, `ModerationRequest.input`, `EmbeddingRequest.input`. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/src/lib.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: emit `@JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true)` on data classes containing `#[serde(flatten)]` fields, and force the flatten field to nullable with default null.** Rust flatten fields collect unknown wire keys into the field's value, but Kotlin has no native equivalent — Jackson's default `FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES` made deserialization fail on every sibling key the flatten was supposed to absorb, and the flatten field itself failed `MissingKotlinParameterException` when missing from the wire JSON. The mitigation is lossy (flatten contents aren't captured) but unblocks deserialization. Fixes liter-llm `ResponseOutputItem` / `ResponseTool` 4-test cluster. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: explicit-loop serializer for `Vec<SealedClass>` newtype variants of untagged sealed classes.** When `UserContent::Parts(Vec<ContentPart>)` was serialized via `mapper.writeValue(gen, value.value)`, Jackson iterated the list and dispatched each element to its runtime-subtype serializer (which has `@JsonSerialize(using = None::class)` to break deserializer recursion) — losing the `"type"` discriminator. Switch to `provider.findValueSerializer(ContentPart::class.java).serialize(...)` in an explicit per-element loop. Local variables use plain names (`elemSerializer`, `elem`) to satisfy ktlint's `backing-property-naming` rule, which would flag double-underscore local names even though they're not class-level properties. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: `DefaultValue::Empty` no longer synthesises constructor calls for `Named` types or zero-values for optional fields.** Two regressions surfaced when the IR started tagging more fields with `DefaultValue::Empty`: (1) `Named(SealedClass)` fields produced `EmbeddingInput()` constructor calls — illegal because sealed classes have protected constructors; (2) optional fields like `dimensions: Int?` and `user: String?` got `0`/`""` defaults instead of `null` because the type-based default match saw the unwrapped inner type, not `Optional`. Fixed by (1) returning `None` from `render_kotlin_default` for non-enum `Named` types (fall through to type-based default), and (2) short-circuiting `kotlin_field_default` to `" = null"` whenever `optional == true && DefaultValue::Empty`. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`)
