- **alef-backend-php: emit `Box::default()` instead of `Box::new(Default::default())` for boxed fallback fields in flat-enum-to-core conversions.** `Box::new(T::default())` trips `clippy::box-default` ("`Box::new(_)` of default value") which is denied as a warning under `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`. The generated PHP umbrella crate (e.g. `kreuzcrawl-php`) failed CI Rust on the `CrawlEvent::Page { result: Box::new(Default::default()) }` fallback emitted by `flat_enum_to_core_match_body`. Replaced all three occurrences in `gen_bindings/types.rs` with `Box::default()` which delegates directly to `<Box<T> as Default>::default`. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/elixir: prefix unused result variables with `_` in e2e fixtures with no assertions.** When a fixture has zero assertions, the generated pattern match `{:ok, result} = ...` binds a variable that is never referenced, causing `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` to fail with `variable "result" is unused`. The Elixir e2e codegen now detects empty assertion lists and prefixes the result variable with `_` (e.g., `{:ok, _result} = ...`), signaling intentional non-use. Streaming fixtures always use the result (to drain chunks) so they remain unprefixed. Added comprehensive unit test coverage. Fixes #306 (tslp CI failing on Elixir e2e). (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`, `src/e2e/tests/elixir_unused_variable_prefix.rs`)

- **alef-core: emit `* alef:hash:` (block-comment style) inside C/C-block headers instead of `// alef:hash:`.** `inject_hash_line` infers the comment style from the trimmed marker line. For a block-comment header the marker is `* This file is auto-generated by alef …`; after `trim()` it becomes `* This file …`, which previously did not match `trimmed.starts_with(" *")` (the leading space was already trimmed away). The detection then fell through to the catch-all `format!("// {HASH_PREFIX}{hash}")`, producing a malformed header with `// alef:hash:` lines inside `/* … */` blocks. Replaced the wrong predicate with `trimmed.starts_with('*')`, and strengthened `test_inject_and_extract_c_block` to assert `* alef:hash:` is present and `// alef:hash:` is absent. (`src/core/hash.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: marshal `Named`-type params and `bool` correctly in record methods.** `emit_record_methods` passed `Named`-type parameters directly to P/Invoke (a managed `record` object where `IntPtr` is required) and passed `bool` params as `bool` instead of `int` (P/Invoke FFI convention: 0/1). Fix: use the existing `native_call_arg`, `emit_named_param_setup`, and `emit_named_param_teardown_indented` helpers so record methods now serialize `Named` params to JSON and obtain handles before the FFI call, wrap calls in `try/finally` to free those handles, and convert `bool` params to `(x ? 1 : 0)` at the call site. Fixes #97 (bool→int), fixes #98 (Named-param handle marshaling). (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: restore nullable config parameter handling to use default instance instead of throwing.** v0.17.13 changed optional `ExtractionConfig? config` codegen from `config ?? new ExtractionConfig()` to `config ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(config))` based on concerns about `required` struct members. However, nullable optional parameters should support null to mean "use the default". The fix distinguishes: required config params throw on null (since the caller passed `null` to a non-optional signature), while optional config params (e.g. `config?`) default to `new T()`. This allows e.g. `ExtractBytesAsync(bytes, mime, config: null)` to succeed with the default extraction config rather than throwing. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

## [0.17.12] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-kotlin: emit `open val` with zero-value defaults instead of `abstract val` for error introspection properties (`statusCode`/`isTransient`/`errorType`).** The Kotlin Android JNI bridge throws a flat `<App>BridgeException` (see `crates/<lib>-jni/src/lib.rs`'s `throw_jni_error`) rather than constructing sealed-class variants. With `abstract val`, every `data class` variant in the sealed hierarchy failed `Class '<App>Error.X' is not abstract and does not implement abstract base class members: val errorType: String` and broke `assembleRelease`. The new `kotlin_zero_value` helper returns the literal zero for `Boolean`/`Byte`/`Short`/`Int`/`Long`/`Float`/`Double`/`String`/nullable types so the sealed class compiles. Variants can still opt into a concrete override when domain code constructs the error directly. Surfaced on liter-llm CI Mobile (`packages/kotlin-android/src/main/kotlin/dev/kreuzberg/literllm/android/LiterLlmError.kt`). (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/tests/docs_emission_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: include synthesised `message: _` label in introspection-method switch patterns for error variants that don't carry a real `message` field.** `emit_error_type_with_imports` prepends `message: String` to error case declarations when none of the variant's fields is named `message` (see `seen_message` logic in `gen_bindings.rs`). The companion switch patterns in the `statusCode`/`isTransient`/`errorType` extensions only iterated `variant.fields` and so produced `(endpoint: _, provider: _)` for a 3-element case `(message: _, endpoint: _, provider: _)`, failing Swift compilation with `tuple pattern has the wrong length for tuple type '(message: String, endpoint: String, provider: String)'`. The wildcard pattern now mirrors the case-declaration logic and prepends `message: _` when no real `message` field exists. Surfaced on liter-llm CI E2E (Swift) on `LiterLlmError.endpointNotSupported` and `LiterLlmError.invalidHeader`. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: dispatch `Optional<UnitEnum>` field conversion through `flatMap` on the bridge optional instead of `?.toString().flatMap` on `String?`.** The previous emission `rb.field()?.toString().flatMap { S(rawValue: $0) }` chained `?.` through both `.toString()` and `.flatMap`, so `.flatMap` ran on the unwrapped `String` value. Swift then dispatched to `Sequence.flatMap` (where `String` is a Sequence of `Character`), producing `cannot convert value of type 'String.Element' (aka 'Character') to expected argument type 'String'` and `cannot convert value of type 'EnumType?' to closure result type 'String?'`. Lifting `flatMap` onto the bridge `Optional<RustString>` directly (`rb.field().flatMap { S(rawValue: $0.toString()) }`) forces `Optional.flatMap` selection. Surfaced on liter-llm CI E2E (Swift) on `ImageUrl.detail` (`ImageDetail?`), `Choice.finishReason` (`FinishReason?`), `ToolChoice.toolType` (`ToolType?`). (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: extend `non_throwing_optional_string_uses_json_bridge` to also match `Option<Primitive>` so the JSON-decode body branch fires for non-throwing `Option<f64>` / `Option<i32>` returns.** Per `needs_json_bridge` in `gen_rust_crate/type_bridge.rs`, ALL `Option<T>` returns are JSON-bridged; the bridge surfaces them as non-optional `RustString`. v0.17.11 only covered `Option<String>`, so `liter-llm`'s `completion_cost(model, …) -> Option<f64>` and `completion_cost_with_cache(…) -> Option<f64>` failed Swift compilation with `cannot convert return expression of type 'RustString' to return type 'Double'`. The predicate now also accepts `TypeRef::Primitive(_)` inside the optional, routing those returns through the JSON-decode body that already handles `Option<String>`. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: wrap free-function bridge returns of client-class types via a new internal `init(_ inner: RustBridge.{Class})` on the emitted Swift client class.** Free functions returning a client class (e.g. liter-llm's `createClient(apiKey:baseUrl:timeoutSecs:maxRetries:modelHint:)` and `createClientFromJson(json:)`) emitted `return try RustBridge.createClient(...)` directly, but the bridge yields `RustBridge.DefaultClient` (low-level swift-bridge handle) while the forwarder signature declares the higher-level `DefaultClient` wrapper, failing Swift compilation with `cannot convert return expression of type 'RustBridge.DefaultClient' to return type 'LiterLlm.DefaultClient'`. `emit_client_class` now adds `internal init(_ inner: RustBridge.{Class}) { self.inner = inner }` so the free-function forwarder can wrap the bridge handle via `let _rb = try RustBridge.{fn}(...); return {Class}(_rb)`. A new `client_class_names` set is threaded from the top-level emitter through `emit_free_function_forwarders` → `emit_single_free_function_forwarder` to drive the new body branch. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/java: pin `pmd.version` to 7.17.0 to match `pmd-core` bundled in `maven-pmd-plugin` 3.28.0.** Dependabot bumps of `net.sourceforge.pmd:pmd-java` to 7.19.0+ produced `mvn verify` failures with `NoSuchMethodError: 'net.sourceforge.pmd.properties.PropertyBuilder$GenericPropertyBuilder net.sourceforge.pmd.properties.PropertyFactory.conventionalEnumProperty(java.lang.String, java.lang.Class)'` because `maven-pmd-plugin` 3.28.0 ships `pmd-core` 7.17.0, and mixing newer `pmd-java` against the older bundled `pmd-core` triggers the API incompatibility. Pinning `pmd.version` to 7.17.0 in both the hardcoded template (`languages/java.rs:113`) and the renovate-tracked constant (`template_versions::maven::PMD`) keeps the two artifacts in lockstep. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/java.rs`, `src/core/template_versions.rs`)

## [0.17.11] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e: fix Elixir test helper to handle struct field values.** E2e Elixir test helper `alef_e2e_item_texts` called `to_string(str)` on all non-atom, non-nil field values, which failed when struct types (e.g., `TreeSitterLanguagePack.StructureKind`) were returned from NIF bindings with error: `protocol String.Chars not implemented for <struct>`. Replaced `to_string(str)` with `inspect(str)`, which safely converts any Elixir value to a string. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)
- **alef-backend-extendr: emit `unsafe impl Send`/`Sync` on the visitor bridge struct.** The visitor bridge holds an `extendr_api::Robj` (a raw R `SEXP` pointer), which is `!Send`/`!Sync`. Since the core `VisitorHandle = Arc<Mutex<dyn HtmlVisitor + Send>>` requires the bridge to be `Send`, generated R bindings failed to compile with `the trait 'Send' is not implemented for '*mut extendr_ffi::SEXPREC'`. Mirrors the magnus backend pattern: emit `unsafe impl Send`/`Sync` on the bridge struct with a `// SAFETY:` comment noting that R is single-threaded and the bridge must only be invoked from the R main thread. (`src/backends/extendr/templates/visitor_bridge.jinja`, `src/backends/extendr/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)
- **alef-backend-swift: JSON-decode `Option<String>` returns on non-throwing free-function forwarders instead of optional-chaining `.toString()`.** v0.17.10 added a `?.toString()` suffix to coerce the bridge return for `Option<String>` results, but that fix was based on the false premise that swift-bridge surfaces the value as `RustString?`. In reality the bridge crate JSON-serializes ALL `Option<T>` returns (see `needs_json_bridge` in `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/type_bridge.rs`: `Optional(_) => true`): for a non-throwing function the bridge declares `-> String` and swift-bridge surfaces it as `-> RustString` (NON-optional). Applying `?.toString()` therefore failed with `cannot use optional chaining on non-optional value of type 'RustString'` — observed in downstream tree-sitter-language-pack on `detectLanguageFromExtension(ext:)`. `emit_single_free_function_forwarder` now detects non-throwing `Option<String>` returns via a new `non_throwing_optional_string_uses_json_bridge` predicate and emits a JSON-decode body (`JSONDecoder().decode(String?.self, from: _rb_data)`) — JSON `null` decodes to `.none`, present values to `.some`. The Optional<String> arm of `forwarder_return_conversion_suffix_inner` now returns an empty suffix to leave the body branch in sole control. The v0.17.10 regression test is rewritten to assert the new JSON-decode body and to negative-assert against the v0.17.10 `?.toString()` pattern. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

## [0.17.10] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-swift: convert `RustString` returns and host-facing DTO arguments in free-function forwarders.** Two related forwarder-codegen gaps surfaced as Swift compile errors in downstream tree-sitter-language-pack: (1) free functions returning `Option<String>` emitted `return RustBridge.detectLanguageFromExtension(ext)` directly, but swift-bridge maps `Option<String>` to `RustString?` regardless of throws-ness, so Swift rejected the body with `cannot convert return expression of type 'RustString?' to return type 'String?'`; (2) free functions taking a Named DTO struct param emitted `return try RustBridge.process(source, config)` where `config` was still the host-facing `ProcessConfig` struct, but the swift-bridge extern requires the low-level `RustBridge.ProcessConfig` class, producing `cannot convert value of type 'TreeSitterLanguagePack.ProcessConfig' to expected argument type 'RustBridge.ProcessConfig'`. `forwarder_return_conversion_suffix_inner` now emits a `?.toString()` suffix for `Optional<String>` returns, and `forwarder_param_signature` now threads `known_dto_names` so it can emit `let _rb_<name> = try <name>.intoRust()` (or the optional-chained variant) and forward the converted local. The function-level `throws` clause widens whenever any param conversion can throw via a new `param_conversion_throws` helper. Three new regression tests pin the three shapes (Optional<String> return, bare Named DTO param, Optional<Named DTO> param). (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

## [0.17.9] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-csharp: pass `bool` directly to P/Invoke methods instead of emitting `(name ? 1 : 0)` at the call site.** The P/Invoke declaration in `gen_bindings::functions.rs` (line 523-525) emits `[MarshalAs(UnmanagedType.U1)] bool <name>` for every `TypeRef::Primitive(PrimitiveType::Bool)` param — i.e., the unmanaged side is a single-byte `bool`, but the managed C# parameter type is `bool`. The wrapper-method body in `gen_bindings::mod.rs::ffi_arg_conversion_for_param` had drifted: it still emitted `(<name> ? 1 : 0)` (the legacy form from when the P/Invoke also took `int`), so generated wrappers like `GraphQLRouteConfig.EnablePlayground` and `SpikardLib.ResponseSetCookie` failed `dotnet build` with `CS1503: Argument N: cannot convert from 'int' to 'bool'`. The non-optional branch now passes `<name>` directly; the optional branch emits `(<name> ?? false)` to collapse `bool?` to `bool` (matching the legacy null-as-false semantics — no FFI sentinel is currently needed for bool? since no method requires distinguishing `Some(false)` from `None`). New regression test `test_bool_param_call_site_matches_pinvoke_bool_decl` pins both sides of the contract: P/Invoke decl keeps `[MarshalAs(UnmanagedType.U1)] bool`, call site passes `<name>` directly with no `? 1 : 0`. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

## [0.17.8] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- Re-released with the v0.17.7 alef-scaffold/alef-codegen fixes plus pyo3 stub doc-emission cleanups already merged on main. No new functional changes versus 0.17.7; bump exists to give downstream consumers a clean tag after the in-flight csharp bool-call-site fix was deferred.

## [0.17.7] - 2026-05-20

### Added

- **alef-scaffold + alef-core: support per-target replacement of the core-crate dependency feature set in the generated FFI Cargo.toml.** New `[crates.ffi.target_dep_overrides]` array (Vec of `{ cfg, features }`) on `FfiConfig` instructs the FFI scaffold to emit the default branch under `[target.'cfg(not(any(<all-cfgs>)))'.dependencies]` and one `[target.'cfg(<cfg>)'.dependencies]` block per override, instead of a single unconditional `[dependencies]` block. Without it, downstream kreuzberg's `kreuzberg-ffi/Cargo.toml` always pulled `kreuzberg` with the full ORT-dependent feature set (`paddle-ocr`, `layout-detection`, `embeddings`, `auto-rotate`), and `cargo ndk --target x86_64 -- check -p kreuzberg-ffi` failed with `ort-sys: ort does not provide prebuilt binaries for the target x86_64-linux-android`. With the override pointing the Android x86_64 emulator triple at kreuzberg's `android-target` feature set the same check succeeds. The empty-overrides path is byte-identical to the previous output. New regression test `test_scaffold_ffi_target_dep_overrides_emit_cfg_blocks` in `crates/alef-scaffold/src/tests.rs`. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/ffi.rs`, `src/core/config/languages.rs`, `src/core/config/mod.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-codegen: detect rustdoc test-attribute fences (`no_run`, `ignore`, `should_panic`, `compile_fail`, `edition*`) as Rust code, drop bodies for foreign-language targets.** Downstream tslp `index.d.ts` had 32 remaining `use tree_sitter_language_pack::...` / `.unwrap()` leaks because the source rustdoc uses ` ```no_run ` fences that v0.17.5's sanitizer didn't recognise as Rust. `is_rust_fence_tag` now matches the full set of rustdoc test-attribute-only tags (including `edition2018`, `edition2021`, etc.) in both `sanitize_rust_idioms_inner` and `detect_first_fence_lang`. Non-Rust language fences (`python`, `javascript`, `typescript`, `php`, …) are unaffected. 13 new unit tests added. (`src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`)

## [0.17.6] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **alef-readme: configure the template-driven README minijinja `Environment` to match `template_env::make_env()` and trim the multi-line TOML `description` before rendering.** `try_template_readme` in `crates/alef-readme/src/lib.rs` constructed its `Environment` via `Environment::new()` without `set_trim_blocks(true)` / `set_lstrip_blocks(true)` / `set_keep_trailing_newline(true)`, so every Jinja control tag (`{% if %}`, `{% include %}`, `{% endif %}`) leaked its trailing newline into the rendered README and every `{% include 'partials/_*.md' %}` dropped the trailing newline of the partial file. The result was double blank lines around every `## Heading` and a missing blank line between hand-included blocks (e.g. between the badges `<div>` and the description paragraph). Compounding the issue, TOML basic multi-line strings (`description = """..."""`) preserve a trailing newline, so `{{ description }}<blank line>## Installation` rendered as `<desc>\n\n\n## Installation` (one extra blank line) before the next section — independent of `trim_blocks` because `{{ … }}` is an output tag, not a block tag. `try_template_readme` now enables the three whitespace settings on the local environment and `.map(|s| s.trim_end().to_string())` strips the description's trailing whitespace before insertion into the template context. Downstream html-to-markdown's `task generate-readme:check` CI step (which runs `alef readme && rumdl fmt && git diff --exit-code`) failed on every regen because rumdl accepts both spacings but the committed READMEs no longer matched the regenerated output. (`crates/alef-readme/src/lib.rs`)

- **alef-cli: include the resolved workspace version in the IR cache key so `alef sync-versions --set …` (and downstream `alef readme`) invalidate the cache automatically on a version bump.** The IR cache key was `sources_hash` only, but `api.version` is read fresh from `version_from` (Cargo.toml) into the IR at extract time. Bumping just the version did not change source contents, so `extract` returned cached IR with the previous version, and `regenerate_readmes` inside `sync_versions` then rendered README badges/install snippets against the stale version (e.g. `filter=v0.3.0-rc.21` after a bump to `rc.22`), failing the consumer's "Check README freshness" CI step. The cache key is now `format!("{source_hash}:{version}")` in `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/extract.rs`, threaded into both `is_ir_cached` and `write_ir_cache`. Verified end-to-end in the kreuzcrawl consumer: `alef sync-versions --set 0.3.0-rc.99` followed by `alef sync-versions --set 0.3.0-rc.23` now refreshes per-binding READMEs in a single pass without `alef cache clear`. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/extract.rs`)

## [0.17.5] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-napi: sanitize Rust code-block examples from emitted docstrings to prevent leaks in .d.ts.** Downstream tree-sitter-language-pack TypeScript bindings contained 61 Rust-code-block leaks in `index.d.ts` JSDoc comments (15× `use tree_sitter_language_pack::...` imports, 8× `.unwrap()`, 8× `Some/None`, 10× `assert_eq!`, etc.) because NAPI-RS function code generation was calling `emit_rustdoc(func.doc)` directly without sanitizing Rust examples. The fix applies `sanitize_rust_idioms(doc, DocTarget::TsDoc)` before emitting rustdoc in functions, enums, variants, types, and methods in `alef-backend-napi::gen_bindings`, so when NAPI-derive extracts Rust doc comments to generate the `.d.ts`, the examples are already clean. (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/{functions,enums,types,mod}.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: emit Elixir `Native.<errname>_<method>` stubs alongside Rust NIF shims for error introspection.** The Rust side of `alef-backend-rustler` already emitted `#[rustler::nif] fn <errname>_status_code/is_transient/error_type(_msg: String)` for every non-sanitized error type, and the public `Elixir.Spikard` wrapper called `Spikard.Native.<errname>_<method>(msg)`, but the corresponding `def <errname>_<method>(_msg), do: :erlang.nif_error(:nif_not_loaded)` placeholders were never emitted into `native.ex`. rustler-precompiled checks at on_load time that every NIF it installs has a matching Elixir stub in the named module and bails with `{:error, {:bad_lib, "Function not found 'Elixir.Spikard.Native':<errname>_<method>/1"}}` when one is missing — every downstream consumer with declared error types failed `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`. `gen_bindings/helpers.rs::gen_native_ex` now iterates `api.errors` with the same `!m.sanitized` filter the Rust NIF emitter uses and writes the missing stubs as `@doc false`. Regression test `error_methods_emit_matching_native_ex_stubs` in `tests/gen_public_api_test.rs` pins the two-sided emission. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/tests/gen_public_api_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp + alef-codegen: route every generated C# `<summary>` through `sanitize_rust_idioms(_, CSharpDoc)` so XML-doc no longer leaks raw Rust idioms.** Generated `GraphQLErrorException.cs` (and every other error/enum/type with rustdoc) shipped raw `# Examples` / ` ```ignore ` code fences / `[`Self::error_code`]` intra-doc links / `Result<T, E>` generics / bare `<` and `>` characters straight into `<summary>` blocks, producing tens of CS1002/CS1519 parse errors. New `DocTarget::CSharpDoc` variant in `src/codegen/doc_emission.rs` drops rust-tagged code fences (matching the TsDoc/JsDoc convention), maps `Vec<u8>` → `byte[]`, `Vec<T>` → `T[]`, `Option<T>` → `T?`, `HashMap<K, V>` → `Dictionary<K, V>`, `Result<T, E>` → `T`, `None` → `null`, strips `# Examples` / `# Arguments` / `# Returns` section headings, and finally XML-escapes remaining `<` / `>` / `&` so the output is safe inside `<summary>`. A companion `sanitize_rust_idioms_keep_sections` preserves headings for callers that have already split sections (so `emit_csharp_doc`'s structured `<param>` / `<returns>` path keeps its rendering). `alef-codegen::error_gen::gen_csharp_error_types` and every doc-emission site in `alef-backend-csharp::gen_bindings::{enums,types,mod}.rs` now route through the helper. 10 new unit tests + a fixture-pinning regression test in `error_gen.rs` + an end-to-end backend test in `src/backends/csharp/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs` cover both the bad GraphQLErrorException input shape and the per-target substitutions. (`src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`, `src/codegen/error_gen.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/{enums,types,mod}.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: nullary free-function forwarders that return a named DTO now wrap the bridge call in the DTO converter instead of returning the `RustBridge.<Dto>` directly.** `public func schemaQueryOnly() -> QueryOnlyConfig` previously emitted `return RustBridge.schemaQueryOnly()`, which Swift rejected with `cannot convert return expression of type 'RustBridge.QueryOnlyConfig' to return type 'Spikard.QueryOnlyConfig'` (same shape for `schemaQueryMutation` / `schemaFull` and any other nullary default-config emitter). `emit_single_free_function_forwarder` now recognises bare `Named(DTO)` returns, binds the bridge result to `_rb`, and returns `try DTO(_rb)` via the existing `init(_ rb: <Dto>Ref) throws` converter; the forwarder signature also widens to `throws` so the converter's error path is honoured. Test `nullary_free_function_returning_named_dto_wraps_bridge_call_in_converter` pins the new body shape. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: `*FromJson` forwarders for enums route through `RustBridge.<enum>FromJson` whenever `emit_enum` falls back to the typealias form.** Earlier the from-JSON emitter unconditionally used `JSONDecoder().decode(<EnumType>.self, ...)`, but data-bearing enums whose payload types are not bridge-safe (per the `gate codable enum synthesis` work in v0.17.2) fall back to a `typealias <EnumType> = RustBridge.<EnumType>` and never get a `Codable` conformance — every such enum produced a fresh Swift compile error at the forwarder. New `enum_emits_codable` helper mirrors `emit_enum`'s Codable/typealias decision; enum branches now route typealias-fallback enums to `RustBridge.<EnumType>FromJson(json)` directly. Test `data_variant_serde_enum_with_opaque_field_falls_back_to_rust_bridge_from_json` pins the typealias-routing path; `data_variant_serde_enum_with_bridge_safe_fields_emits_codable` pins the positive Codable case. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: emit one Kotlin coroutine-wrapper file per opaque client type instead of bundling every wrapper into `DefaultClient.kt`.** Generators that produce more than one Kotlin client wrapper (e.g. spikard's `Router` + `GraphQLRouteConfig`) previously emitted every wrapper class into a single `DefaultClient.kt` AND into per-type `<Type>.kt` files, producing K2-LightTree "Redeclaration" errors at `compileKotlin`. `emit_jvm_client_class` / `emit_jvm_client_class_with_package` now return `Vec<GeneratedFile>` and emit one `<Type>.kt` per opaque client type (the bundled `DefaultClient.kt` is no longer produced when multiple types exist; the single-wrapper case still renders to `DefaultClient.kt` for backward compatibility). Two regression tests in `tests/gen_bindings_test.rs` pin the multi-type-per-file shape and the single-wrapper-fallback. Stale `DefaultClient.kt` files in downstream repos must be deleted manually after upgrade because Kotlin output carries no `alef:hash:` marker for `cleanup_orphaned_files`. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: merge `[crates.java].exclude_types` into the Kotlin wrapper's effective exclude set so Kotlin never emits a wrapper referencing a Java facade the Java backend skipped.** Without this propagation, spikard's `dev.spikard.Router` (excluded at the Java layer) still produced a Kotlin coroutine wrapper that imported the missing class, surfacing as `Unresolved reference 'Router'` / `Unresolved reference 'routeCount'` / `Unresolved reference 'close'` at K2 compile. `effective_kotlin_exclude_types` now unions Kotlin's own exclude list with the Java backend's. Test `jvm_client_wrapper_respects_java_exclude_types` pins the merge. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/java: emit `JsonUtil.fromJson(json, Type.class)` instead of the removed `Type.fromJson(json)` per-DTO factory.** The Java backend centralised JSON deserialisation in `JsonUtil` (removing per-DTO static `fromJson(String)` methods), but the e2e Java test generator continued to emit `var request = ChatCompletionRequest.fromJson("...")` — symbol not found at `javac` for every generated test using the `from_json` options-via path (OcrTest.java, ParityTest.java, RerankTest.java, ResponsesTest.java, SearchTest.java, SmokeTest.java in liter-llm). `render_test_method` in `codegen/java.rs` now emits `var {var} = JsonUtil.fromJson("{json}", {Type}.class)` for the `options_via = "from_json"` arm. `render_test_file` adds `import {pkg}.JsonUtil` whenever `all_options_types` is non-empty (same condition as existing per-DTO type imports). Two regression tests in `src/e2e/tests/java_from_json_codegen.rs` pin the emission shape and the import. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`, `src/e2e/tests/java_from_json_codegen.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: emit `def __init__(self, ...) -> None: ...` stub for opaque types that have a `[workspace.client_constructors.TypeName]` entry.** The `.pyi` stub generator for opaque types (`gen_opaque_type_stub`) iterated `typ.methods` for instance and static method stubs but never emitted `__init__`, so mypy inferred `def __init__(self) -> None` (no args) for every opaque handle class. Every `DefaultClient(api_key=..., base_url=...)` call site in downstream `api.py` was rejected with "Too many arguments for `DefaultClient`". The fix passes `config.client_constructors.get(&typ.name)` to `gen_opaque_type_stub`; when a constructor config is present the stub emits `def __init__(self, param: PythonType, ...) -> None: ...` immediately after the class header. Raw Rust param types (`&str`, `String`, primitive integers, `()`) map to Python equivalents via a new `constructor_rust_type_to_python` helper; unknown types fall back to `Any`. Two regression tests in `src/backends/pyo3/tests/gen_stubs_test.rs` pin both the positive and negative cases. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_stubs.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/tests/gen_stubs_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: known regression — api.py adapter wrapper params and return values not converted between `options.*` layer types and `_internal_bindings.*` opaque types.** `emit_adapter_wrapper` passes `options.ChatCompletionRequest` directly to `engine.chat()` (which expects `_bindings.ChatCompletionRequest`) and returns `_bindings.ChatCompletionResponse` where the signature promises `options.ChatCompletionResponse`. `_to_rust_*` converters exist for input types but no `from_rust_*` converters exist for return types; adding return-type converters requires a new class of generated helpers out of scope for this patch. Fix tracked for follow-up; downstream mypy errors on adapter wrappers remain. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

## [0.17.3] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **Re-publish release with current main as binary.** The v0.17.2 git tag was force-pointed to `b539d258` (`fix(swift): gate codable enum synthesis`) after the original v0.17.2 release was cut, but the GitHub Release archive still shipped the binary built from the original pre-retag commit. Downstream consumers using `install-alef@v1` (which downloads the release binary) ran an older alef than the tag suggested, surfacing as `alef docs` output drift between local dev (built from source at the re-tagged commit) and CI (using the stale release binary). v0.17.3 cuts a fresh release on top of the same code so the GitHub Release binary matches the tag. (A stale local `v0.17.3` tag pointing at an earlier release-prep commit was discarded — it never existed on origin and had no associated GitHub Release.) No code changes between v0.17.2 (`b539d258`) and v0.17.3.

## [0.17.2] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/rust: bind fields_array accessor before len() assertion in e2e tests.** The Rust e2e generator emitted `assert!(chunks.len() >= 2 as usize, ...)` with `chunks` undeclared for non-streaming fixtures whose result struct has a `chunks` (or `imports`, `structure`, etc.) field registered in `fields_array`. The streaming-virtual-field arm in `render_assertion` fires unconditionally on these names (they are in `STREAMING_VIRTUAL_FIELDS`) and hardcodes `chunks` as the variable; for non-streaming calls no such local was bound. The fix emits `let {field} = &{result_var}.{field};` bindings in `render_test_function` for any `fields_array` field that both appears in assertions and collides with a streaming-virtual-field name, matching the shape of the PHP fix in `4a59541e`. A regression unit test in `codegen::rust::test_file::tests` pins the binding-before-assertion ordering. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/test_file.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/rust/assertions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: use language-neutral phrasing in `php_visitor_interface_start.jinja` class-level docblock.** The template hardcoded `ConversionOptions::$visitor` — PHP static-property-access syntax — to describe an instance property, making the generated `HtmlVisitorInterface.php` class-level comment syntactically invalid PHP. Replaced the `Type::$field` reference with "the `visitor` field of `ConversionOptions`", which is unambiguous in every target language. (`src/backends/php/templates/php_visitor_interface_start.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-go: pin method-level regression test for `Option<&str>` getters returning `*string`.** The earlier free-function snapshot (`option_string_return_null_checks_and_boxes_value`) covered the `binding_content` end-to-end path but did not exercise the method-emission path inside `gen_method_wrapper`. A stale spikard `binding.go` surfaced the same shape on `GraphQLRouteConfig.GetDescription()` — `func (h *GraphQLRouteConfig) GetDescription() *string { … return C.GoString(ptr) }` — and `golangci-lint` rejected the `string`/`*string` mismatch. The fix is downstream-only (regenerating spikard with the existing alef code already produces the corrected nil-check + take-address closure), but a new unit test in `alef-backend-go::gen_bindings::methods::tests` (`test_gen_method_wrapper_optional_string_getter_emits_nil_check_and_address`) now pins the method-level shape so future refactors of `gen_method_wrapper` / `go_return_expr` cannot regress this case for instance getters on opaque handles. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: hide internal FFI types (`RustVec<T>`, `RustString`, `intoRust()`) from public Swift API.** Public Swift functions/methods previously exposed swift-bridge transport types in their signatures (e.g. the e2e `batchExtractBytesSync`/`batchExtractBytes`/`batchExtractFilesSync`/`batchExtractFiles` wrappers returned `[ExtractionResultRef]`, leaking the swift-bridge borrowed-Ref class through to user code). The auto-generated free-function forwarders already accepted native Swift types (`[T]`, `String`, `[UInt8]`, `Data`, …) and converted to `RustVec`/`RustString` internally; the four batch e2e wrappers now do the same by draining the returned `RustVec<ExtractionResult>` into an owned `[ExtractionResult]` via `pop()`-and-reverse before returning. `intoRust()` extensions are already emitted under `internal extension Name { … }` (consumed by other generated code, not user code), so their visibility is unchanged. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit `Codable`, `Sendable`, and `Hashable` only for data-bearing Swift enums whose associated values can synthesize those conformances.** Data-bearing serde enums now get native Swift protocol conformances when every payload type is bridge-safe, preserving JSON decoding and value semantics. Enums with opaque or non-Codable payloads fall back to a RustBridge typealias instead of generating Swift code that cannot compile. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/templates/swift_enum_header.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-php: emit `Option<T>` getter return type for Duration fields on Default structs.** The struct emitter wraps non-optional `Duration` fields in `Option<i64>` when `option_duration_on_defaults` is enabled (always true for PHP), but the getter emitter did not apply the same `force_optional` condition — it used bare `i64` for the return type while the storage field was `Option<i64>`, causing E0308. The fix adds the matching `typ.has_default && !field.optional && Duration` check to the getter emit so the return type mirrors the storage type. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: emit `.unwrap_or(default)` instead of manual `match result { Some(val) => val, None => default }` for `Option<Primitive>` returns.** The `gen_owned_value_to_c` helper emitted the manual-match form for every `Optional<T>` return, but when the inner type is a non-bool primitive the match body is just the value itself — clippy flags this as `manual_unwrap_or`. Non-bool primitives in `Optional` position now emit `expr.unwrap_or(null_value)` directly. The bool case still uses the match form because it needs the `as i32` cast. (`src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **alef-codegen, alef-backend-napi, alef-backend-wasm: use `.iter()` instead of `.into_iter()` for `&[T] → Vec<U>` clone-and-map emission (clippy::into_iter_on_ref under -D warnings).** The shared `wrap_return`/`wrap_return_with_mutex` helpers and per-backend equivalents emitted `result.into_iter().map(|v| v.clone().into()).collect()` for the `returns_ref` + `Vec<Named(_)>` case. Calling `.into_iter()` on a slice reference yields the same `&T` iterator as `.iter()`, so under `-D warnings` clippy 1.95 promoted the suggestion to a hard error: every alef-managed `liter-llm-{node,py,php,ffi}` (and previously `-php`, which already had a one-off fix) failed `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`. Switched the `returns_ref` arm in `alef-codegen/src/generators/{functions,binding_helpers}.rs`, `alef-backend-napi/src/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, and `alef-backend-wasm/src/gen_bindings/functions.rs` to `result.iter().map(...).collect()`. The integration test `test_wrap_return_with_mutex_vec_non_opaque_named_ref` was updated to assert the new shape.

- **alef-codegen/doc_emission: strip bare `Some <lowercase noun>` in prose.** The earlier `replace_some_calls` handled only the call form `Some(x)` and `replace_none_keyword` already replaced bare `None` with `null`/`undefined`, but bare `Some` in prose like "(Some values)" or "Some keys leave the previous" slipped through the sanitizer untouched. Real leak in html-to-markdown `PreprocessingOptionsUpdate.java:16`: "Only specified fields (Some values) will override existing options; null values leave the previous" — `null values` was correct (None → null) but `Some values` shouted Rust. New helper `replace_some_keyword_in_prose` runs immediately after `replace_some_calls` and drops `Some` when preceded by a word boundary and followed by a single ASCII space + ASCII-lowercase letter, leaving the following noun intact. `SomeType` (no space), `Some.method()` (followed by `.`), `Some Title` (uppercase follower), and `Some(x)` (already converted upstream) are all preserved. Two new unit tests pin the positive (PreprocessingOptionsUpdate leak) and negative (identifiers / proper nouns / call form) shapes; existing `sanitize_attribute_line_dropped` test relaxed to accept the stripped form alongside the legacy outcomes. (`src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit public-API function and method names in camelCase (was snake_case).** Swift convention is camelCase methods; snake_case names in the userland module made the public API look like a thin Rust transliteration. The swift-bridge `extern "Rust"` block continues to use snake_case Rust idents (ABI requirement); Swift-side public functions and field getters are now generated via lower-camel-case transform of the same Rust ident — the swift-bridge getter extern now emits `#[swift_bridge(swift_name = "<camel>")]` so accessors like `ref.deviceId()` replace `ref.device_id()`, and the inbound-trait `Swift{Trait}Box` delegate protocol now exposes `func processImage(...)` rather than `func process_image(...)`. The internal `alef_<snake>` shim names (matched by swift-bridge `@_cdecl` exports) remain snake_case. Param labels on the delegate protocol/box class methods are also camelCase to match Swift conventions. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/extern_block.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: emit `async def foo(...) -> T: return await engine.foo(...)` for non-streaming DefaultClient methods in api.py.** Previously `emit_adapter_wrapper` applied the streaming `async for item in engine.foo(): yield item` pattern to every adapter regardless of `pattern`, treating `async_method` adapters (which return a single awaitable value) identically to `streaming` adapters (which return an async iterator). The generated api.py wrapped 22 non-streaming methods as async generators with `-> AsyncIterator[()]` return types, producing 70+ mypy errors (`"Coroutine[…]" has no attribute "__aiter__"`, malformed `AsyncIterator[()]` annotation). The fix checks `adapter.pattern`: `AsyncMethod` now emits the `return await` form with the correct return type from `adapter.returns`; `Streaming` retains the `async for … yield` form. The `AsyncIterator` typing import is also gated on having at least one `Streaming` adapter. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: map Rust `String`/`&str` to Python `str` in api.py adapter wrapper signatures.** `emit_adapter_wrapper` used the raw type string from `alef.toml` adapter param definitions directly in the generated Python signature, so `type = "String"` produced `url: String` instead of `url: str`. Python has no `String` builtin; mypy reported `Module "liter_llm.options" has no attribute "String"`. The fix routes adapter param type names through a new `adapter_param_python_type` helper that maps `"String"` and `"&str"` to `"str"` and passes other names through unchanged. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: also map `bytes::Bytes` / `Vec<u8>` to Python `bytes` and `()` to `None` in api.py adapter signatures (params AND return type).** Methods returning `bytes::Bytes` (e.g. `speech`, `file_content`) previously emitted `-> bytes::Bytes` in the wrapper signature — `::` isn't a Python path operator so mypy aborted with `Invalid syntax`. The return-type emission now also goes through `adapter_param_python_type`, which gained `"bytes::Bytes"`/`"Vec<u8>"`/`"&[u8]"` → `bytes` and `"()"` → `None` mappings. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: import adapter return types into api.py so async wrapper signatures resolve at runtime.** When emitting the api.py for adapter-based bindings, only the adapter param types and the `item_type` of streaming adapters were added to the import list. `AsyncMethod` adapter return types (e.g. `BatchObject`, `ResponseObject`, `BatchListResponse`) were referenced bare in `async def foo(...) -> ReturnType` annotations but never imported, producing 20+ `Name "X" is not defined` mypy errors and F821 at import. The collector now also adds `adapter.returns` to `all_type_imports`, skipping names that map to Python builtins (`str`, `bytes`, `None`, `int`, `float`, `bool`). (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: null-check and box `Option<String>` / `Option<Char>` / `Option<Path>` returns instead of dereferencing C pointers directly.** Functions returning `Option<String>` were generating signatures like `func Foo() *string` but emitting bodies with `return C.GoString(ptr)` (which returns bare `string`), causing type mismatches in golangci-lint and govulncheck. The fix adds a closure that null-checks the C pointer and returns either `nil` or a boxed Go string: `func() *string { if ptr == nil { return nil }; defer C.{prefix}_free_string(ptr); s := C.GoString(ptr); return &s }()`. This pattern is consistent with how `Option<Primitive>` returns are already handled and matches idiomatic Go for nullable pointers. New snapshot test `option_string_return_null_checks_and_boxes_value` pins the generated code shape. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/go/tests/snapshot_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit `*FromJson` forwarder for every `from_json`-eligible type (not just first-class structs).** The previous filter gated emission on `can_emit_first_class_struct`, so types with `from_json` capability but no first-class mirror (LayoutDetectionConfig, PdfConfig, PostProcessorConfig, every enum, etc.) lacked the JSON-decode forwarder. Swift e2e and the new enum `intoRust()` extension (from prior commit) both depend on `RustBridge.{typeName}FromJson(json)` existing. Broaden the filter to include all from_json-eligible IR types. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-codegen/doc_emission: add shared `sanitize_rust_idioms(text, DocTarget)` helper to strip Rust idioms leaking into foreign-language docstrings.** Generated PHPDoc / Javadoc / TSDoc inherited rustdoc verbatim, surfacing Rust-only syntax (`Result<T, E>`, `Option<T>`, `Vec<T>`, `HashMap<K, V>`, `Arc<>` / `Box<>` / `Mutex<>` wrappers, `Send + Sync` trait bounds, `'static` lifetimes, `::` path separator, `[`Type::method`]` intra-doc links, `.unwrap()` / `.expect("…")` calls, `Some(x)` / `None` in prose, `pub fn` / `crate::` / `&self` / `&mut self` mentions, `#[…]` attribute macros, and full ```rust ``` code fences) directly to PHP/Java/TS users for whom none of it parses. The new helper applies per-target transformations outside backtick/code-fence regions: intra-doc links collapse to `Type.method`; `Option<T>` rewrites to `T?` (PhpDoc) / `T | null` (JavaDoc) / `T | undefined` (TsDoc/JsDoc); `Vec<T>` to `T[]` (with `Vec<u8>` → `Uint8Array` for TsDoc/JsDoc, `byte[]` for JavaDoc); `HashMap<K, V>` to `Record<K, V>` (TS) / `Map<K, V>` (Java) / `array<K, V>` (PHP); wrapper types stripped; standalone `None` / `Some(...)` substituted; `::` collapsed to `.`; `rust`-tagged code fences dropped for TS, language tag stripped for Java/PHP. 26 unit tests pin the per-target output shape and confirm backtick-protection invariants. The existing per-backend `alef-backend-java::gen_bindings::helpers::sanitize_rust_syntax` body collapses to a single delegation. (`src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: route every PHPDoc emission site through the shared `sanitize_rust_idioms(.., PhpDoc)` helper.** Five sites in `gen_bindings/mod.rs` (type stub at line 1363, enum stub at line 1464, struct class docblock at line 1716, method docblock at line 1747) and the visitor-interface method docstring path in `trait_bridge.rs` (lines 917–921) previously passed `method.doc.lines().next()` raw into Jinja templates, so `VisitorHandle.php` shipped Rust trait-bound prose ("`Arc`-wrapped `Mutex` for thread-safe shared mutation. `Send + Sync` so that types embedding a `VisitorHandle` ... can be shared across threads — required by callers that stash configs inside axum/rmcp/tokio Send-bound contexts.") and class docblocks contained rustdoc intra-doc links of the form `` [`ConversionResult`] ``. After wiring, PHP consumers see PHP-shaped types (`T?`, `array<K, V>`) and clean ``\`Type.method()\`` cross-references. All 39 backend tests + 4 unit tests pass; `task php:lint` clean; e2e pass count preserved (54 pre-existing `ConversionOptionsBuilder`-exclusion failures are unrelated). (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/php/src/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java/gen_visitor: sanitize callback docstrings through the shared helper before emitting the visitor interface.** `gen_visitor::files::gen_visitor_interface` passed `spec.doc` raw into the Jinja template, so the generated `Visitor.java` callback methods inherited rustdoc with `Result<T, E>` / `Option<T>` generics, lifetime markers, and Rust path separators. New `sanitize_callback_doc(text)` helper in `gen_visitor::helpers` delegates to `alef_codegen::doc_emission::sanitize_rust_idioms(text, JavaDoc)` and is now called from the visitor-interface emission path. The record/builder/enum paths in `gen_bindings/types.rs` and `gen_bindings/enums.rs` already flow through `escape_javadoc_line` → `sanitize_rust_syntax` (now delegating to the shared helper per the codegen change above), so all DTOs inherit the cleanup automatically. Java backend build remains green; pre-existing `JsonUtil.fromJson` test-compile regression is unrelated and out of scope. (`src/backends/java/gen_visitor/files.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_visitor/helpers.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: route every rustdoc-emission site through `sanitize_rust_idioms(.., TsDoc)` so generated `.d.ts` files stop leaking Rust idioms into JS/TS tooltips.** Before this change, `src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/functions.rs::emit_rustdoc` was a verbatim pass-through (template `templates/rustdoc.jinja` just emitted `/// {{ line }}` for each input line), so the generated `crates/html-to-markdown-wasm/pkg/*.d.ts` surfaced Rust intra-doc links (`` [`ConversionOptions::builder()`] ``), `Option<T>` / `Vec<u8>` / `Some(…)` / `None` in prose, `.to_string()` literals, and 100+ lines of `rust`-tagged code-fence examples — none of which help JS callers. The rewritten `emit_rustdoc` calls the sanitizer up-front; all 11 doc-emission call sites in `types.rs` (4), `enums.rs` (2), `methods.rs` (1), `functions.rs` (1 + the dispatcher itself) consume the helper transparently. `cargo test -p alef-backend-wasm` (17 lib tests + 2 visitor-substitution tests) passes; `cargo clippy -p alef-backend-wasm -- -D warnings` clean; regenerated `lib.rs` drops 123 lines of `rust`-tagged code examples that no longer reach the JS consumer. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: force Builder emission for types with `#[serde(flatten)]` `serde_json::Value` fields, regardless of field count.** The `@JsonAnySetter` method that absorbs unknown sibling keys during deserialization can only live on a builder setter — not on a record component. Previously, `should_emit_builder` under `Auto` mode skipped the builder for types with fewer than 5 fields (e.g. `ResponseOutputItem`, `ResponseTool`), leaving only `@JsonAnyGetter` on the record with no matching `@JsonAnySetter`. Jackson then rejected every sibling field as "Unrecognized field", and any flattened field whose wire value was an array caused `Cannot deserialize...LinkedHashMap from Array value`. The fix adds an early-return in the `Auto` branch: any type that has at least one `serde_flatten && TypeRef::Json` field now always gets a builder, ensuring `@JsonAnySetter` is emitted for correct round-trip deserialization. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/typescript: drain stream with `for await` in NAPI streaming-error tests.** The TypeScript e2e template for `expects_error` fixtures generated `await expect(async () => { await client.chatStream(...); }).rejects.toThrow()`, which awaits only the stream handle creation. The NAPI binding returns the handle lazily — HTTP errors (401, 400 content-policy) only surface when the first chunk is polled. Introducing `is_streaming_error_call` detection (error fixture + function name contains "stream" + no stream chunks), the template now emits `const _stream = await ...; for await (const _ of _stream) {}` inside the `expect` block so the error propagates before the wrapper exits, matching Python's `async for _ in _iterator` pattern. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/typescript/test_function.jinja`)

- **alef-cli/pipeline/extract: seed include-list expansion from included functions' signatures.** `expand_include_list` previously walked only `include.types` (and their transitively-referenced field/method types) when computing the keep-set, which silently dropped wrapper return structs declared alongside their returning functions if the user listed only the per-element type. The `sanitize_unknown_types` pass then collapsed the function's `Named(...)` return into `String`, and every binding facade emitted the wrong signature (regression observed on kreuzcrawl `batch_scrape`/`batch_crawl` → `BatchScrapeResults`/`BatchCrawlResults`). The expansion now also seeds itself with types referenced by every `include.functions` entry's return type and parameter types — the user opted into the function, so its public signature must survive the filter. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/extract.rs`)

- **alef-e2e validate: stop emitting "missing required input field 'input'" warnings when the call's arg uses the top-level 'input' field (the whole fixture.input is the arg's value — no sub-key lookup applies). Sub-path arg checks (e.g., field = "input.path") still fire correctly.**

- **alef-e2e validate: suppress "skipped for all languages" warning when the fixture's category is in [crates.e2e].exclude_categories — those fixtures are intentionally excluded at the category level, so empty skip.languages with no reason is the correct shape.**

- **alef-publish: skip Jni language in validate() — JNI is a transitive (Java/Kotlin) binding with no standalone package directory.**

- **alef-backend-go: prefix every line of multi-line rustdoc comments with `//` — previously continuation lines after a blank line were emitted bare, breaking the Go parser at the next non-comment line.** The `emit_godoc_summary` renderer in `gen_bindings/types.rs` already passes every line through `go_doc_comment_line.jinja` (which prepends `// `), so the renderer was correct. Added a regression test (`godoc_multiline_summary_continuation_lines_are_prefixed`) that drives the full `GoBackend::generate_bindings` path with a two-paragraph summary and asserts every comment line is `//`-prefixed and no true blank line appears inside the doc block. (`src/backends/go/tests/snapshot_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: emit .iter().cloned().map(Into::into) instead of .into_iter().map(|v| v.clone().into()) for slice-to-owned conversions — fixes clippy::into_iter_on_ref on -D warnings.**

- **alef-e2e/php: bind fields_array accessor before count() assertion in e2e tests.** PHP e2e generator was emitting assertions like `count($chunks)` without first binding the variable, resulting in "Undefined variable $chunks" errors. Now emits `$chunks = $result->getChunks();` (and equivalently for `$imports` and `$structure`) before assertions that reference these fields_array items, matching Python/TypeScript behavior. (`src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/php/test_method.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit `init(_ rb: {Name}Ref) throws` converter on every first-class struct.** First-class Swift struct mirrors lacked an initializer from their FFI-bridge `Ref` counterpart, so swift e2e tests failed with `cannot convert value of type 'LayoutRegionRef' to expected argument type 'LayoutRegion'` (and similar for PageInfoRef, TableRef, etc.). The previous init took the owned `RustBridge.{Name}` class, but `RustVec<Name>` iteration yields the base `{Name}Ref`, so chains like `try rb.layouts().map { try Layout($0) }` did not compile. Change the parameter type to `RustBridge.{Name}Ref`; since swift-bridge emits `{Name}: {Name}RefMut: {Name}Ref`, owned instances still bind via class inheritance. Existing helpers `vec_elem_convert_expr`, `swift_ffi_read_expr`, `forwarder_return_conversion_suffix_with_throws`, and `return_uses_json_bridge` are reused for per-field conversion. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit `RustVec<RustString>` for Rust `Vec<String>` (was `RustVec<String>`, which is not `Vectorizable`).** Swift compilation failed with `Type 'String' does not conform to protocol 'Vectorizable'` because swift-bridge's `RustVec<T>` requires `T: Vectorizable`, satisfied only by `RustString` and primitive scalars. Update `emit_vec_arg` so `intoRust()` field-conversion preludes substitute `RustString` for `String` when wrapped in `RustVec` and wrap each pushed element with `RustString(__elem)`. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit `intoRust()` extension on every Swift enum mirror.** Swift enums emitted by alef previously lacked any way to convert back to the FFI-bridge `RustBridge.<EnumName>` type, forcing swift e2e tests to fail with `<EnumType>.intoRust() undefined` for ExecutionProviderType, CodeContentMode, OcrElementLevel, etc. Add a generated extension that maps each Swift case to its `RustBridge.<Name>` counterpart — covering both unit-variant and data-variant enums. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/ruby: emit post-clear `list_*` assertion after `clear_*` calls.** Ruby e2e tests for plugin registry clear operations (`clear_ocr_backends`, `clear_post_processors`, etc.) previously called the method but did not assert that the list is actually empty afterwards, so tests would pass even if `clear` was a no-op. The codegen now detects function names ending in `_clear` and emits a follow-up call to the corresponding `list_*` function with an assertion that the result is empty, ensuring the test actually validates the clear operation's effect. (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/ruby/test_function.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-napi: emit typed `JsonValue` type alias for `serde_json::Value` fields instead of bare `unknown`.** Fields typed `TypeRef::Json` (e.g. `ActionResult.data`, `BrowserExtras.eval_result`) now emit `readonly data?: JsonValue` in the `.d.ts` instead of `readonly data?: unknown`, where `JsonValue` is a recursive type alias representing arbitrary JSON: `string | number | boolean | null | JsonValue[] | { [key: string]: JsonValue }`. This improves TypeScript inference and enables strict type checking of JSON return values across the binding API. (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/errors.rs`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: kwargs-only initializers, typed RBS fields, Enumerator streaming.** Emits idiomatic Ruby code for three long-standing non-idiomatic patterns: (a) All struct initializers now use kwargs-only signature (e.g. `CrawlConfig.new(max_depth: 10, timeout: 30000)`) instead of positional params — RBS stubs declare `def initialize: (?field1: T, ?field2: T, ...)` with all fields as optional kwargs for config types (has_default=true) or required/optional kwargs for result types. (b) JSON fields typed `TypeRef::Json` now emit `JsonValue` type alias (union of primitives/arrays/hashes/nil) instead of bare `untyped` to improve Sorbet inference — introduced `type json_value` + `type JsonValue` alias in module scope. (c) Streaming methods (`crawl_stream`, `batch_crawl_stream`, etc.) now correctly return `Enumerator[CrawlStreamIterator]` instead of String, matching the Magnus runtime implementation that yields events to a block or returns an iterator. RBS generation receives streaming_method_names set and derives iterator class names from method names for Enumerator type annotation. (`src/backends/magnus/{gen_stubs.rs,type_map.rs,gen_bindings/mod.rs}`)

- **alef-cli/validate_versions: drop `"kreuzberg"` literal from go-doc version heuristic.** `read_go_doc_version` was project-scoped, branching on `lower.contains("kreuzberg")` to find version comments. The `"version"` and `"targets"` substrings cover the universal patterns (`// targets <Project> X.Y.Z`, `// version X.Y.Z`); drop the kreuzberg-specific token so alef stays generic across downstream projects. (`crates/alef-cli/src/commands/validate_versions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: Map Rust `String` return to Go `string` (was `*string`); `Option<String>` keeps `*string` for nullable distinction.** Functions and methods returning Rust `String` now generate Go signatures with bare `string` type (e.g. `func DetectMimeType() string` not `*string`), matching Go idiom where zero value `""` is meaningful. Rust `Option<String>` still maps to `*string` for struct fields and parameter types to represent nullability. Fixed `go_return_expr` to emit `C.GoString(ptr)` directly for non-optional strings instead of wrapping in a closure that returns `*string`, and updated `go_optional_type` to distinguish optional vs bare scalar types at call site. (`src/backends/go/src/{type_map,gen_bindings/{types,functions,methods}}.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp/naming: apply 3+ letter acronym PascalCase rule.** C# convention prefers `Uri`, `Xml`, `Json` over `URI`, `XML`, `JSON` for 3+ letter acronyms. The `csharp_type_name` function now normalizes all-uppercase acronym names (e.g., `URI` → `Uri`, `XML` → `Xml`) to PascalCase before applying preserved-initialism rules (GraphQL, ID, UUID), ensuring type filenames and class names conform to Microsoft's framework design guidelines. (`src/codegen/naming.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: serialize record-type parameters to JSON in static factory methods.** When a static factory method (e.g., `ExtractionResult.FromOcr`) takes a record-type parameter (e.g., `OcrExtractionResult`), the parameter must be serialized to JSON and marshalled through the FFI layer via `{TypeName}FromJson()`, not passed as a raw `nint`. The codegen now detects `TypeRef::Named` parameters in static factories and emits serialize/deserialize wrapper code with try-finally cleanup, matching the pattern used for receiver-based methods. This fixes compile errors when factories receive record-type arguments. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

### Added

- **alef-cli**: Add `no_project_special_casing` integration test that fails CI if codegen introduces literal references to specific downstream projects (kreuzberg, kreuzcrawl, html-to-markdown, tree-sitter-language-pack, liter-llm, spikard) outside test/doc contexts.

- **alef-e2e: add `stream.has_page_event`, `stream.has_error_event`, `stream.has_complete_event`, and `stream.event_count_min` virtual fields for streaming-fixture assertions.** Streaming fixtures previously had no way to assert against per-event-type presence or minimum event counts without relying on `not_empty`-style synthetic assertions that every backend had to emit as `// skipped`. The new virtual fields are resolved by `streaming_assertions.rs` with per-language accessors (Python list comprehensions, TypeScript `Array.some`/`.filter`, Rust iterator combinators, etc.), unskipping streaming assertions across every backend's streaming test suite. (`src/e2e/codegen/streaming_assertions.rs`)

- **alef-e2e: normalize `pages_crawled` / `min_pages` assertion aliases to `count_equals` / `count_min` on the `pages` field.** Fixtures commonly use `crawl.pages_crawled` (or bare `pages_crawled`) with `equals` and `crawl.min_pages` with `greater_than_or_equal` to assert the number of pages a crawl yielded. Neither is a struct field on the call's result type, so every backend was emitting `// skipped: field 'crawl.pages_crawled' not available on result type` for these assertions. A new post-load `normalize_assertions` pass rewrites the alias to a `count_*` assertion targeting the `pages` field that the `crawl`-shaped calls already expose via `result_fields`, letting every backend render the assertion through its existing `count_equals` / `count_min` paths. (`src/e2e/fixture.rs`)

### Changed

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/elixir: emit `mime_type:` keyword-arg for `extract_file_async`.** Elixir NIF signatures for async file extraction expect the optional `mime_type` parameter as a keyword argument, not positional. The codegen was emitting positional arguments, causing `argument error` at runtime. Updated `build_args_and_setup` to format all optional arguments as keyword-list entries (`name: value`) regardless of their primitive type (string, file_path, bytes, etc.), not just for json_object types. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/csharp, alef-e2e/c: build per-call `FieldResolver` from the resolved fixture call's `result_fields`.** Both backends previously constructed a single top-level `FieldResolver` from `e2e_config.result_fields` and reused it for every fixture, so assertions like `pages.length` on a fixture routed to the `crawl` call (whose `[crates.e2e.calls.crawl] result_fields = ["pages", ...]` includes `pages`) were being skipped because the top-level `result_fields` (scoped to the default `scrape` call) does not list `pages`. This matches the per-call resolver pattern already used by the go/java/swift/dart/zig/kotlin/elixir/php/ruby backends and unskips ~250 assertions across the kreuzcrawl C and C# e2e suites (the C# `CrawlTests` alone gained 60+ `result.Pages.Count` assertions). (`src/e2e/codegen/csharp.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/c.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: don't double-wrap `Option<T>` getter return type for already-optional IR fields.** `types.rs:gen_getter_methods` unconditionally applied `mapper.optional(...)` when `field.optional` is true, producing `Option<Option<T>>` getters for fields whose IR type is already `TypeRef::Optional<T>` (e.g. Update DTOs where every field is both `field.optional == true` and `TypeRef::Optional<inner>`). The getter body `self.{name}.clone()` returns the storage type `Option<T>`, which the declared return type `Option<Option<T>>` cannot accept — Rust rejects the implicit `Option<T> → Option<Option<T>>` coercion with E0308. Detect `matches!(field.ty, TypeRef::Optional(_))` and skip the outer wrap in that case. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-napi: exclude `*Builder` / `*Update` DTOs from `From<>` impl emission to match struct-emission filter.** The struct-emission loop already filters `!typ.name.ends_with("Builder") && !typ.name.ends_with("Update")`, but the parallel `From<binding> for core` emission loops did not — they iterated every type and emitted impls referencing `Js{Type}Update`/`Js{Type}Builder` types that were never defined, yielding `cannot find type` E0425 for any DTO with an Update sibling (e.g. `JsConversionOptionsUpdate`). Apply the same filter at all three conversion-emission sites (main loop, pre-seed of `emitted_binding_to_core`, fallthrough loop). (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/dart: drop synthetic `expect(result, isNotNull)` from `not_error` assertion.** Dart rejects `expect(<void>, isNotNull)` with `error: This expression has type 'void' and can't be used`. Trait-bridge `clear_*` fixtures with a `not_error`-only assertion list therefore failed to compile every `*_management_test.dart`. `await` already raises on error, so the explicit `expect` adds nothing — emit only a doc-comment placeholder so the test body remains valid for non-mock fixtures. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit JSONDecoder body for throwing functions whose return type swift-bridge cannot natively bridge.** swift-bridge transports `Vec<Vec<T>>`, `Map<_, _>`, and `Json` returns from `Result<T, _>` functions through `RustString` (serde-serialised). The previous forwarder emitted `return try RustBridge.fn(...)` directly, which the Swift compiler rejected with `cannot convert return expression of type 'RustString' to return type '[[Float]]'`. Detect those return shapes via a new `return_uses_json_bridge` helper and emit a three-line body that `.toString()`s the RustString, converts to `Data`, then decodes with `JSONDecoder` against the declared Swift type. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: coerce `RustString` to native Swift `String` for throwing functions with `String` return.** swift-bridge maps `Result<String, _>` to `throws -> RustString` (the error path forces the union through the RustString ABI), but the public forwarder declares `-> String`. Without the `.toString()` coercion the Swift compiler rejected the body with `cannot convert return expression of type 'RustString' to return type 'String'`. The previous codegen omitted the suffix based on the incorrect assumption that swift-bridge maps `Result<String, _>` directly to native String — only the non-throwing case actually does. Introduce a `throws` parameter to `forwarder_return_conversion_suffix` and use it at call sites. Snapshot + bytes-overload regression test updated. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: stop emitting broken `RustStringRef.toString()` shim, expand `as_str().toString()` at call sites.** The 0.17.1 shim appended `extension RustStringRef { func toString() }` to swift-bridge's `SwiftBridgeCore.swift`, but Swift rejected it with `non-'@objc' instance method 'toString()' is declared in extension of 'RustStringRef' and cannot be overridden` because `RustString` is a subclass of `RustStringRef` and declares its own `toString()` in a separate extension. Swift cannot resolve the override chain across two non-@objc extensions in the same class hierarchy. Replace every emitted `.map { $0.toString() }` for `RustVec<RustString>` (in `vec_elem_convert_expr`, `forwarder_return_conversion_suffix`, and all three optional/required Vec<String> sites in `swift_ffi_read_expr`) with `.map { $0.as_str().toString() }`. The post-process step now strips any legacy shim previously appended by older alef revisions. Snapshot tests + the bulk-nested constructor assertion updated. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-dart: skip regular emission in userland bridge class for trait-bridge `clear_fn` names.** The Rust-side fix landed earlier still left the Dart userland file (`packages/dart/lib/src/{module}.dart`) declaring `static Future<void> clearXxxs()` twice — once by the regular-function forwarder loop and again by `emit_trait_bridge_methods`. Dart rejected the duplicate with "already declared in this scope". (`src/backends/dart/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-zig: drop optional wrapper from `_out_error` in clear/unregister templates.** Templates declared `var _out_error: ?[*c]u8 = null` and passed `&_out_error` to the C function which expects `[*c][*c]u8`. The Zig type checker rejected the implicit `*?[*c]u8 → [*c][*c]u8` coercion. Drop the optional wrapper to match the C signature directly and switch the null-check to a non-optional comparison. (`src/backends/zig/templates/clear_fn_body.jinja`, `src/backends/zig/templates/unregister_fn_body.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-swift: skip regular emission in free-function forwarder pass for trait-bridge `clear_fn` names.** Mirror of the earlier rust-side fix — the userland `Kreuzberg.swift` forwarder pass still emitted `public func clearOcrBackends() throws { try RustBridge.clearOcrBackends() }` alongside the trait-bridge forwarder, causing `invalid redeclaration` for every clear_* across the public Swift module. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: skip regular emission in userland facade for trait-bridge `clear_fn` names.** The previous fix skipped the API-stub class, but the userland `Kreuzberg.php` facade still emitted `clearOcrBackends()` from the regular `api.functions` loop alongside the trait-bridge loop's emission, triggering PHP fatal `Cannot redeclare ...` at extension load. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: drop duplicate trait-bridge `clear_fn` registration.** The fix in 0.17.1 stopped emitting two `pub fn clear_*` definitions, but the Ruby module registration loop (`module.define_module_function(...)`) still emitted the call twice: once from the regular `api.functions` registration pass and once from the trait-bridge registration loop. Skip trait-bridge-managed names in the regular pass so the function is registered exactly once. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-zig: align synthetic `unregister_{snake}` template with `KreuzbergError!void` body.** The bdceae1e migration converted the body and the configured-name signature template to `KreuzbergError!void` style, but the synthetic-name `unregister_fn_signature.jinja` was missed and still emitted `pub fn unregister_{snake}(name: [*c]const u8, out_error: ?*?[*c]u8) i32`. Because the zig backend never instantiates `ZigTraitBridgeGenerator`, only the synthetic path runs, leaving the `out_error` parameter unused and breaking the build with `error: unused function parameter`. Drop the `out_error` parameter and return `KreuzbergError!void` to match the body. (`src/backends/zig/templates/unregister_fn_signature.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-swift: skip regular emission for trait-bridge `clear_fn` names.** Mirror the go/dart/php/napi/magnus/rustler fix. `__swift_bridge__clear_*` was emitted twice — once via the regular function-emission loop and once via the trait-bridge wrappers — breaking `cargo build -p kreuzberg-swift` with `E0428: __swift_bridge__clear_*` is defined multiple times. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go, alef-backend-dart: extend trait-bridge `clear_fn` skip to Go and Dart dispatchers.** Mirror the 0.17.1 magnus/rustler/0.17.2 napi/php fix. Without this filter the Go binding refused to compile (`Clear* redeclared in this block`) and the Dart binding triggered noisy "still multiple objects with same key" warnings from `flutter_rust_bridge_codegen` on every regen. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/src/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-napi, alef-backend-php: extend trait-bridge `clear_fn` skip to NAPI-RS and ext-php-rs dispatchers.** Mirror the 0.17.1 magnus/rustler fix — call `alef_codegen::generators::trait_bridge::is_trait_bridge_managed_fn(name, bridges)` in the napi and php backend dispatchers so trait-bridge `clear_*` functions are not emitted twice. Without this filter the napi crate would refuse to compile (`E0428` duplicate definition) and the ext-php-rs crate would emit colliding method names. (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

## [0.17.1] - 2026-05-20

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-magnus, alef-backend-rustler: skip regular emission for trait-bridge `clear_fn` names.** Functions named in any `trait_bridge.clear_fn` (e.g. `clear_ocr_backends`, `clear_post_processors`) were emitted twice: once by the regular function-emission loop (because `clear_*` takes no `dyn Trait` parameter, so `find_bridge_param` does not match), and once by the trait-bridge code path's `gen_clear_fn`. Result: Magnus/ext Ruby builds failed with `E0428: the name 'clear_*' is defined multiple times`, and Rustler NIF builds aborted at runtime with `Duplicate NIF entry for 'Elixir.Kreuzberg.Native':clear_*/0` — taking down the entire NIF (every `Kreuzberg.Native.*` call became `UndefinedFunctionError`). Add `alef_codegen::generators::trait_bridge::is_trait_bridge_managed_fn(name, bridges)` and use it in both backend dispatchers to skip the regular emission so only the trait-bridge wrapper is generated. (`src/codegen/generators/trait_bridge.rs`, `src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/swift: emit canonical `swift-format` output on first write.** The Swift scaffold emitter produced `Package.swift`, `Examples/Demo/main.swift`, `.editorconfig`, and `.swiftformat` with 4-space indentation and a trailing comma on the single-element `products:` array, while the test stub was missing a blank line between `import XCTest` and `@testable import`. `swift-format` (with its 2-space defaults) rewrote all four files on every `prek run --all-files` after regen. Fix: emit 2-space indentation throughout `Package.swift` and `Demo/main.swift`, drop the trailing comma from the single-element `products` array entry, add the blank line between import groups in the test stub, and align `.editorconfig` (`indent_size = 2`) and `.swiftformat` (`indent = 2`) with `swift-format`'s defaults. Five new assertions in `test_scaffold_swift` pin the canonical form. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/swift.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: emit JNI Cargo.toml in cargo-sort canonical form.** The JNI scaffold emitter (`scaffold_jni`) emitted `[dependencies]` with the umbrella `{config.name}` dep baked at the head of a static list and `[package.metadata.cargo-machete]` placed after `[dependencies]`. `cargo sort` would rewrite the file on every regen because (a) the umbrella name does not sort first alphabetically for most consumers (kreuzcrawl, tree-sitter-language-pack, liter-llm), and (b) cargo-sort hoists `[package.metadata.*]` sub-tables directly after `[package]`. Fix: collect dep lines into a `Vec<String>`, sort alphabetically, and emit `[package.metadata.cargo-machete]` between `[package]` and `[lib]`. Two regression tests assert section order and alphabetised deps. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/jni.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/brew: emit 2-space-indented shell scripts so shfmt does not reformat them.** The brew e2e generator emitted `writeln!` literals with 4-space (and 8-space) shell-block indentation. Every `prek run --all-files` cycle invoked `shfmt -i 2` which rewrote the indentation, leaving every regenerated brew runner script in working-tree diff. Fix: emit 2-space (and 4-space) indents directly in the renderer; add a regression test pinning the canonical indentation depth across the entire emitted runner script. (`src/e2e/codegen/brew.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/ruby: restore `expect(result).not_to be_nil` for `not_error` assertions on non-void returns.** Commit `a1a50adc` narrowed the nil-check suppression too broadly: any fixture whose only assertion was `not_error` skipped the explicit `not_to be_nil` check even when the underlying call returned a non-`Result<()>` value. The result was that ruby `not_error`-only fixtures emitted an unused `let result = ...; end` body — the same empty-body / unused-local pattern that the dart fix corrected. Revert that branch of the template so the explicit `not_to be_nil` returns for non-void results; `Result<()>` (true unit returns) still suppress the nil check. (`src/e2e/templates/ruby/test_function.jinja`)

- **alef-scaffold: emit Rustler NIF crate Cargo.toml in cargo-sort canonical section order** (`[package]` → `[workspace]` → `[lib]` → `[dependencies]`). The previous template placed `[lib]` before `[workspace]` and `[workspace]` after `[dependencies]`, causing `cargo sort` to rewrite the file on every post-generation hook run. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/elixir.rs`)

- **alef-e2e (all 8 backends): thread `adapter.request_type` to `build_args_and_setup` so streaming call sites wrap the mock URL in a typed request object.** Every per-language e2e backend's `build_args_and_setup` helper emitted the raw URL variable as the argument for a `mock_url`-typed call arg, even when the corresponding adapter declares a `request_type` (e.g. `kreuzcrawl::CrawlStreamRequest`). The generated tests passed the bare URL string directly to the streaming adapter, which expects a typed request struct, causing compile/type errors in every language. Fix: thread `adapters: &[AdapterConfig]` from `generate()` through `render_test_file` → `render_test_method/render_test_case` → `build_args_and_setup`. In each `build_args_and_setup`, when the function name matches an adapter with a `request_type`, strip the Rust path prefix and emit a language-idiomatic typed request constructor binding before the call. (`src/e2e/codegen/{python,typescript,go,ruby,java,csharp,elixir,php}.rs`)
