When a core function returns `&[T]`, the emitted wrapper previously called `.into_iter().map(T)` — but `into_iter()` on `&[T]` yields `&T`, and the tuple constructor `T(...)` does not accept `&CoreT`. The fix emits `.iter().map(|x| T(x.clone()))` when `f.returns_ref` is true. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/shims.rs`)
- **alef-backend-dart**: clone refs in Vec-of-Named return wrapper when `returns_ref = true`. Same root cause as the Swift fix: `return_transmute_expr` now accepts `returns_ref: bool` and emits `.iter().map(|x| T::from(x.clone()))` (or opaque `.iter().map(|inner| T { inner: inner.clone() })`) when the function returns a reference slice. (`src/backends/dart/src/gen_rust_crate/bridge_fn.rs`)
- **alef-backend-zig**: emit clear_<trait>s pub fns for the trait-bridge family (was previously omitted, leaving plugin registries un-clearable from Zig consumers).
- **alef-backend-csharp**: emit `Clear()` static method on each trait-bridge nested class (was previously omitted, leaving plugin registries un-clearable from .NET consumers).

### Added

- **alef-backend-magnus: emit Ruby 3.2+ `Data.define` sum types with marker modules instead of class inheritance.** Data-carrying internally-tagged enums now generate a marker module with `interface!` and `abstract!` Sorbet annotations, plus a dispatcher `from_hash(hash)` that routes to the correct variant by discriminator tag. Each variant becomes `<Name> = Data.define(:field1, :field2) do include MarkerModule ... end`, gaining Sorbet-typed attribute readers, variant predicate methods (e.g., `characters?`, `tokenizer?`), and per-variant `from_hash` factories. This is the canonical Ruby 3.2+ idiom for sealed sum types: modules can be mixed into Data classes, so instances return `true` for `is_a?(MarkerModule)` while retaining all Data-class ergonomics (immutability, destructuring, equality). The previous class-inheritance hierarchy pattern caused issues with advanced Ruby idioms and prevented safe integration with other Data-based code. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-core, alef-extract: add `ErrorDef.methods` field and extract whitelisted introspection methods from error enums.** `ErrorDef` in the IR now carries `pub methods: Vec<MethodDef>` (serde-defaulted to `[]`). `alef-extract` walks inherent `impl <ErrorType>` blocks and populates this field with a fixed whitelist — `status_code`, `is_transient`, `error_type` — filtering out all other methods (Display helpers, internal utilities, trait impls). Enables downstream backends to expose per-variant introspection on error types across the FFI without accidentally leaking Rust-only ergonomics. (`src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/functions.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/types.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold, alef-backend-jni: add `[crates.jni] crate_dir` override for JNI crate directory naming.** When `config.name` carries a language-specific suffix (e.g. `"html-to-markdown-rs"`) the default `<config.name>-jni/` derivation produces `crates/html-to-markdown-rs-jni/` — inconsistent with every other binding crate (`*-node`, `*-py`, `*-wasm`, etc.). A new optional field `crate_dir` on `JniConfig` allows consumers to override the base name: `[crates.jni] crate_dir = "html-to-markdown"` places the crate at `crates/html-to-markdown-jni/`. Both `scaffold_jni` and `jni_output_path` now call `config.jni_crate_base()` (which reads the override, falling back to `config.name`). The umbrella dep-table key remains `config.name` (the Cargo package name) with `path = "../<core_crate_dir>"` unchanged. Existing consumers without the override retain identical output — the tslp regression (CHANGELOG [0.16.25]) is unaffected. (`src/core/config/languages.rs`, `src/core/config/resolved/ffi.rs`, `src/backends/jni/src/gen_shims.rs`, `crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/jni.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-swift: emit complex DTOs (Vec/nested-struct/enum fields) as first-class Swift structs.** `first_class_field_supported` now accepts `Vec<T>`, `Optional<Vec<T>>`, and `Named(S)` where S is a known first-class struct or a unit serde enum. Unit serde enums gain `: String, Codable, Sendable, Hashable` conformance with proper raw-value annotations (e.g. `case toolCalls = "tool_calls"`) so structs containing them derive `Codable` automatically. `swift_ffi_read_expr` handles the new field shapes: `Vec<Named>` via `.map { try T($0) }`, `Optional<Vec<Named>>` via `?.map { ... }`, and enum fields via `T(rawValue: rb.field().toString())`. The fixed-point iteration that builds `known_dto_names` is seeded with unit serde enum names so dependent structs resolve in the same pass. The `unit_enum_raw_value` helper computes serde-accurate raw values for `snake_case`, `camelCase`, `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE`, `PascalCase`, and `kebab-case` rename strategies. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/tests/snapshot_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: suppress PMD CPD on intentional FFI boilerplate via `// CPD-OFF` / `// CPD-ON` markers, and correct `checkLastFfiError` modifier to `static`.** The Java codegen emits two boilerplate blocks that are structurally identical across every opaque-type class by design — the `checkLastFfiError`/`STREAM_MAPPER` pair in `streaming_helpers.jinja`, and the JSON-roundtrip FFI tail in `stream_method_named_result.jinja`. PMD CPD flagged the first as a 35-line/202-token duplication across 6 files (DownloadManager, LanguageRegistry, Node, Parser, Tree, TreeCursor) and the second as a 32-line/172-token duplication between `Node.walk()` and `Tree.walk()` — both inherent to the FFI emission pattern; extracting helpers would just hide the duplication behind a private method without removing the underlying boilerplate. Both templates now wrap their body in PMD line-comment markers (`// CPD-OFF` … `// CPD-ON`) so `mvn verify` passes without weakening duplication detection elsewhere. Additionally, `checkLastFfiError` is now `private static void` instead of `private void` — the method uses zero instance state, the prior modifier was an oversight. (`src/backends/java/templates/streaming_helpers.jinja`, `src/backends/java/templates/stream_method_named_result.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-napi: disambiguate static `From` method via typed let-binding.** When a DTO type emits a static `from` method (e.g., `ConversionOptions::from(update: ConversionOptionsUpdate)`), the NAPI backend previously generated `ConversionOptions::from(update.into())`, which is ambiguous to the Rust compiler: both the explicit `From<ConversionOptionsUpdate>` impl AND the blanket `From<T> for T` match, and `update.into()` can produce either. The fix emits a let-binding with an explicitly-typed inner conversion: `let _arg: html_to_markdown_rs::options::ConversionOptionsUpdate = update.into(); ConversionOptions::from(_arg)`, which disambiguates both the outer call and the inner conversion. The param's core type is looked up from the API surface and emitted with its fully-qualified path. (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: honor `exclude_types` in DTO/enum/error emission.** The Kotlin-Android backend was iterating `api.types`, `api.enums`, and `api.errors` directly without applying the per-language `exclude_types` filter configured via `[crates.kotlin_android].exclude_types` (or inherited from `[crates.ffi].exclude_types`). Types in those lists were still emitted as `.kt` data classes, enums, and sealed-class errors. The fix mirrors the Java backend pattern: `effective_exclude_types` collects names from both the FFI and `kotlin_android` config sections; `api_without_excluded_types` builds a filtered `ApiSurface` removing matching types, enums, errors, and any functions or fields that reference them; the filter is applied at the top of `generate_bindings` before any file emission. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/lib.rs`)

- **alef-backend-napi: deref `Box<T>` when converting tagged-enum variant fields to the flattened binding struct.** The tagged-enum core-to-binding match arms in `gen_tagged_enum_core_to_binding` emitted `Some(field.into())` for Named-typed fields, but for `Box<T>` fields (e.g. an internally-tagged variant `CrawlEvent::Page { result: Box<CrawlPageResult> }`) this fails with `E0277: the trait bound JsT: From<Box<T>> is not satisfied` — `Box<T>: Into<JsT>` is not provided. The fix consults `field.is_boxed` and emits `Some((*field).into())` in both the required-Named and binding-struct-Named branches (mirroring the existing synth-field path). Optional `Option<Box<T>>` fields similarly need `f.map(|v| (*v).into())`. (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: emit `_to_json` and `_free` for enum types used as streaming-adapter `item_type`.** The FFI streaming `_next` helper (emitted via `gen_stream_handle_functions`) returns `*mut item_type`, but `enum_pointer_return` was previously populated only from free-function returns, type-method returns, and struct field types — never from streaming adapters' `item_type` field. When the streaming item was an enum (e.g. kreuzcrawl's `CrawlEvent`), the FFI surface exposed `_next` without the matching `_to_json` / `_free`, leaving downstream language bindings (Go, Ruby, Java, C#, …) unable to consume the returned pointer. The fix adds an explicit pass over `config.adapters` that inserts each streaming adapter's enum `item_type` into `enum_pointer_return` before the enum emission loop. (`src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-codegen, alef-backend-csharp: split the shared INITIALISMS list and apply Microsoft's framework-design naming for C#.** The 0.16.71 fix added GraphQL handling to a single INITIALISMS list shared by Go and C#. That list also included `JSON`, `HTTP`, `URL`, etc., which produced names like `ProblemDetailsToJSON` for the user-method wrapper while alef's hardcoded helper declarations stayed at `ProblemDetailsToJson` — a hard-fail when the wrapper called the non-existent declaration. C# now uses a narrow `CSHARP_INITIALISMS = ["GraphQL", "ID", "UUID", "URI"]` list (product names + 2-letter abbreviations only), keeping `Json`/`Http`/`Url` consistent with Microsoft framework design guidelines AND with alef's hardcoded `{Type}ToJson`/`{Type}FromJson`/`{Type}Free` helper sites. Go's wider list is unchanged. (`src/codegen/naming.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/type_map.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: emit `{Type}FromJson` and `{Type}ToJson` DllImport declarations for record types whose only user-facing method is an instance wither (`fn with_*(self, ...) -> Self`).** The wrapper synthesises `var selfHandle = NativeMethods.{Type}FromJson(selfJson)` and `var jsonPtr = NativeMethods.{Type}ToJson(nativeResult)` for those methods, but the previous opaque-param/return collection logic only inserted the owner type when one of its method params/returns was a Named type. Types like `ProblemDetails.with_detail(self, detail: String) -> Self` and `SseEvent.with_id(self, id: String) -> Self` therefore skipped the declaration entirely, leaving the wrapper to reference an undeclared symbol. The owner type is now added to both `opaque_param_types` and `opaque_return_types` for every `method.receiver.is_some()` case. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: apply Go acronym normalization to the inner type name in the JSON-roundtrip default-method emission.** The outer function signature uses `go_type_name(name)` (e.g. `JSONRPCConfig`), but the inline `func() *Foo { ... var result Foo ... }` closure used the raw IR `name` (`JsonRpcConfig`), producing two distinct identifiers that don't resolve. Affected at minimum: `JSONRPCConfigDefault`, `OpenAPIConfigDefault`. Inner references now match the outer signature so Go typechecks cleanly. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/types.rs::1631`)

- **alef-backend-java: gate all builder-dependent emissions on `should_emit_builder` (not the looser `typ.has_default`).** Three sites were still gated on `typ.has_default` after the conditional builder feature landed, causing records that have defaults but fall below the auto-emit threshold to reference a Builder class that wasn't generated: (1) the Jackson `JsonPOJOBuilder` import — Checkstyle flagged it as `UnusedImports` downstream because the `@JsonPOJOBuilder` annotation is only emitted inside `gen_builder_nested_class`, which already runs only when `should_emit_builder` is true; (2) the `@JsonDeserialize(builder = TypeName.Builder.class)` class-level annotation — pointed at a non-existent nested class; (3) the `public static Builder builder()` factory method — its return type `Builder` no longer exists in scope. All three now match the emission condition, so the three stay in lockstep with the actual nested-Builder presence. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

### Added

- **alef-backend-java: conditional builder emission with `[crates.java.dto] builder = "auto" | "always" | "never"`.** The Java backend now gates builder class generation based on field count instead of unconditionally emitting a builder for every type with defaults. The `builder` knob accepts three modes: `"auto"` (default) emits a builder when field count >= 8 OR (has nested type AND count >= 5); `"always"` unconditionally emits builders for types with defaults; `"never"` disables builder emission entirely. This reduces boilerplate in kreuzberg's Java package: records with 2–7 fields no longer carry unnecessary nested `Builder` inner classes. Idiomatic Java 17+ prefers `record(...)` constructors with optional `withX(...)` methods for simple cases; builders shine when a type has many optional fields and complex dependencies. The threshold of 8 (or 5 with nested types) is a heuristic tuned for document-extraction configs: spot-checks confirm `BoundingBox` (4 fields) and `ExtractionConfig` (25 fields) respond appropriately. Configuration in `src/core/config/dto.rs` adds `JavaBuilderMode` enum and `JavaDtoConfig` struct; per-crate plumbing through `[crates.java.dto]` block in `alef.toml`. Implementation in `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs` adds `should_emit_builder()` predicate and `BUILDER_AUTO_THRESHOLD` constant.

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-napi: emit `Symbol.asyncIterator` on streaming iterator classes and correct streaming method TS signature.** The NAPI backend emitted `Promise<string>` for streaming methods (e.g. `chatStream`) because the `.d.ts` generator saw the raw IR return type (`String`) without consulting the streaming-adapter map. The iterator struct was a plain `#[napi]` class with only a `.next()` method, making `for await...of` impossible. Two fixes: (1) `gen_node_body` in `alef-adapters` now marks the iterator struct `#[napi(async_iterator)]` and implements NAPI-RS's `AsyncGenerator` trait instead of a bare `#[napi] impl` block — NAPI-RS sets `Symbol.asyncIterator` on every instance automatically at class-init time; (2) `gen_dts` in `alef-backend-napi` now accepts the `streaming_item_types` map, overrides streaming method return types to `Promise<AsyncGenerator<ItemType, void, undefined>>`, and appends a proper class declaration for each iterator (with `next()`, `[Symbol.asyncIterator]()`). TypeScript callers can now use idiomatic `for await (const chunk of client.chatStream(req)) { ... }` without `@ts-ignore`. (`crates/alef-adapters/src/streaming.rs`, `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/errors.rs`, `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-dart: emit the reverse-direction `From<LocalMirror> for kreuzcrawl::CoreMirror` impl for streaming-adapter request types.** Streaming method bodies use `let core_req: kreuzcrawl::X = req.into();` which requires that direction, but until now alef only emitted `From<kreuzcrawl::X> for LocalX`. Simple request types such as `CrawlStreamRequest { url: String }` have no sanitized fields, so they never entered the `types_needing_from_impl` transitive closure that previously gated `From<LocalT> for CoreT` emission. The fix collects each streaming adapter's param types that are declared as mirror types and emits the mirror-to-core `From` impl for any not already covered. Surfaced as `E0277` in the regenerated `kreuzcrawl-dart` crate's `crawl_stream` / `batch_crawl_stream` methods.

- **alef-backend-napi: emit trait-bridge registration functions (`register_*`) in TypeScript declarations.** The Rust `register_*` impls existed but their `.d.ts` declarations were missing; `gen_dts` now emits `export declare function register*(impl: object): void;` per bridge. Fix in `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/errors.rs::gen_dts`.

- **alef-backend-extendr: emit trait-bridge `register_*` / `unregister_*` / `clear_*` functions in `extendr_module!`, `extendr-wrappers.R`, and `NAMESPACE`.** The R backend already emitted `#[extendr]` wrappers for trait-bridge plugin functions, but they were missing from the three places that make them callable: the `extendr_module!` macro registers `wrap__<symbol>` entry points in the .so, `extendr-wrappers.R` binds them to R functions via `.Call(...)`, and `NAMESPACE` exports them. Because all three iterated only `api.functions` (parsed from Rust source), the synthesised trait-bridge functions were silently dropped — kreuzberg's R package shipped without any plugin registration surface. The fix adds `collect_trait_bridge_functions(config)` which walks `config.trait_bridges` (honouring `exclude_languages = ["r"]`) and is woven into all three emitters. R wrappers are minimal: `register_<trait>(r_backend)` accepts a named list of closures, `unregister_<trait>(name)` accepts a plugin name, `clear_<trait>()` takes nothing. R is dynamically typed so signatures are intentionally untyped; the extendr `Robj` introspection on the Rust side validates required methods. Implementation in `src/backends/extendr/src/gen_bindings.rs`.

- **alef-backend-swift: emit top-level `public func` forwarders for every free function in `api.functions` plus trait-bridge `register_*` / `unregister_*` / `clear_*` entry points.** The Swift host wrapper previously emitted only convenience overloads for functions whose first parameter was `TypeRef::Bytes` or `TypeRef::Path`, hardcoded e2e wrappers for the legacy extraction surface, and `*FromJson` forwarders. Every other public free function on the source Rust crate was reachable only through the auto-generated `RustBridge` module (e.g. `RustBridge.embedTexts(...)`), forcing consumers of the host module to also `import RustBridge` — a violation of the alef binding-parity promise. Similarly, `register_*` / `unregister_*` / `clear_*` plugin entry points were synthesised from `[[trait_bridges]]` and emitted into the swift-bridge Rust crate, but not re-exposed at the host module's top level. The fix adds `emit_free_function_forwarders(api, config, out)` which walks `api.functions`, honours `[crates.swift] exclude_functions`, skips entries already covered by other emission passes (bytes/path overloads, e2e wrappers, JSON forwarders), and emits a `public func {camelName}(...)` forwarder for each remaining entry — converting `String` / `[String]` / `[UInt8]` between Swift-native and swift-bridge runtime types (`RustString`, `RustVec<RustString>`, `RustVec<UInt8>`) at the call boundary. A second pass, `emit_trait_bridge_forwarders(config, out)`, iterates `config.trait_bridges` (filtered to `bind_via = "function_param"` and `exclude_languages` excluding `"swift"`) and emits the matching trio of forwarders, each delegating to `RustBridge.{name}(...)`. Implementation in `src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`.

- **alef-backend-php: emit trait-bridge `register_*` / `unregister_*` / `clear_*` as static methods on the `KreuzbergApi` facade class.** The PHP backend was generating the Rust trait-bridge functions with `#[php_function]` attributes as standalone global functions (visible to PHP as `register_ocr_backend()`, `unregister_ocr_backend()`, etc.), but the facade-class approach expected them to be accessible as static methods on the `KreuzbergApi` class (`KreuzbergApi::registerOcrBackend(...)`, etc.) to match all other bindings. The fix adds iteration over `config.trait_bridges` in `gen_bindings/mod.rs`, emitting wrapper static methods that delegate to the underlying `#[php_function]` implementations. Each trait bridge contributes three methods: `register_*` (accepts a PHP object implementing the bridge interface), `unregister_*` (accepts a plugin name string), and `clear_*` (takes no arguments). This ensures kreuzberg's PHP package exposes the full plugin-registration surface on the native `KreuzbergApi` class, maintaining parity with Python, TypeScript, Ruby, Go, Java, C#, Elixir, and R. Implementation in `src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`.

- **alef-backend-php: drop the invalid `#[php_method]` attribute and rename the `impl` parameter so the trait-bridge facade methods actually compile.** Two follow-up bugs in the prior trait-bridge facade emission: (1) the emitted `#[php_method]` attribute does not exist in `ext-php-rs` (`#[php_impl]` already exposes every `pub fn` to PHP without a per-method annotation), causing 18 `cannot find attribute \`php_method\` in this scope` errors on the downstream `kreuzberg-php` build; (2) the `register_*` body used `impl` as a Rust keyword parameter name, producing 18 `expected identifier, found keyword \`impl\`` parse errors. The fix drops the attribute and renames the parameter to `backend`, and uses a `crate::{name}(...)` qualified call inside the body so the wrapper does not infinitely recurse into itself. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

### Added

- **alef-core: `AdapterConfig` gains a `skip_languages` field that suppresses streaming/callback/async adapter emission for the listed backends.** Mirrors the existing `[[crates.e2e.calls.*]].skip_languages` precedent. Unknown language names fail at config-resolve time with a descriptive error. Useful when a consumer's core crate cannot compile a given binding target (e.g. kreuzcrawl's tokio-runtime engine on `wasm32-unknown-unknown` with no working fetch shim) — instead of force-removing the adapter's surface from `alef.toml` entirely, the adapter stays declared everywhere it works with explicit per-backend opt-out. The filter is applied in `build_adapter_bodies` (so adapter bodies are never inserted for skipped backends) and as a safety early-bail in `streaming::generate_body`. Per-backend collection sites in alef-backend-{php,java,magnus,kotlin,kotlin-android,swift} also skip matching adapters so streaming method registrations and import injections are consistent. Configured as `[[crates.adapters]] skip_languages = ["wasm", "kotlin"]`.

## [0.16.71] - 2026-05-19

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-napi: emit trait-bridge registration functions (`unregister_*`, `clear_*`) in TypeScript declarations.** The NAPI backend was generating the Rust implementations of trait-bridge functions (`#[napi(js_name = "unregisterOcrBackend")]`, etc.) in `lib.rs` but omitting their declarations from `index.d.ts`, making them invisible to TypeScript callers. The `.d.ts` generator now iterates over `config.trait_bridges`, emitting `export declare function unregister*(name: string): void;` and `export declare function clear*(): void;` declarations for each bridge's `unregister_fn` and `clear_fn`. Fix in `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/errors.rs::gen_dts`.

### Added

- **alef-backend-php: emit `HtmlVisitorInterface` for visitor trait bridges so PHP users can implement custom visitors.** The PHP binding previously exposed only the opaque `VisitorHandle` type alias, giving no way for users to implement custom visitor logic. The backend now generates a `HtmlVisitorInterface` with method signatures for every visitor callback (15 methods: `visit_text`, `visit_element_start`, `visit_element_end`, `visit_link`, `visit_image`, `visit_heading`, `visit_code_block`, `visit_code_inline`, `visit_list_item`, `visit_list_start`, `visit_list_end`, `visit_table_start`, `visit_table_row`, `visit_table_end`, `visit_blockquote`). PHP users can now `implement HtmlVisitorInterface` on their callback class and pass instances via `ConversionOptions::$visitor`. The context parameter (`NodeContext`) is automatically hidden from the interface signature — only user-relevant parameters appear. Implementation in `src/backends/php/src/trait_bridge.rs:gen_visitor_interface` and `src/template_env.rs` (template registration).

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: emit `Flow<T>` wrappers on opaque-handle classes for streaming adapters.** The kotlin-android backend now generates streaming instance methods on handle-only wrapper classes (e.g., `CrawlEngineHandle`) when streaming adapters own them. Each wrapper class gets one `fun {streamName}(req): Flow<ItemType>` instance method per streaming adapter, using `callbackFlow { ... }` to drive the JNI `nativeXxxStart/Next/Free` extern triple. This mirrors the existing `alef-backend-kotlin` (JVM) pattern in `jni_emitter.rs:emit_jni_streaming_client_method`. Previously the JNI externs were emitted but no Kotlin wrapper exposed them — callers had no way to consume the stream without dropping to raw JNI. New collector logic in `emit_module_kt` detects handle-only streaming ownership and emits methods with full Jackson mapper initialization and `withContext(Dispatchers.IO)` for each JNI call. Lives in `src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs:217–350`.

### Changed

- **alef-backend-pyo3: emit `@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)` for Python DTO classes.** Modern Python 3.10+ best practice is `slots=True`, which reduces memory overhead and improves attribute access performance with no behavioral change for users. All alef-generated dataclasses now include `slots=True` in the decorator on all `@dataclass` types emitted to `options.py`. The types generated are composed only of primitives and standard collections (no self-referential defaults), so slots compatibility is guaranteed.

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-php: emit `HtmlVisitorInterface` as separate PHP file, not inline Rust code.** The previous implementation (commit 570fa3f4) appended the interface stub to the Rust trait-bridge code via `code.push_str()`, which broke Rust compilation with "expected one of \`!\` or \`::\`, found \`HtmlVisitorInterface\`". The interface must be emitted as a standalone file (`packages/php/src/HtmlVisitorInterface.php`) following the same pattern as opaque-type PHP class stubs in `generate_public_api()`. Also fixed type translation: method signatures now use proper PHP types (`string`, `?string`, `bool`, `int`, `float`) instead of mixing Rust syntax (e.g., `_text: &str`). Fix in `src/backends/php/src/trait_bridge.rs` (new `rust_type_to_php_type()` function) and `src/gen_bindings/mod.rs` (emit interface files in `generate_bindings()` loop).

- **alef-backend-csharp: change visitor-bridge property from `{ get; set; }` to `{ get; init; }`.** Records should enforce immutability semantics. The `IHtmlVisitor?` property in visitor-bridge records was mutable, violating record invariants. Now emitted as init-only (constructor-time initialization only), matching other record properties and enforcing this constraint at compile time. _Breaking change:_ code that was mutating the visitor property after construction will no longer compile — callers must now set it via the property initializer at construction time.

- **alef-backend-java: emit NativeLib MethodHandle for static methods on opaque types.** `gen_native_lib` previously skipped every `method.is_static` entry on opaque types, so the new static-factory emission referenced symbols (`TS_PACK_PARSER_DEFAULT`, `TS_PACK_DOWNLOAD_MANAGER_NEW`, etc.) that NativeLib never declared. Static methods are now emitted alongside instance methods; the only difference is the param-layout vector starts empty rather than with the receiver `ValueLayout.ADDRESS`. Fix in `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/native_lib.rs`.

- **alef-backend-java: use the (String, Throwable) exception constructor in `gen_static_factory_method`.** Emitted `throw new TreeSitterLanguagePackRsException("…")` (single-String arg) but the available constructors are `(int, String)` and `(String, Throwable)` — so the generated Java did not compile. Now emits `new {exception_class}("…", (Throwable) null)` to match the existing instance-method pattern. Fix in `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`.

- **alef-extract: skip `From`/`Into`/`TryFrom`/`TryInto` trait method extraction.** These conversion-trait impls reference Rust-only counterpart types (e.g. `impl From<tree_sitter::Point> for Point` references `tree_sitter::Point`, which has no representation in any target language). Emitting them produced nonsensical signatures like `Point.from(Point p)` in Java/C# and uncompilable code in napi where the input type was ambiguous between the JsX wrapper and the Rust counterpart. `Default` is intentionally retained — `Default::default()` is a legitimate preset constructor we want emitted. Fix in `src/extract/extractor/functions.rs::extract_trait_impl_methods`.

### Added

- **alef-extract: HashSet/BTreeSet/AHashSet/IndexSet/FxHashSet now resolve to Vec<T> in the IR so set-returning public APIs become exposed across all bindings (collapsing the wire shape).**

- **alef-backend-java: keyword-safe sanitiser `safe_java_method_name` for Rust impl-method names.** Rust impl blocks use names that are Java reserved words — `default`, `new`, `class`, etc. — and emitting them verbatim produces non-compiling Java (`public static Parser default()`). Sanitiser renames `default` → `defaultInstance` and `new` → `create`; any other `JAVA_KEYWORDS` collision falls back to a trailing-underscore suffix. Replaces the ad-hoc `if method.name == "default"` check in `emit_record_methods` and wires the new opaque static-factory and instance-method emission through the same helper. Lives in `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`.

- **alef-backend-java: emit static factory methods on opaque-handle classes.** Opaque types in Java had no public constructors / static factories — users could only obtain a `Parser`, `LanguageRegistry`, or `DownloadManager` via top-level static helpers on the main class. Rust impl methods with no `&self` receiver and `-> Self` return (e.g. `Parser::default()`, `LanguageRegistry::default()`, `DownloadManager::new(version)`) now emit as `public static ClassName methodName(...)` on the opaque-handle class itself, with full Panama FFM marshaling. Mirrors the existing `gen_instance_method` pattern. New `gen_static_factory_method` in `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`, called from `gen_opaque_handle_class` after instance-method emission.

- **alef-backend-napi: emit DTO impl-method companions as `#[napi]` free functions.** Previously, `#[napi(object)]` structs (DTOs in the Node backend) dropped every `impl` method from the Rust source, because object-style structs can't carry methods on the JS side. The new `gen_dto_method_fns` in `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/types.rs` emits each non-excluded impl method as a free function named `<typeName><MethodName>` (camelCase), serde-JSON round-tripping the receiver for instance "wither" methods. Static preset constructors (e.g. `ProcessConfig::all()`, `::minimal()`, `::default()`) and instance withers (e.g. `with_chunking(usize)`) are now callable from TypeScript. Call site added at `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs:438`, gated to the non-opaque branch.

- **alef-backend-napi: document that DTO types do NOT emit fluent builder classes.** NAPI-RS (TypeScript/Node.js) intentionally does NOT emit fluent builder classes for DTO types. TypeScript idiomatically uses object literals (`{ headingStyle: 'atx', wrap: true }`) rather than fluent builders (which are idiomatic in Java as nested `Foo.Builder` classes). DTO methods (withers returning `Self`) are exposed as free functions accepting the instance as the first parameter. Added documentation comment to `gen_opaque_struct_methods` in `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/types.rs` clarifying the pattern, rationale, and contrast with the Java backend (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`).

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-csharp: use `to_csharp_name` (not `csharp_type_name`) on `api.crate_name`.** `csharp_type_name` only applies initialism uppercasing — it does not perform snake_case → PascalCase conversion. Since `api.crate_name` is a snake_case crate identifier (e.g. `ts_pack_core`), the previous initialism fix accidentally regressed exception and wrapper class name generation: callers got `ts_pack_coreException` instead of `TsPackCoreException`. Routed through `to_csharp_name` (PascalCase + initialisms) at the two `api.crate_name` call sites in `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/mod.rs`. Includes a `Cow::to_string()` fix in `emit_record_methods` to satisfy the borrow checker. Updated `test_opaque_method_return_wraps_handle_without_to_json` to assert the now-correct `GraphQLRouteConfig` class name.

- **C# backend**: preserve initialisms in type names (e.g. `GraphQLRouteConfig` instead of `GraphQlRouteConfig`), matching Go behaviour. A new `csharp_type_name` function in `alef-codegen/src/naming.rs` applies longest-match initialism restoration to PascalCase IR type names, handling multi-segment initialisms like `GraphQL` that `heck` splits into `Graph`+`Ql`. All C# class name, enum name, and `TypeRef::Named` call sites in `alef-backend-csharp` now route through `csharp_type_name`. `to_csharp_name` (snake_case → PascalCase) also applies the same initialism handling so e.g. `graphql_route_config` → `GraphQLRouteConfig`.

### Documentation

- **README, `skills/alef/`, `.ai-rulez/`: refresh to cover v0.16.69 and capture recurring work patterns.** README adds `[workspace.client_constructors]`, `[crates.<lang>.stubs] emit_docstrings`, source-level `#[alef::skip]`/`#[alef::exclude]` precedence vs config-level filters, polyglot release flow pointer (`.github/workflows/publish.yaml` + `kreuzberg-dev/actions`), the missing CLI subcommands (`docs`, `migrate`, `validate`, `release-metadata`, `check-registry`, `go-tag`, `snippets`), splits Kotlin/JVM and Kotlin/Android into separate rows, and corrects the `--format` default (now `true` on `generate`/`init`/`all`). The `alef` user-facing skill picks up the same `client_constructors`/`emit_docstrings` config sections, the corrected `--format` default, the `cargo install --path crates/alef-cli --force` local-install loop, and a cross-link to the `release-procedure` skill; pre-commit hook revision in the skill bumped to match `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (`v1.1.11`). `.ai-rulez/` gains five new rule files (`binding-audit-pattern`, `upstream-triage`, `sibling-agent-coordination`, `publish-flow`, `local-alef-install`) and a new `binding-audit` slash-command skill; `config.toml` declares both `release-procedure` and `binding-audit` skills explicitly; `CLAUDE.md` regenerated from source.

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-pyo3: .pyi stub generator now emits AsyncIterator[T] return type for streaming methods (was emitting the buffered placeholder return type, mismatching the real async iterator emitted in the Rust shim).** The `gen_stubs` function now builds a lookup from `config.adapters` for `Streaming` pattern entries and overrides the return type of matching methods and free functions. `AsyncIterator` is added to the `from typing import` line automatically when at least one streaming method is present.

- **alef-backend-php: streaming methods now emit a lazy PHP `\Generator` instead of an eager-collected JSON string.** The PHP backend now emits three free functions per streaming adapter (`{owner}_{name}_start`, `_next`, `_free`) that expose an opaque handle to PHP, plus a high-level `public function {method}(...): \Generator` on the owning class that drives the handle via `yield json_decode($chunk, true)`. The originally-emitted eager method body is suppressed for streaming adapters via a `streaming_method_keys` filter in `src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs` so callers see only the Generator. Mirrors the Elixir `Stream.unfold` and Python `AsyncIterator` architectures already in place.

- **alef-adapters: Node streaming methods now return a `#[napi]` iterator class instead of materializing the entire stream into a Vec.** Callers consume items via `await iter.next()` (returns `null` at end-of-stream), matching the existing Python AsyncIterator pattern. The previous behavior collected every chunk into a JS Array eagerly, defeating the point of streaming.

- **alef-adapters, alef-backend-wasm: streaming method infrastructure for WASM (requires core library changes).** Added `gen_wasm_body` streaming adapter and return-type override logic in the WASM backend to emit iterator structs via wasm-bindgen. The pattern mirrors Python/Node: an iterator struct with a `RefCell<futures::channel::mpsc::Receiver>` (no threads in WASM) and async `next()` method, driven by a `wasm_bindgen_futures::spawn_local()` task. **NOTE:** kreuzcrawl's core library gates the streaming methods with `#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]`, preventing WASM from calling them. This is a library design limitation, not an alef limitation — the infrastructure is complete and ready when the core library exposes streaming methods for WASM. See `crates/alef-adapters/src/streaming.rs:495` and `src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/{mod.rs,types.rs,methods.rs}`.

- **alef-backend-zig: Zig streaming methods now return all chunks as a JSON array instead of only the last chunk.** Previously, `_last_json` was overwritten on every iteration, dropping every event except the final one. Now emits `[chunk1,chunk2,...]` so callers receive the full stream.

## [0.16.69] - 2026-05-19

### Added

- **alef-core, alef-codegen: introduce `[workspace.client_constructors.<TypeName>]` config and `gen_opaque_constructor()` shared helper**. New `ClientConstructorConfig` (params + body template + optional `error_type`, default `String`) on `WorkspaceConfig` is plumbed through `NewAlefConfig::resolve` into `ResolvedCrateConfig.client_constructors`. The codegen helper renders `pub fn new(params…) -> Result<Self, ErrType>` from the config body (with `{type_name}` and `{source_path}` placeholders) and accepts a per-backend attribute prefix (`#[new]`, `#[napi(constructor)]`, `#[wasm_bindgen(constructor)]`, `#[php_method]`, `#[frb]`, etc.) so every Rust-host backend can emit a uniform user-defined factory for opaque handles. (`src/core/config/workspace.rs`, `src/core/config/new_config.rs`, `src/core/config/resolved/mod.rs`, `src/codegen/generators/methods.rs`, `src/codegen/generators/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: emit `{prefix}_{type_snake}_new` constructor symbol from `[workspace.client_constructors]` config**. When `client_constructors.<TypeName>` is present, the FFI backend now emits a `#[unsafe(no_mangle)] pub unsafe extern "C" fn {prefix}_{type_snake}_new(params...) -> *mut TypeName` function. The template `type_new.jinja` was missing from the static TEMPLATES list despite being referenced by `gen_type_new()`; it is now registered. (`src/backends/ffi/templates/type_new.jinja`, `src/backends/ffi/src/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: emit `func New<TypeName>(params...) (*TypeName, error)` constructor from `[workspace.client_constructors]` config**. When an opaque type has a `client_constructors` entry, the Go backend now emits a `func New<TypeName>(params...) (*TypeName, error)` CGO wrapper. `*const c_char` params are marshalled via `C.CString()` + `defer C.free()`; other FFI types map to their CGO equivalents. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-zig: emit `pub fn create_<type_snake>(params...) !TypeName` constructor from `[workspace.client_constructors]` config**. When an opaque type has a `client_constructors` entry, the Zig backend emits `pub fn create_<type_snake>(...)  !TypeName`. String params use `allocator.dupeZ` for null-terminated conversion; the allocator param is prepended automatically when string params are present. (`src/backends/zig/src/gen_bindings/opaque_handles.rs`, `src/backends/zig/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: emit `public static TypeName Create(params...)` factory and `[DllImport] static extern IntPtr TypeNameNew(params...)` P/Invoke from `[workspace.client_constructors]` config**. When an opaque type has a `client_constructors` entry, the C# backend emits a `Create` factory method on the SafeHandle class and a matching `NativeMethods.TypeNameNew(...)` P/Invoke declaration. String params use `[MarshalAs(UnmanagedType.LPStr)]`. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-jni: emit `pub unsafe extern "system" fn Java_..._nativeNew<TypeName>(params...) -> jlong` constructor shim from `[workspace.client_constructors]` config**. When an opaque type has a `client_constructors` entry, the JNI backend emits a constructor shim whose symbol follows the `nativeNew<TypeName>` convention. `*const c_char` params are received as `JString` and unmarshalled via `jstring_to_string`. Returns `Box::into_raw` pointer as `jlong` on success, `0` on error. (`src/backends/jni/src/gen_shims.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: emit `external fun nativeNew<TypeName>(params...): Long` and `companion object { fun create(params...): TypeName }` from `[workspace.client_constructors]` config**. When an opaque type has a `client_constructors` entry, the Kotlin JNI emitter declares the `nativeNew<TypeName>` extern in the Bridge object and adds a `create(...)` factory in the DefaultClient companion object. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/jni_emitter.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3, alef-backend-napi, alef-backend-wasm, alef-backend-php, alef-backend-rustler, alef-backend-dart: emit a per-type constructor `impl` block from `[workspace.client_constructors]` config**. Every Rust-host backend that wraps opaque handles now consults the shared `client_constructors` map and appends a constructor impl beside its main impl block: PyO3 emits `#[pymethods] impl T { #[new] pub fn new(...) -> Result<Self, E> { ... } }`, NAPI emits `#[napi] impl T { #[napi(constructor)] ... }`, wasm-bindgen emits `#[wasm_bindgen] impl T { #[wasm_bindgen(constructor)] ... }`, ext-php-rs emits `#[php_impl] impl T { #[php_method] ... }`, Rustler emits a plain `impl T` body, and the Dart `lib.rs` shim emits `impl T { #[frb] pub fn new(...) ... }`. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/src/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`)

- **alef-extract: auto-disambiguate types with the same identifier in different modules by prepending PascalCase parent module name to the second+ collisions**. When two source types share the same identifier in sibling modules (e.g. `crate::SseEvent` and `crate::testing::SseEvent`), the second collision is now renamed by prepending its PascalCase parent module segment (`TestingSseEvent`), keeping the first-seen variant under its original name. Three-way (and deeper) collisions walk further up the module tree until a unique name is found. Sort order is by full source `rust_path` so first-seen-first is deterministic across runs. Eliminates the need for per-collision exclusions in consumer `alef.toml` files. (`src/extract/extractor/disambiguation.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/mod.rs`)

- **alef-extract: unwrap `Arc<Mutex<T>>` and `Arc<RwLock<T>>` to `CoreWrapper::ArcMutex`; peek through `Option<...>`**. `detect_core_wrapper` now inspects the inner type of an `Arc<...>` and returns `CoreWrapper::ArcMutex` when it is a `Mutex<T>` or `RwLock<T>` (last-segment match, so `std::sync::Mutex` and `tokio::sync::Mutex` are treated identically). It also peeks through a leading `Option<...>` so `Option<Arc<Mutex<T>>>` resolves the same as the bare form. `Arc<dyn Trait>` is intentionally left as plain `Arc` — trait-object ownership differs. The type resolver's `Box | Arc | Rc` unwrap arm is extended to also unwrap `Mutex` and `RwLock` to their inner type, so the inner `T` no longer leaks out as `TypeRef::Named("Mutex")` in generated bindings. Enables binding generation for structs that share state via Arc-wrapped synchronization primitives. (`src/extract/extractor/helpers.rs`, `src/extract/type_resolver.rs`, `src/codegen/conversions/mod.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-codegen: fluent-builder methods with `Json` (or `Option<Json>`) params now auto-delegate instead of stubbing to `unimplemented!()`**. `TypeRef::Json` was previously excluded from both `is_delegatable_param` and `is_simple_non_opaque_param`, so a perfectly delegatable shape like `pub fn with_extension(self, key: String, value: serde_json::Value) -> Self` fell through to the unimplemented stub. The binding takes the JSON value as a string and `gen_call_args` already emits `serde_json::from_str(&value)` at the call site to bridge it into the core `serde_json::Value` parameter, so Json is fully delegatable in both directions (return values are serialized via `.to_string()` by `wrap_return_with_mutex_mapped`). All Rust-host backends already depend on `serde_json` (used by Json field round-tripping), so no new dependency. (`src/codegen/shared.rs`, `src/codegen/generators/binding_helpers.rs`, `src/codegen/tests/integration_test.rs`)

- **alef-codegen: explicit adapter bodies now take precedence over auto-generated delegation in `gen_method`/`gen_static_method`**. The adapter override lookup was previously gated behind `!can_delegate` — a method that the codegen could delegate would silently ignore any user-provided adapter body. Now adapter bodies are checked first and unconditionally win; delegation is a fallback. Required to keep adapter overrides honored as more shapes (e.g. Json params, see above) become eligible for auto-delegation. (`src/codegen/generators/methods.rs`)

- **alef-cli: `alef generate` now runs formatters by default (`--format` defaults to `true`)**. Previously `alef generate` silently skipped the post-generation ruff/mix-format/gofmt/etc. pass unless `--format` was explicitly passed, so the written files were pre-formatter content. Downstream prek hooks then reformatted the files, changing their content after `alef:hash:` was computed, causing `alef verify --exit-code` to report every Python/Elixir/Go binding as stale. `alef all` already defaulted `format` to `true`; `alef generate` and `alef init` now match. Consumers who want to skip formatting can still pass `--format=false`. (`crates/alef-cli/src/main.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3, alef-backend-magnus: gate stub docstring emission behind `[crates.{lang}.stubs] emit_docstrings = true`**. Commit c2f18497 made `alef stubs` and `alef generate` unconditionally emit triple-quoted docstrings in `.pyi` files and `#`-comment blocks in `.rbs` files. Projects with `ruff lint.select = ["ALL"]` break on ruff rule PYI021 ("Docstrings should not be included in stub files"). The new `emit_docstrings` field on `StubsConfig` (default `false`) gates all docstring emission in both backends; consumers opt in by adding `emit_docstrings = true` to their `[crates.python.stubs]` or `[crates.ruby.stubs]` section. (`src/core/config/languages.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_stubs.rs`, `src/backends/magnus/gen_stubs.rs`, `src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: add `rt-multi-thread` to the emitted swift crate's tokio features**. v0.16.62 switched the swift-bridge async shims from per-call `Builder::new_current_thread()` to a process-wide `Builder::new_multi_thread()` runtime (so `reqwest`'s lazily-attached connection pool stays alive across calls), but the emitted `packages/swift/rust/Cargo.toml` still pinned `tokio = { features = ["rt", "macros"] }` — and `new_multi_thread` only exists when the `rt-multi-thread` feature is enabled. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack `CI Mobile` as `error[E0599]: no function or associated item named 'new_multi_thread' found for struct 'tokio::runtime::Builder'` on both `aarch64-apple-ios-sim` and `aarch64-apple-ios` cargo-check jobs after the v0.16.68 regen. Bump the emitted tokio dep to `features = ["rt", "rt-multi-thread", "macros"]`; iOS/macOS/Linux all support `rt-multi-thread` so no platform-conditional gating is required. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs`, snapshot fixtures)

### Changed

- **Bump `kreuzberg-dev/pre-commit-hooks` to v1.1.11, `crate-ci/typos` to v1.46.2, `Goldziher/gh-actions-updater` to v0.1.2.** Routine dev-tooling refresh; no behavioral changes. (`.pre-commit-config.yaml`)

## [0.16.68] - 2026-05-19

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-java: emit `checkLastError()` after fallible primitive-return and void-return FFI invocations**. The Java FFI codegen called `checkLastError()` only on the pointer-return path (where a NULL sentinel triggered it); fallible functions whose Rust signature is `Result<T, Error>` with a primitive `T` (e.g. `pub fn download(...) -> Result<usize, Error>` → Java `long`) or with `T = ()` silently dropped errors on the floor because the primitive `0` or the empty return couldn't distinguish "successful no-op" from "FFI set last_error". Now, when `FunctionDef.error_type.is_some()`, both the primitive-return and void-return branches of `gen_sync_function_method` emit `checkLastError()` immediately after the FFI invoke and ptr-cleanup so Java throws the expected `*Exception` on failure. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack `E2E (java)` where `DownloadTest.testDownloadInvalidLanguage` expected an exception for `download(List.of("zzz_definitely_not_a_real_language_xyz"))` but the bound `download` method returned 0 cleanly. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`)

- **alef-e2e: raise vitest `hookTimeout` to 120s and add explicit `teardownTimeout: 30s` in the typescript/wasm vitest.config templates**. Vitest 3.x has an internal worker RPC (`onTaskUpdate`) whose budget tracks `hookTimeout`; long single-file suites (the tslp `smoke.test.ts` runs 305 tests through `process()`, ~63s on macos-arm64) outran the default 10s hook budget and the worker shutdown handshake errored with `[vitest-worker]: Timeout calling "onTaskUpdate"` even though every test passed individually. Raising `hookTimeout` to 120s and pinning `teardownTimeout` at 30s gives the worker enough patience for long alef-generated suites without affecting per-test timing (still 30s via the existing `testTimeout`). (`src/e2e/templates/typescript/vitest.config.ts.jinja`, `src/e2e/templates/wasm/vitest.config.ts.jinja`)

## [0.16.67] - 2026-05-19

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e mock-server: honour `MOCK_SERVER_NO_STDIN_WATCH=1` to wait on SIGTERM instead of stdin EOF**. The standalone mock-server binary's default lifecycle waits for stdin EOF (parent-controlled subprocess pattern, fine for Rust/Node/Python harnesses that spawn it directly). The Swift e2e GitHub Actions job uses a different pattern — it backgrounds the mock-server in a `Start mock-server` step, then expects it to survive across to the next `Run E2E tests` step. When the per-step bash exits the inherited stdin FD closes and the mock-server immediately reads EOF and exits, leaving the test step to fail every smoke test with `error sending request for url (...)`. Add an opt-in env switch that swaps the lifetime watcher to `tokio::signal::ctrl_c()` so the server runs until the job step explicitly terminates it. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/mock_server.rs`)

## [0.16.66] - 2026-05-19

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-swift: share a single process-wide tokio runtime across all async wrappers instead of building one per call**. swift-bridge functions wrap async source calls with `Builder::new_current_thread().enable_all().build().block_on(...)` — fine for self-contained futures, but breaks `reqwest`. `reqwest::Client::builder().build()` lazily attaches its connection pool to the FIRST tokio runtime it sees, then the pool dies when that runtime drops. Every subsequent call (driven by a fresh runtime) hits an orphaned pool and fails with `error sending request for url (...)` — the exact symptom on liter-llm `E2E (swift)` smoke tests after v0.16.61 unblocked compile. Replace the per-call pattern with `crate::__alef_tokio_runtime()` — a `OnceLock<tokio::runtime::Runtime>` initialized once with `Builder::new_multi_thread()` so the pool stays alive for the process lifetime and works across reentrant host threads. Touches `gen_rust_crate/shims.rs` (top-level definition + accessor const), `gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`, and `gen_rust_crate/wrappers.rs`. Snapshot tests updated. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/{mod,shims,trait_bridge,wrappers}.rs`)

## [0.16.65] - 2026-05-19

### Added

- **alef-e2e/swift: emit a shared `TestHelpers.swift` with a `RustString: CustomStringConvertible` extension so XCTest failures print the actual Rust error content**. swift-bridge's `RustString` is an opaque class without a `description`, so any error thrown from a bridge function (`throw RustString(ptr: val.ok_or_err!)`) surfaces in XCTest output as the bare type name — `caught error: "RustBridge.RustString"` — with the actual Rust error message hidden inside the unprinted instance. Every E2E (swift) failure across the polyglot repos was indecipherable as a result. Add a one-time `TestHelpers.swift` per test target with `extension RustString: @retroactive CustomStringConvertible { public var description: String { self.toString() } }`. Diagnostic-only change; no test code modifications, no semantic shift in passing/failing — but failures now print readable error text. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

## [0.16.64] - 2026-05-19

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/swift: `swift_count_target` consults `SwiftFirstClassMap.vec_field_names` so `.count` on `RustVec` method-call accessors stays put**. v0.16.63's blanket `.toString()` injection (predicated only on the accessor ending in `)`) over-eagerly wrapped Vec-typed method-call accessors too — `result.output().toString().count` fails with `value of type 'RustVec<ResponseOutputItem>' has no member 'toString'`. Track per-IR `Vec<T>` (and `Option<Vec<T>>`) field names in a flat `vec_field_names: HashSet<String>` on `SwiftFirstClassMap`, expose `leaf_is_vec_via_swift_map` on `FieldResolver`, and have `swift_count_target` skip the wrap when the leaf is a Vec field. RustVec already exposes `.count` directly. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (swift)` ResponsesTests line 95 after v0.16.63. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

## [0.16.63] - 2026-05-19

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/swift: pin opaque (method-call) syntax after any `ArrayField` segment + inject `.toString()` before `.count` on opaque method-call accessors**. v0.16.62 dispatched per-fixture first-class/opaque syntax for the root, but two follow-on misses remained on liter-llm `E2E (swift)`: (1) array-indexed elements were emitted with property syntax when the element type's first-class Codable struct existed (`_vec_data[0].object`), even though `RustVec[i]` always yields the opaque `RustBridge.T` (its first-class twin is a separate Codable struct used only at FFI boundaries — never produced by indexing). swift compile error: `method 'object' was used as a property; add () to call it` on `ListModelsTests`. (2) The not-empty / is-empty assertion path emitted `result.id().count` for opaque-rooted scalar string fields, but `RustString` does not expose `.count` — only Swift `String` does. swift compile error: `value of type 'RustString' has no member 'count'` on `ResponsesTests`. Fixes: (1) Carry a `via_rust_vec` flag in `render_swift_with_first_class_map` that flips to true on the first `ArrayField` segment and pins opaque syntax for every following segment (advance still tracks `current_type` so first-class metadata stays accurate for cleanups, but the property/method-call decision is overridden). (2) Add `swift_count_target` helper in the swift codegen that wraps an accessor with `.toString()` when it ends in `)` (the opaque method-call signature), leaving first-class property accessors untouched. Wired into both the `not_empty` and `is_empty` length-expression paths. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

## [0.16.62] - 2026-05-19

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/swift: thread per-fixture `root_type` into `SwiftFirstClassMap` so each call's accessor dispatch reflects its actual return type**. v0.16.61 wired the per-segment first-class/opaque dispatcher but the `root_type` was derived once at codegen-build time (a single workspace-wide heuristic), so the dispatcher couldn't tell `chat()` (returns opaque `ChatCompletionResponse`) apart from `createFile()` (returns first-class `FileObject`). The map's default (`is_first_class(None) → true` → property access) was correct for first-class result types but emitted bare property syntax (`result.choices[0]`) for opaque types, surfacing as `function value was used as a property; add () to call it` and `method 'message' was used as a property` on liter-llm `E2E (swift)` SmokeTests/BatchesTests/ChatTests. Add `FieldResolver::with_swift_root_type` (clones the resolver with the map's `root_type` replaced) and `swift_call_result_type` in the Swift codegen which reads `call_config.overrides[<lang>].result_type` across `c, csharp, java, kotlin, go, php` (the same language-agnostic IR-name lookup PHP uses). Each fixture's call now gets its own resolver clone with the correct `root_type`, so opaque types route through method-call access and first-class types stay on property access. Also removed a stray `eprintln!` debug line in `render_test_method` that leaked build noise. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

## [0.16.61] - 2026-05-19

### Added

- **alef-e2e/swift: per-segment first-class/opaque dispatch via `SwiftFirstClassMap`**. v0.16.59 switched render_swift to property access for all field segments, fixing first-class Codable struct paths (FileObject, SystemMessage, etc.) but breaking paths that traverse into typealias-to-opaque types (BatchObject, ResponseObject, etc. where swift-bridge exposes fields as methods). E2E (swift) compile errors: `value of type '() -> RustString' has no member 'count'` and `method 'status' was used as a property; add () to call it`. Add a per-type `SwiftFirstClassMap` (analogous to `PhpGetterMap`) populated from the IR's TypeDef list: first-class types use property access, opaque/typealias types use method-call. Renderer tracks a `current_type` cursor through `field_types` so it picks the right syntax at each segment of a nested path like `result.data[0].id`. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (swift)` in BatchesTests after v0.16.59. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

## [0.16.60] - 2026-05-19

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-csharp: tagged-union enum-variant defaults now populate `tagged_union_enums` set from `api.enums`**. The v0.16.57 fix branched the default-value renderer on `complex_enums.contains(...)`, but `complex_enums` is intentionally empty (untagged enums emit as JsonElement-wrapper classes), so the new `new Base.Variant()` path never fired. Build a separate `tagged_union_enums` set from `api.enums` filtered to serde-tagged data enums and thread it into `gen_record_type`. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (csharp)` continuing to fail CS0119 on `CacheConfig.Backend = CacheBackend.Memory` after v0.16.57. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: PhpGetterMap.needs_getter falls back to bare-name union when the recorded `root_type` does not declare the field**. The workspace-level `root_type` resolution is best-effort and can pick a wrong type for some fixtures (every call gets the same workspace root, but per-fixture calls have different return types). When a recorded `current_type` does not declare `field_name`, the renderer was returning `false` (no getter), causing `$result->data` instead of `$result->getData()` for non-scalar fields on the correct (per-call) return type. Track `all_fields` per type in `PhpGetterMap` and consult the owner-type classification ONLY when that type actually declares the field; otherwise fall through to the bare-name union. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (php)` (76 TypeErrors on `assertCount(N, $result->data)` where the workspace root_type was `ChatCompletionResponse` but `listBatches` returns `BatchListResponse`). (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`, plus test data updated to populate `all_fields`)

## [0.16.59] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/swift: use property access for field segments (no parens), matching first-class Codable struct emission**. `render_swift` and `render_swift_with_optionals` in `src/e2e/field_access.rs` historically added `()` after every field segment because swift-bridge exposed all Rust fields as methods. Since the v0.16.43+ first-class-struct emission, most binding DTOs are `public struct Foo: Codable { public let id: String }` — properties, not methods — and `.id()` raises `cannot call value of non-function type 'String'`. Switch to plain `.id` field access for all segments, drop `()` from array `[N]` and map `[key]` subscripts. Also updated the `.len()` → `.count` assertion paths in `src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs` for non-empty / is-empty checks. The remaining typealias-to-opaque types (request DTOs with Vec/Map/Named fields) are method inputs / streaming outputs in practice, not parents for field-access chains, so property syntax works. A future per-type `SwiftFirstClassMap` (analogous to `PhpGetterMap`) would be needed if a fixture asserts on a field chain rooted in an opaque type. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (swift)` with `cannot call value of non-function type 'String'` errors in FilesTests. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`, plus matching test updates)

## [0.16.58] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-php: revert v0.16.55 camelCase Rust-ident attempt — restore `get_snake_case` and let ext-php-rs convert to camelCase**. v0.16.55 attempted to emit non-scalar field getters as `pub fn getCamelCase(...)` (camelCase Rust ident, no `#[php(getter)]` attribute). But ext-php-rs's `#[php_impl]` macro doesn't surface camelCase Rust idents as callable PHP methods reliably — runtime dispatch still fails with "Call to undefined method getCamelCase()". Revert to the snake_case `get_camel_case_field` Rust ident; ext-php-rs auto-converts that to camelCase `getCamelCaseField()` at the PHP boundary. The `#[php(getter)]` attribute stays dropped (per the v0.16.55 fix description) so the method is callable rather than registered solely as a (broken) property accessor. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

## [0.16.57] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-csharp: tagged-union enum defaults emit `new Base.Variant()` instead of `Base.Variant`**. C# emits serde-tagged data enums as `public abstract record Base { public sealed record Variant() : Base; }` — under that shape `Base.Variant` is a TYPE, not an instance, so `public T Field { get; init; } = Base.Variant;` fails to compile (CS0119 "X is a type, which is not valid in the given context"). Detect via the `complex_enums` set (enums whose variants carry data) and emit `new Base.Variant()` instead. Surfaced when exposing `CacheConfig.backend` (default `Memory`) to C# via the Tower config DTOs. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/php: key getter-field classification by `(owner_type, field_name)` instead of bare field name**. The previous `php_getter_fields: HashSet<String>` lumped every non-scalar field across every `TypeDef` into a single bare-name set, so when two types declared the same field name with different scalarness — e.g. kreuzcrawl `CrawlConfig.content: ContentConfig` (non-scalar) vs `MarkdownResult.content: String` (scalar) — every `->content` access on EVERY type emitted `->getContent()`, including on types where the field is a real PHP property. Generated assertions then fail with "Call to undefined method MarkdownResult::getContent()" on every fixture that asserts a markdown body. Replaced the flat set with a `PhpGetterMap { getters: HashMap<String, HashSet<String>>, field_types: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>>, root_type: Option<String> }`: the renderer tracks a "current type" cursor that starts at the resolved root type and advances through `field_types` at each path segment, looking up `(current_type, field)` in `getters` for the correct per-type classification. `PhpCodegen::generate` builds the map from `type_defs` using the existing `is_php_scalar` predicate (a re-implementation of `is_php_prop_scalar_with_enums` already lived in the codegen) and derives the root type from any backend's `result_type` override (`php`/`c`/`csharp`/`java`/`kotlin`/`go`) or by matching `result_fields` against `TypeDef.fields`. Falls back to the legacy bare-name union when no root type can be determined. Surfaced on kreuzcrawl `E2E (php)` (CI run 26056619876) with 5 errors in `MarkdownTest`. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`)

## [0.16.56] - 2026-05-18

### Added

- **alef-backend-magnus: Ruby `.rbs` stubs now propagate Rust `///` doc comments for fields and methods**. `gen_stubs.rs` was already emitting struct/enum-level doc comments (via `rbs_doc_block.jinja`) but field-level and method-level docs were silently dropped. Field docs now precede each `attr_accessor`/`attr_reader` line and method docs precede each `def ... : (...) -> T` signature. Multi-line docs render as multiple `#` lines; empty lines become bare `#` (matching RBS comment-block conventions). Brings Ruby `.rbs` coverage in line with Python `.pyi` (both now ship every public type/field/method docstring inherited from the upstream Rust source). (`src/backends/magnus/gen_stubs.rs`)

## [0.16.55] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-php: non-scalar field getters now emit as plain PHP methods (`getCamelCase()`), not `#[php(getter)]` property accessors**. ext-php-rs-derive 0.11.7 leaves `get_method_props` as `todo!()`, so `#[php(getter)]` registers ONLY as a property accessor (the runtime callable method is never registered) AND the property accessor itself is broken for non-scalar return types — every `$obj->nonScalarField` and `$obj->getNonScalarField()` raises a fatal "Call to undefined method" error. Drop `#[php(getter)]` from non-scalar field getters and emit a regular `pub fn getCamelCase(&self) -> T` so ext-php-rs surfaces them as callable PHP methods. The Rust ident is now `getPascalCase` (e.g. `getToolCalls`) so the PHP-side name matches the `$obj->getToolCalls()` shape `alef-e2e/src/codegen/php.rs` already emits for non-scalar fields. Property-style `$obj->camelCase` access for non-scalar fields is forgone as a result of the ext-php-rs limitation. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (php)` with 76 runtime errors on `getChoices()`, `getData()`, etc. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

## [0.16.54] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-csharp: tagged-enum file headers now `using System.Collections.Generic;`**. `gen_tagged_union` in `src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/enums.rs` emitted `using System; using System.IO; using System.Text.Json; using System.Text.Json.Serialization;` but not `System.Collections.Generic`. Variants with `Dictionary<K, V>` fields (e.g. `CacheBackend.OpenDal { Dictionary<string, string> Config }`) then fail to compile with CS0246 "type or namespace name 'Dictionary<,>' could not be found". Added the missing using statement. Surfaced when exposing `CacheBackend` to C# via the Tower config DTOs. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/enums.rs`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: unit-variant subclasses no longer emit orphan `sig { void }`**. The generator unconditionally emitted `# @return [void]` + `sig { void }` + `def initialize() super() end` for every variant subclass, but rubocop's `Lint/UselessMethodDefinition` rule strips the trivial `def initialize` body during the regen format pass. The orphan `sig { void }` that remains then trips Sorbet's runtime check ("You called sig twice without declaring a method in between") and the entire `require 'liter_llm'` fails for any consumer of tagged data enums that have unit variants (e.g. `ResponseFormatText`, `ResponseFormatJsonObject`, `ResponseFormatJsonSchema`). Skip the initialize emission entirely when `variant.fields.is_empty()` so unit variants inherit the parent's no-arg initialize, with no orphan sig left behind. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (ruby)` (`ResponseFormatText` triggering `RuntimeError: You called sig twice…`). (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

## [0.16.53] - 2026-05-18

### Added

- **alef-backend-pyo3: propagate Rust `///` doc comments into Python `.pyi` stubs as `"""..."""` docstrings**. `gen_stubs.rs` previously emitted only signatures (`class Foo:`, `model: str`) without any docstrings, leaving Python IDE hovers / `help()` output blank for every binding type and field. Added a small `pyi_docstring` helper that takes a Rust doc string, extracts the first paragraph, joins multi-line text with spaces, sanitises Unicode quote/dash characters via the existing `sanitize_python_doc` helper, escapes embedded `"""` and backslashes, and emits a single-line `"""…"""` indented for class-body inclusion. Hooked it into struct-class headers, struct field annotations, unit-enum class headers, and unit-enum variant declarations. Liter-llm regen now ships ~140 Python class/field docstrings inherited from the upstream Rust source. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_stubs.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/enums.rs` — `sanitize_python_doc` and `class_name_to_docstring` promoted to `pub(crate)`)

## [0.16.52] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-wasm: tagged-enum variant Map fields now bridge through `serde_wasm_bindgen` in both directions**. v0.16.51 added the core→binding `to_value` path for `tagged_enum_core_to_binding_expr` but the symmetric `tagged_enum_binding_to_core_expr` still emitted `val.field.clone().unwrap_or_default()` for non-optional Map fields, producing E0308 in the opposite direction ("expected `HashMap<String, String>`, found `JsValue`"). Added `TypeRef::Map` arms that deserialise the binding `Option<JsValue>` via `serde_wasm_bindgen::from_value` with a `Default` fallback. Surfaced when exposing `liter_llm::tower::CacheBackend::OpenDal { config: HashMap<String, String> }` as a Wasm DTO — the v0.16.51 fix unblocked the From<core> impl but the From<WasmCacheBackend> impl still failed. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/enums.rs`)

## [0.16.51] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-wasm: tagged-enum variant Map fields now use `serde_wasm_bindgen::to_value` instead of passing the raw HashMap**. `tagged_enum_core_to_binding_expr` in `src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/enums.rs` handled `Named` and `Optional<Named>` field shapes for enum variants emitted as flat-struct (the `ConversionConfig::map_uses_jsvalue` path used elsewhere), but `Map<K, V>` (`HashMap`) fell through to the catch-all and the generated `From<core::Enum> for WasmEnum` impl emitted `Some(hashmap)` against an `Option<JsValue>` binding field, producing E0308 ("expected `JsValue`, found `HashMap<...>`"). Added explicit `TypeRef::Map` arms (optional and non-optional, plus the wrapped-Optional case) that serialise the HashMap via `serde_wasm_bindgen::to_value`. Surfaced when exposing `liter_llm::tower::CacheBackend::OpenDal { config: HashMap<String, String> }` as a Wasm DTO. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/enums.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift, alef-docs: bridge data-enum function parameters without stale docs**. Swift free-function shims now declare `Named(enum)` and `Vec<Named(enum)>` parameters as JSON strings at the swift-bridge boundary and deserialize them back to the source enum before calling Rust, avoiding invalid `.0` wrapper unwrapping for data enums such as `PageAction`. Per-language API docs now respect language-specific and FFI-backed `exclude_functions` / `exclude_types` so generated references no longer advertise functions or types omitted from that language surface. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/{extern_block,mod,shims}.rs`, `crates/alef-docs/src/lib.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: async functions with `Vec<Named>` parameters now convert binding vectors before calling core Rust**. The async function path already detected `Vec<Named>` as needing a let-binding, but only emitted serde recovery bindings for direct `Named` params. Generated WASM code could therefore pass `Vec<WasmPageAction>` into a core function expecting `Vec<PageAction>`. The binding now emits an `{param}_core` vector by mapping each element with `Into::into`, matching the synchronous path. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

## [0.16.50] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-swift: methods returning first-class DTOs now wrap the bridge return in a `try T(bridge)` constructor.** The bridge returns `RustBridge.T` but the signature returns the public `T`, failing typecheck; the return is now wrapped via the `init(_ rb:)` extension (adding `throws` as needed). (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-codegen: gate untagged-enum tuple-form conversion match arms on backend opt-in (`ConversionConfig::binding_tuple_form_for_untagged_variants`)**. Only Magnus emits tuple-form variants, but the arms were emitted for all backends causing E0164/E0533; a new opt-in field (default false, set only by Magnus) gates it. (`src/codegen/conversions/mod.rs`, `src/codegen/conversions/enums.rs`, `src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: method signatures now emit on a single canonical line, removing the dependency on gofmt for test assertions**. `gen_method_wrapper` previously built function signatures across three separate template fragments (receiver, params appended inline, closing paren + return type on a new line), producing `func (r *T) Foo(\n) RetType {` for zero-parameter methods. Tests asserting `contains("func (r *T) Foo()")` then depended on gofmt collapsing the newline — failing on CI macos-latest runners where gofmt availability differed. Two new templates (`method_signature_instance.jinja`, `method_signature_static.jinja`) emit the full signature on one canonical line, matching the existing `function_signature.jinja` and `streaming_method_signature.jinja` patterns. gofmt is now a no-op for method signatures. Also fixes `output_dir` path concatenation: commit 0020ca74 replaced `PathBuf::join` with `format!("{output_dir}{file}")`, but `resolve_output_dir` returned paths without a trailing slash (e.g. `"packages/go"`), producing `"packages/gobinding.go"` instead of `"packages/go/binding.go"`. `PathBuf::ends_with("binding.go")` then returned `false` (last component was `"gobinding.go"`), causing all `go_emission_test.rs` tests to panic on `unwrap()`. `output_dir` is now normalised to always end with `/` before use. Removes the `#[cfg_attr(target_os = "macos", ignore)]` guard on `test_unmarshal_bytes_returns_slice_not_pointer`. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/methods.rs`, `src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/go/src/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/go/templates/method_signature_{instance,static}.jinja`, `src/backends/go/tests/`)

- **alef-e2e php: emit `->getCamelCase()` getter-method syntax for non-scalar fields instead of `->camelCase` property syntax**. ext-php-rs-derive 0.11.7 (the latest release compatible with ext-php-rs 0.15.4) leaves `get_method_props` as `todo!()` in its derive macro, so `#[php(getter)]` methods are never surfaced as magic PHP properties — accessing `$obj->nonScalarField` on a field whose Rust getter is `#[php(getter)] pub fn getNonScalarField()` raises a fatal error at runtime. Scalar fields continue to work because `#[php(prop)]` uses a different, working code path. Prior to this fix, every e2e assertion that accessed a Named-struct, `Vec<Named>`, or `Map` field via dot-notation (`$result->metadata->title`) would fail for any kreuzcrawl consumer that targets ext-php-rs 0.15.4. The fix adds a `php_getter_fields: HashSet<String>` to `FieldResolver` (populated via the new `new_with_php_getters` constructor) and a `render_php_with_getters` function that inspects each path segment: fields present in the set emit `->get{CamelCase}()`, all others emit `->camelCase`. The `PhpCodegen::generate` method now accepts the `type_defs` and `enums` IR slices (previously `_type_defs` / `_enums` unused) to compute the getter field set by running an `is_php_scalar` predicate (mirroring `is_php_prop_scalar_with_enums` from `alef-backend-php`) over every field of every exported type, and passes the set to `FieldResolver::new_with_php_getters`. A public `is_php_prop_scalar` wrapper is also exported from `alef-backend-php` as the canonical source of truth. Four unit tests cover: getter method for non-scalar field, property for scalar field, nested non-scalar + scalar chain, and array-field getter. (`src/e2e/field_access.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/php.rs`, `src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/php/src/lib.rs`)

- **alef-cli: `alef all` now always regenerates README files, fixing divergence with `alef readme`**. The `Commands::All` handler passed `overwrite=clean` to `write_scaffold_files_with_overwrite` for README files. When `alef all` is run without `--clean`, `clean=false`, so any README that already exists on disk (e.g. with table columns padded by `rumdl-fmt`) is silently skipped; `finalize_hashes` then re-stamps the hash on the padded content, making it appear valid. The next `alef readme` run (which always uses `overwrite=true`) regenerates compact content from the template and writes different bytes, producing an observable divergence between the two commands. README files are always regenerated from templates — unlike scaffold-once files (Cargo.toml, package.json) they must not preserve user edits — so the fix is to unconditionally pass `overwrite=true`. Regression-covered by `readme_overwrite_false_preserves_existing_content_producing_divergence`. Surfaced on html-to-markdown where `packages/python/README.md` differed between `alef generate` and `alef readme` runs. (`crates/alef-cli/src/main.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/generate.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: switch NIF trait-bridge templates to `.to_owned()` and bind the outer `spawn_blocking` `JoinResult`**. The four `*_method_body.rs.jinja` templates (`sync_method_body`, `trait_sync_method_body`, `trait_async_method_body`, plus the embedded strings in `src/template_env.rs`) emitted `.clone()` on `&[u8]` / `&str` parameters and left the outer `spawn_blocking(...).await` `Result<(), JoinError>` unused. With consumer `-D warnings` on Windows MSVC (kreuzberg's CI), the resulting `noop_method_call` and `unused_must_use` lints fired as errors. Switch parameter copies to `.to_owned()` — returns `Vec<u8>` / `String` for slice refs and falls back to `Clone` for owned types — and bind the `spawn_blocking` `JoinResult` with `let _` so `must_use` is satisfied. (`src/backends/rustler/src/template_env.rs`)

- **alef-backend-dart, alef-backend-go: emit forward-slash `GeneratedFile.path` values on Windows**. `PathBuf::from(dir).join(file)` uses the OS separator, producing mixed paths like `packages/dart/lib/src\traits.dart` when the directory string already contained forward slashes from `resolve_output_dir`. Tests asserting on `to_string_lossy().ends_with("/src/foo.dart")` then failed on Windows.
