Replace with `PathBuf::from(format!("{dir}/{file}"))` throughout `alef-backend-dart` (`gen_bindings`, `gen_ffi`, `gen_rust_crate`) and `alef-backend-go` (`gen_bindings`). `GeneratedFile.path` values are repo-relative virtual paths, not OS-native paths; forward slashes work for file I/O on all platforms including Windows. Fixes 6 `windows-latest` test failures across `alef-backend-dart` and `alef-backend-go`. (`src/backends/dart/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/src/gen_ffi/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/src/gen_rust_crate/{cargo,mod}.rs`, `src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

## [0.16.47] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-magnus: `Default` impl for `#[serde(untagged)]` enums with a tuple-form first variant now emits tuple-form constructor**. Companion to v0.16.46's conversion-arms fix. `first_variant_default` in `src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/classes.rs` unconditionally formatted the default body as struct-form (` {{ _0: Default::default() }}`), so the generated `impl Default for {Enum}` line read `Self::Single { _0: Default::default() }` for tuple variants — failing to compile with E0559 + E0769 the same way the From/Into match arms did before v0.16.46. Branch on `enum_def.serde_untagged && first.is_tuple` so untagged enums emit `Self::Single(Default::default())` instead. Surfaced on liter-llm regen against v0.16.46 producing 6 fresh Magnus compile errors (one per untagged enum: `EmbeddingInput`, `ModerationInput`, `RerankDocument`, `StopSequence`, `ToolChoice`, `UserContent`). (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/classes.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold node: include `aarch64-pc-windows-msvc` in generated `crates/{name}-node/package.json` `napi.targets`**. The scaffold listed five targets (linux x64/arm64 gnu, darwin x64/arm64, win32 x64-msvc) but omitted `aarch64-pc-windows-msvc`, even though the runtime dispatch `index.js` already routes to `{binary}.win32-arm64-msvc.node` / `{binary}-win32-arm64-msvc` optional dep. Consumer publish workflows (e.g. tslp) build the win32-arm64-msvc artifact and then call `napi create-npm-dirs` + `napi artifacts`, which use `napi.targets` as the source of truth and skip creating the platform package directory. `napi artifacts` then fails with `Internal Error: No dist dir found for .../{binary}.win32-arm64-msvc.node`, blocking the Publish Node.js to npm job. Add the missing triple so the platform package is created and the win32-arm64-msvc binary lands in it. Surfaced on tslp `Publish Release` dry-run (run `26020821545`). (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/node.rs`)

## [0.16.46] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-docs: `clean_doc_inline` double-escaped pipes in logical-OR expressions, causing MD056 table cell count violations**. `clean_doc_inline` applied `.replace('|', "\|")` internally, but every call site in `lib.rs` then immediately passes the result through `escape_table_cell`, which also replaces `|` with `\|`. For a doc string containing `||` (e.g. `rows == 0 || cols == 0`), this two-pass escaping produced `\|\|`: CommonMark parses `\` as an escaped backslash (literal `\`) and the following bare `|` as a cell separator, growing a 4-column table row to 6 cells and triggering rumdl MD056. Removed the `.replace` from `clean_doc_inline`, added a doc comment explaining the invariant, and updated the unit test in `doc_cleaning.rs` to assert that `clean_doc_inline` leaves pipes unescaped while the full pipeline (`escape_table_cell(clean_doc_inline(...))`) escapes them exactly once. Surfaced on html-to-markdown CI Lint (rumdl MD056 in `docs/reference/configuration.md`, `api-*.md`, `types.md`). (`crates/alef-docs/src/doc_cleaning.rs`)

- **alef-codegen: untagged-enum From/Into match arms now emit tuple-form for tuple variants**. Commit a715f378 (v0.16.41) made the Magnus enum body template emit tuple-form `Variant(T)` for `#[serde(untagged)]` enums (required so serde untagged can deserialise scalar variants like `RerankDocument::Text(String)`), but the conversion match arms in `src/codegen/conversions/helpers.rs` continued to destructure/construct as struct-form `Variant { _0 }`. Generated Magnus binding code then failed to compile with E0559 `variant 'X::Y' has no field named '_0'` and E0769 `tuple variant written as struct variant` (28 errors on liter-llm). Threads `enum_serde_untagged: bool` through `binding_to_core_match_arm_ext_cfg` and `core_to_binding_match_arm_ext_cfg`; the single call site in `enums.rs` passes `enum_def.serde_untagged`. When set, both helpers emit tuple-form patterns and constructors for tuple variants; tagged/unit enums retain the existing struct-form behaviour. Regression tests in `conversions/mod.rs` cover both directions plus a counter-regression that tagged enums still get struct-form. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (ruby)`. (`src/codegen/conversions/helpers.rs`, `src/codegen/conversions/enums.rs`, `src/codegen/conversions/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: `first_class_types` set now matches actual emission decision, fixing missing `intoRust()` and `Decodable` conformance errors**. The `first_class_types` collection used by Swift method/streaming code generation was filtered with `!is_opaque && has_serde && !fields.is_empty()`, but the actual struct-vs-typealias emission decision (`can_emit_first_class_struct`) additionally requires every field to be primitive/optional/string and a bulk constructor to be emittable. Types failing the stricter check (e.g. `ChatCompletionRequest`, `CreateBatchRequest`, `ChatCompletionChunk`, any DTO with Vec/Map/Named fields) were emitted as `typealias X = RustBridge.X` — but `first_class_types` still considered them first-class, so call sites emitted `try req.intoRust()` (no such method on the typealias) and streaming code emitted `JSONDecoder().decode(ChunkType.self, …)` (opaque RustBridge classes can't auto-conform to Decodable). Align both filters by reusing `can_emit_first_class_struct` to build the set. Additionally, optional `Named` parameters that ARE first-class now emit `try query?.intoRust()` instead of `try query.intoRust()` so the call site type-checks against the swift-bridge-emitted `Optional<RustBridge.T>` argument. Surfaced on liter-llm `E2E (swift)` with `value of type 'CreateBatchRequest' has no member 'intoRust'`, `cannot convert return expression`, and `type 'ChatCompletionChunk' does not conform to 'Decodable'`. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

## [0.16.45] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-rustler: insert blank line between concatenated elixir streaming wrappers**. The `elixir_streaming_start_wrapper.jinja` and `elixir_streaming_next_wrapper.jinja` templates rely on trailing blank lines to separate from the following `@doc`/`def` block, but the shared `end-of-file-fixer` pre-commit hook strips trailing blank lines from template source files. The runtime concatenation in `gen_bindings/mod.rs` therefore produced `end\n@doc "..."` (no blank line), which fails `mix format --check-formatted` ("a blank line is required before each `@doc` that precedes a function definition"). Insert the separator explicitly via `content.push('\n')` between successive `template_env::render` outputs so the boundary is independent of template file contents. Surfaced on liter-llm CI Lint (`packages/elixir/lib/liter_llm.ex`). (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

## [0.16.44] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e kotlin_android: drop redundant `repositories { mavenCentral(); google() }` block from emitted `build.gradle.kts`**. The v0.16.39 settings.gradle.kts emission sets `dependencyResolutionManagement { repositoriesMode.set(RepositoriesMode.FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS) }` and declares `google()` + `mavenCentral()` centrally. Leaving the per-project `repositories { ... }` block in `build.gradle.kts` triggers Gradle "Build was configured to prefer settings repositories over project repositories but repository 'MavenRepo' was added by build file 'build.gradle.kts'" at the `e2e/kotlin_android/` configuration phase. Surfaced on liter-llm's `E2E (kotlin_android)` CI job following the v0.16.43 regen. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin_android.rs`)

## [0.16.43] - 2026-05-18

### Changed

- **publish workflow: move all Linux jobs to self-hosted runners (`runner-medium`, `runner-medium-arm64`) and reorder so `publish-crates` runs before `build-cli`**. Two related infrastructure changes to `.github/workflows/publish.yaml`. (1) Every `ubuntu-latest` job (prepare, validate-versions, check-cratesio, check-github-release, build-homebrew-bottles, upload-release-assets, publish-crates, publish-homebrew, finalize) now targets the self-hosted `runner-medium` label; the `build-cli` matrix uses `runner-medium` for `linux-x86_64` and `runner-medium-arm64` for `linux-aarch64`. macOS (`macos-latest`, `macos-15-intel`) and Windows (`windows-latest`) matrix entries continue to use GitHub-hosted runners. Motivation: GitHub-hosted Linux runners were queuing for 4+ hours behind unrelated workloads, blocking v0.16.41/42/43 from publishing. (2) `build-cli` now `needs: [..., publish-crates]` with `(publish-crates.result == 'success' || == 'skipped')` gating, so the canonical crates.io artifact lands before any platform binary work begins — a crates.io failure (network, dupe version, manifest error) no longer wastes ~30 min of CLI matrix build time. `upload-release-assets` and `build-homebrew-bottles` already transitively depend on `build-cli` so they inherit the new ordering. (`.github/workflows/publish.yaml`)

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-rustler: `gen_rustler_flat_data_enum_from_core` / `_to_core` now use the enum's full `rust_path` instead of `{core_import}::{name}`**. The two helpers in `src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/types.rs` unconditionally hardcoded `format!("{core_import}::{name}")` for the core-side type path. For enums re-exported at the crate root (e.g. `kreuzberg::FormatMetadata`) the short form happens to compile, but for enums living in a nested module (e.g. `DrawingType` at `kreuzberg::extraction::docx::drawing::DrawingType`) the generated `impl From<kreuzberg::DrawingType>` references a path that does not exist, and the consumer NIF crate fails to build with `cannot find DrawingType in kreuzberg`. The fix routes both helpers through `alef_codegen::conversions::core_enum_path`, which preserves `enum_def.rust_path` when it is already fully qualified (matching the alef-backend-wasm path used by the other discovered data enums). Locked in with `test_flat_data_enum_from_core_uses_full_rust_path` asserting the generated `impl From<…>` carries the full module path and never collapses to `kreuzberg::<Name>`. Surfaced on kreuzberg's `E2E (elixir)` and `Build Elixir NIF` publish jobs failing to compile `packages/elixir/native/kreuzberg_nif/src/lib.rs`. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-cli: `finalize_hashes` no longer applies `normalize_whitespace` to non-Rust files**. Language-native formatters (`gofmt`, `oxfmt`/oxlint, `php-cs-fixer`, `ruff`, etc.) have already produced idempotent canonical output by the time `finalize_hashes` runs; the additional `normalize_whitespace` pass mutated that output (e.g. collapsing blank lines that `php-cs-fixer` intentionally inserts after `<?php`) so the on-disk bytes after finalisation differed from what prek's formatter hooks would produce, causing `alef verify` to report a stale hash on every `prek` run. The fix restricts `normalize_whitespace` to `.rs` files only (where it is needed to match a subsequent `cargo fmt`); all other languages hash the raw stripped on-disk content directly, matching what `compute_file_hash` in the verify path computes. Regression-covered by `test_finalize_hashes_non_rust_skips_normalize_whitespace`. Surfaced on html-to-markdown `alef verify` reporting `crates/html-to-markdown-node/index.d.ts` and `packages/go/visitor.go` as stale after every `prek` run. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/generate.rs`)

## [0.16.42] - 2026-05-18

### Changed

- **chore: adopt gh-actions-updater** — add `gh-actions-updater` pre-commit hook and `gh-actions-updater --update .` step in the `update` task to keep GitHub Actions pins current. (`.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `Taskfile.yml`)

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e kotlin_android: migrate the emitted `build.gradle.kts` to the Kotlin 2.x `compilerOptions` DSL**. The legacy `android { kotlinOptions { jvmTarget = "<n>" } }` block was removed as an error in Kotlin 2.x ("Using 'jvmTarget: String' is an error. Please migrate to the compilerOptions DSL"), failing the kotlin_android e2e project at script compilation. Drop the legacy block and emit `kotlin { compilerOptions { jvmTarget = JvmTarget.JVM_<n> } }` at the top level — the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.dsl.JvmTarget` import was already present in the scaffold. Surfaced on liter-llm's `E2E (kotlin_android)` CI job after Kotlin Gradle plugin upgrade. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin_android.rs`)

## [0.16.41] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-go: decode `Vec<DataEnum>` and scalar-tuple variants for `#[serde(untagged)]` enums**. Two related bugs in the Go codegen for untagged enums whose variants include both scalar-tuple shapes (e.g. `Text(String)`) and named struct shapes (e.g. `Object { text: String, … }`): (1) the struct-level custom `UnmarshalJSON` only handled `<DataEnum>` and `Option<DataEnum>` fields; `Vec<DataEnum>` fell through and Go's default unmarshal of a JSON array into `[]<sealed-interface>` failed because Go interfaces are opaque to `encoding/json`. Extended `DataEnumField` with `is_slice`, the raw-shadow struct emission to use `[]json.RawMessage` for slice fields, and the decode loop to iterate the array and call `Unmarshal<Enum>(item)` per element. (2) `gen_data_enum_type` unconditionally skipped tuple fields (`_0`, `_1`, …) on the per-variant struct, so a variant like `Text(String)` ended up as `type RerankDocumentText struct {}` with nowhere to land the inner String. Detect single-tuple-field variants of primitive/String/Char/Path type on untagged enums and emit a named `Value` field of the matching Go type, plus a custom `MarshalJSON`/`UnmarshalJSON` that bypasses the tag-wrapping aux struct and emits/reads the bare JSON scalar. Surfaced on liter-llm's `RerankRequest.documents: []RerankDocument` Go e2e suite. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: emit `#[serde(untagged)]` + tuple-form variants for tuple variants in untagged enums**. The Ruby native-extension enum template was missing the `#[serde(untagged)]` attribute for `#[serde(untagged)]` enums, and unconditionally emitted struct-form variants (`Text { _0: String }`) for tuple variants — regardless of the serde representation. The result was that an untagged enum like `enum RerankDocument { Text(String), Object { text: String, … } }` serialised on the wire as `{"_0":"hello"}` / `{"text":"hello"}` instead of the expected `"hello"` / `{"text":"hello"}`. The Ruby e2e suite, which passes Ruby Strings into `RerankRequest.new(documents: ['…'])`, then failed because serde's untagged-deserialiser saw a bare JSON string and rejected it (no struct-form variant matched). Thread `enum_def.serde_untagged` into the template context, add `is_tuple` to per-variant context, emit `#[serde(untagged)]` when set, and emit tuple-form (`Variant(T)`) instead of struct-form (`Variant { _0: T }`) for tuple variants _only when_ the enum is untagged. Externally and internally tagged enums keep struct-form so the existing `gen_enum_from_*_match_arm` helpers continue to work. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/classes.rs`, `src/backends/magnus/templates/enum_magnus.rs.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: suppress 3 more ktlint rules tripped by generated DTOs**. ktlint 1.x's standard ruleset flags `blank-line-before-declaration`, `no-semi`, and `statement-wrapping` on alef-emitted Kotlin data classes (sealed-class variants without blank lines between them, trailing semicolons inside enums, and chained statements without explicit wrapping). All three are inherent to generated code and not worth a full reformatter post-processing step — extend the existing `@file:Suppress` block to silence them. Surfaced on liter-llm's `packages/kotlin-android/.../LiterLlmError.kt` (24× `blank-line-before-declaration`), `AuthHeaderFormat.kt` (2× `no-semi`, `statement-wrapping`), and `EmbeddingFormat.kt`. Aligned with the existing precedent set by 0fc9719e and bd2f712d. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-jni: allow `let_and_return` clippy lint on `jboolean` shims**. The boolean-conversion shims emit `let result: jboolean = …; result` which clippy's `let_and_return` rejects. Suppress via `#[allow(clippy::let_and_return)]` rather than restructure since the explicit type annotation is load-bearing.

## [0.16.40] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e swift: emit e2e package under `e2e/swift_e2e/` instead of `e2e/swift/` to avoid SwiftPM 6.0 identity collision with `packages/swift/`**. SwiftPM 6.0 derives path-based dependency identity from the path's basename and silently ignores any `.package(name: "...", path: "...")` override (deprecated since 5.5). When the e2e consumer and the binding dep both live in directories named `swift/`, SwiftPM treats them as the same package and resolution fails with `product 'X' required by package 'swift' target '...' not found in package 'swift'`. Surfaced on kreuzberg's `cd e2e/swift && swift build` step. The fix moves the emitted consumer package to `<output>/swift_e2e/`, drops the deprecated `name:` parameter on `.package(path:)`, and uses the path basename (`swift`) for the `.product(package:)` reference — the product `name:` still matches the binding's library declaration (`Kreuzberg`, `LiterLlm`, etc). Consumers must update `[crates.test.swift] e2e` in their `alef.toml` to `cd e2e/swift_e2e && swift test`. Locked in with `package_swift_uses_path_basename_for_product_package_ref` regression test asserting emit path, no `.package(name:`, `.package(path:)`, and basename-based `.product(package:)`. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`, `src/e2e/tests/swift_client_factory.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: emit PSR-12-compliant blank lines after `declare(strict_types=1);` and `namespace X;`** in the facade class, stubs file, and every opaque class file. Previously the three code paths in `gen_bindings/mod.rs` concatenated these statements with no intervening blank line, causing php-cs-fixer to rewrite every generated PHP file and diverge from the alef-managed hash. Fixed by inserting `content.push('\n')` after each relevant render call. Surfaced on html-to-markdown CI Lint (`packages/php/src/HtmlToMarkdown.php`, `VisitorHandle.php`). (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: emit formatter-idempotent `__init__.py`, `api.py`, `exceptions.py`, and `options.py`** to fix ruff/isort violations in generated Python bindings. (1) `__init__.py`: added blank line after module docstring; reordered imports so `._native` (underscore) comes before `.api`/`.exceptions`/`.options` (isort underscore-before-letter); case-insensitive sort for native symbols (`VisitorHandle < VisitResult`). (2) `api.py`: blank line between absolute `import X as _rust` and relative `from .Y import` lines (isort). (3) `exceptions.py`: two leading blank lines moved into `exception_variant_class.jinja` (E302). (4) `options.py`: blank line after enum/dataclass docstrings (E303); blank line before relative imports; fixed `referenced_types` collection to skip `*Update` types and non-emitted return types, eliminating phantom `TYPE_CHECKING` imports (F401). (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/{errors,functions,types}.rs`, `src/backends/pyo3/templates/exception_variant_class.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: emit mix-format-idempotent Elixir for visitor-bearing functions**: blank line after `Map.pop` before `if is_map(visitor) do`; conditional multi-line formatting for the `{:ok, _} = Module.fn_with_visitor(...)` call when it exceeds 98 chars; blank line before `do_visitor_receive_loop`; 6-space indent for the `else` branch body. Surfaced on html-to-markdown CI Lint (`packages/elixir/lib/html_to_markdown.ex`). (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e python: emit pyproject-fmt-idempotent `e2e/python/pyproject.toml`**: `packages = []` (was `[  ]`); dependency list reordered to put the bound package name first (pyproject-fmt alphabetical ordering). (`src/e2e/codegen/python/config.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: drop unused `mut` on `args` in trait-bridge method bodies when the method takes no parameters**. The four `*_method_body.rs.jinja` templates (`sync_method_body`, `trait_sync_method_body`, `trait_async_method_body` — both as standalone files under `templates/` and as embedded strings in `src/template_env.rs`) unconditionally emitted `let mut args = serde_json::Map::new();`. For Plugin trait methods like `initialize()` and `shutdown()` (and any other no-arg trait method) the `args.insert(...)` loop expands to nothing, so the `mut` binding is never used and rustc/clippy flag every emission with `warning: variable does not need to be mutable`. Surfaced on kreuzberg's generated `packages/elixir/native/kreuzberg_nif/src/lib.rs:5159` (initialize) and `:5196` (shutdown) and propagates to every plugin trait bridge (OcrBackend/PostProcessor/Validator/EmbeddingBackend/DocumentExtractor/Renderer). The templates now gate `mut` on the presence of params (`let {% if params %}mut {% endif %}args = …`), so no-arg methods emit `let args = …` and parameterised methods continue to emit `let mut args = …`. Locked in with a regression assertion in `test_plugin_bridge_with_super_trait_generates_plugin_impl` (which uses no-arg `initialize`/`shutdown` via `super_trait = "Plugin"`). (`src/backends/rustler/templates/{sync,trait_sync,trait_async}_method_body.rs.jinja`, `src/backends/rustler/src/template_env.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/tests/trait_bridge_test.rs`)

## [0.16.39] - 2026-05-18

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-wasm: drop `js_name = "<unprefixed>"` override on emitted struct/opaque/enum types so the JS class name matches the Rust struct name (the prefixed form, e.g. `WasmChatCompletionRequest`)**. Previously, wasm-bindgen exported `Wasm<T>` as `T` (unprefixed) via `js_name`, but alef-e2e's TypeScript test generator imports the prefixed Rust identifier (`WasmChatCompletionRequest`). The mismatch made every wasm e2e import resolve to `undefined`, so the first method call (e.g. `WasmChatCompletionRequest.default()`) failed with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'default')`. Removing the `js_name` override aligns the JS API with the e2e import shape. Consumers that want a custom JS class name can still configure `[crates.wasm] type_prefix` (e.g. `""` for unprefixed). Surfaced on every liter-llm wasm e2e fixture. (`src/backends/wasm/templates/{gen_struct,gen_opaque_struct}.jinja`, `src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/enums.rs`)

- **alef-e2e elixir: pass `base_url` as a keyword option to `create_client/2` instead of a bare positional string**. The generated Elixir wrapper signature is `create_client(api_key, opts \\ [])` with `base_url = Keyword.get(opts, :base_url)`, but the e2e codegen was emitting `Module.create_client("test-key", url_string)` — the bare string got passed into `Keyword.get`, which crashes with `FunctionClauseError: no function clause matching in Keyword.get/3`. The codegen now emits `Module.create_client("test-key", base_url: url_value)` everywhere and handles the env-var-fallback branch (real-API vs mock) by building an `[base_url: …]` keyword list (or `[]` when the real API key is present so no base_url override applies). Surfaced as 161× `FunctionClauseError` across liter-llm's Elixir e2e fixtures. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **alef-e2e kotlin_android: emit `settings.gradle.kts` with `pluginManagement { repositories { google(); mavenCentral(); gradlePluginPortal() } }` alongside the existing `build.gradle.kts`**. Without `settings.gradle.kts` declaring the plugin repositories, Gradle cannot resolve `com.android.library` and the e2e project fails at configuration time with `Plugin [id: 'com.android.library', version: '8.7.3'] was not found in any of the following sources`. The new `render_settings_gradle_kotlin_android` helper mirrors the AAR-side `alef-backend-kotlin-android::gen_settings_gradle` template and names the project `<pkg_name>-e2e`. Locked in with a unit test asserting `pluginManagement`, `google()`, `gradlePluginPortal()`, and the correct `rootProject.name`. Surfaced on liter-llm's `E2E (kotlin_android)` CI job. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin_android.rs`)

- **alef-e2e wasm: honor `[crates.wasm] type_prefix` from `alef.toml` in TypeScript e2e generator instead of hardcoding `"Wasm"`** (#27): the wasm binding backend already respects `config.wasm_type_prefix()` when generating bindings (default `"Wasm"`, but can be customized via `[crates.wasm] type_prefix`), but the WASM e2e test generator hardcoded the prefix in all `strip_prefix("Wasm")` calls and the `wasm_class_name` helper. Consumers that customized the prefix (e.g. kreuzcrawl set it to `""`) were forced to ship workaround re-export wrappers like `index.js` to alias generated type names. The fix threads `config.wasm_type_prefix()` from `wasm.rs::generate()` into the TypeScript renderer and replaces all hardcoded `"Wasm"` literals with the configurable prefix. Function signatures updated: `render_test_file`, `render_test_case`, `ts_builder_expression`, `ts_builder_expression_inner`, `build_args_and_setup`, `is_tagged_data_enum`, `derive_nested_types_for_wasm`, `collect_transitive_nested_types_for_wasm`, and `wasm_class_name`. Default behavior unchanged; projects using the default `"Wasm"` prefix get identical output. Added regression tests `wasm_test_file_respects_empty_type_prefix`, `wasm_test_file_respects_custom_type_prefix`, and `wasm_test_file_defaults_to_wasm_prefix` to lock in the configurable behavior. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/typescript/mod.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/wasm.rs`)

- **alef-cli `sweep_orphans` + `cleanup_orphaned_files`: ownership detection requires the `// alef:hash:` line** (#26): both helpers previously accepted any file containing the loose substring `"auto-generated by alef"` or `"DO NOT EDIT"` as alef-owned and therefore deletable. That set matched countless vendored consumer files (cgo headers, swig output, autoconf artefacts, hand-written DO-NOT-EDIT shims), so a stray `alef regen` could silently delete them. Both helpers now defer to `alef_core::hash::extract_hash` — only files carrying the cryptographic `// alef:hash:<hex>` line that alef's own finalizer appends are eligible for sweeping. Surfaced by kreuzcrawl's vendored `packages/go/include/kreuzcrawl.h` and `packages/go/internal/ffi/kreuzcrawl.h` cgo headers being deleted on every regen. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/cleanup.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/generate.rs`)

## [0.16.38] - 2026-05-18

> **Note:** the `alef-e2e wasm type_prefix` (#27) and `sweep_orphans` (#26) fixes previously listed in this section were re-categorised to `[Unreleased]` because the publish workflow for v0.16.38 was cancelled before the source-code changes for those fixes were committed. Both fixes ship in v0.16.39.

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-go: trait-bridge interfaces and trampolines fall back to `json.RawMessage` for excluded named types** — when a Rust trait method (e.g. `DocumentExtractor::extract_bytes -> Result<InternalDocument>`, `Renderer::render(&InternalDocument)`) referenced a type that was excluded from the binding via `#[cfg_attr(alef, alef(skip))]`, the Go codegen emitted `InternalDocument` as the parameter/return type without ever defining that Go type, causing `undefined: InternalDocument` build errors. `gen_trait_bridges_file` now collects names from `ApiSurface::excluded_type_paths` and substitutes any `TypeRef::Named(n)` (or nested `Optional`/`Vec`/`Map` of one) with `TypeRef::Json`, which renders as `json.RawMessage` in the Go interface signature and trampoline declarations. Added explicit `TypeRef::Json` handling in `gen_param_conversion` so the trampoline copies the raw JSON payload through verbatim. Surfaced by kreuzberg's `DocumentExtractor` and `Renderer` trait bridges. (`src/backends/go/src/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: data-enum (sealed-interface) fields default to `nil` in `New{Config}` constructors instead of invalid `T{}` composite literal** — `gen_config_options` fell through to `format!("{}{{}}", go_type_name(name))` for any non-string-enum, non-passthrough Named field, producing `Sizing: ChunkSizing{}` / `Model: EmbeddingModelType{}`. Since those types are Go interfaces (one per variant struct, sealed via an `is{Trait}()` marker), composite literals are invalid (`invalid composite literal type ChunkSizing`). Added an explicit branch that checks `data_enum_names` and emits `nil` (the interface zero value) instead. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: `MarshalJSON` for optional `[]byte` fields no longer dereferences the slice** — for a Rust `Option<Bytes>` field the IR sets `field.optional = true` and `ty = Bytes`; the Go struct field is `[]byte` (slices are already nullable in Go, no pointer needed). The `struct_marshal_bytes_field_pointer.jinja` template wrongly wrote `*v.Field`, producing `invalid operation: cannot indirect v.Data (variable of type []byte)`. Dropped the dereference so the template works for both pointer and non-pointer bytes fields. Surfaced on kreuzberg's `EmailAttachment.data`. (`src/backends/go/templates/struct_marshal_bytes_field_pointer.jinja`)

- **alef-e2e elixir: honor `returns_void` on calls returning `Result<(), E>`**: `src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs` unconditionally emitted `{:ok, result} = Module.fn(...)` whenever `returns_result` was true. For unit-returning fallible functions (Rust `Result<(), E>` → Elixir bare `:ok` on success) the pattern match `{:ok, result} = :ok` raises `MatchError` at runtime. The codegen now emits `:ok = Module.fn(...)` when `call_config.returns_void` is set, mirroring the Ruby/Java patterns. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack's `download_configure_custom_dir`, `download_init_default`, `download_clean_cache` Elixir e2e fixtures.

- **alef-e2e java: array-field `contains`/`contains_all`/`not_contains` assertions now match case-insensitively against `String.valueOf(...)`**: `src/e2e/templates/java/assertion.jinja` emitted `{{ string_expr }}.toString().contains({{ java_val }})` when `field_is_array`. Java record `toString()` renders enum-typed fields in their SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE form (e.g. `kind=FUNCTION`), so a fixture asserting `"Function"` would never match. Switched to `.toString().toLowerCase().contains(String.valueOf({{ val }}).toLowerCase())` so the fixture wording can match the enum variant regardless of casing convention. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack's `process` fixtures asserting `structure` contains `"Function"`/`"Method"`/etc. (`src/e2e/templates/java/assertion.jinja`)

- **alef-e2e php: array-field `contains`/`contains_all` assertions handle object items without throwing on string cast**: `src/e2e/templates/php/assertion.jinja` emitted `(string)$item` inside the `foreach` loop, which raises `Error: Object of class <T> could not be converted to string` when array elements are objects without a `__toString` method (every alef-generated PHP data record). Switched to `is_object($item) ? json_encode($item) : (string)$item` and `stripos(...) !== false` for case-insensitive matching, mirroring the Java fix. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack's `process` fixtures asserting `structure` contains a `StructureItem` kind name. (`src/e2e/templates/php/assertion.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-csharp: check `LastErrorCode()` after numeric `Result`-returning FFI calls in `gen_wrapper_function`**: free-function wrappers like `TreeSitterLanguagePackLib.Download(...)` only consulted `nativeResult == IntPtr.Zero` to detect errors. For functions that return numeric primitives (e.g. `Result<usize, Error>` surfaced as `ulong`) the FFI sets `last_error` and returns the sentinel `0` — which the C# binding silently returned as a legitimate "0 items downloaded" value, swallowing the error. The Rust FFI clears `LastErrorCode` at every call entry, so a non-zero value immediately after the call unambiguously indicates failure. Both the sync and async branches of `gen_wrapper_function` now emit a `LastErrorCode() != 0 → throw GetLastError()` check for non-pointer return types when `func.error_type.is_some()`. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack's `Test_DownloadInvalidLanguage` which expected `TreeSitterLanguagePackException` from `Download(["zzz_definitely_not_a_real_language_xyz"])`. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/methods.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: add `# noqa: F401` to `TYPE_CHECKING` import items in generated `options.py`**: the `type_checking_import_item.jinja` template emitted bare `Name,` lines while the runtime `import_item.jinja` already had `# noqa: TC001`. When a TYPE_CHECKING-only import is referenced through `from __future__ import annotations` deferred evaluation but the local @dataclass / TypedDict field annotations don't textually reference the name (e.g. because the field's type was rewritten to `dict[str, Any]` or because the type is reachable transitively but only appears nested), ruff F401 flags the import as unused. Consumers can't hand-edit (alef-generated file), and `ruff --fix` would diverge from the alef hash. The TC import item now carries `# noqa: F401` to mirror the existing TC001 suppression on runtime imports — both fall in the same generated-binding pragma family. Surfaced on html-to-markdown CI Lint ruff F401 (packages/python/html_to_markdown/options.py: `DocumentStructure`, `PreprocessingOptionsUpdate`, `ProcessingWarning`, `TableData`). (`src/backends/pyo3/templates/type_checking_import_item.jinja`)

## [0.16.37] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-rustler: uniform multi-line case arms in opaque-module streaming wrapper and blank lines before `@doc` in template wrappers**: two separate mix-format violations in generated Elixir. (1) `gen_elixir_opaque_module` in `helpers.rs` rendered the `Stream.unfold` inner `case` block with mixed single-line and multi-line arms (`{:ok, nil} -> nil`, `{:ok, chunk} -> {chunk, h}`) alongside a multi-line arm — mix-format requires all arms to use the same multi-line form when any arm is multi-line, and requires blank lines between multi-line arms. Fixed by rewriting all four arms to multi-line form (`{:ok, nil} ->\n  nil`, etc.) with blank lines between each. Also fixed the outer `case` arms wrapping the `Stream.unfold` call (`{:ok, stream}` and `{:error, reason}`) to use multi-line form and blank lines. (2) `elixir_streaming_start_wrapper.jinja` and `elixir_streaming_next_wrapper.jinja` emitted `end` with no trailing blank line, so the following `@doc` attribute (from the next template) appeared immediately after `end` without a blank line — mix-format requires a blank line before each `@doc` that precedes a function definition. Added a blank line at the end of both templates. Surfaced on liter-llm CI Lint mix-format (packages/elixir/lib/liter_llm/default_client.ex + packages/elixir/lib/liter_llm.ex). (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/templates/elixir_streaming_start_wrapper.jinja`, `src/backends/rustler/templates/elixir_streaming_next_wrapper.jinja`)

## [0.16.36] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e zig: emit `setCwd` only for consumers with `file_path`/`bytes` fixtures**: commit `a3ba3b40` restored `setCwd(b.path("../../test_documents"))` on every `addRunArtifact` step to fix kreuzberg's PDF/image tests, but this unconditional call breaks mock-server-only consumers like kreuzcrawl that have no `test_documents/` directory. When Zig's `RunStep` executes, it calls `chdir(2)` into the target directory before `execve`-ing the test binary; if the directory does not exist the OS returns `ENOENT`, which Zig surfaces as `error: failed to spawn and capture stdio from .zig-cache/o/<hash>/<name>: FileNotFound` — even though the compile step completed successfully and the binary is present in the cache. The fix mirrors the pattern already used by the Ruby, Go, PHP, TypeScript, and Wasm backends: compute `has_file_fixtures` by checking whether any fixture's resolved call args carry type `file_path` or `bytes`, and only emit the `setCwd` line when that flag is true. Three regression tests in `zig_addtest_use_llvm_install.rs` cover both the file-fixture path (setCwd present) and the mock-server-only path (setCwd absent). (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`, `src/e2e/tests/zig_addtest_use_llvm_install.rs`)

## [0.16.35] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e java: honor `returns_void` on calls returning `()` / `Result<(), E>`**: `java/test_method.jinja` unconditionally emitted `var result = <call_expr>;` which the Java compiler rejected with `cannot infer type for local variable result (variable initializer is 'void')` for calls whose Rust signature is `() -> ()` / `() -> Result<(), E>` (e.g. tree-sitter-language-pack's `init`, `configure`, `clean_cache`). Threaded `returns_void` from `CallConfig` through `render_example` into the template; when true the template emits the bare statement form (`<call_expr>;`) instead of the `var` assignment. Mirrors the existing Ruby/C# patterns. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/java/test_method.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: only emit `alias <App>.Native` in reference-only struct modules when the body actually references `Native`**: the generated `defmodule <App>.<Type> do` block unconditionally pushed `alias <App>.Native` at the top, but it was only referenced in the body for types with `has_default = true` (the `new()` constructor calls `Native.<type>_default()`) or non-empty `methods` (each wrapper calls `Native.<nif>`). For reference-only types like tree-sitter-language-pack's `Language` (only `defstruct [:ref]` + `@type t`), the alias was dead and `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` rejected it with `warning: unused alias Native`. The alias is now gated on `typ.has_default || !typ.methods.is_empty()`. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3 `_coerce_enum`: accept PascalCase / camelCase variant names by also trying UPPER_SNAKE_CASE and snake_case derivations**: the generated `_coerce_enum` helper tried `s`, `s.upper()`, `s.lower()`, and `"".join(part.capitalize() for part in s.split("_"))` candidates against the pyclass enum. None of these maps `"AtxClosed"` (a multi-word PascalCase variant name commonly used in JSON fixtures) to `ATX_CLOSED` (the `#[pyo3(name = "…")]` UPPER_SNAKE_CASE form). Single-word variants like `"Atx"` worked accidentally because `s.upper()` already equals `"ATX"`. Multi-word PascalCase variants (`AtxClosed`, `DoubleEqual`, etc.) raised `ValueError: unknown <Enum> value: '<X>'` at runtime. The coercer now also derives a snake_case form (`_pascal_to_snake` helper inserts `_` between lower→upper transitions) and tries `snake`, `snake.upper()`, and `"".join(part.capitalize() for part in snake.split("_"))` candidates, covering the PascalCase ↔ UPPER_SNAKE_CASE round-trip pyo3 enums use. Surfaced on html-to-markdown e2e Python tests (`test_options_heading_style_atx_closed`, `test_options_highlight_double_equal`). (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: remove blank line between `class X:` and its docstring (pydocstyle D211)**: `gen_options_py` and `gen_typeddict` pushed an extra `\n` after the `dataclass_header.jinja` / `typeddict_header.jinja` / `str_enum_class_header.jinja` render and before the `class_docstring.jinja` / `enum_docstring.jinja` render, producing `class X:\n\n    """doc"""` in every emitted `@dataclass`, `TypedDict`, and `(str, Enum)` class. pydocstyle D211 ("No blank lines allowed before class docstring") rejected the blank line at every class definition. The spurious `out.push('\n')` calls at all three sites in `types.rs` are removed. Surfaced on liter-llm CI Lint pydocstyle D211 (packages/python/liter_llm/options.py).

- **alef-backend-rustler: emit multi-line case arms in streaming `Stream.unfold` body (mix-format)**: the `elixir_streaming_unfold_wrapper.jinja` template rendered all three inner `case` arms as single-line (`{:ok, nil} -> nil`, `{:ok, chunk_json} -> {Jason.decode!(…), h}`, `{:error, _} -> nil`). mix-format requires that arms whose bodies are tuples or non-trivial expressions be written in multi-line form with the body on its own indented line and a blank line separating consecutive multi-line arms. The template is updated to use the multi-line form for all three arms. Surfaced on liter-llm CI Lint mix-format (packages/elixir/lib/liter_llm/default_client.ex).

- **alef-e2e ruby: honor `returns_void` on calls returning `()` / `Result<(), E>`**: the Ruby codegen emitted `result = TreeSitterLanguagePack.clean_cache()` followed by an unconditional `expect(result).not_to be_nil` fallback whenever `has_usable` was false (the only assertion in the fixture was `not_error`). For unit-returning functions (Rust `()` → Ruby `nil`) that fallback always failed. Threaded `returns_void` from `CallConfig` through `render_example` into the `ruby/test_function.jinja` template, dropped the result-variable assignment, and gated the nil-check fallback on `!returns_void`. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack's `download_clean_cache`, `download_init_default`, `download_configure_custom_dir` fixtures whose calls (`init`, `configure`, `clean_cache`) all return `Result<(), Error>`. (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/ruby/test_function.jinja`)

- **alef-e2e ruby: stop coercing simple-result equality to `String` for numeric/bool expected values**: when `result_is_simple = true` the codegen wrapped the result in `.to_s.strip` so `equals "foo\n"` would absorb trailing-newline drift, but the same wrapper applied for `equals 0` / `equals true`, producing `expect(result.to_s.strip).to eq(0)` (Ruby `String "0" != Integer 0`). The strip wrapper is now gated on the expected value being a JSON string; numeric and bool comparisons use the raw expression and stay typed. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack's `download_empty_list` fixture (`download([])` returns `usize 0`). (`src/e2e/codegen/ruby.rs`)

## [0.16.34] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-scaffold ruby: add `sorbet-runtime` as a runtime gemspec dependency**: the Magnus backend's `native_rb_wrapper.rb.jinja` template unconditionally emits `require 'sorbet-runtime'` at the top of every generated `packages/ruby/lib/{name}/native.rb` wrapper and uses `T::Sig`, `T.nilable(...)`, and `T::Array[...]` throughout the public-API shim. The gemspec produced by `scaffold_ruby` only declared `rb_sys` as a runtime dependency, so consumers running `bundle install --without development` (the standard CI mode) never installed `sorbet-runtime` and received `LoadError: cannot load such file -- sorbet-runtime` the moment the native wrapper was required — causing the entire `spec/` suite to abort before running a single test. The fix adds `spec.add_dependency 'sorbet-runtime', '~> 0.5'` to the generated gemspec, sourced from the new `tv::gem::SORBET_RUNTIME` constant in `alef-core::template_versions`, consistent with how all other pinned gem versions are managed. A regression test `test_scaffold_ruby_gemspec_includes_sorbet_runtime_dependency` asserts that the rendered gemspec contains the dependency, uses `spec.add_dependency` (not `add_development_dependency`), and carries the `~> 0.5` constraint. Surfaced in kreuzcrawl CI E2E run 25997906829, job 76416254666. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/ruby.rs`, `src/core/template_versions.rs`)

## [0.16.33] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-cli format: `[workspace.format_overrides.<lang>]` custom commands now run even when the language's generated files were not re-written this run**: `format_generated` previously applied the `only_languages` filter uniformly, skipping any language absent from `changed_languages`. For default formatters this optimization is correct, but custom `format_override.command` entries must run whenever the language appears in the generated-file list — even when the content hash matches on-disk content and files were not written. The symptom: adding `[workspace.format_overrides.php]` on an existing repo ran `alef all --format`, got a config-hash cache miss (IR+config changed) but a content-hash hit (generated PHP source is identical), skipped php-cs-fixer because PHP was not in `changed_languages`, computed `alef:hash:` over pre-formatter output, and entered an endless regen loop when prek's own php-cs-fixer hook reformatted and broke `alef verify`. Same bug for any language with a custom override (`[workspace.format_overrides.python]`, etc.). Custom commands now bypass the `only_languages` filter; the filter still applies for built-in default formatters. Two regression tests added. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/format.rs`)

- **alef-cli generate/all: sweep language package directories after cleanup to remove orphaned alef-generated files in directories the current run no longer writes to**: `cleanup_orphaned_files` only walks directories that had files written in the current run. When a backend stops generating files in a directory — e.g. after `generate_public_api` was removed from `alef-backend-wasm`, leaving `packages/wasm/src/index.ts` behind — that directory is never visited and the stale file persists. The `alef generate` and `alef all` pipelines now run a `sweep_orphans` pass after `cleanup_orphaned_files` covering each language's current `package_dir` and `output_dir`, plus the legacy `packages/wasm/` and `packages/typescript/` paths that alef previously wrote public-API shims into. (`crates/alef-cli/src/main.rs`)

- **alef-readme: emit blank line between performance-context line and table header**: `render_performance_table` rendered the "**platform** · `function` · note" context line via the `performance_context.jinja` template (which ends with a single `\n`) and immediately concatenated the `| Document | Size | … |` header on the next line. The resulting README failed CommonMark/GFM MD058 ("Missing blank line before table") and `rumdl fmt` auto-fixed it on every CI run, so consumers' "regenerate then `git diff --exit-code`" gate failed indefinitely (surfaced on html-to-markdown's `Validate READMEs` step across all 9 polyglot READMEs). A second `\n` is now pushed after the context render so the output is `…note\n\n| Document | …`, matching what rumdl produces and what every other Markdown formatter expects between a paragraph and a following table. Covered by a new assertion in `test_render_performance_table_throughput`. (`crates/alef-readme/src/lib.rs`)

- **alef-core `package_dir` Node/Wasm defaults now point at `crates/{name}-{node,wasm}/` instead of dead `packages/{node,wasm}/`**: `ResolvedCrateConfig::package_dir(Language::Node)` returned `"packages/node"` and `package_dir(Language::Wasm)` fell through the `_ => format!("packages/{lang}")` arm to `"packages/wasm"`. Both directories were dead-weight scaffolds that 0.16.32 stopped emitting (the real NAPI-RS + wasm-bindgen npm publish sources live next to the binding crate at `crates/{name}-{node,wasm}/`). Setup/test/clean defaults therefore ran `cd packages/node && pnpm install` against a non-existent directory and timed out (600s) for every consumer on first CI run — surfaced on html-to-markdown's CI Lint at the `alef setup` step. Defaults now resolve to `crates/{self.name}-{node,wasm}` using the crate name, matching where the manifests actually live and where pnpm-workspace already includes them. (`src/core/config/resolved/lookups.rs`)

- **alef-readme: skip `Language::C` and `Language::Jni` by default — they were silently overwriting the Zig README**: the hardcoded fallback used `Language::C | Language::Jni | Language::Zig => packages/zig/README.md` for all three, so when a consumer's `languages = [..., zig, jni, ...]` list included both, the second one to run (Jni) clobbered the Zig template-rendered README with a 30-line hardcoded "Zig Bindings" stub. C and JNI are FFI glue layers, not publishable bindings; their public surface is the host language's README (kotlin-android, ffi). They now skip silently like Rust does, unless a consumer opts in via `[readme.languages.c]` / `[readme.languages.jni]`. (`crates/alef-readme/src/lib.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin jni client emitter: prepend `import` to the `TypeReference` import insert**: when the generated `DefaultClient.kt` instance-method wrapper needed Jackson's `TypeReference<T>` for a generic-container return type, the `imports` BTreeSet received a bare `"com.fasterxml.jackson.core.type.TypeReference"` entry instead of `"import com.fasterxml.jackson.core.type.TypeReference"`. The assembler that rendered the imports concatenated each entry verbatim, producing a top-level expression `com.fasterxml.jackson.core.type.TypeReference` in `DefaultClient.kt` that ktlint rejected with "Expecting a top level declaration" and broke kotlin-android post-generation formatting. Aligned with the eleven sibling `imports.insert(...)` sites in the same file that already use the `import` prefix. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/jni_emitter.rs`)

## [0.16.32] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e java: honor per-call `streaming = false` opt-out in import + assertion gating**: the Java codegen's `ChatCompletionChunk`-import gate and the streaming-virtual-field assertion accessor both used `is_streaming_virtual_field(fld)` directly, ignoring the call config's `streaming` flag. Consumers like tree-sitter-language-pack expose a non-streaming `process` function whose result struct has a real `chunks` field; the unconditional gate spuriously triggered `import …ChatCompletionChunk` and rewrote `count_min` on `chunks` to `chunks.size() >= N` (referencing a non-existent local aggregator) instead of `result.chunks().size() >= N`. Both sites now route through `resolve_is_streaming(fixture, call_config.streaming)`, threading the resolved `is_streaming` into `render_assertion` so the virtual-aggregator path only activates for genuinely streaming fixtures. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold node: drop the false-positive `glibcVersion === undefined` branch from the platform-dispatch loader**: the loader's `isMusl()` heuristic interpreted "report header has no `glibcVersion` field" as "this is a musl system." On Node 22 GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runners the field is sometimes absent on glibc systems, producing a false positive that drove the loader to attempt `./{binaryName}.linux-x64-musl.node` instead of `…-linux-x64-gnu.node` and fail to load any binding. The `=== undefined` branch is removed; the loader still trusts a string `glibcVersion` as "glibc" but otherwise falls through to the unambiguous filesystem check (`/lib64/ld-musl-x86_64.so.1`). (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/node.rs`)

- **alef-readme node: emit the Node README at `crates/{name}-node/README.md` (publish target) instead of `packages/typescript/README.md`**: both `lang_dir_name(Language::Node)` and the hardcoded fallback in `generate_readme_hardcoded` returned `"typescript"` for the directory portion of the Node README path, while the publishable npm package lives at `crates/{name}-node/`. Result: npm publishes shipped without a README, and the file landed at a directory no consumer used. `lang_dir_name` now returns `"node"`, the hardcoded fallback emits `crates/{name}-node/README.md`, and the test `test_generate_node_readme` is updated. The YAML/TOML config key (`lang_code`) remains `"typescript"` so existing per-language `[readme.languages.typescript]` blocks keep resolving. Consumer `alef.toml` files that set an explicit `output_path` are unaffected. (`crates/alef-readme/src/lib.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold wasm: strip `-node` suffix before appending `-wasm` to derive the npm package name**: `scaffold_wasm` previously appended `-wasm` directly to `node_package_name()`, so consumers following the `-node` naming convention (e.g. `@kreuzberg/html-to-markdown-node`) got `@kreuzberg/html-to-markdown-node-wasm` instead of the correct `@kreuzberg/html-to-markdown-wasm`. The fix strips a trailing `-node` before appending; names without that suffix are unchanged. Three new unit tests cover the scoped case, the unscoped case, and the no-suffix fallback. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/wasm.rs`)

### Changed

- **alef-readme: skip Rust by default — no more `packages/rust/README.md` stub**: `generate_readme(Language::Rust, ...)` previously emitted a 25-line stub at `packages/rust/README.md` redirecting users to crates.io. That path collides with consumer repos whose canonical Rust crate README is the source-of-truth `crates/<name>/README.md`, and the stub had to be deleted on every regen. `generate_readmes` now silently skips `Language::Rust` unless the consumer has explicitly opted in via `[readme.languages.rust]` with an `output_path` (or `output`) — opt-in matches the existing per-language configuration pattern used by every other language. The internal `generate_readme` signature changed from `Result<GeneratedFile>` to `Result<Option<GeneratedFile>>` to express the skip cleanly; public `generate_readmes` keeps its existing `Result<Vec<GeneratedFile>>` shape. (`crates/alef-readme/src/lib.rs`)

### Removed

- **alef-scaffold node: stop scaffolding the dead `packages/node/` directory**: `scaffold_node` emitted a parallel npm package at `packages/node/{package.json,index.js,index.d.ts,src/index.d.ts,tsconfig.json,.oxfmtrc.json,.oxlintrc.json}` whose `package.json` declared an unscoped `"name": "<crate>"` that no downstream consumer published — the real npm publish target lives at `crates/<crate>-node/` and is built by NAPI-RS. The seven dead emissions are removed; `scaffold_node` now only emits the crate-level `package.json` + platform-dispatch `index.js` at `crates/<crate>-node/`. Now-orphaned helper bindings (`pkg_dir`, `name`, `keywords_json`, `homepage_json`, `authors_json`, `content`, `dts_content`, `js_content`) and the four assertion blocks pinned to the old `packages/node/` paths in `tests.rs` are dropped (the `test_scaffold_node`, `test_scaffold_multiple`, and `test_scaffold_node_production_features` tests are updated; the three `test_node_scaffold_oxfmt_config_content`/`oxlint_config_content`/`uses_oxc` tests are removed since they asserted on now-gone files). (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/node.rs`, `crates/alef-scaffold/src/tests.rs`)

- **alef-backend-napi: drop `Backend::generate_public_api` (`packages/typescript/src/index.ts` shim)**: the NAPI backend's `generate_public_api` impl emitted a re-export TS module at `packages/typescript/src/index.ts` that no consumer imported from — the real TS types are produced by `napi build` directly into `crates/{name}-node/index.d.ts` next to the published npm package. The shim was dead weight that had to be hand-deleted on every regen. The `generate_public_api` impl is removed (falling back to the default empty impl from `Backend`), the now-orphaned `to_node_name` import is dropped, and the three `test_napi_js_name_*` tests have their `index.ts` assertions removed while retaining the `lib.rs`-side `js_name` assertions. (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: drop `Backend::generate_public_api` (`packages/wasm/src/index.ts` shim)**: the wasm backend's `generate_public_api` impl emitted a re-export TS module at `packages/wasm/src/index.ts` that no consumer imported from — the real `@scope/<pkg>-wasm` npm publish is built directly from `crates/<crate>-wasm/` by `wasm-pack`/`wasm-bindgen` into `dist/`, `dist-node/`, `dist-web/`. The shim was dead weight that had to be hand-deleted on every regen. The `generate_public_api` impl is removed (falling back to the default empty impl from `Backend`), the now-orphaned `to_node_name` import is dropped, the dead `WasmConfig.exclude_reexports` config field is removed from `alef-core` (no consumer set it), and the `test_wasm_index_ts_exports_unprefixed_names` test is dropped. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/core/config/languages.rs`)

## [0.16.31] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-napi trait_bridge: build the visitor-only `ConversionOptions` via struct-literal + `..Default::default()` instead of `let mut opts = Default::default(); opts.field = X;`**: the `or_else(|| { ... })` branch in the napi visitor wrapper code constructed a new `ConversionOptions` by `Default::default()` then field-assigned the visitor handle. Clippy's `field_reassign_with_default` lint (denied by default in consumer projects that build with `-D warnings`) rejects this idiom and demands the struct-literal form. The change keeps the same semantics (every other field still falls through to `Default`) but produces clippy-clean Rust on first emission, so consumers don't need to spawn a follow-up "fix clippy" commit after every alef regen. Surfaced on html-to-markdown's CI Rust (crates/html-to-markdown-node/src/lib.rs:1589). (`src/backends/napi/src/trait_bridge.rs`)

## [0.16.30] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e rust cors: emit only the `axum::http` re-exports actually referenced by the rendered `CorsLayer`**: `render_cors_layer` previously emitted `use axum::http::{HeaderName, HeaderValue, Method};` unconditionally, but `HeaderName` is only emitted when `allow_headers` contains a non-prelude header (i.e. anything outside `content-type`/`authorization`/`accept`), `HeaderValue` only when `allow_origins` is non-empty, and `Method` only when `allow_methods` is non-empty. Fixtures that allow no custom headers (the common case) therefore got an unused `HeaderName` import, tripping `-D unused_imports` in the consumer's cargo build. The import group is now computed from actual use and reduced to one of: nothing, a single `use axum::http::X;`, or the appropriate subset. Covered by five new unit tests in `render_cors_layer::tests`. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/http.rs`)

## [0.16.29] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-pyo3 errors: drop duplicate `\n` after `indented_import_item.jinja` render**: the template already ends in `\n`, but each of the 5 callsites in `errors.rs` appended an extra `out.push('\n')` after `render`, producing `NAME,\n\n` per line — i.e. a blank line between every imported symbol in every multi-line `from .X import (...)` block in the generated `packages/<pkg>/__init__.py`. Ruff format strips these blank lines, breaking the embedded `alef:hash:` line and forcing an endless regen-format cycle on every consumer. Each of the 5 callsites now relies on the template's trailing newline alone. (`src/backends/pyo3/gen_bindings/errors.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler `@type t ::` multi-line emission: indent the first arm to match the `|`-prefixed continuation arms**: the `elixir_enum_type_arm_first.jinja` template emitted `{{ arm }}\n` (no indent), placing the first variant at column 0 while every subsequent `| :variant` line sat at column 10. mix-format `--check-formatted` then rejects the file (it wants the bare arm at column 10 too, aligned with the `|`), and the consumer's `mix-format` pre-commit hook rewrites the file — invalidating the embedded `alef:hash:` line. Template now emits `{{ arm }}\n` (10-space indent), matching `elixir_enum_type_arm_rest.jinja` and producing mix-format-clean output. (`src/backends/rustler/src/template_env.rs`)

- **alef-cli pipeline/generate: `format_rust_content` detects crate edition from nearest `Cargo.toml`**: rustfmt was invoked with a hardcoded `--edition 2024`. When the target crate declares a different edition (e.g. `edition = "2021"`) in its `Cargo.toml`, `cargo fmt` would produce different import sort order and formatting than alef's hardcoded edition, causing drift after `alef generate`. `format_rust_content` now accepts the file path, walks up to find the nearest `Cargo.toml`, parses its `[package] edition` field, and passes the detected edition to rustfmt; falls back to `"2024"` when no `Cargo.toml` is found or the edition field is absent. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/generate.rs`)

## [0.16.28] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e rust: drop unused `use {dep}::{App, RequestContext}` import from http-fixture test files**: `render_test_file` unconditionally emitted `use {module}::{App, RequestContext};` when any fixture in the file declared an `http` block, but `render_http_test_function` references those symbols via their fully-qualified module path (`{dep_name}::App::new()`, `move |_ctx: {dep_name}::RequestContext|`). The use-statement was therefore always unused, tripping `-D unused_imports` in the consumer crate's clippy sweep. The unconditional emission is dropped; the body keeps the fully-qualified paths so no resolution behaviour changes. (`src/e2e/codegen/rust/test_file.rs`)

- **alef-e2e (all backends): `sanitize_ident` now strips underscores left behind after a numeric prefix to avoid `test__foo` names**: fixture ids commonly carry a numeric prefix to control category-file ordering (`24_cookie_samesite_strict.json`). `sanitize_ident` stripped the leading digits but left the underscore separator behind, so generators that prefix the result with `test_` (rust, python, ruby, typescript, elixir, C, …) emitted function names like `test__cookie_samesite_strict`. The function now also strips any leading underscores _that were exposed by stripping digits_, while preserving genuine leading underscores in ids that never had a digit prefix (`_foo` → `_foo`). Covered by four new unit tests in `escape::tests`. (`src/e2e/escape.rs`)

## [0.16.27] - 2026-05-17

### Added

- **alef-scaffold python: `[crates.python] pip_dependencies = [...]`**: declare runtime PyPI dependencies for the alef-scaffolded `pyproject.toml`. Entries are emitted verbatim into `[project] dependencies = [ ... ]` (PEP 508). Needed for downstream crates whose alef-generated `api.py` imports a third-party PyPI package (e.g. `tree-sitter`), so a fresh `pip install <pkg>` resolves the transitive at install time and the sdist smoke-test `import` succeeds. (`src/core/config/languages.rs`, `crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/python.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-cli pipeline/generate: apply `rustfmt` in `finalize_hashes` for `.rs` files so the embedded hash always reflects cargo-fmt-clean content**: `finalize_hashes` previously computed the `alef:hash:` value from the raw on-disk bytes, which could be unformatted when the file was not re-written in the current run (cache hit or generated by an older alef version). A subsequent `cargo fmt` in CI would then reformat the file (reordering imports such as `{c_char, CStr, CString}` → `{CStr, CString, c_char}` and collapsing expanded `if`/`else` blocks), changing the on-disk bytes without updating the hash, causing `alef verify` to report stale. `finalize_hashes` now calls `normalize_content` (which pipes `.rs` files through stdin `rustfmt` with the consumer's `rustfmt.toml`) before computing and injecting the hash, making the recorded hash identical to what `cargo fmt` would produce. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/generate.rs`)

- **alef-cli pipeline/format: drop the `pnpm dlx oxfmt` step from the FFI and WASM formatter sequences**: the FFI step ran `pnpm dlx oxfmt .` over the entire workspace and the WASM step ran `pnpm dlx oxfmt <manifest>` on the generated wasm crate's Cargo.toml. The shared Kreuzberg pre-commit `oxfmt` hook (`kreuzberg-dev/pre-commit-hooks`, `id: oxfmt`) is scoped to `[javascript, jsx, ts, tsx, json, css]` — i.e. it never touches TOML in CI. But oxfmt's default behaviour DOES format TOML, with 2-space indent and collapsed multi-line arrays. That collides with `cargo-sort`'s preserved-indent canonicalisation and with hand-maintained Cargo.toml style (e.g. html-to-markdown's CLI crate Cargo.toml uses 4-space `features = [...]` blocks): oxfmt rewrites them to 2-space, cargo-sort then preserves the 2-space, the embedded `alef:hash:` line ends up describing the pre-oxfmt content, and every subsequent `alef verify` reports the file as stale. `cargo fmt` and `cargo sort` together are sufficient to canonicalise Rust + Cargo.toml; JS/TS/JSON files have their own per-language scaffold + the consumer's own oxfmt hook. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/format.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin (and kotlin-android via the same emitter): use Jackson `TypeReference` for generic-container returns**: the JNI emitter's deserialize branch always emitted `MAPPER.readValue(responseJson, {kotlin_ty}::class.java)`, which is rejected by `kotlinc` whenever `kotlin_ty` carries angle-bracketed generics — e.g. functions returning `Vec<String>` emitted `List<String>::class.java` and failed with `Only classes are allowed on the left-hand side of a class literal`. The emitter now switches to `MAPPER.readValue(responseJson, object : TypeReference<{kotlin_ty}>() {{}})` whenever the rendered Kotlin return type contains `<`, mirroring the kotlin-android facade's existing generic-container handling, and threads the `com.fasterxml.jackson.core.type.TypeReference` import in. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/jni_emitter.rs`)

## [0.16.26] - 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **alef-scaffold: include conditional deps in `cargo-machete` ignored lists for FFI/Node/PHP/Python umbrellas**: when the umbrella crate declares `trait_bridges` or streaming `adapters`, the scaffold conditionally adds `async-trait` / `futures-util` / `tokio` to `[dependencies]` for manifest stability across regens — but the static `[package.metadata.cargo-machete]` block only listed the _unconditional_ deps (`serde_json` and, for FFI/PHP/Python, `tokio`/`pyo3-async-runtimes`). For umbrella crates whose actual API surface doesn't exercise async-trait or streaming codepaths in the generated binding (e.g. html-to-markdown's HtmlVisitor is sync, no streaming adapters), cargo-machete flagged the conditional deps as unused and broke `prek run --all-files`. The ignored list is now built dynamically from `has_trait_bridges` / `has_streaming`, mirroring the dep emission logic exactly so machete stays quiet whenever a dep is emitted unconditionally for codegen stability. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/{ffi.rs,node.rs,php.rs,python.rs}`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: also emit `getrandom 0.3` with `wasm_js` feature alongside `0.4`**: the WASM Cargo.toml emitter only declared `getrandom = { version = "0.4", features = ["wasm_js"] }`, but kreuzberg-wasm has transitive deps that pull in `getrandom 0.3.4` (e.g. through `rand 0.8`/`uuid 1.x` chains). On `wasm32-unknown-unknown`, getrandom 0.3 requires the `wasm_js` feature to provide its backend; without it the build fails with `compile_error!("The wasm_js backend requires the wasm_js feature for getrandom")`. Cargo's additive feature unification means declaring a direct `getrandom 0.3` dep with `features = ["wasm_js"]` enables the feature on the unified 0.3 instance, fixing the WASM build without affecting non-wasm targets. The 0.3 dep is added under a renamed alias `getrandom_03` so both major versions can coexist in the same `[target.cfg]` table. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)
- **alef-e2e zig: restore `setCwd` on test run steps so generated fixture reads resolve**: commit `993ca9ec` removed `setCwd("../../test_documents")` under the assumption that all Zig e2e suites are mock-server-only (kreuzcrawl), but kreuzberg's document-extraction e2e reads fixtures like `pdf/fake_memo.pdf` and `images/test_hello_world.png` via `std.Io.Dir.cwd().readFileAlloc(...)`. Without the chdir every fixture read failed with `error.FileNotFound`, producing 26 test failures across smoke/contract/format_specific/detection/error/async suites. Other languages perform this chdir in a per-suite hook (Go `TestMain`, Python conftest, Kotlin Gradle `workingDir`); Zig has no equivalent so it must happen at the build-step level. `setCwd` is safe here because `.use_llvm = true` already pins the LLVM backend, which resolves the test binary's spawn path to an absolute cache path via `getEmittedBin()` before `convertPathArg` runs — the child cwd only affects fixture lookups, not the binary path. Re-threaded `test_documents_relative_from(0)` into `render_build_zig` and updated the `every_test_artifact_runs_via_addrunartifact_directly` test to assert the chdir is present. (`src/e2e/codegen/zig.rs`, `src/e2e/tests/zig_addtest_use_llvm_install.rs`)
- **alef-backend-zig: null-check raw C pointer before slicing in fallible owned-string returns**: `unwrap_return_expr` for `String`/`Path`/`Json`/`Vec`/`Map` returns built `raw[0.._result_len]` after a `kreuzberg_last_error_code() != 0` check, on the assumption that any error path on the primary `_<fn>` FFI call would still be visible in the thread-local error code when the wrapper queried it. That holds when the FFI implementation calls `set_last_error` on every error branch, but the matching `_<fn>_len()` companion always invokes `clear_last_error()` on entry and some implementations silently return 0 on the error path without re-setting the error code, wiping out the original error. The wrapper then passed the error-code gate and panicked with `attempt to use null value` when constructing the slice over a null pointer (observed in `get_extensions_for_mime("application/x-totally-unknown")`, where `kreuzberg_get_extensions_for_mime_len`'s `Err(_) => 0` arm forgets to call `set_last_error`). Defensive null check now runs first in fallible owned-string returns; threaded the resolved Zig `error_type` into `unwrap_return_expr` so the early return can produce the function's declared error variant. (`src/backends/zig/src/gen_bindings/functions.rs`)

- **alef-e2e java: handle `result_is_option` for bare-result `is_empty`/`not_empty` assertions**: the Java facade exposes `Option<T>`-returning Rust functions as `@Nullable T` (via `.orElse(null)`), so calling `.isEmpty()` on the nullable reference does not compile for record types like `EmbeddingPreset` (`error: method isEmpty() not found on EmbeddingPreset`). The e2e codegen now propagates `result_is_option` through `render_assertion` and emits `assertNull/assertNotNull(result, ...)` for bare-result `is_empty`/`not_empty` assertions on option-returning calls, mirroring the existing `kotlin.rs` and `zig.rs` behaviour. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`)
- **alef-e2e java: coerce nullable numeric fields via `Number::longValue` to fix `long`/`Integer` mismatch**: `Optional.ofNullable(Integer field).orElse(0L)` does not compile because `0L` is a `Long` while the optional value type is `Integer` (e.g. Rust `Option<u32>` maps to Java `@Nullable Integer`). The Java e2e codegen now emits `.map(Number::longValue).orElse(0L)` for numeric comparisons and equality on optional numeric fields, which compiles cleanly for both `Optional<Integer>` and `Optional<Long>`. (`src/e2e/codegen/java.rs`, `src/e2e/templates/java/assertion.jinja`)
- **alef-e2e dart: emit required string args as positional rather than named**: a prior fix indiscriminately switched all string args to named-argument syntax to match the FRB bridge, but the alef-generated Dart facade (e.g. `KreuzbergBridge`) emits required params as positional and only wraps optional params in a `{...}` named block (see `alef-backend-dart/src/gen_bindings/functions.rs`). Calling `KreuzbergBridge.extractBytesSync(content, mimeType: 'application/pdf', config)` produced `Error: No named parameter with the name 'mimeType'` because the facade has positional `mimeType`. The Dart e2e codegen now checks `arg_def.optional` and emits positional literals for required string args, preserving named syntax only for genuinely optional string params. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-e2e swift: emit e2e package at `e2e/swift/` with explicit `name:` on `.package(path:)`**: the Swift e2e codegen was emitting under `e2e/swift_e2e/` to avoid a SwiftPM identity collision between the consumer (`e2e/swift/`) and the dep (`packages/swift/`) — both reduce to the path-last identifier `swift`. The stale `e2e/swift/Package.swift` left over from the original layout still declared a `.testTarget` with no source directory, so `cd e2e/swift && swift test` (the canonical CI command, matching every other language) failed with `error: The package does not contain a buildable target.` The emitter now writes back to `e2e/swift/` for consistency with every other language and disambiguates the dep via `.package(name: "{module}", path: "{pkg_path}")`, which SwiftPM honors as the dep's identity for `.product(name:package:)` resolution. (`src/e2e/codegen/swift.rs`)

## [0.16.25] - 2026-05-17

### Changed

- **ci: restrict `Android Emulator (instrumented tests)` workflow to manual dispatch**: the GitHub-hosted arm64-v8a API 34 emulator on `ubuntu-latest` regularly fails to reach `boot_completed` within the 1800s timeout, blocking unrelated merges. Trigger temporarily reduced to `workflow_dispatch` only until a stable runner/image combination is identified. (`.github/workflows/ci-android-emulator.yaml`)

### Added

- **alef sync-versions: manage `CITATION.cff` at the repo root**: a new optional `[workspace.citation]` section in `alef.toml` declares the project's citation metadata (`title`, `abstract`, `authors` — persons via `family-names`/`given-names` and/or entities via `name`, plus `email`/`orcid`, `repository-code`, `url`, `license`, `date-released`, `doi`). When present, `alef sync-versions` renders a full Citation File Format YAML at `CITATION.cff` using these fields plus the canonical workspace version from `Cargo.toml`; the file is rewritten only when its contents actually change, so the working tree stays clean across reruns. When the section is absent but a hand-authored `CITATION.cff` exists at the repo root, alef falls back to updating only the top-level `version:` scalar (preserving the original quote style — unquoted, single-, or double-quoted). New config types `CitationConfig` and `CitationAuthor` in `alef-core`. (`src/core/config/{output.rs,workspace.rs,resolved/mod.rs,new_config.rs,mod.rs}`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/version.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-csharp: emit excluded types as JsonElement instead of named type**: when a field's type was marked with `#[cfg_attr(alef, alef(skip))]` or `#[doc(hidden)]` (e.g., `CoreProperties` in kreuzberg's `DocxMetadata.core_properties`), the C# codegen treated it as a Pascal-cased type name instead of recognizing it as an excluded type and mapping it to `JsonElement`. This caused deserialization failures with `JsonException: Cannot get StartObject as String` when Rust sent an object but C# expected a string. Fixed by passing the excluded types set from `ApiSurface.excluded_type_paths` to `gen_record_type` and checking it alongside complex enums to determine whether to emit `JsonElement`. (`src/backends/csharp/gen_bindings/{types.rs,mod.rs}`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: emit full `rust_path` for types not re-exported at the crate root**: param-type resolution in `gen_nif_function`, `gen_nif_async_function`, `gen_nif_method`, `gen_nif_async_method`, and `build_default_deser_preamble` was using `format!("{core_import}::{short_name}")` (e.g. `kreuzberg::DrawingType`) instead of the type's full `rust_path` (e.g. `kreuzberg::extraction::docx::drawing::DrawingType`). Types not re-exported at the crate root caused `cannot find DrawingType in kreuzberg` compile errors in the generated Elixir NIF crate. Fixed by building a `types_by_name` lookup map and resolving via `alef_codegen::conversions::core_type_path`. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: omit `mut` from `args_map` in visitor methods with no arguments**: the `visitor_method.rs.jinja` template always emitted `let mut args_map = serde_json::Map::new();`. When the visitor method has zero arguments the map is never mutated, triggering an `unused_mut` clippy warning that fails CI under `-D warnings`. The template now conditionally emits `mut` only when `args` is non-empty. (`src/backends/rustler/src/template_env.rs`)

- **alef snippets gaps: fix false-positive for MkDocs `pymdownx.snippets` multi-base-path includes**: `alef snippets gaps` previously resolved every `--8<-- "target"` include relative to the docs root only. Projects that configure `pymdownx.snippets.base_path = ["docs/snippets", "docs", "."]` — such as spikard — have includes like `--8<-- "CHANGELOG.md"` that resolve to the project root (`./CHANGELOG.md`), not `docs/CHANGELOG.md`. Alef reported these as missing references even though MkDocs renders them correctly. Two changes fix this: (1) `GapConfig` gains an `include_base_paths: Vec<PathBuf>` field; `parse_includes` now searches each base path in order and returns the first candidate that exists on disk, falling back to `docs_dir.join(target)` only when none match, so the missing-references report still shows a real candidate path. (2) `alef snippets gaps` gains a repeatable `--include-base-path <PATH>` CLI flag — pass each path from `pymdownx.snippets.base_path` to match the MkDocs resolver exactly. When the flag is omitted, behaviour is identical to before (only the docs root is searched). (`crates/alef-snippets/src/gaps.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/commands/snippets.rs`)

- **alef-e2e wasm: do not append `/nodejs` to user-supplied `[e2e.packages.wasm].path`**: `render_package_json` always appended `/nodejs` to the resolved package path, on the assumption it was a `pkg/` directory laid out by `wasm-pack build --target nodejs --out-dir pkg/nodejs`. That assumption only holds for the fallback path from `wasm_crate_path()`. When a project sets `[crates.e2e.packages.wasm].path` explicitly (e.g. html-to-markdown's `../../crates/html-to-markdown-wasm`, the crate root whose root-level `package.json` already declares `"main": "pkg/nodejs/..."`), the resulting `file:.../nodejs` dependency pointed at a nonexistent directory, breaking `pnpm install` with `ERR_PNPM_LINKED_PKG_DIR_NOT_FOUND` and corrupting the lockfile. Now `render_package_json` only appends `/nodejs` for the fallback case; explicit paths are used verbatim. (`src/e2e/codegen/wasm.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: skip `serde_wasm_bindgen::from_value` for external-crate types in tagged enums**: when a mixed-type tagged-enum field's `type_rust_path` resolves to a crate other than `core_import` (e.g. `tree_sitter_language_pack::ProcessResult`), the emitter previously generated `serde_wasm_bindgen::from_value::<ext_crate::T>()` unconditionally. Because the external crate is not in the WASM consumer's Cargo dependency graph, this caused `error[E0433]: failed to resolve: use of unresolved module or unlinked crate` and broke WASM, Python wheel macOS, Swift, and Dart iOS builds. The fix detects whether the type's crate prefix differs from `core_import` and falls back to `Default::default()` for those fields. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/enums.rs`)

- **alef-snippets C validator: use unambiguously invalid token in `syntax_fail` test**: the previous fixture (`return ;` in a non-void function) is treated as a warning by some GCC versions on Linux and exits 0 unless `-Werror` is set, causing the test to return `Pass` instead of `Fail`. Replaced with `@@@` — an invalid preprocessing token that is a hard error on all C compilers. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/c.rs`)

- **publish workflow: include `alef-backend-jni` in the crates.io publish order**: the jni backend was added in commit 831dd99d but was not wired into `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` crate order. Topologically, jni must be published after gleam (which has no jni deps) and before kotlin and kotlin-android (which depend on it). (`.github/workflows/publish.yaml`)
