- **alef-backend-napi + alef-e2e: emit tagged-enum discriminator matching Rust serde tag**: NAPI-RS binding generation for tagged enums hardcoded the TS discriminant field as `"kind"` regardless of what the Rust enum declared via `#[serde(tag = "...")]`. This broke deserialization: kreuzcrawl's `AuthConfig` uses `tag = "type"`, so Rust expected payloads like `{ type: "basic", username, password }` but TypeScript was emitting `{ kind: "basic", ... }`, causing deserialization to fail with `Missing field \`type\``. Now alef-backend-napi emits the discriminant name matching the Rust serde tag, and alef-e2e fixture code preserves the actual tag field name instead of rewriting all tags to "kind". Updated test assertions in alef-backend-napi to expect the serde tag name. (`src/backends/napi/src/gen_bindings/enums.rs`, `src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`)

## [0.16.24] - 2026-05-17

### Changed

- **packaging: fold `alef-snippets` into the main `alef` CLI as the `alef snippets` subcommand**: the workspace previously shipped two binaries (`alef-cli` → `alef`, and `alef-snippets`), forcing `cargo install --git ...` callers to disambiguate with `--package alef-cli --bin alef`. The standalone `alef-snippets` bin is removed; its CLI surface (list/validate/parse/audit/gaps) is now reachable as `alef snippets <cmd>`, matching the existing `alef fmt`/`alef lint`/`alef generate` pattern. Companion updates landed in `kreuzberg-dev/actions` (install-alef@v1 drops the `--bin alef` selector; lint-docs@v1 calls `install-alef` + `alef snippets validate`) and `kreuzberg-dev/pre-commit-hooks` v1.1.3 (alef-snippets-validate hook now invokes `alef snippets validate`). (`crates/alef-cli/{Cargo.toml,src/commands/{mod.rs,snippets.rs},src/main.rs}`, `crates/alef-snippets/{Cargo.toml,src/main.rs}`)

- **chore: drop taplo entirely from alef tooling**: CI no longer installs taplo-cli or runs `taplo fmt --check`; `.taplo.toml` is removed. TOML formatting in alef now relies on `cargo-sort` (Cargo.toml ordering) and `cargo-fmt` (inline TOML literals in Rust code) only. (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.taplo.toml`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`)

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: also suppress `CyclomaticComplexMethod` in the generated `@file:Suppress` header**: data-class (de)serializers emitted for sealed `NodeContent`-style discriminated unions (with one branch per variant) sit just above detekt's default cyclomatic-complexity threshold of 15 — for html-to-markdown's 14-variant `NodeContent` they hit 16 (`deserialize`) and 15 (`serialize`). The existing file-level suppress block already silenced `MaxLineLength` / `TooManyFunctions` / etc.; adding `CyclomaticComplexMethod` rounds out the set so refactoring the (de)serializer into per-variant helpers isn't required just to get prek clean. Surfaced on html-to-markdown's prek run (`packages/kotlin-android/src/main/kotlin/dev/kreuzberg/android/NodeContent.kt:95,152`). (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: wrap the FFI-tail block in `convert` and `convertWithVisitorInternal` with `// CPD-OFF` / `// CPD-ON` markers**: PMD CPD flagged the ~15-line FFI marshalling tail (null-check `resultPtr` → `result_to_json` → free → null-check `jsonPtr` → `reinterpret` → free string → `MAPPER.readValue`) as a duplication between the two methods. The block is intentionally identical and extracting it into a helper would require threading the `Arena`, `MAPPER`, and free-handle through a private method for marginal benefit. Wrap both copies with CPD markers in `ffi_class.rs`, matching the precedent the builder-class emitter already uses. Surfaced on html-to-markdown's prek run (`packages/java/src/main/java/dev/kreuzberg/htmltomarkdown/HtmlToMarkdownRs.java:39,85`). (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: add `@file:Suppress` headers to JNI Bridge object + JNI client class kotlin files**: `emit_jni_bridge_object` (e.g. `LiterLlmBridge.kt`) and `emit_jni_client_class` (e.g. `DefaultClient.kt`) emit kotlin files that go through their own assembly path (NOT through `assemble_kt_content` in alef-backend-kotlin-android), so the `@file:Suppress` block added there did not cover them. They now emit their own `@file:Suppress(...)` block listing `MaxLineLength`, `ktlint:standard:max-line-length`, `TooManyFunctions`, `LongParameterList` (+ `LongMethod` on the client class), silencing detekt on JNI external-fun signatures and coroutine method signatures that naturally exceed 120 chars. Surfaced on liter-llm's prek run (`DefaultClient.kt:54,121,133` and `LiterLlmBridge.kt:12`). (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/jni_emitter.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold/ffi: add `futures-util = "0.3"` to `crates/<crate>-ffi/Cargo.toml` when the umbrella crate exposes a Streaming adapter**: the FFI codegen emits `use futures_util::StreamExt; ... stream.next().await` inside the streaming shim, but the FFI scaffold did not add the dep, producing `error[E0432]: unresolved import futures_util` on `cargo check -p <crate>-ffi`. Mirrors the same `has_streaming` check used by the PHP / Python / Ruby / Node scaffolds. Surfaced on liter-llm's prek run (`crates/liter-llm-ffi/src/lib.rs:10609`). (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/ffi.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: suppress `MaxLineLength` violations in generated Kotlin files**: kotlin POJOs / data-class declarations with serde-renamed enum tags and multi-arg constructors regularly exceed the 120-char `MaxLineLength` limit enforced by detekt (and the ktlint `max-line-length` sibling rule). The `@file:Suppress(...)` header emitted at the top of every generated `.kt` file now lists both `MaxLineLength` (detekt) and `ktlint:standard:max-line-length` (ktlint) so the kotlin-android binding no longer flaps on every prek run. Surfaced on liter-llm's prek run (15+ `[MaxLineLength]` violations across `LiterLlm.kt`, `RerankDocument.kt`, `Message.kt`, etc.). (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: add `futures-util = "0.3"` to `crates/<crate>-node/Cargo.toml` when the crate exposes a Streaming adapter**: the napi backend emits `use futures_util::StreamExt;` in streaming method bodies (e.g. `chatStream`), but the node Cargo.toml scaffold did not add the dep — only PHP / Ruby / Python had the conditional `has_streaming` check. `cargo check` failed on `unresolved import futures_util` for every umbrella crate with a chat-stream adapter on the node backend. Mirrors the same pattern used in `alef-scaffold/src/languages/php.rs:38`. Surfaced on liter-llm's prek run (`crates/liter-llm-node/src/lib.rs:992`). (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/node.rs`)

- **alef-e2e dart: emit named-arg syntax for required string parameters**: the dart e2e codegen emitted positional literals for required string args (`_client.cancelBatch('batch-id')`) under the assumption the wrapper convention was POSITIONAL-required. The dart bridge backend (`alef-backend-dart`) emits ALL params as named-required (`Future<BatchObject> cancelBatch({required String batchId})`), so the generated tests failed `missing_required_argument` + `extra_positional_arguments_could_be_named` analyzer errors across `batches_test.dart`, `files_test.dart`, `responses_test.dart`. The `extractFile* → extractBytes*` mime_type remap special-case (kreuzberg) still emits positional. Surfaced on liter-llm's prek run (60+ dart-analyze errors). (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: add `[package.metadata.cargo-machete] ignored = [...]` to FFI / PHP / Node / Python / JNI Cargo.toml scaffolds**: unconditionally-emitted deps (`serde_json`/`tokio`/etc.) are unused on some umbrella crates and flagged by cargo machete; they are now ignored, mirroring the swift backend. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/{ffi,php,node,python,jni}.rs`)

- **alef-backend-pyo3: silence ruff TC001 on the runtime re-export block in `options.py`**: the generated options module does `from .<native> import (CodeBlockStyle, HeadingStyle, ...)` so that downstream users can `from html_to_markdown.options import NewlineStyle` at runtime. Ruff's TC001 saw the imports referenced only in annotations (the file uses `from __future__ import annotations`) and demanded they move under `if TYPE_CHECKING:`, which would break the runtime re-export. The `import_item.jinja` template now appends `# noqa: TC001` to each enum import line, preserving the runtime re-export while satisfying the lint. Tried `as`-alias re-exports first; ruff TC001 still fires through that form. Surfaced on html-to-markdown's prek run (10 TC001 errors in `packages/python/html_to_markdown/options.py`). (`src/backends/pyo3/templates/import_item.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-jni: migrate generated shims to jni 0.22 — `EnvUnowned` in extern sigs, `AttachGuard` body, `JNIString` for throw_new args, `JString::try_to_string` for decode**: jni 0.22 split `JNIEnv` into FFI-safe `EnvUnowned<'frame>` and `Env<'_>` (which carries the real API surface). The pre-0.22 codegen emitted `use jni::JNIEnv;` plus extern shims with `mut env: JNIEnv` bodies that called `env.get_string(&s)?`, `env.new_string(s)`, and `env.throw_new(ERROR_CLASS, msg)` directly, which now (a) emits deprecation warnings for the `JNIEnv` alias, (b) trips `E0599` for `get_string` / `new_string` / `throw_new` on `&mut EnvUnowned`, and (c) trips `E0277` for `throw_new` because 0.22 requires `S: AsRef<JNIStr>` / `T: Desc<JClass>` (Rust `&str` no longer coerces). The codegen now: (1) emits `use jni::{AttachGuard, Env, EnvUnowned};` and declares extern args as `mut env: EnvUnowned`, (2) upgrades to `&mut Env<'_>` inline via `AttachGuard::from_unowned(env.as_raw())` + `borrow_env_mut()` (avoids `EnvUnowned::with_env`'s `FnOnce(&mut Env) -> Result<T, E>` closure shape which would force rewriting every early-return + sentinel path), (3) replaces `env.get_string(&s)?` with `s.try_to_string(env)`, and (4) wraps both `class` and `msg` args to `env.throw_new` in `jni::strings::JNIString::from(...)`. Helpers (`jstring_to_string`, `string_to_jstring`, `throw_jni_error`, `run_or_throw`) now take `&mut Env<'_>`. `_env`-only shims (destructors, streaming free) declare `_env: EnvUnowned` with no body. Verified clean on `cargo clippy -p html-to-markdown-rs-jni` against the regenerated shim. Snapshots refreshed; the 25-test suite still passes. Surfaced on html-to-markdown's prek run. (`src/backends/jni/src/gen_shims.rs`, `src/backends/jni/tests/{gen_shims_test.rs,snapshots/*.snap}`)

- **alef-hook (`alef-verify` / `alef-sync-versions`): resolve consumer `alef.toml` by walking up from CWD**: `_version()` previously read `_hooks_dir().parent / "alef.toml"`, which points at the prek-cached clone of this repo (the dir where `alef_hook.py` lives), NOT the consumer project being verified. That made consumer bumps of `alef.toml` invisible to the hook — it kept downloading the binary version pinned by the prek `rev:` tag, surfacing as `HTTP 404` when the rev's release had no assets uploaded (e.g. kreuzberg pinning prek `rev: v0.16.23` and bumping `alef.toml` to `0.16.24`). `_find_consumer_alef_toml()` now walks up from `Path.cwd()` until it finds an `alef.toml`, matching how prek invokes hooks (CWD = consumer repo root). (`hooks/alef_hook.py`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: fix `@type t` struct field indentation for mix format**: field lines inside `@type t :: %__MODULE__{...}` were indented with 4 spaces; `mix format` requires 10-space alignment to the opening `{` column. Surfaced on kreuzcrawl prek run where 28+ `.ex` files including `CrawlPageResult`, `ContentConfig`, `JsonLdEntry` etc. were reformatted. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: emit space-inside-brackets in pyproject.toml inline arrays**: `pyproject-fmt` rewrites inline arrays from `["x"]` to `[ "x" ]`. The scaffold template now emits the spaced form for `requires`, `keywords`, `features`, `python-packages`, `dev`, `lint.select`, and `lint.per-file-ignores` so the generated pyproject.toml is idempotent under pyproject-fmt. Surfaced on kreuzcrawl prek run against `packages/python/pyproject.toml`. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/python.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: add `@file:Suppress` header to suppress ktlint/detekt violations in generated Kotlin files**: generated Kotlin files violated `ktlint:standard:trailing-comma-on-call-site`, `ktlint:standard:spacing-between-declarations-with-comments`, `ktlint:standard:when-entry-bracing`, `ktlint:standard:annotation`, and detekt's `TooManyFunctions` on ~30 files in kreuzcrawl. A file-level `@file:Suppress(...)` annotation is now emitted at the top of every assembled `.kt` file before the package declaration. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: extend `@file:Suppress` to cover `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`, `spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations`, `blank-line-between-when-conditions`, and `chain-method-continuation`**: ktlint flagged additional rules on kreuzcrawl's generated `MapResult.kt` (missing trailing comma on data class constructor) and `AuthConfig.kt` (spacing around annotated declarations, blank lines between when-conditions, chain method continuation). All four new rules are now included in the `@file:Suppress` block. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: add `ktlint:standard:annotation` to `@file:Suppress` in JNI bridge and client files**: `ktfmt` (run by prek) compacts `@Throws(...)\n    external fun` into a single line, which then triggers ktlint's `standard:annotation` rule. Adding `ktlint:standard:annotation` to both the bridge-object and client-class `@file:Suppress` blocks silences the conflict. Snapshots updated. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/jni_emitter.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: add `// swift-format-ignore-file` directive to generated Swift files**: swift-format reformatted `packages/swift/Sources/Kreuzcrawl/Kreuzcrawl.swift` on every prek run. The `// swift-format-ignore-file` magic comment is now emitted at the top of every generated Swift bindings file, instructing swift-format to skip the file entirely. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: wrap generated Builder class with CPD-OFF/CPD-ON markers**: PMD's copy-paste detector flagged 64-line (213 token) and 19-line (121 token) duplications across `CrawlPageResult.java` and `ScrapeResult.java` builder classes in kreuzcrawl. `// CPD-OFF` and `// CPD-ON` comments are now emitted around the nested builder class so CPD suppresses these intentional structural duplications. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-e2e dart: emit `// ignore_for_file: unused_local_variable` in generated test files**: `final result = await api.method(args)` is emitted for every test case; tests that only assert no error is thrown do not use `result`, producing ~1266 `unused_local_variable` warnings across kreuzcrawl's dart e2e suite. The file-level ignore directive is now prepended after the alef header in every generated `*_test.dart` file. (`src/e2e/codegen/dart.rs`)

- **alef-backend-dart: skip trait-bridge methods that return `alef(skip)`-stripped trait types**: `return_type_references_trait` only inspected `api.types`, which has already had `binding_excluded` items stripped by the time codegen runs. Traits annotated `#[cfg_attr(alef, alef(skip))]` (e.g. kreuzberg's `SyncExtractor`) therefore slipped past the filter, and the resulting `Box<dyn Fn() -> DartFnFuture<Option<kreuzberg::extractors::SyncExtractor>>>` field on the bridge struct failed to compile with `error[E0782]: expected a type, found a trait` (23 errors across `packages/dart/rust/src/{lib.rs,frb_generated.rs}`). A new `ApiSurface::excluded_trait_names: HashSet<String>` is populated alongside `excluded_type_paths` during `strip_binding_excluded`, preserving the `is_trait` flag that would otherwise be lost. The dart trait-bridge filter now consults this set so excluded **traits** are filtered out while excluded **structs** (e.g. `InternalDocument` returned by `extract_bytes`) continue to be emitted by qualified core path. Added unit tests covering in-surface traits, excluded traits, excluded structs, and unrelated named types. (`src/core/ir.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/extract.rs`, `src/backends/dart/src/gen_rust_crate/trait_bridge.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: allow `clippy::missing_safety_doc` in the generated C-FFI lib.rs**: every `pub unsafe extern "C" fn` emitted by the FFI backend lacks a `# Safety` rustdoc section (the safety contract is documented at the C-header / cbindgen layer instead). Downstream consumers running `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` on the FFI crate therefore tripped 13+ `missing_safety_doc` errors per polyglot library. The crate header now includes `#![allow(clippy::missing_safety_doc, clippy::doc_lazy_continuation, clippy::doc_overindented_list_items)]` alongside the existing allow blocks. Surfaced first on kreuzcrawl CI Rust @ `74021aa7`. (`src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: disable `cargo test`/`doctest`/`bench` for the generated `*-swift` crate**: the `[swift_bridge::bridge]` `extern "Swift" { ... }` block emits Rust-to-Swift call sites whose symbols (`__swift_bridge__$<Type>$<method>`) are only resolved when the static/cdylib output is linked into a Swift target. `cargo test --workspace` on a pure-Rust runner (windows-latest, ubuntu-latest without swift-toolchain) would therefore fail with `rust-lld: error: undefined symbol: __swift_bridge__$SwiftHtmlVisitorBox$alef_visit_*` (and ~30 more) during the test-harness link step. The generated `Cargo.toml`'s `[lib]` now sets `test = false`, `doctest = false`, `bench = false` — the cdylib/staticlib build path is unaffected, and the swift package's own `swift test` suite remains the authoritative test surface for this crate. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: emit `extra.pie.binary.url-template` block in PHP composer.json for explicit PIE binary discovery**: PIE's `pre-packaged-binary` download method relies on asset filename pattern matching, which has known edge cases with architecture aliases and libc detection (kreuzberg-dev/html-to-markdown#333). The PHP scaffold now derives the GitHub repo owner/name from `metadata.repository` and emits an `extra.pie.binary.url-template` entry pointing to the releases' archive pattern, giving PIE explicit binary URLs when pattern matching alone is insufficient. The block is only emitted when the repository URL is valid (github.com-based). Template variables (`{Version}`, `{PhpVersion}`, `{Arch}`, `{OS}`, `{Libc}`, `{TSMode}`) follow PIE 1.4.x naming conventions. Also fixes the Composer package name derivation to use the GitHub repo name (e.g. `html-to-markdown`) instead of the crate name (e.g. `html-to-markdown-rs`), ensuring Packagist package names are correct. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/php.rs`)

- **alef-backend-dart: pin `flutter_rust_bridge` with `=`-prefixed strict version**: flutter_rust_bridge enforces exact runtime/compile-time version matching — a caret-pin lets `cargo update` pull in a patch release that the generated bridge code can't talk to. The Rust crate's `Cargo.toml` template now emits `flutter_rust_bridge = "=2.12.0"` so `cargo update` cannot drift off the matching version. (`src/backends/dart/src/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: drop redundant `${...}` braces in error-message interpolation when the next char is not an identifier continuation**: `interpolate_error_message_template` previously always emitted `${fieldN}` for `{N}` placeholders, producing `"HTML parsing error: ${field0}"` for the common single-field `thiserror`-style template. ktlint's `standard:string-template` rule flags this as redundant when the brace is unnecessary (the next character — `"`, `:`, ` `, `)`, etc. — cannot extend the identifier), and the rule is a CI gate in the polyglot host repos. The helper now peeks at the character following the closing brace and emits `$fieldN` when it would be unambiguous, falling back to `${fieldN}` only when the next char is `[A-Za-z0-9_]`. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: scaffold the JNI crate at `crates/<config.name>-jni/` to match the path that `alef-backend-jni::gen_shims` uses for `src/lib.rs`**: `scaffold_jni` derived the JNI crate directory from `config.core_crate_dir()`, while `gen_shims::jni_output_path` derived it from `config.name`. On any workspace where the two differ (e.g. tree-sitter-language-pack with `name = "tree-sitter-language-pack"` and `sources = ["crates/ts-pack-core/src/lib.rs"]`), regen produced an orphan `crates/ts-pack-core-jni/Cargo.toml` with no `src/`, while the actual JNI shim landed under `crates/tree-sitter-language-pack-jni/`. cargo broke the entire workspace with `can't find library 'ts_pack_jni', rename file to 'src/lib.rs' or specify lib.path`, taking cargo-check, cargo-clippy, cargo-fmt, cargo-machete, and cargo-deny down with it. The scaffold now uses `config.name` for the crate directory and the `[package] name`, and uses the cargo package name (`config.name`) as the umbrella dep-table key while keeping `path = "../<core_crate_dir>"` for the on-disk location. Added regression test `scaffold_jni_path_uses_config_name_not_core_crate_dir`. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/jni.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: add `tokio` to the generated Cargo.toml's `[package.metadata.cargo-machete] ignored = [...]` list**: alongside the existing `async-trait` / `serde` entries, `tokio` is now ignored. The Swift backend emits `tokio` unconditionally in `[dependencies]` for stable-manifest reasons (matches the documented "conditionally referenced only when the umbrella crate declares trait bridges or async adapters" pattern). For umbrella crates without trait bridges or async adapters — e.g. tree-sitter-language-pack — `tokio` is genuinely unused, and `cargo machete` correctly flagged it. Listing it in `ignored` matches the same approach the template already uses for the other conditional deps. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack prek run. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/cargo.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: align the closing `}` of `@type t :: %__MODULE__{...}` to 8-space indent so `mix format` accepts it idempotently**: the typespec block opened at 10-space indent for fields (column 11) but closed with `}` (2 spaces), which `mix format` rewrote to 8 spaces. tslp's 19+ struct files (`ProcessConfig`, `ProcessResult`, `FileMetrics`, `Span`, etc.) flapped between emit and format on every prek run. Closing brace now matches the column `mix format` settles on. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack prek run. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: bump the JNI Cargo.toml's `jni` dep from `0.21` to `0.22`**: `alef-backend-jni::gen_shims` emits `use jni::{Env, JNIEnv};` (jni 0.22 split `JNIEnv` into FFI-safe `EnvUnowned<'frame>` and the API-carrying `Env<'_>`) but the scaffold template was still pinning `jni = "0.21"`. tslp's regen produced a `tree-sitter-language-pack-jni` crate that failed `cargo check` with `unresolved import jni::Env`. Bumped the scaffold to match the shim emitter. Surfaced on tree-sitter-language-pack prek run. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/jni.rs`)

## [0.16.23] - 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **alef-core: sanitize Zig enum tag identifiers**: `zig_ident` previously escaped reserved keywords only; serde-renamed tags such as `og:image` or `Content-Type` flowed through unchanged and emitted invalid Zig (`enum fields do not have types` at the colon). The helper now replaces every non-`[A-Za-z0-9_]` character with `_`, prefixes a leading digit with `_`, then runs the result through `zig_safe_name`. Affects every Zig enum variant whose serde rename contains punctuation. (`src/core/keywords.rs`)

- **alef-backend-php: skip `binding_excluded` fields in `pub fn new(...) -> Self` literals**: the named-constructor emitter iterated every field of the IR type and wrote `name: Default::default()` for fields marked `binding_excluded`, but those fields are stripped from the binding struct itself — producing `error[E0560]: struct X has no field named Y` (seen on `DownloadedDocument.content` and `ScrapeResult.screenshot` in kreuzcrawl-php). The constructor now filters `binding_excluded` fields entirely; only `cfg`-gated fields (which stay in the struct under a `#[cfg]` gate) still receive `Default::default()` initializers. Both the `has_serde` and the non-serde branches of `gen_struct_methods_impl` are fixed. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: emit sealed-interface fields as bare interface, not pointer-to-interface**: struct fields whose type is a tagged-data (sealed-interface) enum were being emitted as `*AuthConfig` for both optional and `has_default` positions, producing `cannot use v (variable of interface type AuthConfig) as *AuthConfig value in assignment: AuthConfig does not implement *AuthConfig (type *AuthConfig is pointer to interface, not interface)` at compile time. Go interfaces are already nullable — the interface zero value is `nil` — so taking a pointer to one is never the right shape. `gen_struct_type` detects sealed-interface fields via `data_enum_names` and emits the bare type for both struct declarations and builder option setters; the custom `UnmarshalJSON` emitter also drops the `&` when assigning the decoded variant. `gen_config_options` now takes `data_enum_names` and skips the `&v` address-of path for these fields. Test `test_parent_struct_with_optional_data_enum_field_emits_custom_unmarshal_json` updated to assert the new shape. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/{types.rs,mod.rs}`, `src/backends/go/tests/bug_fixes_test.rs`)

### Changed

- **alef-snippets: parser tolerates malformed YAML frontmatter**: `parse_frontmatter` previously returned `Error::Parse` when a snippet file opened with `---\n` but had no closing `\n---\n` delimiter, or when the YAML between delimiters failed to deserialize. Snippet collections that include partial templates or human-edited drafts would short-circuit the entire run. The parser now treats these cases as "no frontmatter": `SnippetMetadata::default()` plus the original `content` are returned, and validation continues against the body. Strict structural reporting still lives in `frontmatter_status`, which downstream `audit` consumers can opt into. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/parser.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: gaps detection recognises binding-specific language dir names**: `gaps::language_group` previously formed the `{language}` placeholder by string-matching the rendered `Language::to_string()` value, so `docs/snippets/kotlin_android/...` produced no group (`kotlin_android` != `kotlin`) and `gaps` could not flag missing variants for kotlin-android consumers. The matcher now passes each path component through `Language::from_dir_name`, which already understands aliases like `kotlin_android`, `kotlin-android`, `c-sharp`, `dotnet`, `golang`, and the `wasm`/`node` → typescript shortcuts. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/gaps.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: accept `kotlin_android` and `kotlin-android` as kotlin in `from_dir_name`**: directory names used by polyglot consumers (`kotlin_android`, `kotlin-android`) now map to `Language::Kotlin`. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/types.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: go/java/csharp validators wrap bare fragments**: parity with the existing `RustValidator::wrap_if_fragment` path. Snippets that omit the `package main` / `public class` / `Main`-method boilerplate now compile cleanly at syntax level. (1) `GoValidator` splits leading `import (...)` / `import "..."` blocks from the body and wraps the body in `package main; func main() { ... }`; comment-only bodies get a placeholder `_ = 0` statement so the entry point still compiles. (2) `JavaValidator` detects an existing top-level class/interface/enum/record and skips wrapping; otherwise it splits leading `import`/`package` declarations and wraps in `public class Snippet { public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception { ... } }`. (3) `CsharpValidator` wraps comment-only bodies with a `return;` placeholder so the top-level-statements compiler entry point is satisfied; the `is_dependency_error` list also picks up `CS5001` ("Program does not contain a static 'Main' method") for the placeholder fall-through. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/{go,java,csharp}.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: C validator broadens dependency-error pattern**: `CValidator::is_dependency_error` now matches `file not found`, `No such file or directory`, `undeclared identifier`, `implicit declaration`, and `unknown type name` — so snippets that legitimately `#include "html_to_markdown.h"` (or any other host-supplied header) downgrade to Pass at syntax level when the include path is not configured in the validator sandbox, instead of failing CI on a legitimate doc example. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/c.rs`)

### Added

- **alef-snippets: add JSON and YAML in-process validators**: snippets in `` ```json `` / `` ```yaml `` fences are now syntax-checked by deserializing into `serde_json::Value` / `serde_yaml::Value`. Both validators run in-process (no external toolchain required) and report concrete parser error messages on failure. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/{json_validator,yaml_validator}.rs`, `crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/mod.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: add R validator (`Rscript -e 'parse(file=...)'`)**: probes for `Rscript` and `R` on PATH; uses `parse(file=...)` for syntax/compile levels and `Rscript <file>` for run level. Returns `Unavailable` when the R toolchain is missing. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/r.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: register documentation-only languages (`text`, `mermaid`, `powershell`, `xml`, `docker`) with no-op pass-through validators**: doc fences in these formats are now recognised by `Language::from_fence_tag` and short-circuit to `Pass` instead of falling through to `Unknown`. Prevents `audit` from reporting them as `UnknownLanguage` errors. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/documentation.rs`, `crates/alef-snippets/src/types.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: add C validator**: closes the last language gap. The `CValidator` probes `cc`, `clang`, then `gcc` for availability and shells out to the first match. Syntax level uses `cc -fsyntax-only`; Compile compiles to a temp binary via `cc -o <out>`; Run additionally executes the compiled binary. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/c.rs`, `crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/mod.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: add 8 missing language validators (c, csharp, dart, go, java, kotlin, swift, zig)**: the validator registry previously covered bash/elixir/php/python/ruby/rust/toml/typescript (8 languages); snippets in any other language returned `Unavailable`. The 8 new validators each gate on toolchain presence via `which::which(...)` and shell out to the upstream toolchain: `cc -fsyntax-only` (Syntax) / `cc -o <out>` (Compile/Run) for C, `dotnet build --nologo -v quiet` (Syntax/Compile) for C#, `dart analyze --no-fatal-warnings` for Dart, `gofmt -e -l` (Syntax) / `go build` (Compile) / `go run` (Run) for Go, `javac -Xlint:none` (with class-name extraction so the temp file matches the public class) for Java, `kotlinc -d <out>` for Kotlin, `swiftc -parse` (Syntax) / `swiftc -o <out>` (Compile/Run) for Swift, `zig ast-check` (Syntax) / `zig build-exe -fno-emit-bin` (Compile) for Zig. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/{c,csharp,dart,go,java,kotlin,swift,zig}.rs`, `crates/alef-snippets/src/validators/mod.rs`)

- **alef-snippets: wire `audit` and `gaps` CLI subcommands**: the `alef-snippets` binary previously exposed only `list`, `validate`, and `parse`. The `audit` (structural integrity report — frontmatter, fences, includes) and `gaps` (coverage gaps — missing includes, unreferenced snippets, missing language variants, unannotated skips, unknown languages) library entry points are now reachable from the CLI. `--snippets <dir>... --docs <dir>... [--require-frontmatter | --required-languages <list>]`. Both commands exit with a non-zero status when issues are found, so they slot into pre-commit / CI gates. (`crates/alef-snippets/src/main.rs`)

- **workspace: register `alef-snippets` crate + add missing workspace deps**: `crates/alef-snippets` is now a `[workspace.members]` entry and a `[workspace.dependencies]` alias, fixing the prior cargo error `cannot specify features for packages outside of workspace`. Added `tempfile = "3"`, `walkdir = "2"`, and `which = "8"` as workspace dependencies — the alef-snippets crate referenced them via `workspace = true` before any workspace-level declaration existed. (`Cargo.toml`)

## [0.16.22] - 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e: remove vite-plugin-wasm from WASM e2e vitest config**: `wasm-pack build --target nodejs` produces a self-initializing CommonJS bundle that loads the `.wasm` binary via `fs.readFileSync` at import time — it does not require the browser-oriented `vite-plugin-wasm` Vite transform. Including `vite-plugin-wasm` caused all `Wasm*` class exports to be `undefined` when running under wasm-pack 0.13.x (installed by the `curl | sh` installer in CI), because the plugin intercepted the CJS `require()` path and returned an uninitialized `WebAssembly.Module` instead of the module object. Removed `import wasm from 'vite-plugin-wasm'` and `plugins: [wasm()]` from `vitest.config.ts.jinja`, and removed the `vite-plugin-wasm` devDependency from `package.json.jinja`. (`src/e2e/templates/wasm/vitest.config.ts.jinja`, `src/e2e/templates/wasm/package.json.jinja`, `src/e2e/codegen/wasm.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: add `is_sanitized_fixed_tuple_array` predicate for fixed tuple-array fields**: tagged-enum struct fields that were originally `[(K, V); N]` but sanitized to `String` were not being detected as JsValue candidates, causing them to be stored as `Option<String>` in the binding struct rather than `Option<JsValue>`. Added `is_sanitized_fixed_tuple_array` predicate (matching `original_type` that starts with `[(` and contains `);`) and wired it alongside `is_sanitized_tuple_vec` in all three collection sites in `gen_tagged_enum_as_struct`, `gen_tagged_enum_binding_to_core`, and `gen_tagged_enum_core_to_binding`. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/enums.rs`)

- **alef-backend-jni + alef-backend-kotlin: fix JNI exception swallowing in generated Android bridge**: two gaps caused Rust `Result::Err` / panics to be silently lost instead of reaching Kotlin callers as typed exceptions. (1) The generated `Bridge.kt` `external fun` declarations were missing `@Throws(<Bridge>Exception::class)` annotations — without this, JNI-thrown `RuntimeException` is wrapped in `UndeclaredThrowableException` or otherwise bypasses typed `catch` blocks. Every non-destructor `external fun` (top-level functions, instance methods, streaming Start/Next shims) now carries `@Throws`. Destructor shims remain annotation-free (they are infallible drop operations). (2) The generated `throw_jni_error` helper emitted `let _ = env.throw_new(ERROR_CLASS, msg)`, silently discarding failure. If the exception class was not on the classpath (misconfigured AAR) the shim returned a null/zero sentinel with no exception pending, appearing to Kotlin as a valid return value. The helper now checks `is_err()` and falls back to `java/lang/RuntimeException` so the caller always receives an exception. Adds explicit unit tests and updates all snapshots. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/jni_emitter.rs`, `src/backends/jni/src/gen_shims.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: preserve `Option<T>` as `T?` in enum-variant associated values**: `emit_variant_with_data` previously called `mapper.map_type(&f.ty)` without checking `field.optional`, so fields where the extractor had unwrapped `TypeRef::Optional(inner)` into `(ty: inner, optional: true)` were emitted as bare `T` instead of `T?`. The same gap existed in `emit_error` for error-variant fields. Both sites now check `f.optional && !already_optional` and append `?` when the flag is set, matching the existing behavior in `emit_first_class_struct`. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/tests/snapshot_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: emit ktfmt-stable data-class declarations**: the Kotlin data-class emitter now applies ktfmt's 100-character heuristic — declarations whose single-line form fits within 100 chars are emitted as a single line (`data class Foo(val a: Int, val b: T)`); longer declarations stay multi-line. Previously the emitter always used multi-line, causing ktfmt in CI to rewrite every short declaration and break the lint step. The same heuristic applies to sealed-class variant data classes and error-variant data classes. Annotations (`@JsonDeserialize` / `@JsonSerialize`) on variant subclasses force multi-line regardless of length. Field-level KDoc also forces multi-line. Adds 6 new unit tests: short/long coverage for top-level data classes, sealed-class variants, and error variants. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

## [0.16.21] - 2026-05-16

### Added

- **alef-backend-php: emit per-property `@var` PHPDoc on promoted readonly constructor parameters**: generated DTO classes now carry an inline `/** @var T description */` block above every promoted property (and `/** ... @var T */` block for multi-line rustdoc). PHPStan level max now sees the precise generic element type (`@var array<string>`, `@var ?int`) and IDEs surface tooltips on every property. Replaces the prior global `@param` block before the constructor, which only covered array fields and didn't propagate field rustdoc. Adds `php_property_phpdoc()` helper + 153 lines of unit tests covering empty/single-line/multi-line doc forms. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/php/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-core: `KotlinAndroid` package-directory lookup**: `default_package_directory` now maps `Language::KotlinAndroid` to `packages/kotlin-android` explicitly instead of falling through to the generic `packages/{lang}` formatter (which produced `packages/kotlinandroid`). (`src/core/config/resolved/lookups.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: emit Doxygen comments on C headers**: every `extern "C" fn` declaration, `typedef`, opaque-handle wrapper, enum, and enum variant in the generated `.h` files now carries a `/** ... */` Doxygen block derived from the upstream rustdoc. `# Arguments` items render as `\param`, `# Returns` as `\return`, `# Errors` and `# Safety` as `\note`, `# Example` as `\code`/`\endcode`. C/C++ consumers and IDEs (clangd, Xcode, Visual Studio) now surface tooltips on every public FFI symbol. Adds `emit_c_doxygen` to the shared `alef-codegen::doc_emission` module. (`src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/{functions.rs,types.rs,enums.rs}`, snapshot tests)

- **alef-backend-go: emit Godoc on methods + free functions**: every exported method declaration `func (r *Receiver) Method(...)` and every free function `func Name(...)` in generated `.go` files now carries `// Method ...` / `// Name ...` Godoc derived from upstream rustdoc. Comments start with the symbol name (per the `go doc` and `godoc` convention) so IDE tooltips and `pkg.go.dev` render every public binding member. `# Arguments` → `// Arguments:` bullets, `# Returns` → `// Returns ...`, `# Errors` → `// Errors are returned when ...`, `# Example` → `// Example:` indented code block. Also fixes a pre-existing double-prefix glitch where summaries that already started with the symbol name produced `// RootNode RootNode returns ...`. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/go/tests/snapshot_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: close docstring emission gaps at record components, opaque-handle instance methods, and free-function FFI surface**: per-component Javadoc now renders above each `record` field when the multi-line emit path is taken (single-line records skip per-component docs because inlining `/** ... */` inside a comma-joined parameter list produces invalid Java). Opaque-handle instance methods emitted by `gen_instance_method` now carry their rustdoc above the public signature. Free-function static methods in the raw FFI class (`gen_sync_function_method`) emit Javadoc derived from `FunctionDef.doc`. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/{types.rs,ffi_class.rs}`, snapshot tests in `src/backends/java/tests/docs_emission_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-zig: close docstring emission gaps at struct fields and enum variants**: `///` doc comments now render above each struct field declaration inside `pub const Foo = struct { ... };` and above every enum tag — both for unit-only `enum { ... }` and tagged-union `union(enum) { ... }` shapes (void / single-value / struct-variant cases). Type/method/free-function emission was already covered upstream. (`src/backends/zig/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`, snapshot tests in `src/backends/zig/tests/docs_emission_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: close docstring emission gaps at data-class fields, enum variants, and sealed-class variants**: the kotlin-android backend reuses the shared `alef-backend-kotlin` emitter helpers; this change wires `emit_cleaned_kdoc` at every previously-uncovered site (data-class primary-constructor field, plain enum constant, sealed-class nested variant) so KDoc now propagates through to AAR-emitted `.kt` files. The free-function facade, JNI bridge object, and handle-only opaque wrapper class already emitted KDoc at the kotlin-android-local sites. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`, snapshot tests in `src/backends/kotlin-android/tests/docs_emission_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: emit `@moduledoc`/`@typedoc`/`@doc` on DTO modules + enum variants**:
  previously only NIF stubs (function-level) carried rustdoc-derived `@doc` heredocs; the
  generated Elixir DTO `defmodule` blocks, unit-enum variant accessors, and data-enum
  per-variant type aliases emitted no doc attributes, leaving `mix docs` with blank ExDoc
  pages for those modules. Now wires a shared `emit_elixir_doc_attr` helper at every
  applicable site so `mix docs` shows complete coverage: full multi-line `@moduledoc`
  heredocs on struct-bearing modules (DTO + opaque resource wrappers) and enum modules,
  `@typedoc` above each `@type t ::` typespec and above each data-enum per-variant type
  alias, and `@doc` above each unit-enum variant accessor function (preserving
  `@doc → @spec → def` ordering for mix-format compliance). Modules with no rustdoc still
  fall back to `@moduledoc false` to silence ExDoc warnings.
  (`src/backends/rustler/src/template_env.rs`,
  `src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`,
  `src/backends/rustler/tests/elixir_module_doc_test.rs`)

- **alef-codegen: language-aware example filtering (`example_for_target`)**: rustdoc `# Example` blocks fenced as ` ```rust ` (and bare-fenced ` ``` ` which rustdoc treats as Rust) are now suppressed from non-Rust doc renderers — YARD (`render_yard_sections`), JSDoc (`render_jsdoc_sections`), and PHPDoc (`render_phpdoc_sections`). Examples fenced with the target language (` ```php `, ` ```ruby `, ` ```typescript `) flow through verbatim. The prior behavior produced misleading Rust source bodies inside Ruby/PHP/TS doc tooltips. 5 new unit tests plus updated assertions in the existing renderer tests. (`src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: full multi-line `@doc` on functions + methods**: `@doc` strings for NIF functions and methods previously truncated to the first line via `func.doc.lines().next()`, dropping all subsequent paragraphs. The emitter now calls `doc_first_paragraph_joined` to preserve the complete first paragraph (joining wrapped lines) and falls back to `"Function"`/`"Method"` only when rustdoc is empty. Closes the gap left by the v0.16.20 single-line truncation noted in the audit. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs:682,1172`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: YARD `@param`/`@return` on tagged-enum variant classes**: generated Ruby variant subclasses (`class TextContent < ContentPart`) now carry YARD tags on every site that consumers see in IDE tooltips: each `attr_reader` field, the variant `initialize` parameter list, the predicate method (`def text? = true`), and the class-level `from_hash` factory. The variant's fields use either upstream rustdoc (via `emit_yard_doc`) or a synthesized `# @return [SorbetType]` line so `solargraph` and `yard server` always resolve the type. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs:gen_tagged_enum_ruby_classes`)

### Fixed

- **alef-cli/alef-e2e: use `oxfmt` instead of Taplo for generated TOML normalization**:
  the default post-generation formatters now call `pnpm dlx oxfmt` for WASM
  manifests, FFI/workspace formatting, and Rust e2e `Cargo.toml` normalization,
  aligning generated output with Kreuzberg's shared pre-commit hooks.
  (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/format.rs`, `src/e2e/format.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: escape generated `cbindgen.toml` `after_includes` safely**:
  Doxygen blocks embedded in `after_includes` now render as escaped TOML
  multiline strings, so backslash commands such as `\code` and rustdoc examples
  containing triple quotes no longer make `cbindgen.toml` invalid.
  (`src/backends/ffi/templates/cbindgen_toml.jinja`,
  `src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`,
  `src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: emit current `getrandom` for wasm target dependencies**:
  generated WASM `Cargo.toml` files now use `getrandom` `0.4` with `wasm_js`, so downstream
  aggressive dependency upgrades no longer make generated manifests stale.
  (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/mod.rs`,
  `src/backends/wasm/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-e2e: omit non-runnable Python and Node fixtures instead of generating skipped tests**:
  fixtures that have no assertions for the target language, or HTTP 101 upgrade
  fixtures that the standard Python/Node HTTP clients cannot execute, are now
  left out of those generated suites instead of producing skipped placeholders.
  (`src/e2e/codegen/python`,
  `src/e2e/codegen/typescript`)

- **alef-backend-php: promote opaque class parameters after the first optional argument**:
  generated PHP userland class declarations now mirror static extension stubs by
  marking parameters after an optional parameter as nullable with `= null`,
  keeping PHPStan/PHP syntax valid for methods like `TestClient::post`.
  (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`,
  `src/backends/php/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

## [0.16.20] - 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: emit resolved workspace version in generated `build.gradle.kts`**: AAR `version` coordinate was hardcoded to `"0.0.0"`, leaving every published Android artifact tagged as v0.0.0 in Maven local. Now reads `config.resolved_version()` (workspace `version =`) so the generated `build.gradle.kts` carries the real release version. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_build_gradle.rs`)

- **release: include all v0.16.19 fixes**: v0.16.19 publish failed on `cargo fmt --check` due to a stray `eprintln!` in a test landed by a concurrent agent. v0.16.20 carries the same fix set plus the fmt scrub and the kotlin-android AAR version fix.

## [0.16.19] - 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **alef-e2e/typescript: emit synthetic `kind` discriminator on napi serde-tagged-enum fixtures**: `alef-backend-napi` (commit `091514d`) hardcodes `"kind"` as the TypeScript discriminator for every internally-tagged (`#[serde(tag = "...")]`) data enum, but e2e fixture codegen still emitted the original serde-tag name (e.g. `role: "user"` instead of `kind: "user"`), producing `Missing field 'kind'` runtime errors across the entire node smoke/chat suite. Adds `rename_napi_serde_tags_to_kind` + `rename_napi_serde_tags_recursive` helpers that walk fixture JSON and rename any serde-tag key to `"kind"` only when the value matches a known variant of the corresponding tagged-data enum (so plain struct fields sharing the same name are left alone). Wired into both `build_args_and_setup` (node, no `options_type`) and `ts_builder_expression_inner` (node, with `options_type`). WASM fixtures untouched — `serde_wasm_bindgen::from_value` reads original serde attribute names. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/test_file.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: emit custom `UnmarshalJSON` on parent structs containing untagged-enum fields**: structs whose Go-bound fields include any untagged-enum (`#[serde(untagged)]`) named type previously deserialized into the bare struct shape, which left those fields as `nil` because Go's default `json.Unmarshal` doesn't dispatch on shape. The backend now collects every binding field whose type is `TypeRef::Named(n)` or `TypeRef::Optional(TypeRef::Named(n))` where `n` is in `data_enum_names`, and emits a `func (s *Parent) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error` that decodes through an anonymous helper struct using `json.RawMessage` for those fields, then dispatches each raw payload through the matching `Unmarshal<EnumName>(raw)` helper. (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/types.rs:gen_struct_type`)

- **alef-backend-swift: call `.intoRust()` on first-class DTO args at call sites**: the Swift binding's `emit_client_class` previously passed Swift first-class DTO arguments directly into Rust-bridge function calls without invoking `.intoRust()`, leaving the Rust side with a Swift wrapper value instead of the materialised Rust DTO and failing the swift-bridge type check. Each first-class DTO argument now flows through `.intoRust()` at the call site, matching the convention already in place for nested constructor extern calls. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs:emit_client_class`)

- **alef-e2e/kotlin_android: apply Android Gradle Plugin in e2e build.gradle.kts**: the e2e fixture-driven Android test app's generated `build.gradle.kts` referenced `android { ... }` DSL blocks but never applied the `com.android.application` plugin, so `./gradlew assemble` failed with "android extension not found". The codegen now applies the AGP at the top of the test-app gradle script. (`src/e2e/codegen/kotlin_android.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/java: iterate streams via `.iterator()` instead of calling `Iterator` methods on `Stream`**: streaming-assertion codegen called `.hasNext()` / `.next()` directly on `java.util.stream.Stream`, which Java disallows (those methods live on `Iterator`). The codegen now resolves through `.iterator()` first, mirroring the idiomatic Java streaming-consumer pattern. (`src/e2e/codegen/streaming_assertions.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: unwrap `Optional<T>` in facade returns + propagate to stream-method named results**: facade methods whose Rust return is `Option<T>` now call the raw-class method through `.orElse(null)` so the public facade signature (`@Nullable T`) matches the raw class signature (`Optional<T>`). The bytes path is preserved (raw signature already returns `byte[]`, not `Optional<byte[]>`, so unwrapping would be a compile error). The streaming-method-named-result template now distinguishes optional vs non-optional returns at both the early-null check and the success path, wrapping through `Optional.of`/`Optional.empty()` only when the declared return is `Optional<T>`. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/java/templates/facade_class.jinja`, `src/backends/java/templates/stream_method_named_result.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: extend generic-container support to `HashMap<_,_>` and `Option<Vec|Map>` in public signatures**: facade param/return type resolution previously only special-cased `Vec<DTO>` → `List<DTO>` for non-opaque named types, leaving `HashMap<_,_>`, primitive vecs, and any optional-wrapped container falling through to the JNI primitive mapping (which served bare `String` for all generics). Now routes any `Vec<_>` / `Map<_,_>` (including `Option<Vec|Map>`) through the recursive `render_kotlin_type` helper, and switches the Jackson deserialization decision from `is_vec_of_dtos` to the broader `is_generic_container` predicate so `TypeReference<...>` is used for every container return. Public Kotlin signatures now match the Jackson-serialized payload regardless of the inner element type. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: primitive-only direct constructor + shared JSON-fallback predicate**: primitive-only serde DTOs (e.g. `Point { row: u32, column: u32 }`, `ByteRange { start: usize, end: usize }`) now get a positional constructor extern emitted to the swift-bridge `extern "Rust"` block instead of falling through to a JSON-roundtrip path whose Rust-side shim was never emitted. Adds shared `requires_json_fallback` predicate consulted by both the Swift binding side (`gen_bindings.rs`) and the Rust crate side (`gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`) so any type Swift will JSON-encode is guaranteed to have a matching Rust shim. Closes the `RustBridge.pointFromJson` / `byteRangeFromJson` compile gap in tslp's swift binding. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/extern_block.rs`, `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/json_fallback.rs` (new), `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`, `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/wrappers.rs`, snapshot tests in `src/backends/swift/tests/`)

- **alef-cli: per-language setup workdir for swift/kotlin-android/dart/zig**: setup install commands now run from each binding's manifest directory (`packages/swift`, `packages/kotlin-android`, `packages/dart`, `packages/zig`) instead of the repo root. Closes "Could not find Package.swift" timeouts during `alef setup` invocations for languages whose manifest does not live at the workspace root. Other languages (python, node, ruby, php, go, java, csharp, elixir, wasm, ffi) retain current behavior of running from repo root. (`src/core/config/setup_defaults.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/commands.rs`, `src/core/tests/setup_config_test.rs` (new))

- **alef-backend-php: promote static stub parameters after the first optional argument**:
  PHP stubs for static API methods now mark parameters after the first optional
  argument as nullable with `= null`, matching the generated PHP facade and
  keeping PHPStan/PHP syntax valid for Rust functions whose required parameters
  follow optional ones. (`src/backends/php/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`,
  `src/backends/php/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: place Kotlin sample sources under their declared package path**:
  JVM scaffold now writes `Sample.kt` beneath
  `src/main/kotlin/<package>/sample/`, so Detekt package/path validation passes
  for generated Kotlin package names. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/kotlin.rs`,
  `crates/alef-scaffold/src/tests.rs`)

## [0.16.18] - 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-java: import `Optional` for opaque optional method returns**: the opaque-handle import scanner now uses the same public return-type mapper as the generated method signature, so `Option<T>` methods emit both `Optional<T>` and the required `java.util.Optional` import. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/java/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: unwrap Java `Optional` in opaque method wrappers**: Kotlin JVM wrapper classes now append `.orElse(null)` when delegating opaque methods that return Rust `Option<T>`, matching the public Kotlin `T?` signature. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-cli: avoid .NET query-mode shorthand in C# builds**: the default C# build command now uses `dotnet build --verbosity quiet` instead of `-q`, avoiding SDKs where `-q` exits non-zero when `CoreCompile` is not up to date. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/commands.rs`)

## [0.16.17] - 2026-05-16

### Added

- **alef-codegen: YARD doc emission (`emit_yard_doc`, `render_yard_sections`) + Magnus backend wiring**: shared module gains a Ruby YARD-flavoured renderer that translates rustdoc `# Arguments` / `# Returns` / `# Errors` sections into `# @param` / `# @return` / `# @raise` tags. The Magnus backend now emits YARD blocks above generated tagged-enum wrapper classes in `packages/<crate>/lib/*/native.rb`, restoring IDE tooltips and RDoc rendering that were previously a no-op when `gen_bindings/classes.rs` ignored `TypeDef.doc`. 4 new unit tests on the shared module, 1 integration test on the Magnus backend. (`src/codegen/doc_emission.rs`, `src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/magnus/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-extendr: roxygen2 docs on R class envs, enum class envs, and richer free-function blocks**: class envs (`Type <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())`) now carry a roxygen block derived from `TypeDef.doc` with one `#' @field` line per public field; flat data-enum class envs gain a `#' @field` per variant from `EnumVariant.doc`; JSON-passthrough enum class envs gain a title/description block (no `@field` since callers go through the opaque `__inner` JSON blob). Free-function roxygen blocks now parse rustdoc sections via `alef-codegen::parse_rustdoc_sections` + `parse_arguments_bullets` so `# Arguments` bullet bodies drive `#' @param` lines, `# Returns` drives `#' @return`, `# Errors` becomes `#' @section Errors:`, and raw headings stop leaking into the description body. 3 new unit tests. (`src/backends/extendr/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/extendr/templates/r_type_class_env.jinja`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: KDoc emission for types, methods, fields, enum variants**: the kotlin-android backend previously had zero `.doc` references in `gen_bindings.rs`, producing undocumented `.kt` files even when rustdoc was rich. Wires `emit_kdoc(...)` calls at every type/method/field/variant emission site, matching the alef-backend-kotlin pattern. Adds 2 unit tests and updates the snapshot fixture. (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin/src/lib.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`, snapshot)

- **alef-docs: Kotlin/KotlinAndroid, Swift, Dart, Zig signature renderers**: replaces the `// Phase 1: {lang} backend signature generation` stubs in `signatures.rs` with full `render_kotlin_fn_sig`, `render_swift_fn_sig`, `render_dart_fn_sig`, `render_zig_fn_sig`, plus method-signature arms for all four. Kotlin/KotlinAndroid share a renderer; statics emit `@JvmStatic`, fallible functions emit `@Throws(...)`. Swift emits `public static func` / `public func` with `throws` keyword and trailing `-> Ret`. Dart emits optional params as `[Type? name]` (bracketed positional). Zig emits `pub fn name(...) ErrorTy!Ret` for fallible functions, `pub fn name(...) Ret` otherwise; instance methods include a `self: *const Receiver` first param, statics omit it. Closes the per-language API reference docs (`docs/reference/api-{kotlin-android,swift,dart,zig}.md`) for the v3.5.0 binding additions. Adds 17 new unit tests. (`crates/alef-docs/src/signatures.rs`)

- **alef-core: `FieldDef.original_type: Option<String>`** preserves the pre-sanitization Rust type string (e.g. `"Vec<(String, String)>"`) so backends can reconstruct proper serialization logic for tuple types that the sanitizer downgrades. Populated by `sanitize_unknown_types()` when a field's type is rewritten. (`src/core/ir.rs`, `src/extract/extractor/{helpers,types}.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: emit `*_len()` companion for `c_char` returns**: every `pub unsafe extern "C" fn` returning `*mut c_char` now has a sibling `_len() -> usize` that returns the same string's byte length without allocating. Enables safe slice construction in Zig (drops `[:0]const u8` in favour of `[]const u8`) and Java FFM Panama bindings. (`src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/functions.rs`, `src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

### Changed

- **[BREAKING] alef-backend-swift: eliminate JSON roundtrip in DTO `intoRust()`**: Swift DTOs no longer serialize through `JSONEncoder` + Rust-side `from_json` shim. The Swift binding now calls a Rust-side bulk constructor extern via swift-bridge (`#[swift_bridge(init)] fn new(...)`), mirroring the existing direct-field-access pattern in `init(_ rb:) throws`. Internal bridge architecture change; public Swift API unchanged. Affects all DTOs that previously emitted `JSONEncoder().encode(self)` in `intoRust()` — primitive-only, nested-struct (`try self.field.intoRust()`), `Vec<Primitive | String | Named>` (materialised as `RustVec<T>`), and `Option<Primitive | String>` fields all take the direct path. DTOs whose constructor extern is not emitted (no `Default` impl, JSON-bridged Map/Json fields, or fields excluded via `[crates.<crate>.swift] exclude_fields`) keep the JSON fallback. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/extern_block.rs`, tests in `src/backends/swift/tests/snapshot_test.rs`)

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-java: preserve `Optional<T>` in opaque method return signatures**: Java opaque-handle methods now use the public return-type mapper instead of the FFI boundary mapper, so methods returning `Option<String>` emit `Optional<String>` signatures to match their generated `Optional.empty()` / `Optional.of(...)` bodies. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/java/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: keep byte-slice returns as `[]byte` in fallible methods**: Go methods that return `TypeRef::Bytes` but can fail while marshaling a non-opaque receiver now return `([]byte, error)` instead of `(*[]byte, error)`, matching the generated `unmarshalBytes` helper and idiomatic Go slice semantics. (`src/backends/go/src/type_map.rs`, `src/backends/go/tests/bug_fixes_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-go: emit shape-discriminated `Unmarshal` for untagged enums**: `Unmarshal{EnumName}` now splits its emission on `enum_def.serde_untagged`. Tagged enums keep the existing wire-struct-and-switch pattern. Untagged enums (where `enum_def.serde_tag` is `None`) now emit a first-byte shape sniffer (`'"'` → string, `'['` → array, `'{'` → object, else numeric) that gates each variant, then attempts `json.Unmarshal` into each concrete struct in declaration order, returning the first success. Previously the wire struct was empty `struct {}` but the switch unconditionally accessed `wire.Type`, causing `wire.Type undefined (type struct{} has no field or method Type)` Go compile errors on every untagged enum (e.g. `RerankDocument`, `OcrDocument`). (`src/backends/go/src/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/go/tests/bug_fixes_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin: use payload-derived field name in untagged sealed-class serializer's `when` branch**: the custom serializer for newtype/tuple variants emitted literal `value.field0`, but the data-class declarations for those variants already render with payload-derived field names via `kotlin_field_name_with_type` (e.g. `data class Single(val value: String)`). Kotlin compilation failed with `Unresolved reference 'field0'` on `EmbeddingInput`, `ModerationInput`, `RerankDocument`, `StopSequence`, `ToolChoice`, `UserContent`. The `when`-branch now reuses the same helper, computing the correct field name from the field's type. (`src/backends/kotlin/src/gen_bindings/object_wrapper.rs`)

- **alef-backend-kotlin-android: null-coalesce nullable primitive bridge args to JNI defaults**: the facade emits public params like `baseUrl: String? = null`, `timeoutSecs: Long? = null` and passes them through to a JNI `external fun` declaration whose signature is non-nullable. The `bridge_args` closure previously only null-coalesced DTO/Vec<DTO>/opaque param types; primitive scalars (`String?`, `Long?`, `Int?`, `Double?`, `Bool?`) fell through to bare `name`, producing `Argument type mismatch: actual type is 'String?', but 'String' was expected` Kotlin compile errors. Now emits `name ?: <zero>` where zero matches the JNI signature (`""` for `String`, `0L` for `i64/u64`, `0` for `i32/u32`, `0.0` for floats, `false` for `bool`). (`src/backends/kotlin-android/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/kotlin-android/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-cli: re-stamp `test_apps/` hashes after the `all` pipeline finalizes binding hashes**: registry-mode e2e files in `test_apps/` (generated by `alef e2e generate --registry`) carry `alef:hash:<sha>` markers computed from the sources hash of the upstream Rust crate. Any commit that modifies a watched Rust source file rotates the sources hash — but `alef all` never touched `test_apps/`, so their embedded hashes immediately went stale. `alef verify --exit-code` reported 188-257 stale `test_apps/` files on every CI run. The `all` pipeline now collects every alef-headered file under `e2e_config.registry.output` (default `test_apps/`) and re-runs `finalize_hashes` over them with the current sources hash, re-stamping their `alef:hash:` lines without regenerating their content. (`crates/alef-cli/src/main.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/generate.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/mod.rs`)

- **alef-cli: make post-generation formatter warnings actionable**: formatter failures now include both stdout and stderr instead of dropping stdout-only diagnostics. Python package formatting is scoped to the generated stub package when configured, avoiding unrelated hand-written package tests. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/format.rs`)

- **alef-scaffold: suppress php-cs-fixer runtime-version advisories in generated format commands**: PHP package scaffolds now set `setUnsupportedPhpVersionAllowed(true)` in `.php-cs-fixer.dist.php`, run php-cs-fixer format scripts with `--quiet`, and stop using the deprecated `PHP_CS_FIXER_IGNORE_ENV` composer-script prefix. (`crates/alef-scaffold/src/languages/php.rs`, `crates/alef-scaffold/src/tests.rs`)

- **alef-backend-wasm: `Vec<(K, V)>` enum fields use `JsValue` + `serde_wasm_bindgen`**: tagged-enum variants with fields whose original type was `Vec<(String, String)>` (sanitized to `Vec<String>`) are now stored as `Option<JsValue>` in the wasm struct rather than `Option<Vec<String>>`. Binding-to-core reads via `serde_wasm_bindgen::from_value::<Vec<(String, String)>>`, core-to-binding writes via `serde_wasm_bindgen::to_value`. Wire format `[["k","v"],...]` is preserved. Fixes compile errors in `html-to-markdown-wasm` for `NodeContent::MetadataBlock { entries }`. (`src/backends/wasm/gen_bindings/enums.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/extract.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: drop `-> ()` on unit-returning wrapper fns**: `wrappers.rs:gen_method_wrappers` emitted `pub fn parser_reset(client: &mut Parser) -> () { ... }` for any `&mut self` method returning `()` without an error type, which clippy flags as `unused_unit` (denied in tslp under `-D warnings`). Now omits the return clause entirely when `return_ty == "()"`. Affects every Unit-returning method exposed via swift-bridge wrappers. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/wrappers.rs`)

- **alef-backend-ffi: `*_len()` companion uses correct fail-path return**: the `_len()` companion's parameter-validation early-return (null pointer, UTF-8 decode error, etc.) was emitting the primary function's `return std::ptr::null_mut();` instead of `return 0;`, causing E0308 type mismatches (`*mut _` vs `usize`) when the underlying function returns `*mut c_char`. `gen_free_function_len_companion` now passes a synthetic `TypeRef::Primitive(PrimitiveType::Usize)` to `gen_param_conversion` so `null_return_value` resolves to `"0"` for the fail-path. Affects every `*_len()` companion emitted for `String`/`Path`/`Json`/`Named`/`Vec`/`Map`/`Bytes` returning functions. (`src/backends/ffi/src/gen_bindings/functions.rs:gen_free_function_len_companion`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit doc comments on opaque-class methods and first-class struct fields**: methods previously inlined an ad-hoc `for line in method.doc.lines()` loop with a stale comment ("Emit `/// doc` when available."); now route through the shared `emit_doc_comment` helper, matching how types/enums/errors/variants already emit. First-class struct fields surface their rustdoc above the generated `public let` declaration — previously emitted no doc at all. Plain prose passes through verbatim; `# Errors`/`# Returns` flow through as DocC-compatible text. 2 regression tests. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: emit bidirectional `From` impls for `OptionsField` newtype wrappers**: the `OptionsField` bind_via path generates a factory (`make_{trait}_handle`) and options-helper (`{opts}_from_json_with_{field}`) that call `TypeAlias::from(inner)`, `<inner>::from(handle)`, and `OptionsType::from(core_opts)`. These three `From` impls were missing — only enums emitted one-direction `From` via match arms. `emit_options_field_from_impls` now emits all three bidirectional newtype-struct impls (`.0` field access) with a deduplication guard so multiple bridges sharing the same alias or options type don't produce duplicate `impl` blocks. Fixes E0308 and E0277 in every consumer using `bind_via = "options_field"` (e.g. html-to-markdown's `HtmlVisitor` bridge). (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/plugin_inbound.rs`, `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: only emit first-class Swift DTOs for directly bridgeable field shapes**: complex serde DTOs with maps, vectors, nested wrappers, paths, bytes, JSON, or duration fields now remain RustBridge typealiases instead of generating uncompilable Codable/Hashable wrappers that called missing bridge accessors or JSON factory shims. Simple primitive/string DTOs keep first-class Codable wrappers and call snake_case RustBridge accessors. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: require direct RustBridge constructors for first-class DTOs**: Swift first-class DTO emission now uses the same constructor/direct-conversion eligibility as `intoRust()`, so serde DTOs without a generated `#[swift_bridge(init)]` constructor remain RustBridge typealiases instead of calling missing `{type}FromJson` shims. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`, `src/backends/swift/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

## [0.16.16] - 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-java: drop redundant `import java.util.stream.Stream;` from streaming opaque-handle classes**: the streaming method body template (`streaming_iterator_method.jinja`) uses fully-qualified names (`java.util.stream.Stream<T>` in the method signature, `java.util.stream.StreamSupport.stream(...)` in the bridge body), so the unconditional `imports.push("java.util.stream.Stream")` emitted an import that never resolved to a short-form identifier in the file body. Checkstyle's `UnusedImports` rule rejected `DefaultClient.java:12` in liter-llm after regeneration against v0.16.15. Drop the import push; rely on FQNs in the template throughout. Adds a regression test asserting the import is absent in generated opaque-handle facades with streaming adapters. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`, `src/backends/java/tests/gen_bindings_test.rs`)

## [0.16.15] - 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **alef-cli: resolve `PostBuildStep::RunCommand` working directory from `base_dir.join(crate_dir)`**: the dispatcher previously ran every backend-emitted `RunCommand` from the workspace `base_dir`, but commands like `flutter_rust_bridge_codegen generate` expect the binding crate root (where `flutter_rust_bridge.yaml` lives, relative to `crate_dir`). The Dart FRB step now runs in the correct directory and finds its config file. Also adds `src/backends/dart/examples/rewrite_lib_dart.rs` so the post-frb sealed-variant rewriter can be applied as a one-shot when callers bypass alef's build pipeline (e.g. running `task dart:codegen` directly). (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/commands.rs`, `src/backends/dart/examples/rewrite_lib_dart.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: nest Jackson POJO builders inside their owning record**: every DTO record now embeds its `Builder` as `public static final class Builder` rather than emitting `FooBuilder.java` as a sibling top-level class. Cuts the generated file count for the Java package by ~32% (54 fewer `.java` files in liter-llm) and matches idiomatic Java conventions (`Foo.Builder` pattern, mirroring `ImmutableList.Builder`, `LocalDate.Builder`, etc.). The `@JsonDeserialize(builder = ...)` annotation now references `Foo.Builder.class`. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/types.rs`, snapshot tests refreshed)

- **alef-backend-dart: pass generated config to flutter_rust_bridge post-build command**: the Dart backend's post-build `RunCommand` now invokes `flutter_rust_bridge_codegen generate --config-file packages/dart/rust/flutter_rust_bridge.yaml`, matching the root-relative post-build working directory and ensuring FRB reads the emitted `rust_input`/`dart_output` settings instead of failing with "Please provide `rust_input`" or "Cannot find config_file". (`src/backends/dart/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/node: use workspace version for registry test apps when no package version is configured**: Node registry-mode `package.json` now falls back to the crate's resolved Cargo version before the `0.1.0` placeholder, matching the Python/Ruby/PHP/Dart generators and preventing generated `test_apps/node` projects from depending on unpublished `@scope/package@0.1.0` versions. (`src/e2e/codegen/typescript/mod.rs`)

- **alef-e2e/elixir: emit version dependencies for registry test apps**: Elixir registry-mode `mix.exs` now uses the package version from `[e2e.registry.packages.elixir]` or the resolved crate version instead of carrying the local `../../packages/elixir` path into standalone `test_apps`. (`src/e2e/codegen/elixir.rs`)

- **alef-backend-magnus: omit binding-excluded fields from explicit `Default` impls**: Ruby DTOs with field-level defaults now filter `binding_excluded` fields consistently with struct and constructor generation, preventing hidden core fields from being emitted into binding-side `Self { ... }` defaults. (`src/backends/magnus/gen_bindings/classes.rs`)

- **alef-codegen/rustler: keep binding-excluded fields out of config constructors and cfg-gated core literals**: shared default-constructor helpers now filter `binding_excluded` fields before emitting keyword/option constructors, and binding-to-core conversion skips cfg-gated hidden fields so internal implementation details like caches and DI containers do not leak into generated Elixir NIF code or fail when the core feature is disabled. (`src/codegen/config_gen.rs`, `src/codegen/conversions/binding_to_core.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: omit binding-excluded fields from Rust bridge constructors**: Swift Rust bridge extern declarations and wrapper constructor bodies now share the binding-visible field set, preventing hidden core fields from appearing as unbound constructor parameters or serde assignments. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/extern_block.rs`, `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/wrappers.rs`, `src/backends/swift/src/gen_rust_crate/default_construction.rs`)

- **alef-backend-swift: only emit first-class Swift DTOs for directly bridgeable field shapes**: complex serde DTOs with maps, vectors, nested wrappers, paths, bytes, JSON, or duration fields now remain RustBridge typealiases instead of generating uncompilable Codable/Hashable wrappers that called missing bridge accessors or JSON factory shims. Simple primitive/string DTOs keep first-class Codable wrappers and now call snake_case RustBridge accessors. (`src/backends/swift/src/gen_bindings.rs`)

## [0.16.14] - 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **alef-backend-rustler: emit positional arity overloads for single-trailing-optional Elixir facade fns**: facade emission for functions with exactly one trailing optional parameter (e.g. `create_engine(config: Option<String>)`) previously collapsed to a single `def create_engine(opts \\ [])` body that called `Keyword.get(opts, :config)` — but the alef-e2e Elixir codegen always emits positional calls (`Kreuzcrawl.create_engine(engine_config)`), passing a string into a function expecting a `keyword()` list. The mismatch surfaced as `FunctionClauseError` on every e2e test that constructed an engine. The keyword-opts collapse threshold in `gen_bindings/mod.rs` is now `trailing_keyword_count >= 2` (was `> 0`): single trailing optional falls through to the existing arity-variants path (`def create_engine()` + `def create_engine(config)` with `nil` filled for the shortest arity), matching the e2e call shape. Functions with 2+ trailing optionals still collapse to the `opts \\ []` keyword form. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-cli: implement `PostBuildStep::RunCommand` dispatcher**: the runtime arm at `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/commands.rs` was previously a TODO debug-log that silently skipped every emitted `RunCommand` step (notably the Dart flutter_rust_bridge codegen invocation). Now spawns `std::process::Command::new(cmd).args(args).current_dir(base_dir)`, captures stdout/stderr, and propagates non-zero exit status as an `anyhow::Error` so the pipeline aborts. The dart backend's `RunCommand` step (`flutter_rust_bridge_codegen generate`) now executes automatically during `alef generate`. (`crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/commands.rs`)

- **alef-backend-dart: wire `rewrite_frb_sealed_variants` into the post-frb pipeline**: introduces a new `PostBuildStep::PostProcessFile { path, processor }` variant in `alef-core::backend` with `PostProcessor::FrbDartSealedVariants`. The dart backend's `build_config_for` appends this step after the `RunCommand` for `flutter_rust_bridge_codegen`, targeting `../lib/src/<crate>_bridge_generated/lib.dart`. The alef-cli dispatcher reads the file, applies `alef_backend_dart::rewrite_frb_sealed_variants`, and writes back only on change. Sealed-class factory params previously emitted as `{required PdfMetadata field0}` now resolve to `{required PdfMetadata metadata}` matching the kotlin/swift binding surface. (`src/core/backend.rs`, `crates/alef-cli/src/pipeline/commands.rs`, `src/backends/dart/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`, `src/backends/dart/src/frb_rewrite.rs`)

- **alef-backend-rustler: resolve concrete Elixir types for known union payloads**: tagged enum `@type` emission for variants like `FormatMetadata::Pdf(PdfMetadata)` previously emitted `@type pdf :: %{type: :pdf, metadata: map()}` — a loose `map()` typespec that defeats Dialyzer's variant-payload inference. The variant emitter now threads the API surface's known type names through `elixir_field_name_with_type`; payloads matching a known struct emit `Kreuzberg.PdfMetadata.t()`, falling back to `map()` only for opaque/unknown payloads. Field names (`metadata`, `value`, `value0`/`value1`) are unchanged. (`src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/helpers.rs`, `src/backends/rustler/src/gen_bindings/mod.rs`)

- **alef-backend-java: emit `@Nullable` on `Option<T>` parameters in FFI facade methods**: facade method signatures previously rendered `Option<T>` params as bare `String`/`Path`/etc. without nullability annotations, causing static analyzers (IntelliJ, Checker Framework) to flag `null` arguments as false-positives. Now emits `@Nullable T` with an `import org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable;` line whenever any optional parameter is present in the class body. Return types remain `Optional<T>` to preserve existing call sites; switching returns to `@Nullable T` is a follow-up that requires a deeper body refactor. (`src/backends/java/gen_bindings/facade.rs`, `src/backends/java/gen_bindings/ffi_class.rs`, `src/backends/java/templates/facade_file.jinja`)
